Essential AWS Services for Django, DevOps & Cloud Developers

Complete Cloud Services Reference

12 Categories
40+ Services
Django DevOps Focus
Features

Comprehensive AWS services reference with practical examples and best practices: EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, DynamoDB — featuring real-world scenarios and implementation guides for cloud architecture and development.

AWS Cloud DevOps Integration Infrastructure as Code Production Ready Best Practices

📑 Table of Contents

Essential AWS Services for Django, DevOps & Cloud Developers
Navigate through curated AWS services organized by category. Each service includes practical examples, best practices, and Django integration guides.
📍 Navigation Key: 📄 aws_3 This document 📄 aws_1 Compute, Storage, DB, Networking 📄 aws_2 DevTools, Management, Containers, Security
📄 aws_1

⚡ Compute

Virtual servers, serverless functions, and containers

  • Amazon EC2 - Virtual servers for hosting applications
  • AWS Lambda - Serverless functions for event-driven code
  • AWS Elastic Beanstalk - Easy application deployment and management
  • AWS Fargate - Serverless containers
📄 aws_1

💾 Storage

Object storage, block storage, and file systems

  • Amazon S3 - Object storage for static files, media, backups
  • Amazon EBS - Block storage for EC2 instances
  • Amazon EFS - Shared file system for multiple instances
📄 aws_1

🗄️ Database

Relational, NoSQL, and caching solutions

  • Amazon RDS - Managed PostgreSQL/MySQL for Django
  • Amazon Aurora - High-performance PostgreSQL/MySQL compatible
  • Amazon DynamoDB - NoSQL for high-scale applications
  • Amazon ElastiCache - Redis/Memcached for caching
📄 aws_1

🌐 Networking & CDN

VPC, load balancing, DNS, and content delivery

  • Amazon VPC - Virtual private network for resources
  • Elastic Load Balancing (ALB/NLB) - Distribute traffic across instances
  • Route 53 - DNS management and domain routing
  • AWS CloudFront - CDN for static assets and faster content delivery
📄 aws_2

🛠️ Developer Tools

CI/CD automation and code deployment

  • AWS CodePipeline - CI/CD automation
  • AWS CodeBuild - Build and test code
  • AWS CodeDeploy - Automated deployments
  • AWS CodeCommit - Git repository hosting
📄 aws_2

⚙️ Management & Governance

Monitoring, IaC, and server management

  • AWS CloudWatch - Monitoring, logs, and alerts
  • AWS CloudFormation - Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
  • AWS Systems Manager - Server management and automation
  • AWS CloudTrail - Audit logs and API tracking
📄 aws_2

🐳 Container Services

Container orchestration and registry

  • Amazon ECS - Container orchestration
  • Amazon EKS - Managed Kubernetes
  • Amazon ECR - Container image registry
📄 aws_2

🔒 Security & Identity

IAM, authentication, secrets, and encryption

  • AWS IAM - User and permission management
  • AWS Cognito - User authentication for applications
  • AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) - Free SSL/TLS certificates
  • AWS Secrets Manager - Store database credentials and API keys
  • AWS KMS - Encryption key management
📄 aws_3

🔗 Application Integration

Messaging, APIs, and workflow orchestration

  • Amazon SQS - Message queuing for async tasks
  • Amazon SNS - Push notifications and pub/sub messaging
  • Amazon API Gateway - Create and manage REST/GraphQL APIs
  • AWS Step Functions - Workflow orchestration
📄 aws_3

📊 Analytics & Monitoring

Log analysis, tracing, and data queries

  • Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights - Query and analyze logs
  • AWS X-Ray - Distributed tracing and debugging
  • Amazon Athena - SQL queries on S3 data
📄 aws_3

💾 Backup & DR

Centralized backups and archival storage

  • AWS Backup - Centralized backup management
  • Amazon S3 Glacier - Long-term archival storage
📄 aws_3

🚀 Quick Reference

Django stack templates and DevOps guides

  • Typical Django Stack on AWS
  • Daily DevOps Tasks
  • Service Comparison Matrix
  • Cost Calculator Guide

🔗 Application Integration Services

Decouple and scale your anihpj Django job portal with messaging, notifications, and API management services. Build resilient microservices architectures.

Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service)

Category: Messaging & Integration  |  Use Case: Asynchronous Task Processing  |  Pricing: Pay-per-request (Free tier: 1M requests/month)  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS SQS Docs

Fully managed message queuing service for decoupling and scaling microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. Perfect for handling background jobs in your anihpj Django job portal.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Fully Managed Message Queuing Service for Decoupling & Scaling Applications

Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. SQS eliminates the complexity and overhead of managing message-oriented middleware, letting you focus on differentiating work.

🎯 Key Benefits
🛠️ Fully Managed
  • No servers to manage
  • Automatic scaling
  • AWS handles infrastructure
🔄 Highly Available
  • Redundant across AZs
  • 99.9% availability SLA
  • Regional service
⚡ Unlimited Throughput
  • Millions of msgs/second
  • Auto-scales seamlessly
  • No capacity planning
💰 Pay-per-Use
  • No upfront costs
  • Pay only for requests
  • Free tier available
🔒 Secure
  • Encryption at rest/transit
  • IAM integration
  • VPC endpoints support
✅ Reliable
  • Redundant storage
  • Guaranteed delivery
  • Message persistence
💡 When to Use SQS: Perfect for your anihpj Django job portal when you need to:
  • Process job applications asynchronously in the background
  • Send email notifications without blocking web requests
  • Handle resume parsing and skill extraction tasks
  • Generate PDF reports for job postings
  • Decouple microservices (job search, recommendations, analytics)
⚖️ SQS vs. Other Messaging Services
Service Type Pattern Best For
Amazon SQS Message queuing Pull-based Task processing, decoupling
Amazon SNS Pub/sub Push-based Notifications, fan-out
EventBridge Event bus Event routing Complex rules, integrations
Kinesis Streaming Real-time Ordered data, analytics
RabbitMQ Message broker Self-managed Complex routing, AMQP
Kafka Event streaming High-throughput Event sourcing, logs
🏗️ SQS in AWS Ecosystem - Architecture Overview
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ANIHPJ JOB PORTAL WITH SQS                        │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                    WEB TIER (Django)                            │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │   EC2 Instances / ECS Tasks                              │  │ │
│  │  │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  Django Application (anihpj)                     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  - JobPost Views (Create, List, Detail)         │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  - Application Submission Handler                │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  - User Profile Management                       │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │                                                  │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  │  Send messages to SQS queues:          │     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  │  • Email notifications                 │     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  │  • Resume parsing                      │     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  │  • Skill extraction                    │     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  │  • PDF generation                      │     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘     │    │  │ │
│  │  │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                 │                                    │
│                                 ↓ boto3.client('sqs').send_message() │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                    AMAZON SQS QUEUES                            │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │  📬 email-notifications-queue                            │  │ │
│  │  │     Messages: 1,245  │  In-flight: 23                   │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │  📄 resume-processing-queue                              │  │ │
│  │  │     Messages: 89  │  In-flight: 5                        │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │  🔍 skill-extraction-queue (FIFO)                        │  │ │
│  │  │     Messages: 34  │  In-flight: 2                        │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │  💀 dead-letter-queue (DLQ)                              │  │ │
│  │  │     Messages: 3  │  Failed messages for investigation    │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                 │                                    │
│                                 ↓ Poll messages (long polling)       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                    WORKER TIER                                  │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │   Background Workers (EC2/Lambda/ECS)                    │  │ │
│  │  │                                                           │  │ │
│  │  │   📧 Email Worker → SES (Send emails)                     │  │ │
│  │  │   📄 Resume Worker → S3, Textract (Parse resumes)         │  │ │
│  │  │   🔍 Skill Worker → Comprehend (Extract skills)           │  │ │
│  │  │   📊 PDF Worker → Lambda (Generate reports)               │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                 │                                    │
│                                 ↓                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  RDS (PostgreSQL)                                               │ │
│  │  - Update job application status                                │ │
│  │  - Store parsed resume data                                     │ │
│  │  - Log email delivery status                                    │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  CloudWatch (Monitoring)                                        │ │
│  │  - Queue depth metrics                                          │ │
│  │  - Message age alarms                                           │ │
│  │  - Worker processing times                                      │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔄 Message Flow:
1. User submits job application on anihpj portal
2. Django saves application to RDS
3. Django sends message to 'resume-processing-queue'
4. Background worker polls queue
5. Worker downloads resume from S3, parses it
6. Worker updates RDS with extracted data
7. Worker deletes message from queue
8. Worker sends completion notification to 'email-notifications-queue'

🔧 Core Concepts

📚 SQS Fundamentals at a Glance
📬 Queues

Message containers that hold messages until processed. Two types: Standard (unlimited throughput) and FIFO (ordered).

📨 Messages

Data payloads up to 256KB. Contains body, attributes, and metadata. JSON is recommended format.

🔄 Polling

How consumers fetch messages. Long polling (20s) is recommended over short polling to reduce costs.

👁️ Visibility

Timeout period during which a message is hidden from other consumers while being processed.

📬 Queue Types

SQS offers two types of message queues: Standard and FIFO. Each has different characteristics and use cases.

📊 Standard Queue vs FIFO Queue
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    QUEUE TYPE COMPARISON                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  📬 STANDARD QUEUE (Default)                                      │
│  ├─ Throughput: Unlimited (millions of messages/sec)             │
│  ├─ Delivery: At-least-once (may deliver duplicates)             │
│  ├─ Ordering: Best-effort (not guaranteed)                       │
│  ├─ Latency: Low latency                                         │
│  └─ Use Cases:                                                    │
│     ✅ Email notifications (duplicates acceptable)                │
│     ✅ Image processing (order doesn't matter)                    │
│     ✅ Log aggregation                                            │
│     ✅ Most async tasks in anihpj                                 │
│                                                                   │
│  🔢 FIFO QUEUE (First-In-First-Out)                              │
│  ├─ Throughput: 3,000 msgs/sec (300 with batching)              │
│  ├─ Delivery: Exactly-once processing                            │
│  ├─ Ordering: Strict message ordering preserved                  │
│  ├─ Latency: Slightly higher than standard                       │
│  └─ Use Cases:                                                    │
│     ✅ Bank transactions (must be ordered)                        │
│     ✅ Job application workflow steps                             │
│     ✅ Inventory management                                       │
│     ✅ Critical order-dependent tasks                             │
│                                                                   │
│  Queue Naming Convention:                                        │
│  • Standard: my-queue-name                                       │
│  • FIFO: my-queue-name.fifo (MUST end with .fifo)               │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📊 Detailed Queue Feature Comparison
Feature Standard Queue FIFO Queue
Throughput Unlimited (nearly unlimited TPS) 3,000 TPS (300 batches/sec)
Ordering Best-effort ordering Strict FIFO ordering
Delivery At-least-once (may duplicate) Exactly-once processing
Deduplication No built-in deduplication 5-minute deduplication window
Message Groups N/A Message group IDs for parallel processing
Best For anihpj Email notifications, resume parsing, batch jobs Application workflow steps, payment processing
📨 Messages

Messages are the units of data that you send to and receive from SQS queues. Each message can contain up to 256 KB of text data in any format.

📦 Message Anatomy
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SQS MESSAGE STRUCTURE                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Message ID: 12345abc-6789-def0-1234-567890abcdef                │
│  ├─ Unique identifier assigned by SQS                            │
│  └─ Use for tracking and deduplication                           │
│                                                                   │
│  Receipt Handle: AQEBwJnK...xyz (long string)                    │
│  ├─ Token for deleting/modifying message                         │
│  └─ Changes each time message is received                        │
│                                                                   │
│  Message Body: (Max 256 KB)                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ {                                                          │  │
│  │   "task_type": "resume_parsing",                          │  │
│  │   "job_application_id": 12345,                            │  │
│  │   "resume_url": "s3://anihpj/resumes/john_doe.pdf",       │  │
│  │   "user_id": 789,                                         │  │
│  │   "submitted_at": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z",                 │  │
│  │   "priority": "high"                                      │  │
│  │ }                                                          │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Message Attributes: (Optional, max 10 attributes)               │
│  ├─ application_type: String → "job_seeker"                      │
│  ├─ priority: Number → 5                                         │
│  ├─ created_by: String → "anihpj-web-01"                         │
│  └─ retry_count: Number → 0                                      │
│                                                                   │
│  System Attributes: (Set by SQS)                                 │
│  ├─ SentTimestamp: 1705488600000                                │
│  ├─ ApproximateReceiveCount: 1                                  │
│  ├─ ApproximateFirstReceiveTimestamp: 1705488605000             │
│  └─ SenderId: AIDAI...XYZ                                        │
│                                                                   │
│  Metadata:                                                       │
│  ├─ Size: 2.4 KB / 256 KB                                        │
│  ├─ Age: 5 seconds                                               │
│  ├─ Visibility Timeout: 30 seconds (default)                    │
│  └─ Retention Period: 4 days (default, max 14 days)             │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
💡 Message Size Limits:
  • Standard Message: Up to 256 KB
  • Extended Payload (via S3): Up to 2 GB using SQS Extended Client Library
  • Best Practice for anihpj: If resume data > 256 KB, store in S3 and send S3 URL in message
🔄 Polling Mechanisms

Consumers retrieve messages from SQS using polling. There are two types: short polling and long polling.

⚡ Short Polling vs Long Polling
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    POLLING COMPARISON                             │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  ⚡ SHORT POLLING (Default - Not Recommended)                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Consumer → [ReceiveMessage] → SQS                         │  │
│  │              ↓ Immediate response (0 sec wait)             │  │
│  │              Returns: Messages (if available) or Empty     │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Checks only SUBSET of SQS servers                         │  │
│  │  ├─ May return empty even if messages exist elsewhere     │  │
│  │  └─ Requires frequent polling = Higher costs              │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Timeline:                                                 │  │
│  │  Request → Response [0ms] → Wait [1000ms] → Request again │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  ⏳ LONG POLLING (Recommended - More Efficient)                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Consumer → [ReceiveMessage] → SQS                         │  │
│  │              ↓ Waits up to 20 seconds                      │  │
│  │              Returns: Messages as soon as available        │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Checks ALL SQS servers for messages                       │  │
│  │  ├─ More reliable message retrieval                       │  │
│  │  └─ Reduces empty responses = Lower costs                 │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Timeline:                                                 │  │
│  │  Request → Wait [0-20s] → Response with messages          │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Cost Comparison (1M messages):                                  │
│  • Short Polling: 1M requests × $0.40/million = $0.40           │
│  • Long Polling: 50K requests × $0.40/million = $0.02 (95% ↓)   │
│                                                                   │
│  ✅ For anihpj: Always use Long Polling!                         │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
👁️ Visibility Timeout

When a consumer receives a message, it becomes temporarily invisible to other consumers. This prevents duplicate processing.

⏱️ Visibility Timeout Workflow
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 VISIBILITY TIMEOUT LIFECYCLE                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Step 1: Message in Queue (Visible)                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 SQS Queue                                              │  │
│  │  [Message A] ← Visible to all consumers                    │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           │ Consumer polls queue                  │
│                           ↓                                       │
│  Step 2: Message Retrieved (Invisible)                           │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 SQS Queue                                              │  │
│  │  [Message A - INVISIBLE] ← Hidden for 30 seconds (default) │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  👷 Worker Processing                                       │  │
│  │  • Parse resume                                            │  │
│  │  • Extract skills                                          │  │
│  │  • Update database                                         │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           │ Within 30 seconds                     │
│                           ↓                                       │
│  Step 3a: SUCCESS - Delete Message                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ✅ Processing Complete                                     │  │
│  │  Worker calls: DeleteMessage(receipt_handle)               │  │
│  │  → Message removed from queue permanently                  │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Step 3b: TIMEOUT - Message Returns to Queue                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ⏰ 30 seconds elapsed, no delete call                      │  │
│  │  → Message becomes visible again                           │  │
│  │  → Another consumer can process it                         │  │
│  │  → ApproximateReceiveCount increments                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Step 3c: EXTEND - Extend Visibility Timeout                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  🔄 Processing taking longer than expected                  │  │
│  │  Worker calls: ChangeMessageVisibility(receipt, +30)       │  │
│  │  → Timeout extended by 30 more seconds                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Visibility Timeout Settings:                                    │
│  • Minimum: 0 seconds (immediate visibility)                     │
│  • Maximum: 12 hours                                             │
│  • Default: 30 seconds                                           │
│  • Recommended for anihpj:                                       │
│    - Email sending: 30 seconds                                   │
│    - Resume parsing: 5 minutes (300 seconds)                     │
│    - PDF generation: 2 minutes (120 seconds)                     │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
⚠️ Common Visibility Timeout Mistakes:
  • Too Short: Message returns to queue before processing completes → duplicate processing
  • Too Long: Failed messages stuck invisible for too long → delayed retry
  • Best Practice: Set timeout to 6× average processing time for safety margin

🚀 Getting Started with SQS for Django

📋 Quick Start Checklist
1️⃣
Prerequisites

AWS Account, IAM user with SQS permissions, AWS CLI configured

2️⃣
Install SDK

pip install boto3 - AWS SDK for Python

3️⃣
Create Queue

Create Standard or FIFO queue via console, CLI, or SDK

4️⃣
Integrate

Add SQS client to Django, send messages, build consumers

📋 Prerequisites
Before You Begin:
  • ✅ AWS Account created
  • ✅ IAM user with SQS permissions
  • ✅ AWS CLI installed and configured
  • ✅ Boto3 library installed: pip install boto3
  • ✅ Your anihpj Django project ready
🔐 Required IAM Permissions
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "sqs:CreateQueue",
        "sqs:DeleteQueue",
        "sqs:GetQueueUrl",
        "sqs:GetQueueAttributes",
        "sqs:SetQueueAttributes",
        "sqs:ListQueues",
        "sqs:SendMessage",
        "sqs:SendMessageBatch",
        "sqs:ReceiveMessage",
        "sqs:DeleteMessage",
        "sqs:DeleteMessageBatch",
        "sqs:ChangeMessageVisibility",
        "sqs:PurgeQueue"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:*:*:anihpj-*"
    }
  ]
}

Save as anihpj-sqs-policy.json and attach to your IAM user or role

🎯 Create Your First Queue (Step-by-Step)
Method 1: AWS Console (Visual)
Step 1: Navigate to SQS Console
┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. Sign in to AWS Console                 │
│ 2. Search for "SQS" in services           │
│ 3. Click "Simple Queue Service"           │
│ 4. Click "Create queue" button            │
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 2: Configure Queue Details
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Choose queue type:                                      │
│ ● Standard  (Recommended for anihpj)                    │
│ ○ FIFO                                                  │
│                                                         │
│ Queue name: anihpj-email-notifications                  │
│ (FIFO queues must end with .fifo)                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 3: Configuration Settings
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Visibility timeout: [300] seconds                       │
│ (Time message is hidden after retrieval)               │
│                                                         │
│ Message retention period: [4] days                     │
│ (How long messages stay in queue)                      │
│                                                         │
│ Delivery delay: [0] seconds                            │
│ (Initial delay before message is available)            │
│                                                         │
│ Maximum message size: [256] KB (max)                   │
│                                                         │
│ Receive message wait time: [20] seconds                │
│ (Enable long polling - RECOMMENDED)                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 4: Access Policy (Optional)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Choose method:                                          │
│ ● Basic (Recommended for beginners)                     │
│ ○ Advanced (JSON policy editor)                         │
│                                                         │
│ Who can send messages: Only queue owner                │
│ Who can receive messages: Only queue owner             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 5: Encryption (Optional but Recommended)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Server-side encryption: [Enabled]                       │
│ ☑ Enable SSE                                            │
│                                                         │
│ Choose AWS KMS key:                                     │
│ ● AWS managed key (aws/sqs) - Free                      │
│ ○ Customer managed key - $1/month                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 6: Dead-letter Queue (Recommended)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ☑ Enable dead-letter queue                              │
│                                                         │
│ Choose existing queue: [Create new DLQ]                │
│ New queue name: anihpj-email-notifications-dlq          │
│                                                         │
│ Maximum receives: [3]                                   │
│ (After 3 failed attempts, send to DLQ)                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 7: Tags (Optional)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Key: Environment    Value: production                   │
│ Key: Application    Value: anihpj                       │
│ Key: Purpose        Value: email-notifications          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 8: Review and Create
[Create Queue] ← Click to finish!
Method 2: AWS CLI (Fast)
# Create standard queue
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-email-notifications \
    --attributes '{
        "VisibilityTimeout": "300",
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "345600",
        "ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds": "20",
        "DelaySeconds": "0"
    }' \
    --tags Key=Environment,Value=production Key=Application,Value=anihpj

# Output:
# {
#     "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-email-notifications"
# }

# Create FIFO queue (for ordered processing)
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-job-workflow.fifo \
    --attributes '{
        "FifoQueue": "true",
        "ContentBasedDeduplication": "true",
        "VisibilityTimeout": "300"
    }'

# Create dead-letter queue
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-email-notifications-dlq

# Get queue URL (for use in code)
aws sqs get-queue-url --queue-name anihpj-email-notifications

# List all your queues
aws sqs list-queues --queue-name-prefix anihpj
Method 3: Python Boto3 (Programmatic)
# anihpj/utils/sqs_setup.py
import boto3
import json

def create_anihpj_queues():
    """Create all necessary SQS queues for anihpj job portal"""
    sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
    
    queues_config = {
        'anihpj-email-notifications': {
            'VisibilityTimeout': '30',
            'MessageRetentionPeriod': '345600',  # 4 days
            'ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds': '20',  # Long polling
        },
        'anihpj-resume-processing': {
            'VisibilityTimeout': '300',  # 5 minutes for parsing
            'MessageRetentionPeriod': '1209600',  # 14 days
            'ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds': '20',
        },
        'anihpj-skill-extraction': {
            'VisibilityTimeout': '180',  # 3 minutes
            'MessageRetentionPeriod': '604800',  # 7 days
            'ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds': '20',
        }
    }
    
    created_queues = {}
    
    for queue_name, attributes in queues_config.items():
        try:
            response = sqs.create_queue(
                QueueName=queue_name,
                Attributes=attributes,
                tags={
                    'Environment': 'production',
                    'Application': 'anihpj',
                    'ManagedBy': 'django'
                }
            )
            created_queues[queue_name] = response['QueueUrl']
            print(f"✅ Created: {queue_name}")
            print(f"   URL: {response['QueueUrl']}")
        except sqs.exceptions.QueueNameExists:
            # Queue already exists, get its URL
            response = sqs.get_queue_url(QueueName=queue_name)
            created_queues[queue_name] = response['QueueUrl']
            print(f"ℹ️  Already exists: {queue_name}")
    
    return created_queues

if __name__ == '__main__':
    queues = create_anihpj_queues()
    print(f"\n🎉 Total queues: {len(queues)}")
📤 Send & Receive Messages
Sending Messages from Django
# anihpj/jobpost/tasks.py
import boto3
import json
from django.conf import settings

class SQSClient:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.queue_urls = {
            'email': 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-email-notifications',
            'resume': 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-resume-processing',
        }
    
    def send_email_task(self, recipient, subject, template, context):
        """Send email notification task to SQS"""
        message_body = json.dumps({
            'task_type': 'send_email',
            'recipient': recipient,
            'subject': subject,
            'template': template,
            'context': context,
            'timestamp': str(timezone.now())
        })
        
        try:
            response = self.sqs.send_message(
                QueueUrl=self.queue_urls['email'],
                MessageBody=message_body,
                MessageAttributes={
                    'priority': {
                        'DataType': 'Number',
                        'StringValue': '5'
                    },
                    'application': {
                        'DataType': 'String',
                        'StringValue': 'anihpj'
                    }
                }
            )
            print(f"✅ Email task queued: {response['MessageId']}")
            return response['MessageId']
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Failed to queue email: {e}")
            return None
    
    def send_resume_processing_task(self, application_id, resume_url):
        """Send resume processing task to SQS"""
        message_body = json.dumps({
            'task_type': 'parse_resume',
            'application_id': application_id,
            'resume_url': resume_url,
            'requested_at': str(timezone.now())
        })
        
        response = self.sqs.send_message(
            QueueUrl=self.queue_urls['resume'],
            MessageBody=message_body,
            DelaySeconds=0  # Process immediately
        )
        return response['MessageId']

# Usage in views
from django.utils import timezone

def submit_job_application(request, job_id):
    # ... create application ...
    
    # Send email notification asynchronously
    sqs_client = SQSClient()
    sqs_client.send_email_task(
        recipient=application.user.email,
        subject='Application Received',
        template='emails/application_received.html',
        context={'job_title': application.job.title}
    )
    
    # Queue resume processing
    if application.resume_file:
        sqs_client.send_resume_processing_task(
            application_id=application.id,
            resume_url=application.resume_file.url
        )
    
    return JsonResponse({'status': 'success'})
Receiving Messages (Worker)
# anihpj/workers/email_worker.py
import boto3
import json
import time
from django.core.mail import send_mail
from django.template.loader import render_to_string

class EmailWorker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.queue_url = 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-email-notifications'
    
    def process_messages(self):
        """Poll queue and process email tasks"""
        print("🔄 Email worker started. Polling for messages...")
        
        while True:
            try:
                # Receive messages (long polling)
                response = self.sqs.receive_message(
                    QueueUrl=self.queue_url,
                    MaxNumberOfMessages=10,  # Batch processing
                    WaitTimeSeconds=20,  # Long polling
                    MessageAttributeNames=['All'],
                    AttributeNames=['All']
                )
                
                messages = response.get('Messages', [])
                
                if not messages:
                    print("📭 No messages. Waiting...")
                    continue
                
                print(f"📬 Received {len(messages)} messages")
                
                for message in messages:
                    try:
                        # Parse message body
                        body = json.loads(message['Body'])
                        receipt_handle = message['ReceiptHandle']
                        
                        # Process the email task
                        self.send_email(
                            recipient=body['recipient'],
                            subject=body['subject'],
                            template=body['template'],
                            context=body['context']
                        )
                        
                        # Delete message after successful processing
                        self.sqs.delete_message(
                            QueueUrl=self.queue_url,
                            ReceiptHandle=receipt_handle
                        )
                        print(f"✅ Email sent and message deleted")
                        
                    except Exception as e:
                        print(f"❌ Error processing message: {e}")
                        # Message will return to queue after visibility timeout
                
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                print("\n⏹️  Worker stopped by user")
                break
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"❌ Worker error: {e}")
                time.sleep(5)  # Wait before retrying
    
    def send_email(self, recipient, subject, template, context):
        """Send email using Django's email backend"""
        html_content = render_to_string(template, context)
        send_mail(
            subject=subject,
            message='',  # Plain text version
            from_email='noreply@anihpj.com',
            recipient_list=[recipient],
            html_message=html_content,
            fail_silently=False
        )

if __name__ == '__main__':
    worker = EmailWorker()
    worker.process_messages()

🔑 Key Features

🎯 SQS Key Features Overview
💀 Dead Letter Queues
  • Isolate failed messages
  • Automatic after X retries
  • Offline debugging
  • Prevent poison messages
⏰ Delay Queues
  • 0-15 minute delays
  • Queue or message level
  • Scheduled processing
  • Rate limiting support
🔢 FIFO Queues
  • Exactly-once delivery
  • Strict ordering
  • Message groups
  • Deduplication built-in
🔒 Encryption
  • SSE-SQS (AWS managed)
  • SSE-KMS (customer keys)
  • TLS in transit
  • Zero performance impact
💀 Dead Letter Queues (DLQ)

A Dead Letter Queue isolates messages that can't be processed successfully, preventing them from clogging your main queue.

🔄 DLQ Workflow
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               DEAD LETTER QUEUE WORKFLOW                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Step 1: Main Queue                                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 anihpj-resume-processing                               │  │
│  │  Message: { "application_id": 123, "resume_url": "..." }  │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓ Worker receives                       │
│  Step 2: Processing Attempt #1                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ❌ Failed: Resume file not found in S3                     │  │
│  │  → Message returns to queue                                │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓ After visibility timeout              │
│  Step 3: Processing Attempt #2                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ❌ Failed: Still not found                                 │  │
│  │  → ApproximateReceiveCount = 2                             │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓ Third attempt                         │
│  Step 4: Processing Attempt #3 (Max Receives)                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ❌ Failed: Persistent error                                │  │
│  │  → ApproximateReceiveCount = 3 (threshold reached!)        │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓ SQS automatically moves               │
│  Step 5: Dead Letter Queue                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  💀 anihpj-resume-processing-dlq                            │  │
│  │  Message stored for investigation                          │  │
│  │  → Set CloudWatch alarm                                    │  │
│  │  → Alert dev team                                          │  │
│  │  → Manual review required                                  │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Benefits:                                                       │
│  ✅ Prevent infinite retry loops                                 │
│  ✅ Main queue stays healthy                                     │
│  ✅ Debug failed messages offline                                │
│  ✅ Pattern detection (common failures)                          │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Configure DLQ with Boto3
# Create DLQ and attach to main queue
import boto3

sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')

# 1. Create dead-letter queue
dlq_response = sqs.create_queue(
    QueueName='anihpj-resume-processing-dlq',
    Attributes={
        'MessageRetentionPeriod': '1209600'  # 14 days (max)
    }
)
dlq_url = dlq_response['QueueUrl']

# 2. Get DLQ ARN
dlq_attrs = sqs.get_queue_attributes(
    QueueUrl=dlq_url,
    AttributeNames=['QueueArn']
)
dlq_arn = dlq_attrs['Attributes']['QueueArn']

# 3. Create main queue with DLQ configuration
main_response = sqs.create_queue(
    QueueName='anihpj-resume-processing',
    Attributes={
        'VisibilityTimeout': '300',
        'ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds': '20',
        'RedrivePolicy': json.dumps({
            'deadLetterTargetArn': dlq_arn,
            'maxReceiveCount': '3'  # Move to DLQ after 3 failed attempts
        })
    }
)

print(f"✅ Main queue: {main_response['QueueUrl']}")
print(f"💀 DLQ: {dlq_url}")
⏰ Delay Queues

Delay queues postpone delivery of messages for a specified duration, useful for scheduled tasks or rate limiting.

⏲️ Delay Queue Use Cases
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    DELAY QUEUE SCENARIOS                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Scenario 1: Delayed Welcome Email                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  User signs up → Send message with 1-hour delay            │  │
│  │  → Give time for email verification first                  │  │
│  │  → Then send welcome email with tips                       │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Scenario 2: Retry with Backoff                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  API call failed → Retry after 30 seconds                  │  │
│  │  Still failed → Retry after 60 seconds                     │  │
│  │  Still failed → Retry after 120 seconds                    │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Scenario 3: Scheduled Report Generation                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  User requests report → Send message with 5-min delay      │  │
│  │  → Allows batch aggregation                                │  │
│  │  → Reduces database load                                   │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Delay Settings:                                                 │
│  • Queue-level delay: 0-900 seconds (15 minutes max)            │
│  • Message-level delay: Overrides queue delay                    │
│  • FIFO queues: Delays respected for each message group         │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Send Message with Delay
# Send message with custom delay
response = sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    MessageBody=json.dumps({
        'task': 'send_welcome_email',
        'user_id': 123,
        'email': 'user@example.com'
    }),
    DelaySeconds=3600  # 1 hour delay
)

# Or set default delay for entire queue
sqs.set_queue_attributes(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    Attributes={
        'DelaySeconds': '300'  # 5 minutes default for all messages
    }
)
🔢 FIFO Queues (Ordered Processing)

FIFO queues guarantee message order and exactly-once processing, critical for workflows where sequence matters.

🎯 FIFO Features
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    FIFO QUEUE FEATURES                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. Message Ordering                                             │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Send Order:    Msg1 → Msg2 → Msg3 → Msg4                  │  │
│  │  Receive Order: Msg1 → Msg2 → Msg3 → Msg4 (GUARANTEED)     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  2. Message Deduplication                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Send: Msg A (dedup ID: abc123)                            │  │
│  │  Send: Msg A (dedup ID: abc123) ← Duplicate in 5 min       │  │
│  │  Result: Only 1 message delivered                          │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  3. Message Groups (Parallel Processing)                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Group "user-123": Msg1 → Msg2 → Msg3                      │  │
│  │  Group "user-456": Msg4 → Msg5 → Msg6                      │  │
│  │  → Process groups in parallel                              │  │
│  │  → Messages within group stay ordered                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Use Cases for anihpj:                                           │
│  ✅ Job application workflow steps (apply → screen → interview)  │
│  ✅ Payment processing (charge → confirm → receipt)              │
│  ✅ Document versioning (v1 → v2 → v3)                           │
│  ✅ Order fulfillment (order → pack → ship → deliver)            │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Working with FIFO Queues
# Create FIFO queue
sqs.create_queue(
    QueueName='anihpj-job-workflow.fifo',  # MUST end with .fifo
    Attributes={
        'FifoQueue': 'true',
        'ContentBasedDeduplication': 'true',  # Auto dedup based on body
        'VisibilityTimeout': '300'
    }
)

# Send message to FIFO queue
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl='https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-job-workflow.fifo',
    MessageBody=json.dumps({
        'step': 'initial_screening',
        'application_id': 123,
        'status': 'pending'
    }),
    MessageGroupId='application-123',  # REQUIRED for FIFO
    MessageDeduplicationId='app-123-step-1'  # Optional if ContentBasedDeduplication=true
)

# Send multiple messages in order
workflow_steps = [
    {'step': 'initial_screening', 'status': 'pending'},
    {'step': 'technical_interview', 'status': 'scheduled'},
    {'step': 'hr_interview', 'status': 'scheduled'},
    {'step': 'offer_letter', 'status': 'draft'}
]

for idx, step_data in enumerate(workflow_steps):
    sqs.send_message(
        QueueUrl=fifo_queue_url,
        MessageBody=json.dumps(step_data),
        MessageGroupId=f'application-{application_id}',
        MessageDeduplicationId=f'app-{application_id}-step-{idx}'
    )
    print(f"✅ Queued step {idx + 1}: {step_data['step']}")
🔒 Encryption & Security

SQS provides multiple layers of security including encryption at rest and in transit, IAM policies, and VPC endpoints.

🛡️ Security Layers
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SQS SECURITY ARCHITECTURE                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Layer 1: Encryption in Transit (HTTPS/TLS)                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Client → [TLS 1.2+] → SQS API Endpoint                    │  │
│  │  ✅ All API calls encrypted                                 │  │
│  │  ✅ No configuration needed (automatic)                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Layer 2: Encryption at Rest (KMS)                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Message arrives → Encrypted with KMS key → Stored          │  │
│  │  Message retrieved → Decrypted with KMS key → Delivered     │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Options:                                                   │  │
│  │  • AWS Managed Key (aws/sqs) - Free                        │  │
│  │  • Customer Managed Key - $1/month + API costs             │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Layer 3: IAM Policies (Access Control)                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Who can send messages?                                    │  │
│  │  Who can receive messages?                                 │  │
│  │  Who can manage queue?                                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Layer 4: Queue Policies (Resource-based)                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Allow S3 to send messages                                 │  │
│  │  Allow SNS to publish to queue                             │  │
│  │  Cross-account access                                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  Layer 5: VPC Endpoints (Private Access)                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  EC2 in VPC → VPC Endpoint → SQS                           │  │
│  │  ✅ No internet gateway needed                              │  │
│  │  ✅ Traffic stays within AWS network                        │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Enable Encryption
# Enable encryption when creating queue
sqs.create_queue(
    QueueName='anihpj-secure-queue',
    Attributes={
        'KmsMasterKeyId': 'alias/aws/sqs',  # Use AWS managed key
        'KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds': '300',  # 5 minutes (default)
        'SqsManagedSseEnabled': 'true'  # Enable SSE-SQS (simpler, no KMS costs)
    }
)

# Or enable on existing queue
sqs.set_queue_attributes(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    Attributes={
        'SqsManagedSseEnabled': 'true'
    }
)

# IAM policy for queue access
queue_policy = {
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {"AWS": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-web-role"},
            "Action": "sqs:SendMessage",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-email-notifications"
        },
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {"AWS": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-worker-role"},
            "Action": ["sqs:ReceiveMessage", "sqs:DeleteMessage"],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-email-notifications"
        }
    ]
}

sqs.set_queue_attributes(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    Attributes={'Policy': json.dumps(queue_policy)}
)

💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

💵 SQS Pricing Overview
📬 Standard Queue
  • $0.40 per million requests
  • 1M free requests/month
  • Lower cost per request
  • Best for high volume
🔢 FIFO Queue
  • $0.50 per million requests
  • 1M free requests/month
  • Higher cost per request
  • Worth it for ordering
📊 Data Transfer
  • In: Free
  • Out to AWS: Free
  • Out to Internet: Standard rates
  • Cross-region: Per GB fee
🆓 Free Tier
  • 1M requests/month (always)
  • Both Standard and FIFO
  • All request types included
  • Perfect for small apps
💵 Pricing Model

SQS uses a simple pay-per-request pricing model with a generous free tier.

💸 SQS Pricing Breakdown
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SQS PRICING (US-EAST-1)                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  FREE TIER (First 12 Months)                                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 Standard Queues: 1 Million requests/month              │  │
│  │  🔢 FIFO Queues: 1 Million requests/month                  │  │
│  │  ✅ Applies to all request types (send, receive, delete)   │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  STANDARD QUEUES (After Free Tier)                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  First 1 Million requests/month: FREE (always)             │  │
│  │  Next 100 Billion requests: $0.40 per million             │  │
│  │  Over 100 Billion: $0.24 per million                      │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Example for anihpj (10M requests/month):                  │  │
│  │  • First 1M: $0                                            │  │
│  │  • Next 9M: 9 × $0.40 = $3.60                              │  │
│  │  • Total: $3.60/month                                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  FIFO QUEUES (Higher Cost)                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  First 1 Million requests/month: FREE (always)             │  │
│  │  Next requests: $0.50 per million                          │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Example (1M requests/month):                              │  │
│  │  • All free!                                               │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Example (10M requests/month):                             │  │
│  │  • First 1M: $0                                            │  │
│  │  • Next 9M: 9 × $0.50 = $4.50                              │  │
│  │  • Total: $4.50/month                                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  ADDITIONAL COSTS                                                │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📦 Data Transfer:                                         │  │
│  │     • Within same region: FREE                             │  │
│  │     • To internet: $0.09/GB (first 10 TB)                  │  │
│  │     • Cross-region: $0.02/GB                               │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  🔐 KMS Encryption (if using customer key):                │  │
│  │     • Key storage: $1/month                                │  │
│  │     • API requests: $0.03 per 10,000 requests              │  │
│  │     • Recommendation: Use AWS managed key (free!)          │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  📊 CloudWatch (Optional):                                 │  │
│  │     • Basic metrics: FREE                                  │  │
│  │     • Custom metrics: $0.30 per metric/month               │  │
│  │     • Alarms: $0.10 per alarm/month                        │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
💡 Cost Optimization Strategies
🎯 10 Ways to Reduce SQS Costs
1. ⏳ USE LONG POLLING (CRITICAL!)
   Short polling: 1M empty requests = $0.40
   Long polling: 50K requests = $0.02
   Savings: 95% reduction ✅
   
   # Enable long polling
   sqs.set_queue_attributes(
       QueueUrl=queue_url,
       Attributes={'ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds': '20'}
   )

2. 📦 BATCH OPERATIONS
   Send 10 messages individually: 10 requests
   Send 10 messages in batch: 1 request
   Savings: 90% reduction ✅
   
   # Batch send
   sqs.send_message_batch(
       QueueUrl=queue_url,
       Entries=[
           {'Id': '1', 'MessageBody': msg1},
           {'Id': '2', 'MessageBody': msg2},
           # ... up to 10 messages
       ]
   )

3. 🗑️ DELETE MESSAGES PROMPTLY
   Don't let processed messages linger
   Set shorter retention for temporary queues
   
   # Set 1-day retention for temporary queues
   Attributes={'MessageRetentionPeriod': '86400'}

4. 🔄 AVOID EXCESSIVE POLLING
   Bad: Poll every 100ms when queue is empty
   Good: Use long polling (20 seconds)
   
5. 📊 USE STANDARD QUEUES WHEN POSSIBLE
   Standard: $0.40 per million
   FIFO: $0.50 per million
   Only use FIFO when ordering is critical

6. 🔐 USE AWS MANAGED ENCRYPTION KEY
   AWS managed (aws/sqs): FREE
   Customer managed: $1/month + API costs
   
7. 🎯 CONSOLIDATE QUEUES
   10 small queues → Higher overhead
   2-3 larger queues → More efficient
   Use message attributes to route

8. ⏱️ OPTIMIZE VISIBILITY TIMEOUT
   Too long → Messages stuck unnecessarily
   Too short → Duplicate processing = double cost
   
9. 📍 SAME-REGION ARCHITECTURE
   Cross-region data transfer costs money
   Keep producers and consumers in same region

10. 📈 MONITOR WITH CLOUDWATCH
    Track ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible
    Set alarms for queue depth
    Optimize worker count based on metrics

📈 Scalability & Performance

⚡ SQS Scalability Overview
📬
Standard Queue

Unlimited TPS, 120K in-flight messages, best-effort ordering

🔢
FIFO Queue

300 TPS (3,000 with batching), 20K in-flight messages, strict ordering

📦
Batch Operations

Up to 10 messages per batch, reduces API calls, lowers cost

🔄
Auto Scaling

Fully managed scaling, no provisioning needed, handles traffic spikes

⚡ Throughput Limits

SQS scales automatically to handle your workload with minimal limits.

📊 Throughput Specifications

│                    SQS THROUGHPUT LIMITS                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  STANDARD QUEUES                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  API Calls: Unlimited (nearly)                             │  │
│  │  ├─ SendMessage: Unlimited TPS                             │  │
│  │  ├─ ReceiveMessage: Unlimited TPS                          │  │
│  │  └─ DeleteMessage: Unlimited TPS                           │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Batch Operations: 10 messages per request                 │  │

│  │  └─ DeleteMessageBatch: 10 msgs/request                    │  │
│  │                                                             │  │

│  │  (Messages received but not deleted)                       │  │
│  │                                                             │  │

│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  FIFO QUEUES                                                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Without Batching:                                         │  │
│  │  ├─ 300 TPS (transactions per second)                      │  │
│  │  └─ ~26 million messages/day                               │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  With Batching (10 msgs/request):                          │  │

│  │  └─ ~260 million messages/day                              │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Per Message Group:                                        │  │
│  │  ├─ Up to 300 TPS per group                                │  │
│  │  └─ Use multiple groups for higher throughput              │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  Example: 10 message groups                                │  │
│  │  = 3,000 TPS total (with batching)                         │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  QUEUE LIMITS                                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Queues per account: Unlimited                             │  │
│  │  Message size: 256 KB (2 GB with Extended Client)          │  │
│  │  Message retention: 1 minute to 14 days                    │  │
│  │  Visibility timeout: 0 seconds to 12 hours                 │  │
│  │  Delay: 0 to 900 seconds (15 minutes)                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📦 Batch Operations

Batching dramatically improves throughput and reduces costs by processing multiple messages in a single API call.

Batch Send Example
# Send multiple email notifications in batch
def send_batch_email_notifications(user_emails):
    """Send email tasks in batches of 10"""
    sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
    queue_url = 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-email-notifications'
    
    # Prepare batch entries (max 10 per batch)
    batch_size = 10
    for i in range(0, len(user_emails), batch_size):
        batch = user_emails[i:i + batch_size]
        
        entries = []
        for idx, email_data in enumerate(batch):
            entries.append({
                'Id': str(idx),  # Unique within this batch
                'MessageBody': json.dumps({
                    'recipient': email_data['email'],
                    'subject': email_data['subject'],
                    'template': 'job_alert.html',
                    'context': email_data['context']
                }),
                'MessageAttributes': {
                    'priority': {
                        'DataType': 'Number',
                        'StringValue': str(email_data.get('priority', 5))
                    }
                }
            })
        
        # Send batch
        response = sqs.send_message_batch(
            QueueUrl=queue_url,
            Entries=entries
        )
        
        # Handle results
        if 'Successful' in response:
            print(f"✅ Sent {len(response['Successful'])} messages")
        
        if 'Failed' in response:
            print(f"❌ Failed {len(response['Failed'])} messages")
            for failure in response['Failed']:
                print(f"   ID {failure['Id']}: {failure['Message']}")

# Batch receive and delete
def process_batch():
    sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
    queue_url = 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-email-notifications'
    
    # Receive up to 10 messages
    response = sqs.receive_message(
        QueueUrl=queue_url,
        MaxNumberOfMessages=10,  # Max batch size
        WaitTimeSeconds=20
    )
    
    messages = response.get('Messages', [])
    if not messages:
        return
    
    # Process all messages
    delete_entries = []
    for msg in messages:
        try:
            process_email(json.loads(msg['Body']))
            
            # Add to delete batch
            delete_entries.append({
                'Id': msg['MessageId'],
                'ReceiptHandle': msg['ReceiptHandle']
            })
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")
    
    # Delete all successfully processed messages in batch
    if delete_entries:
        sqs.delete_message_batch(
            QueueUrl=queue_url,
            Entries=delete_entries
        )
🔄 Horizontal Scaling

Scale your SQS consumers horizontally by adding more workers without any configuration changes.

🚀 Auto-Scaling Workers
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│             HORIZONTAL SCALING ARCHITECTURE                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Low Load (100 messages/minute)                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 SQS Queue: 100 messages                                 │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  👷 Worker 1 (EC2/ECS)                                       │  │
│  │  Processing: 100 msgs/min (sufficient)                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│                                                                   │
│  Medium Load (500 messages/minute)                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 SQS Queue: 500 messages                                 │  │
│  │  CloudWatch: ApproximateNumberOfMessages > 200              │  │
│  │  → Trigger Auto Scaling Group                              │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓                                       │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐   │
│  │ 👷 Worker 1      │  │ 👷 Worker 2      │  │ 👷 Worker 3  │   │
│  │ 166 msgs/min     │  │ 167 msgs/min     │  │ 167 msgs/min │   │
│  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────┘   │
│                                                                   │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│                                                                   │
│  High Load (2,000 messages/minute)                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  📬 SQS Queue: 2,000 messages                               │  │
│  │  CloudWatch: ApproximateNumberOfMessages > 500              │  │
│  │  → Scale to 10 workers                                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ↓                                       │
│  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐         │
│  │Worker1│  │Worker2│  │Worker3│  │Worker4│  │Worker5│         │
│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min│         │
│  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘         │
│  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐  ┌───────┐         │
│  │Worker6│  │Worker7│  │Worker8│  │Worker9│  │Worker10│        │
│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min│  │200/min │        │
│  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘  └───────┘         │
│                                                                   │
│  Benefits:                                                       │
│  ✅ Workers don't need to know about each other                  │
│  ✅ No coordination required                                     │
│  ✅ Add/remove workers anytime                                   │
│  ✅ Automatic load distribution by SQS                           │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🎓 Advanced Topics

🎯 SQS Advanced Features Overview
⏳ Long Polling
  • Wait up to 20 seconds
  • Reduces empty responses
  • Lowers API costs
  • Recommended approach
🏷️ Message Attributes
  • Metadata without body change
  • Up to 10 attributes
  • String, Number, Binary
  • Filtering & routing
💀 Dead Letter Queues
  • Capture failed messages
  • Set max receive count
  • Debug & reprocess
  • Prevent message loss
⏰ Delay Queues
  • 0-900 second delays
  • Queue-level setting
  • Per-message override
  • Scheduled processing
⏳ Long Polling

Long polling reduces empty responses and API costs by waiting for messages to arrive.

Short Polling vs Long Polling Comparison
# SHORT POLLING (Bad - Don't use!)
while True:
    response = sqs.receive_message(
        QueueUrl=queue_url,
        WaitTimeSeconds=0  # Returns immediately
    )
    if not response.get('Messages'):
        time.sleep(1)  # Manual wait
        # Results in many empty API calls = Higher cost!

# LONG POLLING (Good - Recommended!)
while True:
    response = sqs.receive_message(
        QueueUrl=queue_url,
        MaxNumberOfMessages=10,
        WaitTimeSeconds=20,  # Wait up to 20 seconds
        MessageAttributeNames=['All']
    )
    # If no messages, SQS waits internally
    # Returns as soon as messages arrive
    # Fewer API calls = Lower cost!
🏷️ Message Attributes

Add metadata to messages without modifying the message body, useful for filtering and routing.

Using Message Attributes
# Send message with attributes
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    MessageBody=json.dumps({'user_id': 123, 'action': 'welcome_email'}),
    MessageAttributes={
        'priority': {
            'DataType': 'Number',
            'StringValue': '5'  # 1-10 scale
        },
        'email_type': {
            'DataType': 'String',
            'StringValue': 'transactional'
        },
        'campaign_id': {
            'DataType': 'String',
            'StringValue': 'onboarding_2026'
        },
        'retry_count': {
            'DataType': 'Number',
            'StringValue': '0'
        },
        'created_at': {
            'DataType': 'String',
            'StringValue': '2026-01-17T10:30:00Z'
        }
    }
)

# Receive and read attributes
response = sqs.receive_message(
    QueueUrl=queue_url,
    MessageAttributeNames=['All']  # Get all attributes
)

for message in response.get('Messages', []):
    body = json.loads(message['Body'])
    attributes = message.get('MessageAttributes', {})
    
    priority = int(attributes.get('priority', {}).get('StringValue', 5))
    email_type = attributes.get('email_type', {}).get('StringValue', 'unknown')
    
    print(f"Processing {email_type} email with priority {priority}")
🔍 Message Deduplication (FIFO)

FIFO queues automatically deduplicate messages within a 5-minute window.

Deduplication Strategies
# Method 1: Content-Based Deduplication (Automatic)
# Enable when creating queue
sqs.create_queue(
    QueueName='anihpj-workflow.fifo',
    Attributes={
        'FifoQueue': 'true',
        'ContentBasedDeduplication': 'true'  # SHA-256 hash of body
    }
)

# Send messages - SQS automatically deduplicates based on body
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=fifo_queue_url,
    MessageBody='{"action": "send_email", "user_id": 123}',
    MessageGroupId='user-123'
)

# Duplicate within 5 minutes is ignored automatically!
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=fifo_queue_url,
    MessageBody='{"action": "send_email", "user_id": 123}',  # Same body
    MessageGroupId='user-123'
)

# Method 2: Explicit Deduplication ID
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=fifo_queue_url,
    MessageBody=json.dumps({
        'action': 'send_email',
        'user_id': 123,
        'timestamp': '2026-01-17T10:30:00Z'  # Body changes each time
    }),
    MessageGroupId='user-123',
    MessageDeduplicationId='email-user-123-welcome'  # Custom ID
)

# Same deduplication ID within 5 minutes = ignored
sqs.send_message(
    QueueUrl=fifo_queue_url,
    MessageBody=json.dumps({
        'action': 'send_email',
        'user_id': 123,
        'timestamp': '2026-01-17T10:31:00Z'  # Different body
    }),
    MessageGroupId='user-123',
    MessageDeduplicationId='email-user-123-welcome'  # Same ID = deduplicated!
)
⭐ Priority Queues (Pattern)

SQS doesn't have built-in priority, but you can implement it using multiple queues.

Multi-Queue Priority Pattern
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PRIORITY QUEUE PATTERN                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  📬 anihpj-tasks-high (Critical: Password resets, payments)      │
│  📬 anihpj-tasks-medium (Important: Application confirmations)   │
│  📬 anihpj-tasks-low (Bulk: Newsletter, weekly digests)          │
│                                                                   │
│  Worker Processing Logic:                                        │
│  1. Check high priority queue first                              │
│  2. If empty, check medium priority                              │
│  3. If empty, check low priority                                 │
│  4. Repeat                                                        │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
# Priority queue worker
class PriorityWorker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.queues = {
            'high': 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-tasks-high',
            'medium': 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-tasks-medium',
            'low': 'https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123/anihpj-tasks-low'
        }
    
    def process(self):
        while True:
            # Try high priority first
            messages = self.receive_from_queue('high')
            if messages:
                self.process_messages(messages, priority='high')
                continue
            
            # Then medium priority
            messages = self.receive_from_queue('medium')
            if messages:
                self.process_messages(messages, priority='medium')
                continue
            
            # Finally low priority
            messages = self.receive_from_queue('low')
            if messages:
                self.process_messages(messages, priority='low')
            
            time.sleep(1)  # Brief pause if all queues empty
    
    def receive_from_queue(self, priority):
        response = self.sqs.receive_message(
            QueueUrl=self.queues[priority],
            MaxNumberOfMessages=10,
            WaitTimeSeconds=1  # Short wait for priority check
        )
        return response.get('Messages', [])

✨ Best Practices

🏆 SQS Best Practices Summary
🔒
Security
