        'company': 'TechCorp'
    }),
    Subject='New Job Posted',
    MessageAttributes={
        # String attribute
        'event_type': {
            'DataType': 'String',
            'StringValue': 'job_posted'
        },
        # String attribute for location filtering
        'location': {
            'DataType': 'String',
            'StringValue': 'New York'
        },
        # Number attribute for salary filtering
        'salary_min': {
            'DataType': 'Number',
            'StringValue': '120000'
        },
        # String array (as JSON)
        'skills': {
            'DataType': 'String.Array',
            'StringValue': json.dumps(['Python', 'Django', 'AWS'])
        },
        # Binary attribute (e.g., compressed data)
        'metadata': {
            'DataType': 'Binary',
            'BinaryValue': b'compressed_binary_data'
        }
    }
)
🔒 Encryption & Security
Enabling Encryption
# Create encrypted topic with KMS
sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1')

# Create topic with server-side encryption
topic = sns.create_topic(
    Name='anihpj-secure-notifications',
    Attributes={
        'KmsMasterKeyId': 'alias/aws/sns'  # AWS managed key
        # Or use custom key: 'KmsMasterKeyId': 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/xxx'
    }
)

# Enable encryption on existing topic
sns.set_topic_attributes(
    TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-job-alerts',
    AttributeName='KmsMasterKeyId',
    AttributeValue='alias/anihpj-sns-key'
)
🔒 SNS Security Best Practices:
  • ✅ Enable server-side encryption (SSE) with KMS
  • ✅ Use IAM policies for least-privilege access
  • ✅ Implement topic access policies to restrict publishers/subscribers
  • ✅ Use VPC endpoints for private connectivity
  • ✅ Enable CloudTrail logging for audit
  • ✅ Validate and sanitize message content

💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

💵 SNS Pricing Overview
📤 Publishes
  • $0.50 per million requests
  • First 1M free/month
  • Includes all topic types
  • FIFO slightly higher
📬 Deliveries
  • HTTP: $0.60 per million
  • SQS/Lambda: Free
  • Email: $2.00 per 100K
  • SMS: Varies by country
📱 Mobile Push
  • $0.50 per million
  • First 1M free/month
  • iOS, Android, Fire OS
  • Direct or topic publish
🆓 Free Tier
  • 1M publishes/month
  • 100K HTTP deliveries
  • 1K email deliveries
  • 1M mobile push
💵 Pricing Model
💸 SNS Pricing Breakdown

│                                                                   │

│  │  Lambda:         FREE (no additional charge)               │  │

│                                                                   │

💡 Cost Optimization Strategies
💰 Cost Optimization Tips:
  • Use Message Filtering: Reduce unnecessary deliveries by filtering at subscription level
  • Prefer SQS/Lambda: SNS → SQS and SNS → Lambda deliveries are free
  • Batch Operations: Use PublishBatch API to reduce API calls
  • Consolidate Messages: Combine related events into single messages
  • Use Raw Message Delivery: Reduces message size overhead for SQS
  • Monitor Usage: Set CloudWatch billing alarms

📈 Scalability & Performance

⚡ SNS Scalability Overview
📬
Standard Topics

Unlimited throughput, millions of messages per second, auto-scaling

🔢
FIFO Topics

300 TPS default, up to 3,000 with batching, strict ordering

📦
Batch Publishing

Up to 10 messages per batch, reduces API calls, improves throughput

🔄
Delivery Retry

Configurable retry policies, exponential backoff, DLQ support

⚡ Throughput Limits
SNS Service Limits
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SNS SERVICE LIMITS                             │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  TOPICS                                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Topics per account: 100,000 (soft limit)                  │  │
│  │  Subscriptions per topic: 12,500,000                       │  │
│  │  Filter policies per account: 200 (default)                │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  MESSAGES                                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Message size: 256 KB max                                  │  │
│  │  Message attributes: 10 per message                        │  │
│  │  Attribute name size: 256 characters                       │  │
│  │  Attribute value size: 256 KB (total with message)        │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  THROUGHPUT                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Standard Topics:                                          │  │
│  │  • Publish: Unlimited (soft: 30K TPS per topic)           │  │
│  │  • Delivery: Unlimited                                     │  │
│  │                                                             │  │
│  │  FIFO Topics:                                              │  │
│  │  • Publish: 300 TPS (3,000 with batching)                 │  │
│  │  • Per message group: 300 TPS                              │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📦 Batch Publishing
Using PublishBatch for High Throughput
# Batch publishing for efficiency
import boto3
import json

sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1')

def batch_publish_jobs(jobs, topic_arn):
    """Publish multiple job notifications in batches of 10"""
    
    results = []
    
    # Process in batches of 10 (SNS limit)
    for i in range(0, len(jobs), 10):
        batch = jobs[i:i+10]
        
        entries = []
        for idx, job in enumerate(batch):
            entries.append({
                'Id': str(idx),
                'Message': json.dumps({
                    'event': 'job_posted',
                    'job_id': job['id'],
                    'title': job['title']
                }),
                'Subject': f"New Job: {job['title']}",
                'MessageAttributes': {
                    'location': {
                        'DataType': 'String',
                        'StringValue': job['location']
                    }
                }
            })
        
        response = sns.publish_batch(
            TopicArn=topic_arn,
            PublishBatchRequestEntries=entries
        )
        
        results.append({
            'successful': len(response.get('Successful', [])),
            'failed': len(response.get('Failed', []))
        })
    
    return results

# Example: Publish 100 job notifications
jobs = [{'id': i, 'title': f'Job {i}', 'location': 'NYC'} for i in range(100)]
results = batch_publish_jobs(jobs, 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-jobs')
# Only 10 API calls instead of 100!
🔄 Delivery Policies
Configuring Delivery Retry Policy
# Set HTTP delivery policy with retries
sns.set_subscription_attributes(
    SubscriptionArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:topic:subscription-id',
    AttributeName='DeliveryPolicy',
    AttributeValue=json.dumps({
        "healthyRetryPolicy": {
            "numRetries": 10,
            "minDelayTarget": 1,      # 1 second minimum delay
            "maxDelayTarget": 60,     # 60 seconds maximum delay
            "numNoDelayRetries": 2,   # 2 immediate retries
            "numMinDelayRetries": 3,  # 3 retries at min delay
            "backoffFunction": "exponential"  # or "linear", "arithmetic"
        },
        "throttlePolicy": {
            "maxReceivesPerSecond": 100  # Rate limit deliveries
        },
        "requestPolicy": {
            "headerContentType": "application/json"
        }
    })
)

🎓 Advanced Topics

🎯 SNS Advanced Features Overview
📱 Mobile Push
  • iOS (APNs)
  • Android (FCM/GCM)
  • Fire OS (ADM)
  • Platform applications
📲 SMS Messaging
  • Worldwide delivery
  • Promotional/Transactional
  • Sender ID support
  • Opt-out management
📧 Email Notifications
  • Subscription confirmation
  • Plain text or JSON
  • Subject line support
  • Unsubscribe links
📨 Raw Delivery
  • No JSON wrapper
  • Direct message body
  • Smaller payload size
  • SQS/HTTP endpoints
📱 Mobile Push Notifications
Setting Up Mobile Push
# Mobile push notification setup
import boto3
import json

sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1')

# 1. Create platform application (one-time setup)
# For iOS (APNs)
ios_app = sns.create_platform_application(
    Name='anihpj-ios',
    Platform='APNS',  # or 'APNS_SANDBOX' for development
    Attributes={
        'PlatformCredential': 'YOUR_APNS_PRIVATE_KEY',
        'PlatformPrincipal': 'YOUR_APNS_CERTIFICATE'
    }
)

# For Android (FCM)
android_app = sns.create_platform_application(
    Name='anihpj-android',
    Platform='GCM',
    Attributes={
        'PlatformCredential': 'YOUR_FCM_SERVER_KEY'
    }
)

# 2. Register device token (when user installs app)
def register_device(platform_app_arn, device_token, user_id):
    endpoint = sns.create_platform_endpoint(
        PlatformApplicationArn=platform_app_arn,
        Token=device_token,
        CustomUserData=user_id
    )
    return endpoint['EndpointArn']

# 3. Send push notification to device
def send_push_notification(endpoint_arn, title, message, data=None):
    # Format message for different platforms
    payload = {
        'default': message,
        'APNS': json.dumps({
            'aps': {
                'alert': {'title': title, 'body': message},
                'sound': 'default',
                'badge': 1
            },
            'data': data or {}
        }),
        'GCM': json.dumps({
            'notification': {'title': title, 'body': message},
            'data': data or {}
        })
    }
    
    return sns.publish(
        TargetArn=endpoint_arn,
        Message=json.dumps(payload),
        MessageStructure='json'
    )

# Example: Notify user about job application status
send_push_notification(
    endpoint_arn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:endpoint/...',
    title='Application Update',
    message='Your application for Senior Developer has been reviewed!',
    data={'application_id': '123', 'action': 'view_status'}
)
📲 SMS Messaging
Sending SMS Messages
# SMS messaging with SNS
import boto3

sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1')

# Set SMS preferences (account-level)
sns.set_sms_attributes(
    attributes={
        'DefaultSMSType': 'Transactional',  # or 'Promotional'
        'DefaultSenderID': 'anihpj',
        'MonthlySpendLimit': '100',  # USD
        'UsageReportS3Bucket': 'anihpj-sms-logs'
    }
)

# Send SMS directly to phone number
def send_sms(phone_number, message):
    return sns.publish(
        PhoneNumber=phone_number,  # E.164 format: +1234567890
        Message=message,
        MessageAttributes={
            'AWS.SNS.SMS.SenderID': {
                'DataType': 'String',
                'StringValue': 'anihpj'
            },
            'AWS.SNS.SMS.SMSType': {
                'DataType': 'String',
                'StringValue': 'Transactional'
            }
        }
    )

# Example: Send interview reminder
send_sms(
    phone_number='+1234567890',
    message='Reminder: Your interview for Senior Developer at TechCorp is tomorrow at 10 AM. - anihpj'
)
📧 Email Notifications
Email Subscriptions
# Subscribe email to topic
sns.subscribe(
    TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-job-alerts',
    Protocol='email',
    Endpoint='user@example.com'
)
# User receives confirmation email and must click link to confirm

# Subscribe with JSON format (for programmatic processing)
sns.subscribe(
    TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-system-alerts',
    Protocol='email-json',
    Endpoint='alerts@anihpj.com'
)

# Publish with subject line
sns.publish(
    TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-job-alerts',
    Subject='New Jobs Matching Your Profile',  # Email subject
    Message='We found 5 new jobs matching your skills: Python, Django, AWS...'
)
📨 Raw Message Delivery
Enabling Raw Message Delivery
# Raw delivery sends message body directly without SNS JSON wrapper
# Useful for SQS and HTTP endpoints

# Enable raw delivery on subscription
sns.set_subscription_attributes(
    SubscriptionArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:topic:sub-id',
    AttributeName='RawMessageDelivery',
    AttributeValue='true'
)

# Without raw delivery, SQS receives:
# {
#   "Type": "Notification",
#   "MessageId": "xxx",
#   "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:...",
#   "Subject": "...",
#   "Message": "{\"job_id\": 123}",  ← Your message is escaped JSON
#   "Timestamp": "...",
#   ...
# }

# With raw delivery, SQS receives:
# {"job_id": 123}  ← Just your message directly!

# Benefits:
# ✅ Smaller message size
# ✅ No need to parse SNS wrapper
# ✅ Simpler consumer code
# ✅ Message attributes preserved

✨ Best Practices

🏆 SNS Best Practices Summary
🔒
Security

Enable encryption, use IAM policies, implement access policies, VPC endpoints

⚡
Performance

Batch publishing, message filtering, raw delivery for SQS endpoints

📊
Monitoring

CloudWatch metrics, delivery status logging, CloudTrail for audit

🔄
Reliability

Delivery retry policies, DLQ for failed deliveries, idempotent subscribers

🔒 Security Checklist
🛡️ SNS Security Best Practices:
  • ✅ Enable Encryption: Use SSE with AWS KMS for at-rest encryption
  • ✅ IAM Policies: Use least-privilege access for publish/subscribe
  • ✅ Topic Policies: Restrict who can publish and subscribe
  • ✅ VPC Endpoints: Keep traffic within AWS network
  • ✅ CloudTrail: Enable API logging for audit
  • ✅ Message Validation: Validate content before publishing
  • ✅ HTTPS Only: Use HTTPS endpoints for HTTP subscriptions
⚡ Performance Tips
Performance Optimization Guide
1. 📦 Use Batch Publishing
   ✅ PublishBatch API (up to 10 messages)
   Result: 10× fewer API calls

2. 🔍 Implement Message Filtering
   ✅ Filter at subscription level
   Result: Reduce unnecessary deliveries

3. 📨 Enable Raw Message Delivery
   ✅ For SQS and HTTP endpoints
   Result: Smaller payloads, simpler parsing

4. 🔢 Use FIFO Topics Wisely
   ⚠️ Only when ordering is required
   Standard topics have unlimited throughput

5. 📊 Monitor and Optimize
   ✅ Track NumberOfMessagesPublished
   ✅ Monitor delivery success rates
   ✅ Set up billing alerts
🔧 Error Handling
Robust Error Handling
# Robust SNS publishing with error handling
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class RobustSNSPublisher:
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name=region)
    
    def publish_with_retry(self, topic_arn, message, max_retries=3):
        """Publish with exponential backoff retry"""
        import time
        
        for attempt in range(max_retries):
            try:
                response = self.sns.publish(
                    TopicArn=topic_arn,
                    Message=message
                )
                logger.info(f"Published message: {response['MessageId']}")
                return response
                
            except ClientError as e:
                error_code = e.response['Error']['Code']
                
                if error_code == 'ThrottlingException':
                    # Exponential backoff
                    wait_time = (2 ** attempt) + random.uniform(0, 1)
                    logger.warning(f"Throttled, retrying in {wait_time}s")
                    time.sleep(wait_time)
                    
                elif error_code == 'InvalidParameterException':
                    logger.error(f"Invalid parameter: {e}")
                    raise  # Don't retry invalid parameters
                    
                elif error_code == 'AuthorizationErrorException':
                    logger.error(f"Authorization error: {e}")
                    raise  # Don't retry auth errors
                    
                else:
                    logger.error(f"SNS error: {e}")
                    if attempt == max_retries - 1:
                        raise
                    time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
        
        raise Exception("Max retries exceeded")
📊 Monitoring & Alerts
CloudWatch Monitoring Setup
# Set up CloudWatch alarms for SNS monitoring
import boto3

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')

# Alarm: High number of failed deliveries
cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
    AlarmName='anihpj-sns-delivery-failures',
    ComparisonOperator='GreaterThanThreshold',
    EvaluationPeriods=2,
    MetricName='NumberOfNotificationsFailed',
    Namespace='AWS/SNS',
    Period=300,
    Statistic='Sum',
    Threshold=10.0,
    ActionsEnabled=True,
    AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:alert-team'],
    AlarmDescription='High SNS delivery failures',
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'TopicName', 'Value': 'anihpj-job-notifications'}
    ]
)

# Alarm: Sudden spike in publishes (potential abuse)
cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
    AlarmName='anihpj-sns-publish-spike',
    ComparisonOperator='GreaterThanThreshold',
    EvaluationPeriods=1,
    MetricName='NumberOfMessagesPublished',
    Namespace='AWS/SNS',
    Period=60,
    Statistic='Sum',
    Threshold=10000.0,  # Adjust based on normal traffic
    ActionsEnabled=True,
    AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:alert-team'],
    AlarmDescription='Unusual spike in SNS publishes',
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'TopicName', 'Value': 'anihpj-job-notifications'}
    ]
)

💼 Common Use Cases

🎯 SNS Common Use Cases Overview
🚨
Application Alerts

System errors, threshold breaches, security incidents, deployment notifications

⚙️
Workflow Triggers

Event-driven architectures, Lambda triggers, step function integration

📡
Fan-out Patterns

One event to multiple systems, parallel processing, data replication

📱
User Notifications

Email alerts, SMS reminders, mobile push, multi-channel messaging

🚨 Application Alerts
System Alert Implementation for anihpj
# alerts/sns_alerts.py - Application alerting system
import boto3
import json
from django.conf import settings
from functools import wraps

class AlertService:
    SEVERITY_LEVELS = ['info', 'warning', 'error', 'critical']
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name=settings.AWS_REGION)
        self.topic_arn = settings.SNS_ALERTS_TOPIC_ARN
    
    def send_alert(self, title, message, severity='warning', context=None):
        """Send alert to operations team"""
        payload = {
            'title': title,
            'message': message,
            'severity': severity,
            'application': 'anihpj',
            'environment': settings.ENVIRONMENT,
            'context': context or {}
        }
        
        return self.sns.publish(
            TopicArn=self.topic_arn,
            Message=json.dumps(payload),
            Subject=f'[{severity.upper()}] anihpj: {title}',
            MessageAttributes={
                'severity': {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': severity}
            }
        )
    
    def alert_on_error(self, title):
        """Decorator to alert on function errors"""
        def decorator(func):
            @wraps(func)
            def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
                try:
                    return func(*args, **kwargs)
                except Exception as e:
                    self.send_alert(
                        title=title,
                        message=str(e),
                        severity='error',
                        context={'function': func.__name__, 'args': str(args)}
                    )
                    raise
            return wrapper
        return decorator

# Usage
alerts = AlertService()

@alerts.alert_on_error('Resume Processing Failed')
def process_resume(application_id):
    # If this fails, alert is automatically sent
    pass

# Manual alerts
alerts.send_alert(
    title='High Database Latency',
    message='Database query time exceeded 5s threshold',
    severity='warning',
    context={'avg_latency': '5.2s', 'query_count': 150}
)
⚙️ Workflow Triggers
Event-Driven Workflow Architecture
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              EVENT-DRIVEN WORKFLOW FOR ANIHPJ                     │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. JOB POSTED EVENT                                             │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Django → SNS Topic: job-posted                            │  │
│  │            │                                                │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Update Search Index                │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Generate Job Recommendations       │  │
│  │            ├──→ SQS: Email Notification Queue              │  │
│  │            └──→ Lambda: Update Analytics Dashboard         │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  2. APPLICATION SUBMITTED EVENT                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Django → SNS Topic: application-submitted                  │  │
│  │            │                                                │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Parse Resume (AI)                  │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Extract Skills                      │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Match to Requirements               │  │
│  │            ├──→ SQS: Send Confirmation Email               │  │
│  │            └──→ Lambda: Notify Employer                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  3. INTERVIEW SCHEDULED EVENT                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Django → SNS Topic: interview-scheduled                    │  │
│  │            │                                                │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: Send Calendar Invite                │  │
│  │            ├──→ Lambda: SMS Reminder (1 day before)        │  │
│  │            └──→ Lambda: Update Application Status           │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📡 Fan-out Patterns
Complete Fan-out Implementation
# services/event_publisher.py - Fan-out event system
import boto3
import json
from django.conf import settings
from django.db.models.signals import post_save
from django.dispatch import receiver
from jobpost.models import Job, JobApplication

class EventPublisher:
    """Centralized event publishing for fan-out patterns"""
    
    TOPICS = {
        'job_posted': 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-job-posted',
        'job_updated': 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-job-updated',
        'application_submitted': 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-app-submitted',
        'user_registered': 'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-user-registered',
    }
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.sns = boto3.client('sns', region_name=settings.AWS_REGION)
    
    def publish(self, event_type, data, attributes=None):
        """Publish event to appropriate topic"""
        topic_arn = self.TOPICS.get(event_type)
        if not topic_arn:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown event type: {event_type}")
        
        message_attributes = {
            'event_type': {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': event_type},
            'timestamp': {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': str(datetime.utcnow())}
        }
        if attributes:
            for key, value in attributes.items():
                message_attributes[key] = {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': str(value)}
        
        return self.sns.publish(
            TopicArn=topic_arn,
            Message=json.dumps(data),
            MessageAttributes=message_attributes
        )

publisher = EventPublisher()

# Django signals for automatic event publishing
@receiver(post_save, sender=Job)
def on_job_saved(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):
    event_type = 'job_posted' if created else 'job_updated'
    publisher.publish(
        event_type=event_type,
        data={
            'job_id': instance.id,
            'title': instance.title,
            'company': instance.company.name,
            'location': instance.location,
            'job_type': instance.job_type
        },
        attributes={
            'location': instance.location,
            'job_type': instance.job_type
        }
    )

@receiver(post_save, sender=JobApplication)
def on_application_submitted(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):
    if created:
        publisher.publish(
            event_type='application_submitted',
            data={
                'application_id': instance.id,
                'job_id': instance.job.id,
                'applicant_id': instance.user.id
            }
        )

🔧 Troubleshooting

🚨 Common SNS Issues & Solutions
❌
Delivery Failures

Messages not reaching subscribers, endpoint errors, retry exhaustion

🔐
Permission Issues

Access denied errors, IAM policy misconfigurations, cross-account access

🔍
Filtering Problems

Filter policies not working, unexpected message delivery, policy syntax

❓
FAQ & Common Questions

Message ordering, duplicates, throughput limits, cost optimization

❌ Delivery Failures
Troubleshooting Delivery Issues
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SNS DELIVERY FAILURE DIAGNOSTIC FLOWCHART           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│                    Message Not Delivered?                         │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ▼                                       │
│               ┌───────────────────────┐                          │
│               │ Check CloudWatch Logs │                          │
│               │ for SNS topic metrics │                          │
│               └───────────┬───────────┘                          │
│                           │                                       │
│       ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────┐                  │
│       ▼                   ▼                   ▼                  │
│  ┌─────────┐       ┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────┐             │
│  │ HTTP/S  │       │   Lambda    │     │   SQS    │             │
│  │Endpoint │       │ Subscriber  │     │  Queue   │             │
│  └────┬────┘       └──────┬──────┘     └────┬─────┘             │
│       │                   │                  │                   │
│       ▼                   ▼                  ▼                   │
│  • Endpoint              • Lambda           • Queue policy       │
│    accessible?             errors?            allows SNS?       │
│  • Returns 2xx?          • IAM role OK?     • Encryption key    │
│  • SSL cert valid?       • Timeout?           access?           │
│  • Subscription          • Memory limit?    • DLQ configured?   │
│    confirmed?                                                    │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Debugging Delivery Failures
# debugging/sns_diagnostics.py - Delivery failure debugging
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class SNSDiagnostics:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sns = boto3.client('sns')
        self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
        self.logs = boto3.client('logs')
    
    def check_topic_health(self, topic_arn):
        """Comprehensive topic health check"""
        results = {
            'topic_exists': False,
            'subscription_count': 0,
            'pending_confirmations': 0,
            'recent_failures': 0,
            'issues': []
        }
        
        # Check topic exists
        try:
            attrs = self.sns.get_topic_attributes(TopicArn=topic_arn)
            results['topic_exists'] = True
            results['subscription_count'] = int(attrs['Attributes'].get('SubscriptionsConfirmed', 0))
            results['pending_confirmations'] = int(attrs['Attributes'].get('SubscriptionsPending', 0))
        except self.sns.exceptions.NotFoundException:
            results['issues'].append('Topic does not exist')
            return results
        
        # Check recent delivery metrics
        metrics = self.cloudwatch.get_metric_data(
            MetricDataQueries=[
                {
                    'Id': 'failures',
                    'MetricStat': {
                        'Metric': {
                            'Namespace': 'AWS/SNS',
                            'MetricName': 'NumberOfNotificationsFailed',
                            'Dimensions': [{'Name': 'TopicName', 'Value': topic_arn.split(':')[-1]}]
                        },
                        'Period': 3600,
                        'Stat': 'Sum'
                    }
                }
            ],
            StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=24),
            EndTime=datetime.utcnow()
        )
        
        if metrics['MetricDataResults'][0]['Values']:
            results['recent_failures'] = sum(metrics['MetricDataResults'][0]['Values'])
        
        if results['pending_confirmations'] > 0:
            results['issues'].append(f'{results["pending_confirmations"]} subscriptions pending confirmation')
        
        if results['recent_failures'] > 0:
            results['issues'].append(f'{results["recent_failures"]} delivery failures in last 24h')
        
        return results
    
    def check_subscription_status(self, topic_arn):
        """Check all subscriptions for a topic"""
        subscriptions = []
        paginator = self.sns.get_paginator('list_subscriptions_by_topic')
        
        for page in paginator.paginate(TopicArn=topic_arn):
            for sub in page['Subscriptions']:
                status = {
                    'endpoint': sub['Endpoint'],
                    'protocol': sub['Protocol'],
                    'confirmed': sub['SubscriptionArn'] != 'PendingConfirmation',
                    'issues': []
                }
                
                if not status['confirmed']:
                    status['issues'].append('Subscription pending confirmation')
                
                if sub['Protocol'] in ['http', 'https']:
                    # HTTP endpoints might be unreachable
                    status['issues'].append('Verify endpoint is accessible and returns 2xx')
                
                subscriptions.append(status)
        
        return subscriptions

# Usage
diagnostics = SNSDiagnostics()
health = diagnostics.check_topic_health('arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-notifications')
print(f"Issues found: {health['issues']}")
🔐 Permission Issues
⚠️ Common IAM Permission Errors
  • AccessDeniedException: Missing sns:Publish permission on topic
  • AuthorizationError: Cross-account access requires resource-based policy
  • InvalidParameter: KMS key access denied for encrypted topics
  • SubscriptionConfirmation fails: Endpoint URL not accessible or returns error
IAM Policy Troubleshooting
// PROBLEM: Lambda can't publish to SNS topic
// SOLUTION: Add this policy to Lambda execution role

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "sns:Publish",
                "sns:GetTopicAttributes"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-*"
        },
        // If topic uses KMS encryption
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "kms:GenerateDataKey",
                "kms:Decrypt"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/your-key-id",
            "Condition": {
                "StringEquals": {
                    "kms:ViaService": "sns.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}

// PROBLEM: Cross-account SNS access denied
// SOLUTION: Add resource-based policy to SNS topic

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "AllowCrossAccountPublish",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {
                "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::OTHER_ACCOUNT_ID:role/PublisherRole"
            },
            "Action": "sns:Publish",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications"
        }
    ]
}
🔍 Filtering Problems
Filter Policy Debugging
# Filter policies must match MessageAttributes, not message body

# WRONG - Filter policy won't work without matching MessageAttributes
sns.publish(
    TopicArn=topic_arn,
    Message=json.dumps({'event_type': 'job_posted'})  # This is the body!
)

# RIGHT - Include MessageAttributes that match your filter policy
sns.publish(
    TopicArn=topic_arn,
    Message=json.dumps({'event_type': 'job_posted'}),
    MessageAttributes={
        'event_type': {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': 'job_posted'}  # This is what filters use!
    }
)

# Filter policy examples:
filter_policies = {
    # Match exact value
    "event_type": ["job_posted"],
    
    # Match multiple values (OR)
    "event_type": ["job_posted", "job_updated"],
    
    # Numeric matching
    "salary": [{"numeric": [">=", 50000, "<=", 150000]}],
    
    # Prefix matching
    "location": [{"prefix": "US-"}],
    
    # Anything-but matching
    "status": [{"anything-but": "draft"}],
    
    # Check attribute exists
    "urgent": [{"exists": True}]
}

# Test your filter policy
def test_filter_policy(filter_policy, message_attributes):
    """
    Simulate filter policy matching (simplified)
    """
    for key, conditions in filter_policy.items():
        if key not in message_attributes:
            return False
        
        attr_value = message_attributes[key]['StringValue']
        
        # Check if any condition matches
        matched = False
        for condition in conditions:
            if isinstance(condition, str):
                if attr_value == condition:
                    matched = True
            elif isinstance(condition, dict):
                if 'prefix' in condition:
                    if attr_value.startswith(condition['prefix']):
                        matched = True
        
        if not matched:
            return False
    
    return True
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Question Answer
Are messages delivered in order? Standard topics: No, best-effort ordering. FIFO topics: Yes, strict ordering within message groups
Can messages be duplicated? Standard: Yes, at-least-once delivery. FIFO: Exactly-once delivery with deduplication
How long are messages retained? SNS doesn't retain messages. Once delivered (or delivery fails), message is gone. Use SQS for persistence.
Max message size? 256 KB (262,144 bytes). For larger payloads, store in S3 and publish reference URL.
Max subscribers per topic? 12.5 million for SMS, 100,000 for other protocols. Use fan-out to SQS for unlimited.
Can I unsubscribe users programmatically? Yes, using sns.unsubscribe(SubscriptionArn='...'). Store subscription ARN after subscribing.

📚 API & CLI Reference

🛠️ SNS API Overview

  • DeleteTopic
  • ListTopics
  • GetTopicAttributes
  • SetTopicAttributes
🔗 Subscription Operations
  • Subscribe
  • Unsubscribe
  • ConfirmSubscription
  • ListSubscriptions
  • GetSubscriptionAttributes
📤 Publish Operations
  • Publish
  • PublishBatch
  • SetSMSAttributes
  • GetSMSAttributes
  • CheckIfPhoneNumberIsOptedOut
📱 Mobile Push
  • CreatePlatformApplication
  • CreatePlatformEndpoint
  • ListEndpointsByPlatformApp
  • GetEndpointAttributes
  • DeleteEndpoint
💻 AWS CLI Commands
Essential SNS CLI Commands
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TOPIC MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Create standard topic
aws sns create-topic --name anihpj-notifications

# Create FIFO topic
aws sns create-topic --name anihpj-events.fifo \
    --attributes FifoTopic=true,ContentBasedDeduplication=true

# List all topics
aws sns list-topics

# Get topic attributes
aws sns get-topic-attributes \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications

# Set topic attribute (encryption)
aws sns set-topic-attributes \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --attribute-name KmsMasterKeyId \
    --attribute-value alias/aws/sns

# Delete topic
aws sns delete-topic \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUBSCRIPTION MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Subscribe email
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint admin@anihpj.com

# Subscribe SQS queue
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --protocol sqs \
    --notification-endpoint arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-queue

# Subscribe Lambda
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --protocol lambda \
    --notification-endpoint arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:process-notification

# List subscriptions for topic
aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications

# Add filter policy to subscription
aws sns set-subscription-attributes \
    --subscription-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications:abc123 \
    --attribute-name FilterPolicy \
    --attribute-value '{"event_type": ["job_posted"]}'

# Unsubscribe
aws sns unsubscribe \
    --subscription-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications:abc123

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PUBLISHING MESSAGES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Simple publish
aws sns publish \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --message "New job posted: Senior Python Developer"

# Publish with subject (for email)
aws sns publish \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --subject "New Job Alert" \
    --message "A new job matching your criteria has been posted."

# Publish with message attributes
aws sns publish \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-notifications \
    --message '{"job_id": 123, "title": "Python Developer"}' \
    --message-attributes '{"event_type":{"DataType":"String","StringValue":"job_posted"}}'

# Publish to FIFO topic
aws sns publish \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-events.fifo \
    --message "Application submitted" \
    --message-group-id "app-123" \
    --message-deduplication-id "unique-id-456"

# Send SMS
aws sns publish \
    --phone-number +1234567890 \
    --message "Your interview is scheduled for tomorrow at 2 PM"
🐍 Boto3 SDK Reference
Complete Boto3 SNS Operations
# Complete SNS SDK reference for anihpj
import boto3
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

class SNSManager:
    """Comprehensive SNS management class"""
    
    def __init__(self, region_name='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('sns', region_name=region_name)
        self.resource = boto3.resource('sns', region_name=region_name)
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # TOPIC OPERATIONS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def create_topic(self, name: str, fifo: bool = False, tags: Dict = None) -> str:
        """Create SNS topic"""
        attributes = {}
        if fifo:
            if not name.endswith('.fifo'):
                name += '.fifo'
            attributes['FifoTopic'] = 'true'
            attributes['ContentBasedDeduplication'] = 'true'
        
        params = {'Name': name}
        if attributes:
            params['Attributes'] = attributes
        if tags:
            params['Tags'] = [{'Key': k, 'Value': v} for k, v in tags.items()]
        
        response = self.client.create_topic(**params)
        return response['TopicArn']
    
    def delete_topic(self, topic_arn: str) -> None:
        """Delete SNS topic"""
        self.client.delete_topic(TopicArn=topic_arn)
    
    def list_topics(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all topic ARNs"""
        topics = []
        paginator = self.client.get_paginator('list_topics')
        for page in paginator.paginate():
            topics.extend([t['TopicArn'] for t in page['Topics']])
        return topics
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # SUBSCRIPTION OPERATIONS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def subscribe(
        self,
        topic_arn: str,
        protocol: str,
        endpoint: str,
        filter_policy: Optional[Dict] = None,
        raw_message_delivery: bool = False
    ) -> str:
        """Subscribe to topic"""
        params = {
            'TopicArn': topic_arn,
            'Protocol': protocol,
            'Endpoint': endpoint,
            'ReturnSubscriptionArn': True
        }
        
        attributes = {}
        if filter_policy:
            attributes['FilterPolicy'] = json.dumps(filter_policy)
        if raw_message_delivery and protocol in ['sqs', 'http', 'https']:
            attributes['RawMessageDelivery'] = 'true'
        
        if attributes:
            params['Attributes'] = attributes
        
        response = self.client.subscribe(**params)
        return response['SubscriptionArn']
    
    def set_filter_policy(self, subscription_arn: str, filter_policy: Dict) -> None:
        """Update subscription filter policy"""
        self.client.set_subscription_attributes(
            SubscriptionArn=subscription_arn,
            AttributeName='FilterPolicy',
            AttributeValue=json.dumps(filter_policy)
        )
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # PUBLISH OPERATIONS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def publish(
        self,
        topic_arn: str,
        message: str,
        subject: Optional[str] = None,
        attributes: Optional[Dict] = None,
        message_group_id: Optional[str] = None,  # For FIFO
        deduplication_id: Optional[str] = None   # For FIFO
    ) -> str:
        """Publish message to topic"""
        params = {
            'TopicArn': topic_arn,
            'Message': message
        }
        
        if subject:
            params['Subject'] = subject
        
        if attributes:
            params['MessageAttributes'] = {
                k: {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': str(v)}
                for k, v in attributes.items()
            }
        
        if message_group_id:
            params['MessageGroupId'] = message_group_id
        if deduplication_id:
            params['MessageDeduplicationId'] = deduplication_id
        
        response = self.client.publish(**params)
        return response['MessageId']
    
    def publish_batch(
        self,
        topic_arn: str,
        messages: List[Dict]
    ) -> Dict:
        """Batch publish up to 10 messages"""
        entries = []
        for i, msg in enumerate(messages[:10]):
            entry = {
                'Id': str(i),
                'Message': msg['message']
            }
            if 'subject' in msg:
                entry['Subject'] = msg['subject']
            if 'attributes' in msg:
                entry['MessageAttributes'] = {
                    k: {'DataType': 'String', 'StringValue': str(v)}
                    for k, v in msg['attributes'].items()
                }
            entries.append(entry)
        
        return self.client.publish_batch(
            TopicArn=topic_arn,
            PublishBatchRequestEntries=entries
        )

# Usage example
sns_manager = SNSManager()

# Create topic with tags
topic_arn = sns_manager.create_topic(
    'anihpj-notifications',
    tags={'Environment': 'production', 'Application': 'anihpj'}
)

# Subscribe with filter
subscription_arn = sns_manager.subscribe(
    topic_arn=topic_arn,
    protocol='sqs',
    endpoint='arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123:anihpj-queue',
    filter_policy={'event_type': ['job_posted', 'job_updated']}
)

# Publish message
message_id = sns_manager.publish(
    topic_arn=topic_arn,
    message=json.dumps({'job_id': 123, 'title': 'Python Dev'}),
    subject='New Job Posted',
    attributes={'event_type': 'job_posted', 'location': 'US-NY'}
)

🔗 AWS Integration

🌐 SNS Integration with AWS Services
⚡ Lambda Integration

Trigger Lambda functions automatically when messages are published

  • Event-driven processing
  • Automatic scaling
  • No polling required
📦 SQS Integration

Fan-out pattern: SNS to multiple SQS queues

  • Buffered message processing
  • Dead letter queues
  • Different processing speeds
🎯 EventBridge Integration

Route SNS events to EventBridge for complex routing

  • Advanced filtering rules
  • Cross-account/region
  • Schema registry
🐍 Django Integration

Native SNS integration for web applications

  • Celery task triggers
  • Django signals
  • Async notifications
⚡ Lambda Integration
SNS to Lambda Architecture
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SNS → LAMBDA INTEGRATION FOR ANIHPJ                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  ┌───────────┐     ┌───────────┐     ┌────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Django   │────▶│ SNS Topic │────▶│  Lambda Functions      │  │
│  │  anihpj   │     │           │     │                        │  │
│  └───────────┘     └─────┬─────┘     │  • process_application │  │
│                          │           │  • send_notifications  │  │
│                          │           │  • update_analytics    │  │
│                          │           │  • trigger_workflows   │  │
│                          │           └────────────────────────┘  │
│                          │                                       │
│                          │           ┌────────────────────────┐  │
│                          └──────────▶│  SQS Dead Letter       │  │
│                                      │  (Failed invocations)  │  │
│                                      └────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Lambda Function for SNS Events
# lambda_function.py - Process SNS events
import json
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-events')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Process SNS notifications for anihpj
    
    SNS wraps the message in a specific structure:
    event = {
        'Records': [{
            'Sns': {
                'TopicArn': '...',
                'Message': '{"job_id": 123, ...}',  # Your actual message
                'MessageAttributes': {...},
                'Subject': '...',
                'Timestamp': '...'
            }
        }]
    }
    """
    processed = []
    
    for record in event.get('Records', []):
        sns_message = record.get('Sns', {})
        
        # Parse the actual message
        try:
            message_body = json.loads(sns_message.get('Message', '{}'))
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            message_body = {'raw': sns_message.get('Message')}
        
        # Get message attributes
        attributes = {}
        for key, value in sns_message.get('MessageAttributes', {}).items():
            attributes[key] = value.get('Value', value.get('StringValue'))
        
        # Process based on event type
        event_type = attributes.get('event_type', 'unknown')
        
        if event_type == 'job_posted':
            result = process_job_posted(message_body)
        elif event_type == 'application_submitted':
            result = process_application(message_body)
        elif event_type == 'interview_scheduled':
            result = process_interview(message_body)
        else:
            result = {'status': 'unknown_event', 'event_type': event_type}
        
        # Store event for audit
        table.put_item(Item={
            'event_id': sns_message.get('MessageId'),
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'event_type': event_type,
            'payload': message_body,
            'result': result
        })
        
        processed.append(result)
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps({
            'processed': len(processed),
            'results': processed
        })
    }

def process_job_posted(message):
    """Handle new job posting"""
    job_id = message.get('job_id')
    # Update search index, send notifications, etc.
    return {'job_id': job_id, 'action': 'indexed'}

def process_application(message):
    """Handle new application"""
    app_id = message.get('application_id')
    # Parse resume, score candidate, etc.
    return {'application_id': app_id, 'action': 'processed'}

def process_interview(message):
    """Handle interview scheduling"""
    interview_id = message.get('interview_id')
    # Send calendar invites, reminders, etc.
    return {'interview_id': interview_id, 'action': 'scheduled'}
📦 SQS Integration (Fan-out)
SNS to SQS Fan-out Setup
# infrastructure/sns_sqs_fanout.py
import boto3
import json

class FanoutSetup:
    """Set up SNS to SQS fan-out pattern"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.sns = boto3.client('sns')
        self.sqs = boto3.client('sqs')
    
    def setup_fanout(self, topic_name: str, queues: list):
        """
        Create SNS topic with multiple SQS subscribers
        
        Args:
            topic_name: Name for SNS topic
            queues: List of dicts with 'name' and optional 'filter_policy'
        """
        # Create SNS topic
        topic_response = self.sns.create_topic(Name=topic_name)
        topic_arn = topic_response['TopicArn']
        
        subscriptions = []
        
        for queue_config in queues:
            # Create SQS queue
            queue_response = self.sqs.create_queue(
                QueueName=queue_config['name'],
                Attributes={
                    'MessageRetentionPeriod': '86400',  # 1 day
                    'VisibilityTimeout': '300'  # 5 minutes
                }
            )
            queue_url = queue_response['QueueUrl']
            
            # Get queue ARN
            queue_attrs = self.sqs.get_queue_attributes(
                QueueUrl=queue_url,
                AttributeNames=['QueueArn']
            )
            queue_arn = queue_attrs['Attributes']['QueueArn']
            
            # Set queue policy to allow SNS
            policy = {
                "Version": "2012-10-17",
                "Statement": [{
                    "Effect": "Allow",
                    "Principal": {"Service": "sns.amazonaws.com"},
                    "Action": "sqs:SendMessage",
                    "Resource": queue_arn,
                    "Condition": {
                        "ArnEquals": {"aws:SourceArn": topic_arn}
                    }
                }]
            }
            self.sqs.set_queue_attributes(
                QueueUrl=queue_url,
                Attributes={'Policy': json.dumps(policy)}
            )
            
            # Subscribe queue to topic
            sub_params = {
                'TopicArn': topic_arn,
                'Protocol': 'sqs',
                'Endpoint': queue_arn,
                'Attributes': {'RawMessageDelivery': 'true'}
            }
            
            # Add filter policy if specified
            if 'filter_policy' in queue_config:
                sub_params['Attributes']['FilterPolicy'] = json.dumps(
                    queue_config['filter_policy']
                )
            
            sub_response = self.sns.subscribe(**sub_params)
            subscriptions.append({
                'queue': queue_config['name'],
                'subscription_arn': sub_response['SubscriptionArn']
            })
        
        return {
            'topic_arn': topic_arn,
            'subscriptions': subscriptions
        }

# Usage for anihpj
fanout = FanoutSetup()

result = fanout.setup_fanout(
    topic_name='anihpj-events',
    queues=[
        {
            'name': 'anihpj-email-notifications',
            'filter_policy': {'notification_type': ['email']}
        },
        {
            'name': 'anihpj-sms-notifications',
            'filter_policy': {'notification_type': ['sms']}
        },
        {
            'name': 'anihpj-analytics',
            'filter_policy': {}  # Receives all messages
        },
        {
            'name': 'anihpj-search-index',
            'filter_policy': {'event_type': ['job_posted', 'job_updated', 'job_deleted']}
        }
    ]
)

print(f"Topic ARN: {result['topic_arn']}")
for sub in result['subscriptions']:
    print(f"  - {sub['queue']}: {sub['subscription_arn']}")
🎯 EventBridge Integration
SNS to EventBridge Pattern
# SNS can send events to EventBridge for advanced routing
import boto3
import json

events_client = boto3.client('events')
sns_client = boto3.client('sns')

# Option 1: SNS directly puts events to EventBridge
# (Use EventBridge rule to match SNS events)

# Create EventBridge rule to catch SNS notifications
rule_response = events_client.put_rule(
    Name='anihpj-sns-to-eventbridge',
    EventPattern=json.dumps({
        "source": ["aws.sns"],
        "detail-type": ["SNS Notification"],
        "detail": {
            "TopicArn": ["arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:anihpj-events"]
        }
    }),
    State='ENABLED',
    Description='Route anihpj SNS events to EventBridge targets'
)

# Add targets
events_client.put_targets(
    Rule='anihpj-sns-to-eventbridge',
    Targets=[
        {
            'Id': 'lambda-processor',
            'Arn': 'arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123:function:process-events'
        },
        {
            'Id': 'step-function-workflow',
            'Arn': 'arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123:stateMachine:anihpj-workflow',
            'RoleArn': 'arn:aws:iam::123:role/EventBridgeInvokeStepFunction'
        }
    ]
)

# Option 2: Publish directly to EventBridge from Django
def publish_to_eventbridge(event_type: str, detail: dict):
    """Alternative: bypass SNS and publish directly to EventBridge"""
    events_client.put_events(
        Entries=[
            {
                'Source': 'anihpj.jobpost',
                'DetailType': event_type,
                'Detail': json.dumps(detail),
                'EventBusName': 'default'
            }
        ]
    )
🐍 Django Integration
Complete Django SNS Integration
# anihpj/sns_integration.py - Complete Django SNS setup
import boto3
import json
from django.conf import settings
from django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_delete
from django.dispatch import receiver

class DjangoSNSIntegration:
    """
    Complete SNS integration for Django applications
    
    Features:
    - Automatic event publishing on model changes
    - Celery task triggering via SNS
    - Webhook handling for SNS confirmations
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.sns = boto3.client(
            'sns',
            region_name=getattr(settings, 'AWS_REGION', 'us-east-1'),
            aws_access_key_id=getattr(settings, 'AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID', None),
            aws_secret_access_key=getattr(settings, 'AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY', None)
        )
        self.topics = getattr(settings, 'SNS_TOPICS', {})
    
    def get_topic_arn(self, topic_key: str) -> str:
        """Get topic ARN by key"""
        return self.topics.get(topic_key)
    
    def publish_event(self, topic_key: str, event_data: dict, attributes: dict = None):
        """Publish event to SNS topic"""
        topic_arn = self.get_topic_arn(topic_key)
        if not topic_arn:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown topic: {topic_key}")
        
        message_attributes = {}
        if attributes:
            for key, value in attributes.items():
                message_attributes[key] = {
                    'DataType': 'String',
                    'StringValue': str(value)
                }
        
        return self.sns.publish(
            TopicArn=topic_arn,
            Message=json.dumps(event_data),
            MessageAttributes=message_attributes
        )

# Global instance
sns_integration = DjangoSNSIntegration()

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Django Signal Handlers
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from jobpost.models import Job, JobApplication

@receiver(post_save, sender=Job)
def publish_job_event(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):
    """Publish job events to SNS"""
    event_type = 'job_created' if created else 'job_updated'
    
    sns_integration.publish_event(
        topic_key='job_events',
        event_data={
            'event_type': event_type,
            'job_id': instance.id,
            'title': instance.title,
            'company_id': instance.company_id,
            'status': instance.status,
            'created_at': str(instance.created_at)
        },
        attributes={
            'event_type': event_type,
            'status': instance.status
        }
    )

@receiver(pre_delete, sender=Job)
def publish_job_deleted(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    """Publish job deletion event"""
    sns_integration.publish_event(
        topic_key='job_events',
        event_data={
            'event_type': 'job_deleted',
            'job_id': instance.id,
            'title': instance.title
        },
        attributes={'event_type': 'job_deleted'}
    )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SNS Webhook View (for HTTP subscriptions)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from django.http import HttpResponse, JsonResponse
from django.views.decorators.csrf import csrf_exempt
from django.views.decorators.http import require_POST
import urllib.request

@csrf_exempt
@require_POST
def sns_webhook(request):
    """
    Handle SNS HTTP endpoint notifications
    
    SNS sends:
    1. SubscriptionConfirmation - Confirm subscription
    2. Notification - Actual message
    3. UnsubscribeConfirmation - Subscription removed
    """
    try:
        payload = json.loads(request.body)
        message_type = request.headers.get('x-amz-sns-message-type')
        
        if message_type == 'SubscriptionConfirmation':
            # Confirm subscription by visiting SubscribeURL
            subscribe_url = payload.get('SubscribeURL')
            urllib.request.urlopen(subscribe_url)
            return HttpResponse('Subscription confirmed')
        
        elif message_type == 'Notification':
            # Process actual notification
            message = json.loads(payload.get('Message', '{}'))
            
            # Process based on your application logic
            process_sns_notification(message, payload)
            
            return HttpResponse('OK')
        
        elif message_type == 'UnsubscribeConfirmation':
            return HttpResponse('Unsubscribed')
        
        return HttpResponse('Unknown message type', status=400)
    
    except Exception as e:
        return JsonResponse({'error': str(e)}, status=500)

def process_sns_notification(message: dict, raw_payload: dict):
    """Process incoming SNS notification"""
    # Example: Trigger Celery task
    from celery import current_app
    
    event_type = message.get('event_type')
    
    if event_type == 'job_created':
        current_app.send_task('tasks.index_job', args=[message['job_id']])
    elif event_type == 'application_submitted':
        current_app.send_task('tasks.process_application', args=[message['application_id']])
Django Settings Configuration
# settings.py - SNS configuration for anihpj

# AWS Credentials (use IAM roles in production)
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = os.environ.get('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID')
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = os.environ.get('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY')
AWS_REGION = os.environ.get('AWS_REGION', 'us-east-1')

# SNS Topic ARNs
SNS_TOPICS = {
    'job_events': os.environ.get('SNS_TOPIC_JOB_EVENTS'),
    'application_events': os.environ.get('SNS_TOPIC_APPLICATION_EVENTS'),
    'notifications': os.environ.get('SNS_TOPIC_NOTIFICATIONS'),
    'alerts': os.environ.get('SNS_TOPIC_ALERTS'),
}

# urls.py - Add SNS webhook endpoint
from django.urls import path
from .sns_integration import sns_webhook

urlpatterns = [
    # ... other urls
    path('webhooks/sns/', sns_webhook, name='sns_webhook'),
]

🏛️ Compliance & Governance

✅ SNS Compliance Overview
🏥 Healthcare (HIPAA)
  • BAA required with AWS
  • Enable KMS encryption
  • Audit all access
  • No PHI in topics without encryption
💳 Finance (PCI DSS)
  • Encrypt at rest and transit
  • Restrict topic access
  • Enable CloudTrail
  • Annual assessments
🏛️ Government (FedRAMP)
  • Use AWS GovCloud
  • FIPS 140-2 encryption
  • Continuous monitoring
  • Access controls
🌍 International (GDPR)
  • Data residency controls
  • Right to deletion
  • Consent management
  • Data processing records
📜 AWS SNS Certifications
Certification Status Key Requirements
SOC 1/2/3 ✅ Compliant Annual audits, internal controls
ISO 27001 ✅ Certified Information security management
PCI DSS ✅ Level 1 Cardholder data protection
HIPAA ✅ Eligible Requires BAA, encryption mandatory
FedRAMP ✅ Authorized Government workloads (GovCloud)
GDPR ✅ Compliant EU data protection regulation
🔐 Security Summary
SNS Security Architecture

│                                                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │

│  │ • No public internet exposure                              │  │

│  │ • IAM policies (least privilege)                           │  │
│  │ • Resource-based policies                                  │  │
│  │ • Cross-account access controls                            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ▼                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ LAYER 3: ENCRYPTION                                        │  │
│  │ • Server-side encryption (KMS)                             │  │
│  │ • TLS 1.2+ for transit                                     │  │
│  │ • Customer-managed keys for sensitive data                 │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                       │
│                           ▼                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ LAYER 4: MONITORING & AUDIT                                │  │
│  │ • CloudTrail for all API calls                             │  │
│  │ • CloudWatch metrics and alarms                            │  │
│  │ • AWS Config rules for compliance                          │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │

🎯 SNS Section Complete!

This comprehensive AWS SNS documentation covers all essential topics for building production-ready notification systems with the anihpj Django job posting application. Key sections include:

  • Overview - Pub/Sub messaging fundamentals
  • Core Concepts - Topics, subscriptions, publishers, filtering
  • Getting Started - Prerequisites, IAM setup, first topic
  • Key Features - Fan-out, FIFO, message attributes, encryption
  • Pricing - Cost breakdown and optimization
  • Scalability - Throughput limits, batch publishing
  • Advanced Topics - Mobile push, SMS, email
  • Best Practices - Security, performance, monitoring
  • Use Cases - Alerts, workflows, fan-out patterns
  • Troubleshooting - Delivery failures, permissions, filtering
  • API Reference - CLI commands, Boto3 SDK
  • Integration - Lambda, SQS, EventBridge, Django
  • Compliance - HIPAA, PCI, FedRAMP, GDPR

Amazon API Gateway

Category: Networking & Content Delivery  |  Use Case: API Management & Integration  |  Pricing: Pay per request, HTTP APIs from $1.00/million  |  Last Updated: December 2025  |  Documentation: AWS API Gateway Docs

Fully managed service for creating, publishing, maintaining, monitoring, and securing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs at any scale with built-in authentication, throttling, and caching.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Amazon API Gateway is a fully managed service that makes it easy for developers to create, publish, maintain, monitor, and secure APIs at any scale. It acts as a "front door" for applications to access data, business logic, or functionality from backend services such as Lambda, EC2, or any web application.

🌐 What is Amazon API Gateway?

Amazon API Gateway enables you to build RESTful APIs and WebSocket APIs that are optimized for serverless workloads and HTTP backends. It handles all the tasks involved in accepting and processing up to hundreds of thousands of concurrent API calls, including traffic management, authorization and access control, monitoring, and API version management.

📡 REST APIs

Full-featured API with resource-based architecture, caching, request/response transformation, and API keys

⚡ HTTP APIs

Lower latency and cost-optimized APIs for simpler use cases with automatic deployments

🔌 WebSocket APIs

Real-time two-way communication for chat apps, streaming dashboards, and notifications

🔒 Fully Managed

Automatic scaling, security, traffic management, and monitoring without server management

🎯 Key Benefits
🚀 Rapid Development
  • Quick API creation (minutes)
  • Multiple integration types
  • Auto-generated SDKs
  • Built-in testing tools
  • Swagger/OpenAPI support
🔐 Security Built-in
  • AWS IAM authorization
  • Amazon Cognito integration
  • API keys and usage plans
  • Lambda authorizers (custom)
  • AWS WAF integration
📊 Monitoring & Analytics
  • CloudWatch metrics (real-time)
  • X-Ray distributed tracing
  • Access logs (detailed)
  • Usage analytics & reporting
  • Custom alarms & notifications
💰 Cost-Effective
  • Pay per request model
  • No minimum fees or upfront costs
  • Free tier: 1M calls/month (12 months)
  • Caching reduces backend load
  • HTTP APIs 70% cheaper than REST
📈 Scalability
  • Automatic scaling (unlimited)
  • Handles traffic spikes instantly
  • No manual intervention needed
  • Global edge locations
  • 99.95% SLA availability
🛠️ Developer Friendly
  • AWS Console UI
  • CLI & SDK support (all languages)
  • Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation, Terraform)
  • API import/export
  • Comprehensive documentation
💡 When to Use API Gateway: Perfect for your anihpj Django job portal when building microservices, serverless backends, mobile app APIs, or when you need robust API management features like throttling, caching, and custom domain names.
⚖️ API Gateway vs Traditional API Servers
Feature API Gateway Traditional (Django/Express)
Infrastructure Fully managed, serverless Self-managed EC2/containers
Scaling Automatic, unlimited Manual configuration required
Caching Built-in, configurable per endpoint Requires Redis/Memcached setup
Rate Limiting Native throttling & usage plans Custom middleware needed
Authentication IAM, Cognito, Lambda authorizers JWT, session-based (custom code)
Monitoring CloudWatch, X-Ray integrated Separate APM tools needed
Cost Model Pay per million requests Pay for servers 24/7
SSL/TLS Free AWS-managed certificates Manual cert management
