🔧 Core Concepts

🎯 API Gateway Core Components
📡
API Types

REST, HTTP, WebSocket - choose based on use case and features needed

🗂️
Resources & Methods

URL paths and HTTP verbs that define your API structure

🔗
Integrations

Backend connections to Lambda, HTTP endpoints, AWS services, or mock responses

🎯
Stages & Deployments

Environment snapshots (dev, staging, prod) with independent configurations

📡 API Types
API Types Comparison
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    API GATEWAY API TYPES                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  REST API                     HTTP API              WebSocket     │
│  ┌─────────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────┐  │
│  │ Full-featured   │         │ Simplified   │      │ Real-time│  │
│  │ • Caching       │         │ • Lower cost │      │ • Bi-dir │  │
│  │ • API keys      │         │ • Auto CORS  │      │ • Chat   │  │
│  │ • Usage plans   │         │ • JWT auth   │      │ • Stream │  │
│  │ • Transform     │         │ • OIDC       │      │ • Push   │  │
│  │ • Validation    │         │ • Faster     │      │          │  │
│  └─────────────────┘         └──────────────┘      └──────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  WHEN TO USE:                                                     │
│  • REST API: Complex transformations, caching, API management    │
│  • HTTP API: Simple proxy to Lambda/backend, lower cost          │
│  • WebSocket: Chat, live updates, streaming data                 │
│                                                                   │
│  PRICING (per million requests):                                 │
│  • REST API: $3.50 (first 333M)                                  │
│  • HTTP API: $1.00 (first 300M) - 71% cheaper!                   │
│  • WebSocket: $1.00 per million messages                         │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🗂️ Resources & Methods
anihpj API Resource Structure
REST API: api.anihpj.com/v1

/                           (Root resource)
├── /jobs                   GET    - List all jobs
│   ├── /search            POST   - Search jobs with filters
│   ├── /{jobId}           GET    - Get specific job
│   │   ├── /apply         POST   - Submit application
│   │   └── /similar       GET    - Get similar jobs
│   └── /featured          GET    - Featured jobs
│
├── /companies             GET    - List companies
│   └── /{companyId}       GET    - Get company profile
│       └── /jobs          GET    - Jobs by company
│
├── /users                 
│   ├── /profile           GET    - Get user profile
│   │                      PUT    - Update profile
│   ├── /applications      GET    - User's applications
│   └── /saved-jobs        GET    - Saved jobs
│                          POST   - Save a job
│
├── /applications          
│   └── /{applicationId}   GET    - Get application status
│                          PATCH  - Update application
│
└── /admin                 (Requires admin role)
    ├── /jobs              POST   - Create job
    │   └── /{jobId}       PUT    - Update job
    │                      DELETE - Delete job
    └── /analytics         GET    - Platform analytics

METHODS:
• GET    - Retrieve resources
• POST   - Create new resources
• PUT    - Update entire resource
• PATCH  - Partial update
• DELETE - Remove resource
• OPTIONS- CORS preflight
🔗 Integration Types
Integration Type Use Case Example for anihpj
Lambda Serverless backend logic Job search, matching algorithm, resume parsing
HTTP Proxy Forward to existing HTTP endpoint Django application on EC2, external job boards API
AWS Service Direct AWS service integration DynamoDB for analytics, S3 for resume uploads, SQS for async processing
Mock Return static response Health check endpoint, API documentation endpoint
VPC Link Private VPC resources Internal microservices, private ALB, RDS proxy
Integration Flow Diagram
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              API GATEWAY INTEGRATION FLOW                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  Client Request                                                   │
│       │                                                           │
│       ▼                                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │ 1. METHOD REQUEST                                   │          │
│  │    • Authorization (Cognito, IAM, Lambda)           │          │
│  │    • API Key validation                             │          │
│  │    • Request validation (schema, parameters)        │          │
│  └────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                       ▼                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │ 2. INTEGRATION REQUEST                              │          │
│  │    • Mapping template (transform request)           │          │
│  │    • Header mapping                                 │          │
│  │    • Query string mapping                           │          │
│  └────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                       ▼                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │ 3. BACKEND INTEGRATION                              │          │
│  │    Lambda │ HTTP │ AWS Service │ Mock │ VPC Link    │          │
│  └────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                       ▼                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │ 4. INTEGRATION RESPONSE                             │          │
│  │    • Status code mapping                            │          │
│  │    • Response template (transform response)         │          │
│  │    • Header manipulation                            │          │
│  └────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                       ▼                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │ 5. METHOD RESPONSE                                  │          │
│  │    • Response models                                │          │
│  │    • CORS headers                                   │          │
│  │    • Content-Type                                   │          │
│  └────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                       ▼                                           │
│  Client Response                                                  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🎯 Stages & Deployments
Stage Management for anihpj
# Stage deployment strategy for anihpj

# Development Stage
# URL: https://api-dev.anihpj.com
# - Verbose logging enabled
# - No caching
# - Higher rate limits for testing
# - CloudWatch detailed metrics
# - X-Ray tracing enabled

# Staging Stage
# URL: https://api-staging.anihpj.com
# - Production-like configuration
# - Caching enabled (short TTL)
# - Rate limiting matches production
# - Used for UAT and load testing

# Production Stage
# URL: https://api.anihpj.com
# - Optimized caching (5-15 min TTL)
# - Strict rate limiting
# - Minimal logging (errors only)
# - WAF enabled
# - Custom domain with SSL

# Stage Variables (Environment-specific configuration)
STAGE_VARIABLES = {
    'dev': {
        'lambdaAlias': 'dev',
        'dbEndpoint': 'dev-db.anihpj.internal',
        'cacheEnabled': 'false',
        'logLevel': 'DEBUG'
    },
    'staging': {
        'lambdaAlias': 'staging',
        'dbEndpoint': 'staging-db.anihpj.internal',
        'cacheEnabled': 'true',
        'logLevel': 'INFO'
    },
    'prod': {
        'lambdaAlias': 'prod',
        'dbEndpoint': 'prod-db.anihpj.internal',
        'cacheEnabled': 'true',
        'logLevel': 'ERROR'
    }
}

# Canary deployments for safe rollouts
# Deploy to 10% traffic, monitor, then promote to 100%
🔐 Authorizers

API Gateway authorizers control access to your APIs. Choose from IAM, Cognito User Pools, or Lambda authorizers for custom authentication logic.

Authorizer Types for anihpj
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    API GATEWAY AUTHORIZERS                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. AWS IAM AUTHORIZER                                            │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│     │ • Uses IAM policies and roles                          │   │
│     │ • Best for: Internal AWS services, B2B integrations    │   │
│     │ • Signature: AWS Signature Version 4                   │   │
│     │ • Cost: Free (no additional charges)                   │   │
│     │                                                         │   │
│     │ USE CASE: Admin APIs, internal microservices           │   │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                   │
│  2. COGNITO USER POOLS AUTHORIZER                                 │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│     │ • Uses JWT tokens from Cognito                         │   │
│     │ • Best for: User authentication (web/mobile apps)      │   │
│     │ • Automatic token validation                           │   │
│     │ • User pools, groups, MFA support                      │   │
│     │                                                         │   │
│     │ USE CASE: anihpj user login, job seeker profiles       │   │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                   │
│  3. LAMBDA AUTHORIZER (Custom)                                    │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│     │ • Custom authorization logic in Lambda function        │   │
│     │ • Best for: Third-party tokens, custom auth schemes    │   │
│     │ • Types: TOKEN or REQUEST                              │   │
│     │ • Caching support (reduces Lambda calls)               │   │
│     │                                                         │   │
│     │ USE CASE: Partner API keys, custom OAuth, legacy auth  │   │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Lambda Authorizer Example (Python)
import json
import jwt
from datetime import datetime

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Custom Lambda authorizer for anihpj API
    Validates custom JWT tokens from partner integrations
    """
    token = event['authorizationToken']
    method_arn = event['methodArn']
    
    try:
        # Validate JWT token
        decoded = jwt.decode(
            token, 
            'your-secret-key',
            algorithms=['HS256']
        )
        
        user_id = decoded['user_id']
        role = decoded.get('role', 'user')
        
        # Generate IAM policy
        policy = generate_policy(
            principal_id=user_id,
            effect='Allow',
            resource=method_arn,
            context={'role': role, 'user_id': user_id}
        )
        
        return policy
        
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        raise Exception('Unauthorized: Token expired')
    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
        raise Exception('Unauthorized: Invalid token')

def generate_policy(principal_id, effect, resource, context=None):
    """Generate IAM policy document"""
    policy = {
        'principalId': principal_id,
        'policyDocument': {
            'Version': '2012-10-17',
            'Statement': [{
                'Action': 'execute-api:Invoke',
                'Effect': effect,
                'Resource': resource
            }]
        }
    }
    
    if context:
        policy['context'] = context
    
    return policy
📋 Request/Response Models

Models define the structure of request/response payloads using JSON Schema. They enable request validation and automatic documentation generation.

Request Model for Job Creation (anihpj)
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#",
  "title": "CreateJobRequest",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["title", "description", "location", "salary_range"],
  "properties": {
    "title": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 5,
      "maxLength": 100,
      "description": "Job title"
    },
    "description": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 50,
      "maxLength": 5000,
      "description": "Detailed job description"
    },
    "location": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["city", "country"],
      "properties": {
        "city": {"type": "string"},
        "country": {"type": "string"},
        "remote": {"type": "boolean", "default": false}
      }
    },
    "salary_range": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["min", "max", "currency"],
      "properties": {
        "min": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0},
        "max": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0},
        "currency": {"type": "string", "enum": ["USD", "EUR", "GBP"]}
      }
    },
    "skills": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {"type": "string"},
      "minItems": 1,
      "maxItems": 20,
      "uniqueItems": true
    },
    "employment_type": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["full-time", "part-time", "contract", "internship"]
    },
    "experience_level": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["entry", "mid", "senior", "lead"]
    }
  }
}
💡 Model Benefits:
  • Request Validation: Automatic validation before backend execution
  • Documentation: Auto-generated API docs with payload examples
  • Cost Savings: Invalid requests rejected at API Gateway (no Lambda invocation)
  • Consistency: Enforce data structure across all endpoints

🚀 Getting Started with API Gateway

📋 Getting Started Checklist
✅
Step 1: Prerequisites

AWS account, IAM permissions, backend services ready

🎯
Step 2: Create API

Choose API type (REST/HTTP), configure settings

🔗
Step 3: Add Integrations

Connect to Lambda, HTTP endpoints, or AWS services

🚀
Step 4: Deploy & Test

Deploy to stage, test endpoints, monitor metrics

📋 Prerequisites
⚙️ Required IAM Permissions
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "apigateway:*",
                "lambda:InvokeFunction",
                "lambda:GetFunction",
                "iam:PassRole",
                "logs:CreateLogGroup",
                "logs:CreateLogStream",
                "logs:PutLogEvents"
            ],
            "Resource": "*"
        }
    ]
}
🎯 Create REST API (Step-by-Step)
Method 1: AWS Console
1. Open API Gateway Console
   └─ https://console.aws.amazon.com/apigateway

2. Click "Create API"
   ├─ Choose "REST API" (not Private)
   └─ Click "Build"

3. Configure API Settings
   ├─ Protocol: REST
   ├─ API name: anihpj-api
   ├─ Description: anihpj Job Portal API
   ├─ Endpoint Type: Regional (or Edge-optimized for global)
   └─ Click "Create API"

4. Create Resources
   ├─ Click "Actions" → "Create Resource"
   ├─ Resource Name: jobs
   ├─ Resource Path: /jobs
   └─ Enable CORS: ✓

5. Create Methods
   ├─ Select /jobs resource
   ├─ Click "Actions" → "Create Method"
   ├─ Choose "GET" from dropdown
   └─ Click checkmark

6. Configure Integration
   ├─ Integration type: Lambda Function
   ├─ Use Lambda Proxy integration: ✓
   ├─ Lambda Region: us-east-1
   ├─ Lambda Function: anihpj-get-jobs
   └─ Click "Save"

7. Deploy API
   ├─ Click "Actions" → "Deploy API"
   ├─ Deployment stage: [New Stage]
   ├─ Stage name: dev
   └─ Click "Deploy"

8. Test Your API
   └─ Copy Invoke URL: https://abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/dev
Method 2: AWS CLI
# Create REST API
aws apigateway create-rest-api \
    --name "anihpj-api" \
    --description "anihpj Job Portal API" \
    --endpoint-configuration types=REGIONAL

# Output: Note the 'id' field (REST API ID)
# REST_API_ID="abc123xyz"

# Get root resource ID
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id $REST_API_ID

# ROOT_RESOURCE_ID="xyz789"

# Create /jobs resource
aws apigateway create-resource \
    --rest-api-id $REST_API_ID \
    --parent-id $ROOT_RESOURCE_ID \
    --path-part "jobs"

# JOBS_RESOURCE_ID="def456"

# Create GET method
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id $REST_API_ID \
    --resource-id $JOBS_RESOURCE_ID \
    --http-method GET \
    --authorization-type "NONE"

# Setup Lambda integration
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id $REST_API_ID \
    --resource-id $JOBS_RESOURCE_ID \
    --http-method GET \
    --type AWS_PROXY \
    --integration-http-method POST \
    --uri "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-get-jobs/invocations"

# Grant API Gateway permission to invoke Lambda
aws lambda add-permission \
    --function-name anihpj-get-jobs \
    --statement-id apigateway-invoke \
    --action lambda:InvokeFunction \
    --principal apigateway.amazonaws.com \
    --source-arn "arn:aws:execute-api:us-east-1:123456789012:$REST_API_ID/*/*"

# Deploy to dev stage
aws apigateway create-deployment \
    --rest-api-id $REST_API_ID \
    --stage-name dev

# Test the API
curl https://$REST_API_ID.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/dev/jobs
Method 3: Python (Boto3)
# api_gateway_setup.py - Create REST API programmatically
import boto3
import json

class APIGatewaySetup:
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('apigateway', region_name=region)
        self.lambda_client = boto3.client('lambda', region_name=region)
        self.account_id = boto3.client('sts').get_caller_identity()['Account']
        self.region = region
    
    def create_rest_api(self, api_name, description):
        """Create REST API"""
        response = self.client.create_rest_api(
            name=api_name,
            description=description,
            endpointConfiguration={'types': ['REGIONAL']}
        )
        return response['id']
    
    def get_root_resource(self, api_id):
        """Get root resource ID"""
        resources = self.client.get_resources(restApiId=api_id)
        for resource in resources['items']:
            if resource['path'] == '/':
                return resource['id']
    
    def create_resource(self, api_id, parent_id, path_part):
        """Create API resource"""
        response = self.client.create_resource(
            restApiId=api_id,
            parentId=parent_id,
            pathPart=path_part
        )
        return response['id']
    
    def create_method(self, api_id, resource_id, http_method, auth_type='NONE'):
        """Create HTTP method"""
        self.client.put_method(
            restApiId=api_id,
            resourceId=resource_id,
            httpMethod=http_method,
            authorizationType=auth_type
        )
    
    def create_lambda_integration(self, api_id, resource_id, http_method, lambda_arn):
        """Create Lambda proxy integration"""
        uri = f'arn:aws:apigateway:{self.region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/{lambda_arn}/invocations'
        
        self.client.put_integration(
            restApiId=api_id,
            resourceId=resource_id,
            httpMethod=http_method,
            type='AWS_PROXY',
            integrationHttpMethod='POST',
            uri=uri
        )
        
        # Grant permission to API Gateway
        lambda_name = lambda_arn.split(':')[-1]
        try:
            self.lambda_client.add_permission(
                FunctionName=lambda_name,
                StatementId=f'apigateway-{api_id}-invoke',
                Action='lambda:InvokeFunction',
                Principal='apigateway.amazonaws.com',
                SourceArn=f'arn:aws:execute-api:{self.region}:{self.account_id}:{api_id}/*/*'
            )
        except self.lambda_client.exceptions.ResourceConflictException:
            print(f"Permission already exists for {lambda_name}")
    
    def deploy_api(self, api_id, stage_name):
        """Deploy API to stage"""
        response = self.client.create_deployment(
            restApiId=api_id,
            stageName=stage_name
        )
        return f"https://{api_id}.execute-api.{self.region}.amazonaws.com/{stage_name}"

# Usage
setup = APIGatewaySetup()

# Create API
api_id = setup.create_rest_api('anihpj-api', 'anihpj Job Portal API')
root_id = setup.get_root_resource(api_id)

# Create /jobs resource
jobs_id = setup.create_resource(api_id, root_id, 'jobs')

# Add GET method with Lambda integration
setup.create_method(api_id, jobs_id, 'GET')
setup.create_lambda_integration(
    api_id, 
    jobs_id, 
    'GET', 
    f'arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:{setup.account_id}:function:anihpj-get-jobs'
)

# Deploy
api_url = setup.deploy_api(api_id, 'dev')
print(f"API deployed to: {api_url}/jobs")
⚡ Create HTTP API (Simpler & Cheaper)
HTTP API Setup (AWS CLI)
# Create HTTP API (simpler than REST API)
aws apigatewayv2 create-api \
    --name "anihpj-http-api" \
    --protocol-type HTTP \
    --target "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-get-jobs"

# Output includes API ID and default endpoint
# API_ID="abc123"

# Create route for specific path
aws apigatewayv2 create-route \
    --api-id $API_ID \
    --route-key "GET /jobs" \
    --target "integrations/$INTEGRATION_ID"

# Create integration to Lambda
aws apigatewayv2 create-integration \
    --api-id $API_ID \
    --integration-type AWS_PROXY \
    --integration-uri "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-get-jobs" \
    --payload-format-version "2.0"

# HTTP API auto-creates $default stage
# URL: https://$API_ID.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/jobs

# Enable CORS (automatic)
aws apigatewayv2 update-api \
    --api-id $API_ID \
    --cors-configuration AllowOrigins="https://anihpj.com",AllowMethods="GET,POST",AllowHeaders="*"
🧪 Testing APIs

API Gateway provides multiple ways to test your APIs before deploying to production, including console testing, Postman, and automated testing with AWS SDK.

Testing Methods
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     API TESTING APPROACHES                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. AWS CONSOLE TEST                                              │
│     • Built-in test UI in API Gateway console                    │
│     • No deployment needed for testing                           │
│     • View request/response headers, body, logs                  │
│     • Best for: Quick validation during development               │
│                                                                   │
│  2. POSTMAN / INSOMNIA                                            │
│     • Full-featured API testing clients                          │
│     • Collection management, environment variables                │
│     • Automated test scripts                                      │
│     • Best for: Manual testing, API documentation                 │
│                                                                   │
│  3. AWS CLI / SDK                                                 │
│     • Programmatic testing with AWS SDK                          │
│     • Integration with CI/CD pipelines                           │
│     • Automated regression testing                               │
│     • Best for: Automated testing, DevOps workflows               │
│                                                                   │
│  4. LOAD TESTING (Artillery, K6, JMeter)                          │
│     • Performance and stress testing                             │
│     • Verify throttling and rate limits                          │
│     • Identify bottlenecks before production                     │
│     • Best for: Production readiness validation                   │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Python Testing Script (Boto3)
import boto3
import json
import requests
from datetime import datetime

# Test anihpj API Gateway endpoint
class APIGatewayTester:
    def __init__(self, api_url, api_key=None):
        self.api_url = api_url
        self.api_key = api_key
    
    def test_get_jobs(self):
        """Test GET /jobs endpoint"""
        headers = {}
        if self.api_key:
            headers['x-api-key'] = self.api_key
        
        response = requests.get(
            f"{self.api_url}/jobs",
            headers=headers
        )
        
        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = response.json()
        assert 'jobs' in data
        print(f"✓ GET /jobs: {len(data['jobs'])} jobs returned")
        return data
    
    def test_create_job(self, job_data):
        """Test POST /jobs endpoint"""
        headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
        if self.api_key:
            headers['x-api-key'] = self.api_key
        
        response = requests.post(
            f"{self.api_url}/jobs",
            headers=headers,
            json=job_data
        )
        
        assert response.status_code == 201
        data = response.json()
        assert 'job_id' in data
        print(f"✓ POST /jobs: Job created with ID {data['job_id']}")
        return data
    
    def test_rate_limiting(self):
        """Test API rate limits"""
        requests_made = 0
        start_time = datetime.now()
        
        while (datetime.now() - start_time).seconds < 1:
            response = requests.get(f"{self.api_url}/jobs")
            requests_made += 1
            
            if response.status_code == 429:  # Too Many Requests
                print(f"✓ Rate limit triggered after {requests_made} requests")
                return True
        
        print(f"Made {requests_made} requests in 1 second")
        return False

# Run tests
if __name__ == "__main__":
    tester = APIGatewayTester(
        api_url="https://api.anihpj.com/v1",
        api_key="your-api-key-here"
    )
    
    # Run test suite
    tester.test_get_jobs()
    
    job_data = {
        "title": "Senior Python Developer",
        "description": "Join our amazing team...",
        "location": {"city": "San Francisco", "country": "USA"},
        "salary_range": {"min": 120000, "max": 180000, "currency": "USD"},
        "skills": ["Python", "Django", "AWS", "PostgreSQL"]
    }
    tester.test_create_job(job_data)
    tester.test_rate_limiting()

🔑 Key Features

⭐ API Gateway Key Features
🔐 Authentication & Authorization

IAM, Cognito, Lambda authorizers, API keys - multiple auth methods for different needs

🚦 Throttling & Rate Limiting

Protect backend from traffic spikes, usage plans for different client tiers

⚡ Response Caching

Reduce backend load and latency with configurable TTL caching per endpoint

🌐 CORS Support

Built-in CORS configuration for web applications, automatic OPTIONS handling

🔐 Authentication & Authorization
Auth Method Best For Setup Complexity Cost
IAM AWS service-to-service calls Low Free
Cognito User authentication (B2C/B2B) Medium Pay per MAU
Lambda Authorizer Custom auth logic, 3rd party tokens High (most flexible) Lambda invocation cost
API Keys Partner integrations, rate limiting Low Free
JWT (HTTP API) Standard token validation Low Free
Cognito Integration for anihpj
# Configure Cognito authorizer for user endpoints
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create Cognito User Pool Authorizer
authorizer_response = apigw.create_authorizer(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    name='anihpj-cognito-auth',
    type='COGNITO_USER_POOLS',
    providerARNs=[
        'arn:aws:cognito-idp:us-east-1:123456789012:userpool/us-east-1_ABC123'
    ],
    identitySource='method.request.header.Authorization'
)

# Apply to protected endpoints
apigw.update_method(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='POST',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/authorizationType',
            'value': 'COGNITO_USER_POOLS'
        },
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/authorizerId',
            'value': authorizer_response['id']
        }
    ]
)

# Client-side: Include JWT token in requests
# Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
Lambda Custom Authorizer
# lambda_authorizer.py - Custom authorization logic
def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Custom authorizer for anihpj API
    Validates custom tokens, checks user roles, etc.
    """
    token = event['authorizationToken']  # e.g., "Bearer "
    method_arn = event['methodArn']
    
    # Extract token
    token_parts = token.split(' ')
    if len(token_parts) != 2 or token_parts[0] != 'Bearer':
        raise Exception('Unauthorized')
    
    auth_token = token_parts[1]
    
    # Validate token (custom logic)
    try:
        user = validate_token(auth_token)  # Your validation logic
        
        # Generate IAM policy
        policy = generate_policy(
            user['user_id'], 
            'Allow', 
            method_arn,
            context={
                'userId': user['user_id'],
                'email': user['email'],
                'role': user['role']
            }
        )
        return policy
    except Exception as e:
        raise Exception('Unauthorized')

def validate_token(token):
    """Validate custom JWT or session token"""
    import jwt
    import os
    
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token, 
            os.environ['JWT_SECRET'], 
            algorithms=['HS256']
        )
        return payload
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        raise Exception('Token expired')
    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
        raise Exception('Invalid token')

def generate_policy(principal_id, effect, resource, context=None):
    """Generate IAM policy document"""
    policy = {
        'principalId': principal_id,
        'policyDocument': {
            'Version': '2012-10-17',
            'Statement': [{
                'Action': 'execute-api:Invoke',
                'Effect': effect,
                'Resource': resource
            }]
        }
    }
    
    if context:
        policy['context'] = context
    
    return policy

Create Usage Plan with Boto3
# Create usage plans for different client tiers
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create Usage Plan
usage_plan = apigw.create_usage_plan(
    name='anihpj-premium-tier',
    description='Premium tier for enterprise partners',
    apiStages=[{
        'apiId': 'your-api-id',
        'stage': 'prod'
    }],
    throttle={
        'rateLimit': 5000.0,  # Requests per second
        'burstLimit': 10000   # Maximum burst
    },
    quota={
        'limit': 100000,       # Requests per period
        'period': 'DAY'        # DAY, WEEK, or MONTH
    }
)

# Create API Key
api_key = apigw.create_api_key(
    name='partner-acme-corp',
    description='API key for ACME Corp',
    enabled=True
)

# Associate API key with usage plan
apigw.create_usage_plan_key(
    usagePlanId=usage_plan['id'],
    keyId=api_key['id'],
    keyType='API_KEY'
)

print(f"API Key: {api_key['value']}")
print("Include in requests: x-api-key: ")
⚡ Caching
Enable Caching for Job Listings
# Enable caching at stage level (0.5GB cache)
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/cacheClusterEnabled,value=true \
        op=replace,path=/cacheClusterSize,value=0.5

# Cache sizes: 0.5GB, 1.6GB, 6.1GB, 13.5GB, 28.4GB, 58.2GB, 118GB, 237GB
# Pricing: $0.020 per hour per GB

# Override cache TTL for specific method
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --resource-id resource-id \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/cacheKeyParameters,value=method.request.querystring.category \
        op=replace,path=/cacheNamespace,value=jobs-cache

# Cache key based on query parameters
# /jobs?category=engineering → Cached separately
# /jobs?category=marketing → Different cache entry
💡 Caching Best Practices for anihpj
  • Public job listings: 15-minute TTL (low change frequency)
  • Job search results: 5-minute TTL (balance freshness vs load)
  • User profile: No cache (user-specific data)
  • Company profiles: 30-minute TTL (rarely change)
  • Featured jobs: 10-minute TTL (updated frequently)
🌐 CORS Configuration
Enable CORS for anihpj Web App
# Enable CORS for REST API
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Add OPTIONS method for preflight requests
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='OPTIONS',
    authorizationType='NONE'
)

# Mock integration for OPTIONS
apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='OPTIONS',
    type='MOCK',
    requestTemplates={
        'application/json': '{"statusCode": 200}'
    }
)

# Add CORS headers to method response
apigw.put_method_response(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='OPTIONS',
    statusCode='200',
    responseParameters={
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers': True,
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Methods': True,
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin': True
    }
)

# Add CORS headers to integration response
apigw.put_integration_response(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='OPTIONS',
    statusCode='200',
    responseParameters={
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers': "'Content-Type,X-Amz-Date,Authorization,X-Api-Key'",
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Methods': "'GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS'",
        'method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin': "'https://anihpj.com'"
    }
)

# For Lambda proxy integration, return CORS headers in Lambda response
def lambda_handler(event, context):
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'headers': {
            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://anihpj.com',
            'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type,Authorization',
            'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,OPTIONS'
        },
        'body': json.dumps({'jobs': [...]})
    }

Create Usage Plan with API Key (Boto3)
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create usage plan
usage_plan = apigw.create_usage_plan(
    name='anihpj-basic-tier',
    description='Basic tier for small partners',
    throttle={
        'rateLimit': 100.0,  # Requests per second
        'burstLimit': 200    # Burst capacity
    },
    quota={
        'limit': 100000,     # Total requests
        'period': 'DAY'      # Period: DAY, WEEK, MONTH
    }
)

# Create API key
api_key = apigw.create_api_key(
    name='partner-acme-corp',
    description='API key for ACME Corp',
    enabled=True
)

# Associate API key with usage plan
apigw.create_usage_plan_key(
    usagePlanId=usage_plan['id'],
    keyId=api_key['id'],
    keyType='API_KEY'
)

# Associate usage plan with API stage
apigw.update_usage_plan(
    usagePlanId=usage_plan['id'],
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'add',
            'path': '/apiStages',
            'value': 'api-id:prod'
        }
    ]
)

print(f"API Key: {api_key['value']}")
print(f"Usage Plan ID: {usage_plan['id']}")

# Test API with key
import requests

headers = {
    'x-api-key': api_key['value']
}

response = requests.get(
    'https://api.anihpj.com/jobs',
    headers=headers
)

print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
print(f"Remaining requests: {response.headers.get('X-Amzn-RateLimit-Limit')}")
💡 Usage Plan Best Practices:
  • Multiple Tiers: Offer different plans for different customer segments
  • Generous Free Tier: Attract developers with a useful free tier
  • Monitor Usage: Track usage patterns to optimize tier limits
  • Overage Handling: Define clear policies for quota overages
  • Key Rotation: Implement API key rotation policies for security

💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

💵 API Gateway Pricing Overview
📡 REST API
  • $3.50 per million requests (first 333M)
  • $2.80 per million (next 667M)
  • Caching: $0.020/hour per GB
⚡ HTTP API
  • $1.00 per million requests (first 300M)
  • $0.90 per million (300M-1B)
  • 71% cheaper than REST API
🔌 WebSocket API
  • $1.00 per million messages
  • $0.25 per million connection minutes
  • First 1B messages: $1.00/M
🎁 Free Tier
  • 1M API calls/month (12 months)
  • REST, HTTP, WebSocket included
  • Ideal for development/testing
💵 Detailed Pricing Model
Pricing Breakdown by Request Volume
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                API GATEWAY PRICING TIERS                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  REST API PRICING (US East - N. Virginia)                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ First 333 million requests/month    $3.50 per million     │  │
│  │ Next 667 million requests/month     $2.80 per million     │  │
│  │ Next 19 billion requests/month      $2.38 per million     │  │
│  │ Over 20 billion requests/month      $1.51 per million     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  HTTP API PRICING                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ First 300 million requests/month    $1.00 per million     │  │
│  │ Next 700 million requests/month     $0.90 per million     │  │
│  │ Over 1 billion requests/month       $0.80 per million     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  CACHE PRICING (Optional - REST API Only)                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ 0.5 GB cache    $0.020/hour  = $14.40/month               │  │
│  │ 1.6 GB cache    $0.038/hour  = $27.36/month               │  │
│  │ 6.1 GB cache    $0.200/hour  = $144.00/month              │  │
│  │ 13.5 GB cache   $0.250/hour  = $180.00/month              │  │
│  │ 28.4 GB cache   $0.500/hour  = $360.00/month              │  │
│  │ 58.2 GB cache   $1.000/hour  = $720.00/month              │  │
│  │ 118 GB cache    $1.900/hour  = $1,368.00/month            │  │
│  │ 237 GB cache    $3.800/hour  = $2,736.00/month            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  DATA TRANSFER                                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Data transfer OUT: $0.09 per GB (after first 10 TB)       │  │
│  │ Data transfer IN: Free                                     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
💡 Cost Optimization Strategies
💰 Cost Optimization for anihpj
  1. Choose HTTP API for simple endpoints - 71% cheaper, perfect for Lambda proxy
  2. Enable caching for read-heavy endpoints - Reduce backend Lambda invocations
  3. Use CloudFront in front of API Gateway - Edge caching reduces API calls
  4. Implement proper throttling - Prevent abuse and unnecessary costs
  5. Compress responses - Enable gzip to reduce data transfer costs
  6. Use regional endpoints - Cheaper than edge-optimized for local traffic
  7. Batch operations where possible - Fewer API calls = lower cost
📊 Cost Calculator for anihpj
Monthly Cost Estimate
# anihpj API Gateway Cost Calculator
def calculate_apigw_cost(monthly_requests, api_type='HTTP', cache_size=None):
    """
    Calculate monthly API Gateway costs
    
    Args:
        monthly_requests: Total API requests per month
        api_type: 'REST' or 'HTTP'
        cache_size: Cache size in GB (REST API only)
    """
    cost = 0
    
    if api_type == 'REST':
        # REST API pricing tiers
        if monthly_requests <= 333_000_000:
            cost += (monthly_requests / 1_000_000) * 3.50
        elif monthly_requests <= 1_000_000_000:
            cost += (333_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 3.50
            cost += ((monthly_requests - 333_000_000) / 1_000_000) * 2.80
        else:
            cost += (333_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 3.50
            cost += (667_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 2.80
            cost += ((monthly_requests - 1_000_000_000) / 1_000_000) * 2.38
        
        # Add cache cost if enabled
        if cache_size:
            cache_pricing = {
                0.5: 14.40, 1.6: 27.36, 6.1: 144.00, 13.5: 180.00,
                28.4: 360.00, 58.2: 720.00, 118: 1368.00, 237: 2736.00
            }
            cost += cache_pricing.get(cache_size, 0)
    
    elif api_type == 'HTTP':
        # HTTP API pricing tiers
        if monthly_requests <= 300_000_000:
            cost += (monthly_requests / 1_000_000) * 1.00
        elif monthly_requests <= 1_000_000_000:
            cost += (300_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 1.00
            cost += ((monthly_requests - 300_000_000) / 1_000_000) * 0.90
        else:
            cost += (300_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 1.00
            cost += (700_000_000 / 1_000_000) * 0.90
            cost += ((monthly_requests - 1_000_000_000) / 1_000_000) * 0.80
    
    return round(cost, 2)

# Example: anihpj monthly usage
scenarios = {
    'Current (Small Scale)': {
        'requests': 5_000_000,  # 5M requests/month
        'api_type': 'HTTP',
        'cache_size': None
    },
    'Growing (Medium Scale)': {
        'requests': 50_000_000,  # 50M requests/month
        'api_type': 'REST',
        'cache_size': 0.5  # 0.5 GB cache
    },
    'Mature (Large Scale)': {
        'requests': 500_000_000,  # 500M requests/month
        'api_type': 'REST',
        'cache_size': 6.1  # 6.1 GB cache
    }
}

print("┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐")
print("│         ANIHPJ API GATEWAY COST SCENARIOS              │")
print("├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤")

for scenario, config in scenarios.items():
    cost = calculate_apigw_cost(**config)
    print(f"│ {scenario:30s}                         │")
    print(f"│   Requests: {config['requests']:,}                      │")
    print(f"│   API Type: {config['api_type']}                              │")
    if config['cache_size']:
        print(f"│   Cache: {config['cache_size']} GB                            │")
    print(f"│   Monthly Cost: ${cost:,.2f}                         │")
    print("├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤")

print("└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘")

# Output:
# Current (Small Scale): $5.00/month
# Growing (Medium Scale): $189.40/month ($175 API + $14.40 cache)
# Mature (Large Scale): $1,648.50/month ($1,504.50 API + $144 cache)

# Cost savings by using HTTP API instead of REST:
# 5M requests: $5 (HTTP) vs $17.50 (REST) = 71% savings
# 50M requests: $50 (HTTP) vs $175 (REST) = 71% savings
🎁 AWS Free Tier for API Gateway

API Gateway offers a generous free tier for the first 12 months after signup, plus ongoing free limits for HTTP APIs.

API Gateway Free Tier Benefits
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                AWS API GATEWAY FREE TIER                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  12-MONTH FREE TIER (New AWS Accounts)                           │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  REST APIs:                                                │  │
│  │  • 1 million API calls per month                          │  │
│  │  • Receive up to 750,000 calls                            │  │
│  │  • Send up to 250,000 calls                               │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  HTTP APIs:                                                │  │
│  │  • 1 million API calls per month                          │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  WebSocket APIs:                                           │  │
│  │  • 1 million messages (sent or received)                  │  │
│  │  • 750,000 connection minutes                             │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  Messages API:                                             │  │
│  │  • 1 million messages per month                           │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  ALWAYS FREE (No Time Limit)                                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │  HTTP APIs:                                                │  │
│  │  • Up to 300 million requests per month                   │  │
│  │    at $1.00 per million (ongoing low cost)                │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  WHAT'S NOT INCLUDED IN FREE TIER:                               │
│  • Caching (charges apply immediately)                           │
│  • Custom domain names (Route 53 charges)                        │
│  • Data transfer out (standard AWS data transfer rates)          │
│  • CloudWatch Logs (if detailed logging enabled)                 │
│  • Lambda invocations (separate Lambda free tier applies)        │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
anihpj Free Tier Strategy
# Maximize AWS free tier for anihpj development

# Month 1-12: Development & Testing
# Use: HTTP API + Lambda functions
# Expected usage:
#   - API Gateway: 500K requests/month (within free tier)
#   - Lambda: 1M requests/month (within free tier)
#   - DynamoDB: 25 GB storage, 25 RCU/WCU (within free tier)
# 
# Monthly cost: $0 for core services! 🎉

# Development best practices to stay within free tier:
DEVELOPMENT_CONFIG = {
    'api_type': 'HTTP',  # Cheaper than REST API
    'caching': False,     # Avoid cache charges
    'logging_level': 'ERROR',  # Minimize CloudWatch costs
    'lambda_memory': 512,  # Balance cost and performance
    'dynamodb_capacity': 'ON_DEMAND',  # Pay per request
}

# Monitor usage to avoid surprises
def monitor_free_tier_usage():
    \"\"\"
    Check AWS Free Tier usage dashboard:
    https://console.aws.amazon.com/billing/home#/freetier
    
    Set up billing alerts:
    - Alert at $1, $5, $10 thresholds
    - Email notifications for free tier limits
    \"\"\"
    import boto3
    
    cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    # Create billing alarm
    cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
        AlarmName='anihpj-free-tier-alert',
        MetricName='EstimatedCharges',
        Namespace='AWS/Billing',
        Statistic='Maximum',
        Period=21600,  # 6 hours
        EvaluationPeriods=1,
        Threshold=10.0,  # $10 threshold
        ComparisonOperator='GreaterThanThreshold',
        AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:billing-alerts']
    )

# After 12 months: Optimize for cost
# Expected costs (50M requests/month):
#   - HTTP API: $50/month
#   - Lambda: $8/month (with optimization)
#   - Total: ~$58/month for scalable API platform
💡 Pro Tips for Free Tier:
  • Use HTTP APIs: Always free tier pricing is better than REST APIs
  • Regional Endpoints: Use regional instead of edge-optimized to avoid CloudFront charges
  • Minimize Caching: Cache is not included in free tier
  • Monitor Usage: Set up billing alerts before hitting limits
  • Optimize Lambda: Combine with Lambda free tier for maximum savings

📈 Scalability & Performance

🚀 Scalability Features
⚡
Auto-Scaling

Automatic scaling to handle traffic spikes without configuration

🌍
Global Distribution

Edge-optimized endpoints with CloudFront for low latency worldwide

🔌
WebSocket Support

Real-time bidirectional communication for live features

💾
Caching Layer

Reduce backend load with configurable response caching

⚡ Service Limits
Limit Type Default Value Can Increase?
Regional APIs (steady-state) 10,000 requests/second ✅ Yes (via support ticket)
Regional APIs (burst) 5,000 requests ✅ Yes
Edge-optimized APIs 600 requests/second across all regions ✅ Yes
Timeout 29 seconds (integration timeout) ❌ No (hard limit)
Payload size 10 MB (request/response) ❌ No
WebSocket connection duration 2 hours (idle timeout: 10 minutes) ❌ No
WebSocket message size 128 KB ❌ No
Custom domain names 120 per account ✅ Yes
🚀 Performance Optimization
Optimization Techniques
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           API GATEWAY PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. CACHING STRATEGY                                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Enable caching for:                                        │  │
│  │ ✓ Public job listings (15 min TTL)                        │  │
│  │ ✓ Company profiles (30 min TTL)                           │  │
│  │ ✓ Search results (5 min TTL)                              │  │
│  │ ✓ Static content (60 min TTL)                             │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │ Skip caching for:                                          │  │
│  │ ✗ User-specific data (profiles, applications)             │  │
│  │ ✗ Real-time data (notifications, messages)                │  │
│  │ ✗ POST/PUT/DELETE operations                              │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  2. COMPRESSION                                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Enable gzip compression:                                   │  │
│  │ • Reduce payload size by 70-90%                            │  │
│  │ • Lower data transfer costs                                │  │
│  │ • Faster response times                                    │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │ Lambda response (return compressed):                       │  │
│  │   headers: {'Content-Encoding': 'gzip'}                    │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  3. REGIONAL vs EDGE-OPTIMIZED                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Regional: Better for same-region traffic                   │  │
│  │ • Lower latency (no CloudFront hop)                        │  │
│  │ • Lower cost                                               │  │
│  │ • 10,000 req/sec default limit                             │  │
│  │                                                            │  │
│  │ Edge-optimized: Better for global traffic                  │  │
│  │ • CloudFront caching at edge locations                     │  │
│  │ • Lower latency worldwide                                  │  │
│  │ • Higher cost, lower throughput limit                      │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
│  4. BACKEND OPTIMIZATION                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ • Use Lambda provisioned concurrency for critical paths    │  │
│  │ • Enable connection pooling for database queries           │  │
│  │ • Implement backend caching (Redis/DynamoDB)               │  │
│  │ • Use async processing for heavy operations                │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔌 WebSocket APIs
WebSocket API for Real-time Features
# WebSocket API for anihpj live notifications
import boto3
import json

# Lambda handler for WebSocket connections
def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Handle WebSocket events
    
    Routes:
    - $connect: User connects to WebSocket
    - $disconnect: User disconnects
    - $default: Handle custom messages
    - sendNotification: Send real-time notification
    """
    route_key = event['requestContext']['routeKey']
    connection_id = event['requestContext']['connectionId']
    
    if route_key == '$connect':
        return handle_connect(connection_id, event)
    elif route_key == '$disconnect':
        return handle_disconnect(connection_id)
    elif route_key == 'sendNotification':
        return handle_notification(connection_id, event)
    else:
        return {'statusCode': 200}

def handle_connect(connection_id, event):
    """Store connection in DynamoDB"""
    dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
    table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-websocket-connections')
    
    # Extract user info from query parameters or authorizer
    user_id = event['queryStringParameters'].get('userId')
    
    table.put_item(Item={
        'connectionId': connection_id,
        'userId': user_id,
        'connectedAt': int(time.time())
    })
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}

def handle_disconnect(connection_id):
    """Remove connection from DynamoDB"""
    dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
    table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-websocket-connections')
    
    table.delete_item(Key={'connectionId': connection_id})
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}

def handle_notification(connection_id, event):
    """Process and route notification"""
    body = json.loads(event.get('body', '{}'))
    message = body.get('message', '')
    
    # Send response back to client
    send_to_connection(connection_id, {'notification': message})
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}

def send_to_connection(connection_id, data):
    """Send message to specific WebSocket connection"""
    apigw_client = boto3.client(
        'apigatewaymanagementapi',
        endpoint_url=f'https://{event["requestContext"]["domainName"]}/{event["requestContext"]["stage"]}'
    )
    
    apigw_client.post_to_connection(
        ConnectionId=connection_id,
        Data=json.dumps(data)
    )

# Client-side JavaScript for WebSocket connection
"""
// Connect to WebSocket API
const ws = new WebSocket('wss://abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/prod?userId=user123');

ws.onopen = () => {
    console.log('WebSocket connected');
};

ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    const notification = JSON.parse(event.data);
    displayNotification(notification);
};

ws.onerror = (error) => {
    console.error('WebSocket error:', error);
};

ws.onclose = () => {
    console.log('WebSocket disconnected');
};

// Send message
ws.send(JSON.stringify({
    action: 'sendNotification',
    message: 'New job application received'
}));
"""
🌍 Edge-Optimized APIs
CloudFront Integration for Global Performance

Edge-optimized API Gateway endpoints automatically use Amazon CloudFront to reduce latency for globally distributed clients.

🌐 Global Distribution
  • 200+ CloudFront edge locations
  • Automatic DNS routing
  • TLS termination at edge
⚡ Low Latency
  • Request caching at edge
  • Reduced backend load
  • Faster global response
💰 Cost Effective
  • Included with API Gateway
  • No extra CloudFront fees
  • Bandwidth optimization
🔒 Enhanced Security
  • DDoS protection (Shield)
  • AWS WAF integration
  • ACM certificate support
⚖️ Endpoint Type Comparison
Endpoint Type Best For CloudFront Latency Use Case
Edge-Optimized Global users ✅ Automatic Low worldwide Public APIs, mobile apps
Regional Single region ❌ Manual setup Low in region Internal APIs, compliance
Private VPC only ❌ Not applicable Low in VPC Microservices, internal
Create Edge-Optimized API
# Python - Create edge-optimized REST API
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway', region_name='us-east-1')

# Create REST API with edge-optimized endpoint
api = apigw.create_rest_api(
    name='anihpj-global-api',
    description='Edge-optimized API for global job board',
    endpointConfiguration={
        'types': ['EDGE']  # Uses CloudFront automatically
    },
    minimumCompressionSize=1024  # Enable compression
)

print(f"API ID: {api['id']}")
print(f"Edge endpoint: https://{api['id']}.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com")

# AWS CLI equivalent
"""
aws apigateway create-rest-api \
    --name anihpj-global-api \
    --endpoint-configuration types=EDGE \
    --minimum-compression-size 1024 \
    --region us-east-1
"""

🎯 Performance Tips:

  • Enable Caching: Use CloudFront caching at edge locations for GET requests
  • Response Compression: Set minimumCompressionSize to compress responses
  • Regional Backend: Place Lambda functions in same region as API Gateway
  • Custom Domain: Use CloudFront distribution for custom domain with ACM certificate
  • HTTP API Option: Consider HTTP APIs for even lower latency (70% cheaper)
Edge Optimization Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Global Users                              │
└────────┬────────────────┬────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
         │                │                │
    [London User]    [Tokyo User]    [Sydney User]
         │                │                │
         ▼                ▼                ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Amazon CloudFront (200+ Edge Locations)            │
│  • Automatic with Edge-Optimized                                │
│  • TLS termination at edge                                      │
│  • Response caching (TTL: 0-3600s)                             │
│  • Compression (gzip, brotli)                                   │
└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │ (Origin request)
                         ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          API Gateway (us-east-1 - Regional)                     │
│  • Request validation                                           │
│  • Authorization (Cognito, Lambda, IAM)                        │
│  • Throttling & usage plans                                     │
│  • API Gateway caching (optional)                               │
└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          Lambda Functions (us-east-1)                           │
│  • Business logic                                               │
│  • Data processing                                              │
└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │
                         ▼
                   [DynamoDB/RDS]
Convert Regional to Edge-Optimized
# Update existing API endpoint configuration
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/endpointConfiguration/types/REGIONAL,value=EDGE

# Verify the change
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --query 'endpointConfiguration.types' \
    --output table

# Expected output: EDGE

🎓 Advanced Topics

🔬 Advanced Features
🌍 Custom Domains

Use your own domain (api.anihpj.com) with SSL certificates

✅ Request Validation

JSON schema validation before reaching backend

🗺️ Mapping Templates

Transform requests/responses using VTL templates

🔗 VPC Links

Private integration with VPC resources via NLB

🌍 Custom Domain Names
Setup Custom Domain for anihpj
# Step 1: Request ACM certificate (must be in us-east-1 for edge-optimized)
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1

# Step 2: Validate certificate via DNS (add CNAME record to Route 53)
# Check certificate status
aws acm describe-certificate \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123:certificate/abc123 \
    --region us-east-1

# Step 3: Create custom domain in API Gateway
aws apigateway create-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --certificate-arn arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123:certificate/abc123 \
    --endpoint-configuration types=EDGE \
    --security-policy TLS_1_2

# Step 4: Create base path mapping
aws apigateway create-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage prod \
    --base-path v1  # Optional: api.anihpj.com/v1

# Step 5: Add CNAME in Route 53
# Point api.anihpj.com to the CloudFront distribution domain
# (provided in the create-domain-name response)

# Final URL: https://api.anihpj.com/v1/jobs
✅ Request Validation
JSON Schema Validation
# Define request validator
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create request validator
validator = apigw.create_request_validator(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    name='validate-body-and-params',
    validateRequestBody=True,
    validateRequestParameters=True
)

# Define JSON schema model for job creation
job_model = {
    "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#",
    "title": "JobCreateModel",
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "minLength": 5,
            "maxLength": 100
        },
        "description": {
            "type": "string",
            "minLength": 20,
            "maxLength": 5000
        },
        "salary": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                "min": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0},
                "max": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0}
            },
            "required": ["min", "max"]
        },
        "location": {
            "type": "string",
            "pattern": "^[A-Z]{2}-[A-Z]{2,3}$"  # e.g., US-NY
        },
        "jobType": {
            "type": "string",
            "enum": ["full-time", "part-time", "contract", "internship"]
        },
        "skills": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {"type": "string"},
            "minItems": 1,
            "maxItems": 20
        }
    },
    "required": ["title", "description", "location", "jobType"]
}

# Create model
model = apigw.create_model(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    name='JobCreateModel',
    contentType='application/json',
    schema=json.dumps(job_model)
)

# Apply to POST method
apigw.update_method(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='POST',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/requestValidatorId',
            'value': validator['id']
        },
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/requestModels/application~1json',
            'value': 'JobCreateModel'
        }
    ]
)

# Now invalid requests return 400 Bad Request before reaching Lambda
# Example error response:
{
    "message": "Invalid request body",
    "errors": [
        {"path": "title", "message": "String is too short (3 chars), minimum 5"},
        {"path": "jobType", "message": "Value 'freelance' is not in enum"}
    ]
}
🗺️ Mapping Templates (VTL)
Transform Request/Response
# Request Mapping Template (Apache Velocity)
# Transform incoming request before sending to backend

## Example: Convert query parameters to JSON body for DynamoDB
{
    "TableName": "anihpj-jobs",
    "Key": {
        "jobId": {
            "S": "$input.params('jobId')"
        }
    },
    "ProjectionExpression": "jobId, title, company, salary, #loc",
    "ExpressionAttributeNames": {
        "#loc": "location"
    }
}

## Example: Add metadata to request
{
    "requestId": "$context.requestId",
    "sourceIp": "$context.identity.sourceIp",
    "userAgent": "$context.identity.userAgent",
    "requestTime": "$context.requestTimeEpoch",
    "httpMethod": "$context.httpMethod",
    "resourcePath": "$context.resourcePath",
    "stage": "$context.stage",
    
    ## Include original body
    "body": $input.json('$')
}

## Response Mapping Template
## Transform DynamoDB response to API response
#set($inputRoot = $input.path('$'))
{
    "job": {
        "id": "$inputRoot.Item.jobId.S",
        "title": "$inputRoot.Item.title.S",
        "company": "$inputRoot.Item.company.S",
        "salary": {
            "min": $inputRoot.Item.salary.M.min.N,
            "max": $inputRoot.Item.salary.M.max.N
        },
        "location": "$inputRoot.Item.location.S"
    },
    "metadata": {
        "requestId": "$context.requestId",
        "responseTime": $context.responseLatency
    }
}
🔗 VPC Links (Private Integration)
Connect to Private Resources
# Scenario: API Gateway → VPC Link → NLB → Private ECS/EC2

# Step 1: Create Network Load Balancer in VPC
aws elbv2 create-load-balancer \
    --name anihpj-internal-nlb \
    --type network \
    --scheme internal \
    --subnets subnet-abc123 subnet-def456 \
    --ip-address-type ipv4

# Step 2: Create VPC Link
aws apigateway create-vpc-link \
    --name anihpj-vpc-link \
    --target-arns arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123:loadbalancer/net/anihpj-internal-nlb/abc123

# Wait for VPC Link to be AVAILABLE
aws apigateway get-vpc-link --vpc-link-id abc123

# Step 3: Create integration with VPC Link
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --resource-id resource-id \
    --http-method GET \
    --type HTTP_PROXY \
    --integration-http-method GET \
    --uri http://anihpj-internal-nlb-abc123.elb.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/api/jobs \
    --connection-type VPC_LINK \
    --connection-id abc123

# Now API Gateway can access private resources in VPC
# Use case: Private Django application on ECS, internal microservices
🐤 Canary Deployments
Safe Production Releases with Canary Deployments

Canary deployments let you test new API versions by routing a small percentage of traffic to the new stage while keeping most traffic on the stable version.

🎯 Gradual Rollout
  • Control traffic percentage
  • 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%, 100%
  • Stage variable overrides
📊 Real-time Monitoring
  • CloudWatch metrics split
  • Error rate comparison
  • Latency tracking (p50/p99)
⚡ Instant Rollback
  • Zero-downtime revert
  • Delete canary deployment
  • Automatic on alarm trigger
🧪 A/B Testing
  • Feature flag testing
  • User group segmentation
  • Performance comparison
⚖️ Deployment Strategy Comparison
Deployment Type Traffic Split Rollback Time Risk Level Use Case
Canary (5%) 5% new, 95% old Immediate 🟢 Very Low Critical APIs
Canary (10%) 10% new, 90% old Immediate 🟡 Low Standard releases
Canary (50%) 50% new, 50% old Immediate 🟠 Medium A/B testing
All-at-once 100% new Redeploy old 🔴 High Minor changes only
Create Canary Deployment
# Python - Deploy with 10% canary
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create deployment with canary settings
deployment = apigw.create_deployment(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    stageName='prod',
    description='Release v2.0 with job filters',
    canarySettings={
        'percentTraffic': 10.0,  # 10% to canary, 90% to prod
        'useStageCache': False,   # Don't use prod cache for canary
        'stageVariableOverrides': {
            'lambdaVersion': '2'   # Point to new Lambda version
        }
    }
)

print(f"Deployment ID: {deployment['id']}")
print(f"10% traffic going to canary version")

# Monitor canary metrics
"""
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/ApiGateway \
    --metric-name Count \
    --dimensions Name=ApiName,Value=anihpj-api Name=Stage,Value=prod \
    --start-time 2026-01-17T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2026-01-17T01:00:00Z \
    --period 300 \
    --statistics Sum
"""

# If successful, promote canary to 100%
apigw.update_stage(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    stageName='prod',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'remove',
            'path': '/canarySettings'  # Promotes canary to 100%
        }
    ]
)

# If issues found, delete canary (instant rollback)
apigw.update_stage(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    stageName='prod',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'remove',
            'path': '/canarySettings'
        }
    ]
)

🎯 Canary Best Practices:

  • Start Small: Begin with 5-10% traffic for critical APIs
  • Monitor Closely: Watch error rates, latency (p50, p99), throttling
  • Gradual Increase: 10% → 25% → 50% → 100% over hours/days
  • Automated Rollback: Use CloudWatch alarms to auto-rollback on errors
  • A/B Testing: Use stage variables to test feature flags
  • Cost Aware: Canary deployments don't incur extra API Gateway charges
Canary Deployment Workflow
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   CANARY DEPLOYMENT WORKFLOW                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

PHASE 1: Deploy Canary (10%)
┌─────────────────┐
│  100 Requests   │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ├──────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┐
         │ 10%          │ 90%                                │
         ▼              ▼                                    ▼
┌────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Canary Version │  │      Production Version (Stable)          │
│   Lambda v2    │  │           Lambda v1                       │
│  New Features  │  │       Current Features                    │
└────────────────┘  └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                              │
         ▼                              ▼
   Monitor Metrics              Baseline Metrics
   • Error rate                 • Error rate: 0.1%
   • Latency p99                • Latency p99: 200ms
   • Success rate               • Success rate: 99.9%

PHASE 2: Analysis (1-24 hours)
✅ Success Criteria Met:
   • Canary error rate ≤ Prod error rate
   • Canary p99 latency ≤ Prod p99 * 1.2
   • No critical errors

   → Promote canary to 100% ✅

❌ Failure Detected:
   • Canary error rate > Prod error rate
   • Timeouts increased
   • 5xx errors

   → Instant rollback (delete canary) ❌

PHASE 3: Full Deployment
┌─────────────────┐
│  100 Requests   │
└────────┬────────┘
         │ 100%
         ▼
┌────────────────────────┐
│   Production Version   │
│      Lambda v2         │
│    (Promoted)          │
└────────────────────────┘
📦 Binary Media Types
Handling Binary Content (Images, PDFs, Files)

By default, API Gateway treats all responses as UTF-8 text. Configure binary media types to handle images, PDFs, zip files, and other binary content.

🖼️ Image Support
  • JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP
  • Base64 encoding/decoding
  • Profile pictures, logos
📄 Document Handling
  • PDF, DOCX, Excel files
  • Resume downloads
  • Report generation
📦 Archive Files
  • ZIP, TAR, GZIP
  • Bulk downloads
  • Backup exports
⚠️ Size Limits
  • 10MB max (API Gateway)
  • Use S3 pre-signed URLs
  • For files > 10MB
💡 When to Use Binary Media: Perfect for anihpj when you need:
  • Candidate resume uploads (PDF, DOCX)
  • Profile picture uploads/downloads (JPEG, PNG)
  • Company logo serving
  • Job description PDF exports
  • Application package downloads (ZIP)
Enable Binary Media Types
# Python - Configure binary media types
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Update API to support binary media types
apigw.update_rest_api(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'add',
            'path': '/binaryMediaTypes/image~1jpeg'  # image/jpeg
        },
        {
            'op': 'add',
            'path': '/binaryMediaTypes/image~1png'   # image/png
        },
        {
            'op': 'add',
            'path': '/binaryMediaTypes/application~1pdf'  # application/pdf
        },
        {
            'op': 'add',
            'path': '/binaryMediaTypes/application~1zip'  # application/zip
        }
    ]
)

# AWS CLI equivalent
"""
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --patch-operations \
        op=add,path=/binaryMediaTypes/image~1jpeg \
        op=add,path=/binaryMediaTypes/image~1png \
        op=add,path=/binaryMediaTypes/application~1pdf
"""
Lambda Function - Return Binary Content
# Lambda function to serve resume PDFs for anihpj job board
import json
import base64
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Serve candidate resume PDFs"""
    
    # Get resume ID from path
    resume_id = event['pathParameters']['resumeId']
    
    # Download from S3
    response = s3.get_object(
        Bucket='anihpj-resumes',
        Key=f'resumes/{resume_id}.pdf'
    )
    
    pdf_content = response['Body'].read()
    
    # Return base64-encoded binary content
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'headers': {
            'Content-Type': 'application/pdf',
            'Content-Disposition': f'attachment; filename="resume_{resume_id}.pdf"'
        },
        'body': base64.b64encode(pdf_content).decode('utf-8'),
        'isBase64Encoded': True  # Critical flag!
    }

# API Gateway routes:
# GET /resumes/{resumeId}/download → Lambda → S3 PDF → Base64 → Client
Upload Binary Content (Image Upload)
# Lambda - Handle profile picture uploads
import json
import base64
import boto3
import uuid

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Handle profile picture uploads for candidates"""
    
    # API Gateway sends binary content as base64
    image_data = base64.b64decode(event['body'])
    
    # Get content type from header
    content_type = event['headers'].get('Content-Type', 'image/jpeg')
    
    # Generate unique filename
    image_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    extension = content_type.split('/')[-1]  # jpeg, png, etc.
    
    # Upload to S3
    s3.put_object(
        Bucket='anihpj-profile-pictures',
        Key=f'profiles/{image_id}.{extension}',
        Body=image_data,
        ContentType=content_type,
        ACL='public-read'
    )
    
    image_url = f"https://anihpj-profile-pictures.s3.amazonaws.com/profiles/{image_id}.{extension}"
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        'body': json.dumps({
            'message': 'Profile picture uploaded',
            'imageUrl': image_url,
            'imageId': image_id
        })
    }

# Client upload:
"""
curl -X POST https://api.anihpj.com/profile/upload \
  -H "Content-Type: image/jpeg" \
  --data-binary @profile.jpg
"""

📦 Binary Media Best Practices:

  • Set Content-Type: Always specify correct MIME type in headers
  • Payload Limits: API Gateway has 10MB request/response limit (use S3 pre-signed URLs for larger files)
  • isBase64Encoded Flag: Must set to true in Lambda response for binary content
  • S3 Integration: For large files, use S3 pre-signed URLs instead of proxying through API Gateway
  • Common Types: image/*, application/pdf, application/zip, application/octet-stream
  • Testing: Use Postman or curl --data-binary to test binary uploads

✨ Best Practices

🎯 Production Best Practices
🔒
Security

Implement proper authentication, enable WAF, use HTTPS only

⚡
Performance

Enable caching, compression, use appropriate API type

🔧
Error Handling

Consistent error responses, proper status codes, retries

📊
Monitoring

CloudWatch metrics, X-Ray tracing, access logs, alarms

🔒 Security Best Practices
🛡️ Security Checklist for anihpj API
  • ✅ Use HTTPS only - Disable HTTP, enforce TLS 1.2+
  • ✅ Implement authentication - Cognito for users, IAM for services, API keys for partners
  • ✅ Enable WAF - Protect against SQL injection, XSS, DDoS
  • ✅ Use resource policies - Restrict access by IP, VPC, AWS account
  • ✅ Enable CloudTrail - Audit all API Gateway configuration changes
  • ✅ Implement rate limiting - Prevent abuse with throttling and usage plans
  • ✅ Validate input - Use request validators and JSON schemas
  • ✅ Sanitize output - Prevent information leakage in error messages
  • ✅ Use private integrations - VPC Links for internal services
  • ✅ Rotate credentials - Regular rotation of API keys and secrets
⚡ Performance Best Practices
Performance Optimization Guide
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          API GATEWAY PERFORMANCE BEST PRACTICES                   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│  1. CHOOSE THE RIGHT API TYPE                                     │
│     • HTTP API: Simple Lambda proxy, lower latency, 71% cheaper  │
│     • REST API: Need caching, transformations, API management     │
│     • WebSocket: Real-time bidirectional communication            │
│                                                                   │
│  2. ENABLE CACHING                                                │
│     • Cache GET requests for public data                          │
│     • Set appropriate TTL (5-30 minutes typical)                  │
│     • Use cache keys wisely (query params, headers)               │
│     • Monitor cache hit ratio in CloudWatch                       │
│                                                                   │
│  3. USE COMPRESSION                                               │
│     • Enable gzip compression in Lambda responses                 │
│     • Reduces payload size by 70-90%                              │
│     • Lower data transfer costs                                   │
│                                                                   │
│  4. OPTIMIZE BACKEND                                              │
│     • Lambda: Provisioned concurrency for critical endpoints      │
│     • Connection pooling for database queries                     │
│     • Async processing for heavy operations                       │
│     • Backend caching (Redis, DynamoDB)                           │
│                                                                   │
│  5. MINIMIZE PAYLOAD SIZE                                         │
