│     • Return only required fields                                 │
│     • Implement pagination for lists                              │
│     • Use GraphQL for flexible field selection                    │
│                                                                   │
│  6. LEVERAGE CDN                                                  │
│     • CloudFront in front of API Gateway                          │
│     • Cache at edge locations                                     │
│     • Reduce API Gateway calls                                    │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 Error Handling
Consistent Error Response Format
# Lambda function - Standardized error handling
import json
from datetime import datetime

class APIError(Exception):
    """Base exception for API errors"""
    def __init__(self, status_code, error_code, message, details=None):
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.error_code = error_code
        self.message = message
        self.details = details or {}

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """API handler with consistent error responses"""
    try:
        # Your business logic
        result = process_request(event)
        
        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'headers': get_cors_headers(),
            'body': json.dumps({
                'success': True,
                'data': result,
                'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
            })
        }
    
    except APIError as e:
        return error_response(e.status_code, e.error_code, e.message, e.details)
    
    except ValueError as e:
        return error_response(400, 'INVALID_INPUT', str(e))
    
    except PermissionError as e:
        return error_response(403, 'FORBIDDEN', 'Access denied')
    
    except Exception as e:
        # Log unexpected errors
        print(f"Unexpected error: {str(e)}")
        return error_response(500, 'INTERNAL_ERROR', 'An unexpected error occurred')

def error_response(status_code, error_code, message, details=None):
    """Generate standardized error response"""
    return {
        'statusCode': status_code,
        'headers': get_cors_headers(),
        'body': json.dumps({
            'success': False,
            'error': {
                'code': error_code,
                'message': message,
                'details': details or {},
                'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                'requestId': context.aws_request_id if context else None
            }
        })
    }

def get_cors_headers():
    """CORS headers for all responses"""
    return {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://anihpj.com',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type,Authorization',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }

# Example error responses:
"""
400 Bad Request:
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "code": "INVALID_INPUT",
        "message": "Job title must be at least 5 characters",
        "details": {"field": "title", "minLength": 5},
        "timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z",
        "requestId": "abc-123-def"
    }
}

404 Not Found:
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "code": "NOT_FOUND",
        "message": "Job not found",
        "details": {"jobId": "123"},
        "timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z",
        "requestId": "abc-123-def"
    }
}

429 Too Many Requests:
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "code": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
        "message": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds",
        "details": {"retryAfter": 60, "limit": 100, "window": "minute"},
        "timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z",
        "requestId": "abc-123-def"
    }
}
"""
📊 Monitoring & Alerts
CloudWatch Monitoring Setup
# Set up CloudWatch alarms for API Gateway
import boto3

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

# Alarm 1: High error rate (5xx errors)
cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
    AlarmName='anihpj-api-high-5xx-errors',
    ComparisonOperator='GreaterThanThreshold',
    EvaluationPeriods=2,
    MetricName='5XXError',
    Namespace='AWS/ApiGateway',
    Period=300,
    Statistic='Sum',
    Threshold=50,  # Alert if more than 50 5xx errors in 5 minutes
    ActionsEnabled=True,
    AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:ops-alerts'],
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'ApiName', 'Value': 'anihpj-api'},
        {'Name': 'Stage', 'Value': 'prod'}
    ]
)

# Alarm 2: High latency
cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
    AlarmName='anihpj-api-high-latency',
    ComparisonOperator='GreaterThanThreshold',
    EvaluationPeriods=3,
    MetricName='Latency',
    Namespace='AWS/ApiGateway',
    Period=60,
    Statistic='Average',
    Threshold=3000,  # Alert if avg latency > 3 seconds
    ActionsEnabled=True,
    AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:ops-alerts'],
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'ApiName', 'Value': 'anihpj-api'},
        {'Name': 'Stage', 'Value': 'prod'}
    ]
)

# Alarm 3: Low cache hit ratio
cloudwatch.put_metric_alarm(
    AlarmName='anihpj-api-low-cache-hits',
    ComparisonOperator='LessThanThreshold',
    EvaluationPeriods=5,
    MetricName='CacheHitCount',
    Namespace='AWS/ApiGateway',
    Period=300,
    Statistic='Sum',
    Threshold=1000,  # Alert if cache hits drop below 1000 in 5 min
    ActionsEnabled=True,
    AlarmActions=['arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123:ops-alerts'],
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'ApiName', 'Value': 'anihpj-api'},
        {'Name': 'Stage', 'Value': 'prod'}
    ]
)

# Enable detailed CloudWatch metrics
apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')
apigw.update_stage(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    stageName='prod',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/*/*/metrics/enabled',
            'value': 'true'
        },
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/*/*/logging/loglevel',
            'value': 'INFO'  # or 'ERROR' for production
        }
    ]
)

# Enable X-Ray tracing
apigw.update_stage(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    stageName='prod',
    patchOperations=[
        {
            'op': 'replace',
            'path': '/tracingEnabled',
            'value': 'true'
        }
    ]
)
🔢 API Versioning Strategies
Managing Multiple API Versions

API versioning allows you to make breaking changes while maintaining backward compatibility for existing clients.

🔗 URI Versioning
  • /v1/jobs, /v2/jobs
  • Most explicit & visible
  • Easy caching
  • ⭐ Recommended
📋 Header Versioning
  • X-API-Version: 2
  • Clean URLs
  • Flexible routing
  • Not browser-friendly
❓ Query Parameter
  • /jobs?version=2
  • Simple to implement
  • Cache challenges
  • Less professional
🔧 Stage Variables
  • dev/staging/prod stages
  • No code changes
  • Environment-based
  • Limited flexibility
💡 Versioning for anihpj: Recommended approach:
  • Use URI versioning: /v1/jobs, /v2/applications
  • Support old versions for 12-24 months
  • Document changes: Maintain detailed changelog
  • Deprecation warnings: Use Sunset header to warn clients
  • Backward compatibility: Add fields, don't remove them
⚖️ Versioning Strategy Comparison
Strategy Implementation Pros Cons Example
URI Versioning Path segment ✅ Simple, visible, cacheable ❌ Multiple APIs to maintain /v1/jobs, /v2/jobs
Header Versioning Custom header ✅ Clean URLs, flexible ❌ Not visible in browser X-API-Version: 2
Query Parameter ?version=2 ✅ Simple, visible ❌ Cache issues, messy /jobs?version=2
Stage Variables Stage config ✅ No code changes ❌ Limited flexibility dev/staging/prod
URI Versioning (Recommended for anihpj)
# Python - Create multiple API versions
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create API with v1 resources
api = apigw.create_rest_api(
    name='anihpj-api',
    description='Job board API with versioning'
)

root_id = apigw.get_resources(restApiId=api['id'])['items'][0]['id']

# Create /v1 resource
v1_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    parentId=root_id,
    pathPart='v1'
)

# Create /v1/jobs resource
v1_jobs = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    parentId=v1_resource['id'],
    pathPart='jobs'
)

# Add GET method for v1 (old response format)
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    resourceId=v1_jobs['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    authorizationType='NONE'
)

apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    resourceId=v1_jobs['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    type='AWS_PROXY',
    integrationHttpMethod='POST',
    uri=f'arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123:function:getJobs-v1/invocations'
)

# Create /v2 resource with improved response format
v2_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    parentId=root_id,
    pathPart='v2'
)

v2_jobs = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    parentId=v2_resource['id'],
    pathPart='jobs'
)

apigw.put_method(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    resourceId=v2_jobs['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    authorizationType='NONE'
)

apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId=api['id'],
    resourceId=v2_jobs['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    type='AWS_PROXY',
    integrationHttpMethod='POST',
    uri=f'arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123:function:getJobs-v2/invocations'
)

print("API versioned: /v1/jobs and /v2/jobs")
Header Versioning with Lambda
# Lambda function - Handle multiple versions based on header
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Route request based on API version header"""
    
    # Get version from header (default to v1)
    api_version = event['headers'].get('X-API-Version', '1')
    
    if api_version == '1':
        # Old response format (array only)
        jobs = get_jobs_from_db()
        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'body': json.dumps(jobs)
        }
    
    elif api_version == '2':
        # New response format (with metadata)
        jobs = get_jobs_from_db()
        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'body': json.dumps({
                'version': '2.0',
                'count': len(jobs),
                'jobs': jobs,
                'pagination': {
                    'page': 1,
                    'pageSize': 20,
                    'totalPages': 5
                },
                'timestamp': '2026-01-17T12:00:00Z'
            })
        }
    
    else:
        # Unsupported version
        return {
            'statusCode': 400,
            'body': json.dumps({
                'error': 'Unsupported API version',
                'supportedVersions': ['1', '2']
            })
        }

def get_jobs_from_db():
    return [
        {'id': 1, 'title': 'Python Developer', 'salary': 120000},
        {'id': 2, 'title': 'DevOps Engineer', 'salary': 130000}
    ]

# Client usage:
"""
# v1 request
curl https://api.anihpj.com/jobs \
  -H "X-API-Version: 1"

# v2 request with enhanced response
curl https://api.anihpj.com/jobs \
  -H "X-API-Version: 2"
"""

🔢 Versioning Best Practices:

  • URI Versioning Recommended: Most explicit and cacheable (/v1/jobs, /v2/jobs)
  • Semantic Versioning: Use major versions only (v1, v2, v3) not (v1.2.3)
  • Deprecation Policy: Support old versions for 12-24 months minimum
  • Sunset Header: Return Sunset: date header to warn about deprecation
  • Backward Compatibility: Add fields, don't remove them in minor updates
  • Default Version: Always have a default version if no version specified
  • Documentation: Clearly document changes between versions
📚 API Documentation Generation
Automatic Documentation with OpenAPI (Swagger)

API Gateway can automatically generate and host interactive API documentation using OpenAPI 3.0 specifications.

📖 OpenAPI 3.0
  • Industry standard format
  • Auto-generation from API
  • Export as JSON/YAML
🎨 Interactive UI
  • Swagger UI integration
  • Try-it-out functionality
  • Request/response examples
🔄 Import/Export
  • Import OpenAPI specs
  • Create APIs from docs
  • Version control specs
🛠️ SDK Generation
  • Auto-generate client SDKs
  • iOS, Android, JavaScript
  • Postman collections
💡 Documentation Best Practices for anihpj:
  • Keep it updated: Export OpenAPI spec with every deployment
  • Include examples: Real job listing and application samples
  • Document errors: All possible error codes and messages
  • Authentication guide: Clear instructions for getting API keys/tokens
  • Rate limits: Document throttling limits and quotas
Export API as OpenAPI Specification
# Export existing API Gateway as OpenAPI 3.0 JSON
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/json \
    --output text > anihpj-api-openapi.json

# Export as YAML format
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/yaml \
    --output text > anihpj-api-openapi.yaml

# Export with API Gateway extensions (for re-importing)
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --parameters extensions='apigateway' \
    --output text > anihpj-api-full.json
OpenAPI Specification Example (anihpj Jobs API)
# anihpj-api-openapi.yaml
openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: anihpj Job Board API
  description: REST API for job listings, applications, and candidate management
  version: 2.0.0
  contact:
    name: anihpj Support
    email: api@anihpj.com
  license:
    name: MIT

servers:
  - url: https://api.anihpj.com/v2
    description: Production server
  - url: https://dev-api.anihpj.com/v2
    description: Development server

paths:
  /jobs:
    get:
      summary: List all job postings
      description: Returns paginated list of active job postings
      operationId: listJobs
      parameters:
        - name: page
          in: query
          description: Page number (1-indexed)
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
        - name: limit
          in: query
          description: Number of jobs per page
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
            maximum: 100
        - name: location
          in: query
          description: Filter by location
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  jobs:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Job'
                  pagination:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Pagination'
        '400':
          description: Invalid parameters
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
      security:
        - ApiKeyAuth: []
    
    post:
      summary: Create new job posting
      description: Create a new job listing (employers only)
      operationId: createJob
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/JobInput'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Job created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Job'
        '401':
          description: Unauthorized
      security:
        - CognitoAuth: []

  /jobs/{jobId}:
    get:
      summary: Get job details
      operationId: getJob
      parameters:
        - name: jobId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Job details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Job'
        '404':
          description: Job not found

components:
  schemas:
    Job:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        title:
          type: string
        company:
          type: string
        location:
          type: string
        salary:
          type: integer
        description:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
    
    JobInput:
      type: object
      required:
        - title
        - company
        - location
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
        company:
          type: string
        location:
          type: string
        salary:
          type: integer
        description:
          type: string
    
    Pagination:
      type: object
      properties:
        page:
          type: integer
        pageSize:
          type: integer
        totalPages:
          type: integer
        totalItems:
          type: integer
  
  securitySchemes:
    ApiKeyAuth:
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      name: X-API-Key
    CognitoAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
Host Documentation with API Gateway
# Python - Create documentation version
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Create documentation version
doc_version = apigw.create_documentation_version(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    documentationVersion='2.0.0',
    stageName='prod',
    description='anihpj API v2.0 - Added job filtering and pagination'
)

# Documentation will be available at:
# https://abc123xyz.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/prod/docs

print(f"Documentation published: {doc_version['version']}")

# AWS CLI - Import OpenAPI spec to create/update API
"""
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body file://anihpj-api-openapi.yaml \
    --fail-on-warnings

# Update existing API from OpenAPI
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --mode merge \
    --body file://anihpj-api-openapi.yaml
"""

📚 Documentation Best Practices:

  • Use OpenAPI 3.0: Industry standard, tool support (Swagger UI, Postman)
  • Include Examples: Provide sample requests/responses for every endpoint
  • Error Codes: Document all possible HTTP status codes and error formats
  • Authentication: Clearly explain how to obtain and use API keys/tokens
  • Rate Limits: Document throttling limits and quotas
  • Versioning: Maintain separate docs for each API version
  • Interactive Docs: Use Swagger UI, Redoc, or AWS Console for testing
  • Changelog: Keep version history with breaking changes highlighted

💼 Common Use Cases

🎯 API Gateway Use Cases
🔮
Microservices Gateway

Single entry point for multiple backend services

⚡
Serverless Backend

Lambda-powered APIs without managing servers

📱
Mobile Backend

Scalable API for mobile apps with authentication

🔗
Legacy Modernization

Add modern API layer to existing applications

🔮 Microservices Gateway
anihpj Microservices Architecture
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         MICROSERVICES GATEWAY FOR ANIHPJ                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                   │
│                    API Gateway (api.anihpj.com)                   │
│                              │                                    │
│        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐             │
│        │                     │                     │             │
│        ▼                     ▼                     ▼             │
│  ┌──────────┐         ┌──────────┐         ┌──────────┐          │
│  │ /jobs/*  │         │ /users/* │         │/search/* │          │
│  └────┬─────┘         └────┬─────┘         └────┬─────┘          │
│       │                    │                    │                │
│       ▼                    ▼                    ▼                │
│  Lambda/ECS           Lambda/ECS           Lambda/ECS            │
│  Job Service          User Service         Search Service        │
│  • Create job         • Profile CRUD       • Elasticsearch       │
│  • Update job         • Auth               • ML matching         │
│  • List jobs          • Applications       • Recommendations     │
│                                                                   │
│  FEATURES PER SERVICE:                                            │
│  ✓ Independent deployment                                        │
│  ✓ Service-specific auth rules                                   │
│  ✓ Different rate limits                                         │
│  ✓ Isolated scaling                                              │
│                                                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
⚡ Serverless Backend
Complete Serverless Stack
# SAM template for serverless anihpj API
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31

Resources:
  # HTTP API (cheaper, simpler)
  AnihpjApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::HttpApi
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      CorsConfiguration:
        AllowOrigins:
          - https://anihpj.com
        AllowMethods:
          - GET
          - POST
          - PUT
          - DELETE
        AllowHeaders:
          - '*'
      Auth:
        Authorizers:
          CognitoAuthorizer:
            IdentitySource: $request.header.Authorization
            JwtConfiguration:
              Audience:
                - !Ref CognitoUserPoolClient
              Issuer: !Sub https://cognito-idp.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${CognitoUserPool}

  # Lambda Functions
  GetJobsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      CodeUri: src/jobs/
      Handler: get_jobs.handler
      Runtime: python3.11
      Environment:
        Variables:
          JOBS_TABLE: !Ref JobsTable
      Events:
        GetJobs:
          Type: HttpApi
          Properties:
            ApiId: !Ref AnihpjApi
            Path: /jobs
            Method: GET

  CreateJobFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      CodeUri: src/jobs/
      Handler: create_job.handler
      Runtime: python3.11
      Environment:
        Variables:
          JOBS_TABLE: !Ref JobsTable
      Events:
        CreateJob:
          Type: HttpApi
          Properties:
            ApiId: !Ref AnihpjApi
            Path: /jobs
            Method: POST
            Auth:
              Authorizer: CognitoAuthorizer

  # DynamoDB Table
  JobsTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: anihpj-jobs
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: jobId
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: jobId
          KeyType: HASH

Outputs:
  ApiUrl:
    Value: !Sub "https://${AnihpjApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod"
📱 Mobile Backend
Mobile App API with Cognito Auth
# Mobile SDK usage example (Python/JavaScript)

# Client-side (React Native/iOS/Android)
"""
import { Auth } from 'aws-amplify';
import axios from 'axios';

// Configure Amplify
Auth.configure({
    region: 'us-east-1',
    userPoolId: 'us-east-1_ABC123',
    userPoolWebClientId: 'abc123def456'
});

// Login and get JWT token
const user = await Auth.signIn('user@anihpj.com', 'password');
const token = user.signInUserSession.idToken.jwtToken;

// Make authenticated API request
const response = await axios.get('https://api.anihpj.com/v1/jobs', {
    headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`
    }
});

const jobs = response.data;
"""

# Server-side Lambda (validates token automatically via Cognito authorizer)
def lambda_handler(event, context):
    # User info available from authorizer
    user_id = event['requestContext']['authorizer']['claims']['sub']
    email = event['requestContext']['authorizer']['claims']['email']
    
    # Fetch user-specific data
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps({
            'jobs': get_recommended_jobs(user_id),
            'profile': get_user_profile(user_id)
        })
    }
🔄 Legacy System Migration
Modernize Legacy Applications with API Gateway

Use API Gateway as a facade to gradually migrate from monolithic legacy systems to modern microservices without disrupting existing clients.

🌳 Strangler Fig Pattern
  • Gradual replacement
  • One endpoint at a time
  • No big-bang migration
🔀 Traffic Routing
  • Route legacy vs new
  • Canary testing
  • Feature flags
🔄 Data Sync
  • Dual-write pattern
  • Legacy + new DB
  • Data consistency checks
⏪ Rollback Safety
  • Keep legacy running
  • Easy rollback
  • Risk mitigation
💡 Migration Timeline for anihpj Django Monolith:
  • Month 1-2: Deploy API Gateway as HTTP proxy to Django
  • Month 3-4: Migrate read endpoints (GET /jobs) to Lambda
  • Month 5-6: Migrate write endpoints (POST /applications)
  • Month 7-9: Migrate complex features (search, recommendations)
  • Month 10-12: Decommission Django, 100% microservices
  • Expected savings: 70% cost reduction, 10x faster deployments
Strangler Fig Pattern for Legacy Migration
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               STRANGLER FIG MIGRATION PATTERN                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

PHASE 1: Initial State (Legacy Only)
┌────────────┐
│  Clients   │
└──────┬─────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  Legacy Monolith     │
│  • Java/Spring       │
│  • On-premise        │
│  • Slow deployments  │
└──────────────────────┘

PHASE 2: Add API Gateway Facade
┌────────────┐
│  Clients   │
└──────┬─────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│   API Gateway        │
│   (Route 100% to     │
│    legacy)           │
└──────┬───────────────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  Legacy Monolith     │
│  HTTP Integration    │
└──────────────────────┘

PHASE 3: Migrate One Feature at a Time
┌────────────┐
│  Clients   │
└──────┬─────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│      API Gateway (Router)         │
│  • /jobs → Lambda (NEW 20%)      │
│  • /users → Legacy (80%)         │
│  • /applications → Legacy (100%) │
└────────┬────────────────┬────────┘
         │                │
    [NEW]│                │[OLD]
         ▼                ▼
┌────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
│ Lambda + DDB   │  │   Legacy     │
│ Microservices  │  │  Monolith    │
└────────────────┘  └──────────────┘

PHASE 4: Complete Migration
┌────────────┐
│  Clients   │
└──────┬─────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│      API Gateway                  │
│  • 100% routed to microservices  │
└────────┬─────────────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│    Microservices Architecture    │
│  • Lambda Functions              │
│  • DynamoDB, RDS                 │
│  • Fast deployments              │
│  • Independent scaling           │
└─────────────────────────────────┘

Legacy system decommissioned ✅
API Gateway HTTP Integration to Legacy System
# Python - Route traffic between legacy and new systems
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Example: Migrate /jobs endpoint gradually
# Create resource and method
api_id = 'abc123xyz'
root_id = apigw.get_resources(restApiId=api_id)['items'][0]['id']

jobs_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api_id,
    parentId=root_id,
    pathPart='jobs'
)

# Add GET method
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=jobs_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    authorizationType='NONE'
)

# OPTION 1: Route to legacy system (HTTP Integration)
apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=jobs_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    type='HTTP',  # Direct HTTP integration
    integrationHttpMethod='GET',
    uri='http://legacy-server.anihpj.com/api/jobs',  # Legacy endpoint
    connectionType='INTERNET',  # Or VPC_LINK for private
    requestParameters={
        'integration.request.header.X-Legacy-Auth': "'secret-token'"
    }
)

# OPTION 2: Gradually migrate to Lambda (use canary deployment)
"""
1. Deploy Lambda version of /jobs endpoint
2. Create canary with 10% traffic to Lambda
3. Monitor for 24 hours
4. Increase to 25% → 50% → 100% over weeks
5. Decommission legacy endpoint
"""

# AWS CLI - HTTP Integration to legacy
"""
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --resource-id xyz789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --type HTTP \
    --integration-http-method GET \
    --uri http://legacy-server.anihpj.com/api/jobs
"""
Request/Response Transformation for Legacy APIs
# Legacy system returns XML, but clients expect JSON
# Use API Gateway mapping templates to transform

# Step 1: Create integration response with transformation
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Add integration response with VTL template
apigw.put_integration_response(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    resourceId='resource-id',
    httpMethod='GET',
    statusCode='200',
    responseTemplates={
        'application/json': '''
        #set($inputRoot = $input.path('$'))
        {
            "jobs": [
                #foreach($job in $inputRoot.jobs.job)
                {
                    "id": "$job.id",
                    "title": "$job.title",
                    "company": "$job.company",
                    "salary": $job.salary
                }#if($foreach.hasNext),#end
                #end
            ]
        }
        '''
    },
    responseParameters={
        'method.response.header.Content-Type': "'application/json'"
    }
)

# Legacy XML response:
"""

    
        1
        Python Developer
        TechCorp
        120000
    

"""

# Transformed JSON response:
"""
{
    "jobs": [
        {
            "id": "1",
            "title": "Python Developer",
            "company": "TechCorp",
            "salary": 120000
        }
    ]
}
"""

🔄 Legacy Migration Best Practices:

  • Strangler Fig Pattern: Gradually replace legacy functionality, not big-bang migration
  • Start Small: Migrate read-only endpoints first (GET requests)
  • Dual Write: Write to both legacy and new systems during transition
  • Canary Deployments: Test new endpoints with 5-10% traffic first
  • Monitoring: Compare error rates, latency between legacy and new
  • Rollback Plan: Keep legacy system running until new system proven
  • Data Migration: Sync data between legacy DB and new DynamoDB/RDS
  • Authentication Bridge: API Gateway can translate legacy auth tokens
  • Timeline: Plan 6-18 month migration for large monoliths
Migration Checklist for anihpj Legacy System
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           ANIHPJ LEGACY MIGRATION ROADMAP                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  MONTH 1-2: Setup & Planning                                    │
│    ✅ Deploy API Gateway in front of legacy system              │
│    ✅ Configure HTTP integration to existing Django app         │
│    ✅ Set up CloudWatch monitoring and alarms                   │
│    ✅ Create test suite for all endpoints                       │
│                                                                  │
│  MONTH 3-4: Migrate Read Operations                             │
│    ✅ GET /jobs → Lambda + DynamoDB (canary 10%)               │
│    ✅ GET /companies → Lambda + DynamoDB (canary 10%)          │
│    ✅ Monitor for 2 weeks, increase to 100%                     │
│                                                                  │
│  MONTH 5-6: Migrate Write Operations                            │
│    ✅ POST /applications → Lambda (dual-write to legacy)       │
│    ✅ POST /jobs → Lambda (dual-write)                         │
│    ✅ Verify data consistency between systems                   │
│                                                                  │
│  MONTH 7-9: Migrate Complex Features                            │
│    ✅ Search functionality → ElasticSearch + Lambda             │
│    ✅ File uploads → S3 + Lambda                                │
│    ✅ Email notifications → SES + Lambda                        │
│                                                                  │
│  MONTH 10-12: Decommission Legacy                               │
│    ✅ All traffic routed to microservices                       │
│    ✅ Legacy system in read-only mode                           │
│    ✅ Final data migration and validation                       │
│    ✅ Shut down legacy servers                                  │
│                                                                  │
│  RESULTS:                                                        │
│    • 70% cost reduction                                         │
│    • 10x faster deployments                                     │
│    • 99.99% availability                                        │
│    • Independent service scaling                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Troubleshooting

❌ Common Errors & Solutions
Error Cause Solution
403 Forbidden Missing/invalid authentication Check API key, IAM permissions, Cognito token validity
429 Too Many Requests Rate limit exceeded Implement exponential backoff, request limit increase
502 Bad Gateway Lambda error, timeout, or invalid response Check Lambda logs, ensure proper response format
504 Gateway Timeout Integration timeout (29s limit) Optimize backend, use async processing for long tasks
Missing CORS headers OPTIONS method not configured Add OPTIONS method with CORS headers
Integration not found Lambda permission missing Add lambda:InvokeFunction permission to API Gateway
🐛 Debugging Techniques
Enable Detailed Logging
# Enable execution logging and access logging
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/\*/\*/logging/loglevel,value=INFO \
        op=replace,path=/accessLogSettings/destinationArn,value=arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123:log-group:api-gateway-access-logs \
        op=replace,path=/accessLogSettings/format,value='$context.requestId $context.error.message $context.error.messageString'

# View logs in CloudWatch
aws logs tail /aws/apigateway/anihpj-api --follow

# Enable X-Ray tracing for request flow visualization
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/tracingEnabled,value=true
🌐 CORS Troubleshooting
⚠️ Common CORS Issues
  • Preflight fails: Add OPTIONS method with 200 response
  • Missing headers in response: Include Access-Control-Allow-* headers in ALL responses (including errors)
  • Credentials not working: Set Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true and specific origin (not *)
  • Custom headers blocked: List all custom headers in Access-Control-Allow-Headers
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Question Answer
Can I use my own domain? Yes, configure custom domain with ACM certificate and Route 53 CNAME
What's the max timeout? 29 seconds (hard limit). Use async processing for longer operations
How to version my API? Use stages (/dev, /prod) or path-based (/v1, /v2) versioning
Can I call private VPC resources? Yes, use VPC Link with Network Load Balancer
REST API vs HTTP API - which to choose? HTTP API for simple use cases (71% cheaper). REST API for caching, transformations, API keys
How to handle file uploads? Use S3 presigned URLs instead of uploading through API Gateway (10MB limit)

📚 API & CLI Reference

💻 AWS CLI Commands
Essential API Gateway CLI Commands
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# API MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# List all APIs
aws apigateway get-rest-apis

# Get API details
aws apigateway get-rest-api --rest-api-id your-api-id

# Export API definition (OpenAPI/Swagger)
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type swagger \
    --accepts application/json \
    swagger.json

# Import API from OpenAPI spec
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body file://swagger.json

# Delete API
aws apigateway delete-rest-api --rest-api-id your-api-id

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEPLOYMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Create deployment
aws apigateway create-deployment \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --description "Release v2.1.0"

# Update stage settings
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/cacheClusterEnabled,value=true \
        op=replace,path=/cacheClusterSize,value=0.5

# Create canary deployment (10% traffic)
aws apigateway create-deployment \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --canary-settings \
        percentTraffic=10.0,useStageCache=false

# Promote canary to production
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/canarySettings

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MONITORING
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Get stage metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/ApiGateway \
    --metric-name Count \
    --dimensions Name=ApiName,Value=anihpj-api Name=Stage,Value=prod \
    --start-time 2026-01-17T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2026-01-17T23:59:59Z \
    --period 3600 \
    --statistics Sum

# Get error metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/ApiGateway \
    --metric-name 5XXError \
    --dimensions Name=ApiName,Value=anihpj-api \
    --start-time 2026-01-17T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2026-01-17T23:59:59Z \
    --period 300 \
    --statistics Sum
🐍 Boto3 SDK Reference
Complete API Gateway SDK Operations
# Complete API Gateway management with Boto3
import boto3
import json

class APIGatewayManager:
    """Comprehensive API Gateway management"""
    
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('apigateway', region_name=region)
        self.account_id = boto3.client('sts').get_caller_identity()['Account']
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # API CRUD OPERATIONS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def list_apis(self):
        """List all REST APIs"""
        response = self.client.get_rest_apis()
        return response['items']
    
    def get_api_url(self, api_id, stage='prod'):
        """Get API invoke URL"""
        return f"https://{api_id}.execute-api.{self.client.meta.region_name}.amazonaws.com/{stage}"
    
    def export_api(self, api_id, stage, output_file='api-spec.json'):
        """Export API as OpenAPI/Swagger"""
        response = self.client.get_export(
            restApiId=api_id,
            stageName=stage,
            exportType='swagger',
            accepts='application/json'
        )
        
        with open(output_file, 'wb') as f:
            f.write(response['body'].read())
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # USAGE PLANS & API KEYS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def create_complete_usage_plan(self, tier_name, daily_limit, rate_limit, burst):
        """Create usage plan with API key"""
        # Create usage plan
        plan = self.client.create_usage_plan(
            name=f'anihpj-{tier_name}-tier',
            throttle={'rateLimit': rate_limit, 'burstLimit': burst},
            quota={'limit': daily_limit, 'period': 'DAY'}
        )
        
        # Create API key
        key = self.client.create_api_key(
            name=f'{tier_name}-key-{int(time.time())}',
            enabled=True
        )
        
        # Associate key with plan
        self.client.create_usage_plan_key(
            usagePlanId=plan['id'],
            keyId=key['id'],
            keyType='API_KEY'
        )
        
        return {
            'planId': plan['id'],
            'apiKey': key['value'],
            'limits': {
                'daily': daily_limit,
                'rate': rate_limit,
                'burst': burst
            }
        }
    
    def get_usage_stats(self, usage_plan_id, start_date, end_date):
        """Get usage statistics"""
        return self.client.get_usage(
            usagePlanId=usage_plan_id,
            startDate=start_date,
            endDate=end_date
        )
    
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # DEPLOYMENT
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    def deploy_with_canary(self, api_id, stage, canary_percent=10):
        """Deploy with canary release"""
        deployment = self.client.create_deployment(
            restApiId=api_id,
            stageName=stage,
            canarySettings={
                'percentTraffic': canary_percent,
                'useStageCache': False
            }
        )
        
        print(f"Canary deployment created: {canary_percent}% traffic")
        return deployment['id']
    
    def promote_canary(self, api_id, stage):
        """Promote canary to full production"""
        self.client.update_stage(
            restApiId=api_id,
            stageName=stage,
            patchOperations=[
                {'op': 'remove', 'path': '/canarySettings'}
            ]
        )
        print(f"Canary promoted to 100% traffic")

# Usage examples
manager = APIGatewayManager()

# Create tiered usage plans
premium_plan = manager.create_complete_usage_plan(
    tier_name='premium',
    daily_limit=100000,
    rate_limit=5000,
    burst=10000
)

# Deploy with canary
manager.deploy_with_canary('your-api-id', 'prod', canary_percent=10)

# Monitor for 10 minutes, then promote
import time
time.sleep(600)
manager.promote_canary('your-api-id', 'prod')
📦 CloudFormation Templates
Infrastructure as Code with CloudFormation

Define your entire API Gateway infrastructure as code for reproducible deployments across environments.

📋 Declarative IaC
  • YAML/JSON templates
  • Version control friendly
  • Reusable across accounts
🔄 Stack Management
  • Create/Update/Delete stacks
  • Dependency management
  • Rollback on failures
🎯 Change Sets
  • Preview changes
  • Before applying to prod
  • Risk mitigation
🔗 Cross-Stack Refs
  • Export/Import values
  • Modular templates
  • DRY principle
💡 CloudFormation Benefits for anihpj:
  • Repeatable deployments: Same template for dev/staging/prod
  • Version control: Track infrastructure changes in Git
  • AWS native: No third-party tools needed
  • Free service: Only pay for resources created
  • Disaster recovery: Recreate entire stack from template
Complete API Gateway Stack (anihpj Jobs API)
# cloudformation-api-gateway.yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'anihpj Job Board API Gateway with Lambda integration'

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: prod
    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, prod]
    Description: Deployment environment
  
  ApiStageName:
    Type: String
    Default: v1
    Description: API stage name

Resources:
  # REST API
  AnihpjRestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'anihpj-api-${Environment}'
      Description: Job board REST API
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - EDGE  # Edge-optimized for global users
      MinimumCompressionSize: 1024  # Enable compression
      Policy:  # Resource policy (optional)
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'execute-api:Invoke'
            Resource: '*'

  # /jobs resource
  JobsResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      ParentId: !GetAtt AnihpjRestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: jobs

  # GET /jobs method
  GetJobsMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref JobsResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE  # Or AWS_IAM, COGNITO_USER_POOLS
      RequestParameters:
        method.request.querystring.page: false
        method.request.querystring.limit: false
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY  # Lambda proxy integration
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${GetJobsFunction.Arn}/invocations'
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200
          ResponseModels:
            application/json: Empty

  # POST /jobs method
  PostJobsMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref JobsResource
      HttpMethod: POST
      AuthorizationType: COGNITO_USER_POOLS
      AuthorizerId: !Ref CognitoAuthorizer
      RequestValidatorId: !Ref RequestValidator
      RequestModels:
        application/json: !Ref JobRequestModel
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${CreateJobFunction.Arn}/invocations'

  # Request model for validation
  JobRequestModel:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Model
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      ContentType: application/json
      Name: JobRequest
      Schema:
        $schema: 'http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#'
        type: object
        required:
          - title
          - company
          - location
        properties:
          title:
            type: string
            minLength: 5
            maxLength: 100
          company:
            type: string
            minLength: 2
          location:
            type: string
          salary:
            type: integer
            minimum: 0
          description:
            type: string

  # Request validator
  RequestValidator:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RequestValidator
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      Name: ValidateBodyAndParams
      ValidateRequestBody: true
      ValidateRequestParameters: true

  # Cognito Authorizer
  CognitoAuthorizer:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Authorizer
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      Name: CognitoAuth
      Type: COGNITO_USER_POOLS
      IdentitySource: method.request.header.Authorization
      ProviderARNs:
        - !GetAtt UserPool.Arn

  # Cognito User Pool (for authentication)
  UserPool:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPool
    Properties:
      UserPoolName: !Sub 'anihpj-users-${Environment}'
      AutoVerifiedAttributes:
        - email
      Schema:
        - Name: email
          Required: true
          Mutable: false

  # Lambda function - Get Jobs
  GetJobsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub 'anihpj-getJobs-${Environment}'
      Runtime: python3.11
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              return {
                  'statusCode': 200,
                  'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
                  'body': json.dumps({'jobs': []})
              }
      Environment:
        Variables:
          TABLE_NAME: !Ref JobsTable

  # Lambda function - Create Job
  CreateJobFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub 'anihpj-createJob-${Environment}'
      Runtime: python3.11
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              return {
                  'statusCode': 201,
                  'body': json.dumps({'message': 'Job created'})
              }

  # DynamoDB table
  JobsTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub 'anihpj-jobs-${Environment}'
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: jobId
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: jobId
          KeyType: HASH

  # IAM Role for Lambda
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: DynamoDBAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - dynamodb:GetItem
                  - dynamodb:PutItem
                  - dynamodb:Query
                  - dynamodb:Scan
                Resource: !GetAtt JobsTable.Arn

  # Lambda permissions for API Gateway
  GetJobsInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref GetJobsFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${AnihpjRestApi}/*/*'

  CreateJobInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref CreateJobFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${AnihpjRestApi}/*/*'

  # API Deployment
  ApiDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn:
      - GetJobsMethod
      - PostJobsMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      Description: !Sub 'Deployment for ${Environment}'

  # API Stage
  ApiStage:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Stage
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
      DeploymentId: !Ref ApiDeployment
      StageName: !Ref ApiStageName
      Description: !Sub '${Environment} stage'
      TracingEnabled: true  # Enable X-Ray
      MethodSettings:
        - ResourcePath: /*
          HttpMethod: '*'
          LoggingLevel: INFO
          DataTraceEnabled: true
          MetricsEnabled: true
          ThrottlingBurstLimit: 5000
          ThrottlingRateLimit: 2000
      Variables:
        environment: !Ref Environment

  # CloudWatch Log Group for API Gateway
  ApiLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub '/aws/apigateway/anihpj-${Environment}'
      RetentionInDays: 7

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: API Gateway endpoint URL
    Value: !Sub 'https://${AnihpjRestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${ApiStageName}'
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ApiEndpoint'
  
  ApiId:
    Description: API Gateway ID
    Value: !Ref AnihpjRestApi
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ApiId'

# Deploy:
# aws cloudformation create-stack \
#   --stack-name anihpj-api-prod \
#   --template-body file://cloudformation-api-gateway.yaml \
#   --parameters ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=prod \
#   --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

📦 CloudFormation Best Practices:

  • Use Parameters: Make templates reusable across environments (dev, staging, prod)
  • DependsOn: Ensure proper resource creation order (deployment depends on methods)
  • Outputs: Export values for cross-stack references
  • Nested Stacks: Break large templates into smaller, manageable stacks
  • Change Sets: Preview changes before applying to production
  • Stack Policies: Protect critical resources from accidental updates
🏗️ Terraform Configuration
Multi-Cloud IaC with Terraform

Terraform provides a cloud-agnostic way to define API Gateway infrastructure with better state management and modularity.

Terraform API Gateway Module (anihpj)
# main.tf - Complete API Gateway with Lambda
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
}

# Variables
variable "aws_region" {
  default = "us-east-1"
}

variable "environment" {
  default = "prod"
}

variable "api_stage_name" {
  default = "v1"
}

# REST API
resource "aws_api_gateway_rest_api" "anihpj_api" {
  name        = "anihpj-api-${var.environment}"
  description = "Job board REST API for anihpj"
  
  endpoint_configuration {
    types = ["EDGE"]  # Edge-optimized
  }
  
  minimum_compression_size = 1024  # Enable compression

  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
    Project     = "anihpj"
  }
}

# /jobs resource
resource "aws_api_gateway_resource" "jobs" {
  rest_api_id = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  parent_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.root_resource_id
  path_part   = "jobs"
}

# GET /jobs method
resource "aws_api_gateway_method" "get_jobs" {
  rest_api_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  resource_id   = aws_api_gateway_resource.jobs.id
  http_method   = "GET"
  authorization = "NONE"
  
  request_parameters = {
    "method.request.querystring.page"  = false
    "method.request.querystring.limit" = false
  }
}

# Lambda integration for GET /jobs
resource "aws_api_gateway_integration" "get_jobs_lambda" {
  rest_api_id             = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  resource_id             = aws_api_gateway_resource.jobs.id
  http_method             = aws_api_gateway_method.get_jobs.http_method
  integration_http_method = "POST"
  type                    = "AWS_PROXY"
  uri                     = aws_lambda_function.get_jobs.invoke_arn
}

# POST /jobs method (with Cognito auth)
resource "aws_api_gateway_method" "post_jobs" {
  rest_api_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  resource_id   = aws_api_gateway_resource.jobs.id
  http_method   = "POST"
  authorization = "COGNITO_USER_POOLS"
  authorizer_id = aws_api_gateway_authorizer.cognito.id
  
  request_validator_id = aws_api_gateway_request_validator.body_validator.id
  
  request_models = {
    "application/json" = aws_api_gateway_model.job_request.name
  }
}

# Request validator
resource "aws_api_gateway_request_validator" "body_validator" {
  rest_api_id           = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  name                  = "ValidateBodyAndParams"
  validate_request_body = true
  validate_request_parameters = true
}

# Request model (JSON Schema)
resource "aws_api_gateway_model" "job_request" {
  rest_api_id  = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  name         = "JobRequest"
  description  = "Job creation request model"
  content_type = "application/json"
  
  schema = jsonencode({
    "$schema" = "http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#"
    type      = "object"
    required  = ["title", "company", "location"]
    properties = {
      title = {
        type      = "string"
        minLength = 5
        maxLength = 100
      }
      company = {
        type      = "string"
        minLength = 2
      }
      location = {
        type = "string"
      }
      salary = {
        type    = "integer"
        minimum = 0
      }
      description = {
        type = "string"
      }
    }
  })
}

# Cognito User Pool
resource "aws_cognito_user_pool" "users" {
  name = "anihpj-users-${var.environment}"
  
  auto_verified_attributes = ["email"]
  
  schema {
    name                = "email"
    attribute_data_type = "String"
    required            = true
    mutable             = false
  }
  
  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# Cognito Authorizer
resource "aws_api_gateway_authorizer" "cognito" {
  name          = "CognitoAuth"
  rest_api_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  type          = "COGNITO_USER_POOLS"
  provider_arns = [aws_cognito_user_pool.users.arn]
  
  identity_source = "method.request.header.Authorization"
}

# Lambda function - Get Jobs
resource "aws_lambda_function" "get_jobs" {
  filename      = "lambda_get_jobs.zip"
  function_name = "anihpj-getJobs-${var.environment}"
  role          = aws_iam_role.lambda_exec.arn
  handler       = "index.lambda_handler"
  runtime       = "python3.11"
  
  environment {
    variables = {
      TABLE_NAME = aws_dynamodb_table.jobs.name
    }
  }
  
  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# DynamoDB Table
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "jobs" {
  name         = "anihpj-jobs-${var.environment}"
  billing_mode = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
  hash_key     = "jobId"
  
  attribute {
    name = "jobId"
    type = "S"
  }
  
  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# IAM Role for Lambda
resource "aws_iam_role" "lambda_exec" {
  name = "anihpj-lambda-role-${var.environment}"
  
  assume_role_policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Action = "sts:AssumeRole"
      Effect = "Allow"
      Principal = {
        Service = "lambda.amazonaws.com"
      }
    }]
  })
}

# Attach basic Lambda execution policy
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "lambda_basic" {
  role       = aws_iam_role.lambda_exec.name
  policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole"
}

# DynamoDB access policy
resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "lambda_dynamodb" {
  name = "DynamoDBAccess"
  role = aws_iam_role.lambda_exec.id
  
  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [{
      Effect = "Allow"
      Action = [
        "dynamodb:GetItem",
        "dynamodb:PutItem",
        "dynamodb:Query",
        "dynamodb:Scan"
      ]
      Resource = aws_dynamodb_table.jobs.arn
    }]
  })
}

# Lambda permission for API Gateway
resource "aws_lambda_permission" "apigw_lambda" {
  statement_id  = "AllowAPIGatewayInvoke"
  action        = "lambda:InvokeFunction"
  function_name = aws_lambda_function.get_jobs.function_name
  principal     = "apigateway.amazonaws.com"
  source_arn    = "${aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.execution_arn}/*/*"
}

# API Deployment
resource "aws_api_gateway_deployment" "api" {
  rest_api_id = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  
  depends_on = [
    aws_api_gateway_integration.get_jobs_lambda,
    aws_api_gateway_method.get_jobs,
    aws_api_gateway_method.post_jobs
  ]
  
  lifecycle {
    create_before_destroy = true
  }
}

# API Stage
resource "aws_api_gateway_stage" "api" {
  deployment_id = aws_api_gateway_deployment.api.id
  rest_api_id   = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
  stage_name    = var.api_stage_name
  
  xray_tracing_enabled = true  # Enable X-Ray
  
  access_log_settings {
    destination_arn = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.api_logs.arn
    format = jsonencode({
      requestId      = "$context.requestId"
      ip             = "$context.identity.sourceIp"
      caller         = "$context.identity.caller"
      user           = "$context.identity.user"
      requestTime    = "$context.requestTime"
      httpMethod     = "$context.httpMethod"
      resourcePath   = "$context.resourcePath"
      status         = "$context.status"
      protocol       = "$context.protocol"
      responseLength = "$context.responseLength"
    })
  }
  
  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# CloudWatch Log Group
resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "api_logs" {
  name              = "/aws/apigateway/anihpj-${var.environment}"
  retention_in_days = 7
}

# Outputs
output "api_endpoint" {
  description = "API Gateway endpoint URL"
  value       = "https://${aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id}.execute-api.${var.aws_region}.amazonaws.com/${var.api_stage_name}"
}

output "api_id" {
  description = "API Gateway ID"
  value       = aws_api_gateway_rest_api.anihpj_api.id
}

output "user_pool_id" {
  description = "Cognito User Pool ID"
  value       = aws_cognito_user_pool.users.id
}

# Deploy:
# terraform init
# terraform plan -out=tfplan
# terraform apply tfplan

🏗️ Terraform Best Practices:

  • Remote State: Use S3 backend with DynamoDB locking for team collaboration
  • Modules: Create reusable modules for API Gateway patterns
  • Variables: Parameterize everything (regions, environment names, instance sizes)
  • Data Sources: Reference existing resources instead of hardcoding ARNs
  • Lifecycle Rules: Use create_before_destroy for zero-downtime deployments
  • Workspaces: Manage multiple environments (dev, staging, prod) separately
  • Terraform Cloud: Use for CI/CD integration and collaborative planning

🔗 AWS Integration

⚡ Lambda Integration
Lambda Proxy Integration (Recommended)
# Lambda function optimized for API Gateway proxy integration
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    API Gateway proxy integration event structure:
    
    event = {
        'httpMethod': 'GET',
        'path': '/jobs/123',
        'pathParameters': {'jobId': '123'},
        'queryStringParameters': {'category': 'engineering'},
        'headers': {'Authorization': 'Bearer token...'},
        'body': '{"title": "Senior Developer"}',  # JSON string
        'requestContext': {
            'authorizer': {...},  # From Cognito/Lambda authorizer
            'identity': {'sourceIp': '1.2.3.4'},
            'requestId': 'abc-123'
        }
    }
    """
    
    # Parse HTTP method
    method = event['httpMethod']
    path = event['path']
    
    # Parse query parameters
    params = event.get('queryStringParameters') or {}
    
    # Parse body (if present)
    body = None
    if event.get('body'):
        try:
            body = json.loads(event['body'])
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            return error_response(400, 'Invalid JSON in request body')
    
    # Route based on method and path
    if method == 'GET' and path == '/jobs':
        return get_jobs(params)
    elif method == 'POST' and path == '/jobs':
        return create_job(body)
    elif method == 'GET' and '/jobs/' in path:
        job_id = event['pathParameters']['jobId']
        return get_job(job_id)
    else:
        return error_response(404, 'Not found')

def get_jobs(params):
    """List jobs with optional filtering"""
    category = params.get('category')
    limit = int(params.get('limit', 20))
    
    # Fetch from DynamoDB
    jobs = fetch_jobs_from_db(category, limit)
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'headers': cors_headers(),
        'body': json.dumps({
            'jobs': jobs,
            'count': len(jobs)
        })
    }

def cors_headers():
    """Standard CORS headers"""
    return {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://anihpj.com',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type,Authorization',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }

def error_response(status_code, message):
    """Standardized error response"""
    return {
        'statusCode': status_code,
        'headers': cors_headers(),
        'body': json.dumps({'error': message})
    }
🗄️ DynamoDB Direct Integration
Direct DynamoDB Integration (No Lambda)
# Create integration directly to DynamoDB
# Use case: Simple CRUD operations without custom logic

# Request mapping template (VTL)
{
    "TableName": "anihpj-jobs",
    "Key": {
        "jobId": {
            "S": "$input.params('jobId')"
        }
    }
}

# Integration setup
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id your-api-id \
    --resource-id resource-id \
    --http-method GET \
    --type AWS \
    --integration-http-method POST \
    --uri "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:dynamodb:action/GetItem" \
    --credentials "arn:aws:iam::123:role/APIGatewayDynamoDBRole"

# Benefits: Lower cost (no Lambda), lower latency
# Drawbacks: No custom business logic, VTL templates can be complex
🔐 Cognito Integration
Complete Cognito Setup
# Full Cognito authentication flow
import boto3

cognito = boto3.client('cognito-idp')
apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# Step 1: Create User Pool
user_pool = cognito.create_user_pool(
    PoolName='anihpj-users',
    Policies={
        'PasswordPolicy': {
            'MinimumLength': 8,
            'RequireUppercase': True,
            'RequireLowercase': True,
            'RequireNumbers': True
        }
    },
    AutoVerifiedAttributes=['email'],
    Schema=[
        {'Name': 'email', 'Required': True},
        {'Name': 'name', 'Required': True}
    ]
)

# Step 2: Create User Pool Client
client = cognito.create_user_pool_client(
    UserPoolId=user_pool['UserPool']['Id'],
    ClientName='anihpj-web-client',
    ExplicitAuthFlows=['USER_PASSWORD_AUTH']
)

# Step 3: Create Cognito Authorizer in API Gateway
authorizer = apigw.create_authorizer(
    restApiId='your-api-id',
    name='anihpj-cognito-auth',
    type='COGNITO_USER_POOLS',
    providerARNs=[user_pool['UserPool']['Arn']],
    identitySource='method.request.header.Authorization'
)

print(f"User Pool ID: {user_pool['UserPool']['Id']}")
print(f"Client ID: {client['UserPoolClient']['ClientId']}")
print(f"Authorizer ID: {authorizer['id']}")
🐍 Django Integration
Django as API Gateway Backend
# anihpj Django behind API Gateway (via ALB + VPC Link)

# settings.py
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['internal-alb-abc123.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com', 'api.anihpj.com']

# Trust API Gateway headers
USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST = True
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')

# views.py - API endpoints
from rest_framework.decorators import api_view
from rest_framework.response import Response

@api_view(['GET'])
def list_jobs(request):
    """List jobs - called via API Gateway → VPC Link → ALB → Django"""
    category = request.GET.get('category')
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(category=category) if category else Job.objects.all()
    
    return Response({
        'jobs': JobSerializer(jobs, many=True).data,
        'count': jobs.count()
    })

# API Gateway passes custom headers
# X-Forwarded-For: Original client IP
# X-API-Key: API key if used
# X-Amz-Request-Id: Request ID for tracing
⚙️ Step Functions Integration
Orchestrate Complex Workflows with Step Functions

Integrate API Gateway with AWS Step Functions to build serverless workflows that coordinate multiple AWS services for complex business processes.

🔀 Workflow Orchestration
  • Visual workflow designer
  • Multi-step processes
  • Parallel/sequential execution
⏱️ Long-Running Tasks
  • Up to 1 year duration
  • Wait states (hours/days)
  • Human approvals
🔄 Error Handling
  • Automatic retries
  • Catch & fallback logic
  • Compensating transactions
📊 Execution Tracking
  • Visual execution history
  • Detailed logging
  • Debugging workflows
💡 Step Functions for anihpj Job Applications:
  • Perfect for: Multi-step workflows exceeding 29s (API Gateway timeout)
  • Use case: Application processing (validate → save → resume upload → background check → notify employer)
  • Workflow types: Standard (long-running) vs Express (high-throughput)
  • Cost: Express workflows are 5x cheaper for high volume
  • Integration: Direct AWS service integration (DynamoDB, SNS, SES)
anihpj Job Application Workflow
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          JOB APPLICATION PROCESSING WORKFLOW                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

API Gateway: POST /applications
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│  Start Execution      │
│  (Step Functions)     │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 1. Validate           │
│    Application        │
│    (Lambda)           │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
     [Valid?] ───No──→ [Return Error]
           │
          Yes
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 2. Save to            │
│    DynamoDB           │
│    (DynamoDB Task)    │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 3. Upload Resume      │
│    to S3              │
│    (Lambda)           │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 4. Run Background     │
│    Check (3rd party)  │
│    (Lambda + Wait)    │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
     [Passed?] ───No──→ [Notify Rejection]
           │
          Yes
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 5. Notify Employer    │
│    (SNS)              │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌───────────────────────┐
│ 6. Send Confirmation  │
│    Email (SES)        │
└──────────┬────────────┘
           │
           ▼
     [Complete] → Return Success to API Gateway
API Gateway → Step Functions Integration
# Python - Create API Gateway integration with Step Functions
import boto3
import json

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')
sts = boto3.client('sts')
account_id = sts.get_caller_identity()['Account']

# Create /applications resource
api_id = 'abc123xyz'
root_id = apigw.get_resources(restApiId=api_id)['items'][0]['id']

applications_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api_id,
    parentId=root_id,
    pathPart='applications'
)

# POST method
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=applications_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='POST',
    authorizationType='AWS_IAM'
)

# AWS service integration (Step Functions)
state_machine_arn = f"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:{account_id}:stateMachine:anihpj-application-workflow"

apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=applications_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='POST',
    type='AWS',  # AWS service integration
    integrationHttpMethod='POST',
    uri=f'arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:states:action/StartExecution',
    credentials=f'arn:aws:iam::{account_id}:role/ApiGatewayStepFunctionsRole',
    requestTemplates={
        'application/json': json.dumps({
            "stateMachineArn": state_machine_arn,
            "input": "$util.escapeJavaScript($input.json('$'))"
        })
    }
)

# Integration response
apigw.put_integration_response(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=applications_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='POST',
    statusCode='200',
    responseTemplates={
        'application/json': json.dumps({
            "executionArn": "$input.json('$.executionArn')",
            "startDate": "$input.json('$.startDate')",
            "message": "Application submitted successfully"
        })
    }
)

# Method response
apigw.put_method_response(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=applications_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='POST',
    statusCode='200',
    responseModels={'application/json': 'Empty'}
)

print("Step Functions integration created!")
Step Functions State Machine Definition
{
  "Comment": "Job application processing workflow for anihpj",
  "StartAt": "ValidateApplication",
  "States": {
    "ValidateApplication": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:validateApplication",
      "Next": "SaveToDynamoDB",
      "Catch": [{
        "ErrorEquals": ["ValidationError"],
        "Next": "ValidationFailed"
      }]
    },
    "SaveToDynamoDB": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::dynamodb:putItem",
      "Parameters": {
        "TableName": "anihpj-applications",
        "Item": {
          "applicationId": {"S.$": "$.applicationId"},
          "jobId": {"S.$": "$.jobId"},
          "candidateEmail": {"S.$": "$.email"},
          "submittedAt": {"S.$": "$$.State.EnteredTime"},
          "status": {"S": "pending"}
        }
      },
      "Next": "UploadResumeToS3"
    },
    "UploadResumeToS3": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:uploadResume",
      "Next": "BackgroundCheck"
    },
    "BackgroundCheck": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:runBackgroundCheck",
      "Next": "WaitForBackgroundCheck"
    },
    "WaitForBackgroundCheck": {
      "Type": "Wait",
      "Seconds": 300,
      "Next": "CheckBackgroundStatus"
    },
    "CheckBackgroundStatus": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:checkBackgroundStatus",
      "Next": "BackgroundCheckPassed"
    },
    "BackgroundCheckPassed": {
      "Type": "Choice",
      "Choices": [{
        "Variable": "$.backgroundCheck.status",
        "StringEquals": "passed",
        "Next": "NotifyEmployer"
      }],
      "Default": "NotifyRejection"
    },
    "NotifyEmployer": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
      "Parameters": {
        "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:employer-notifications",
        "Subject": "New Job Application",
        "Message.$": "$.applicationDetails"
      },
      "Next": "SendConfirmationEmail"
    },
    "SendConfirmationEmail": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::aws-sdk:ses:sendEmail",
      "Parameters": {
        "Source": "noreply@anihpj.com",
        "Destination": {
          "ToAddresses.$": "States.Array($.email)"
        },
        "Message": {
          "Subject": {
            "Data": "Application Submitted Successfully"
          },
          "Body": {
            "Text": {
              "Data": "Your application has been submitted and is under review."
            }
          }
        }
      },
      "End": true
    },
    "NotifyRejection": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
      "Parameters": {
        "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:candidate-notifications",
        "Subject": "Application Status",
        "Message": "Thank you for your interest. Unfortunately, we cannot proceed at this time."
      },
      "End": true
    },
    "ValidationFailed": {
      "Type": "Fail",
      "Error": "ValidationError",
      "Cause": "Application data is invalid"
    }
  }
}

⚙️ Step Functions + API Gateway Best Practices:

  • Async Processing: Use Step Functions for workflows taking > 29 seconds (API Gateway timeout)
  • Status Endpoint: Create GET /applications/{executionArn} to check workflow status
  • Error Handling: Implement retry logic and catch blocks in state machine
  • Callbacks: Use task tokens for human approvals or external system integration
  • Express Workflows: Use for high-volume, short-duration workflows (< 5 min)
  • Standard Workflows: Use for long-running processes (up to 1 year)
  • Cost Optimization: Express workflows are 5x cheaper for high-throughput
📦 S3 Integration (Direct Proxy)
Serve Files Directly from S3 via API Gateway

Create a serverless file API by integrating API Gateway directly with S3, eliminating the need for Lambda functions to serve static files.

🚀 No Lambda Needed
  • Direct S3 integration
  • Lower costs (no Lambda)
  • No cold starts
⚡ Lower Latency
  • Fewer hops
  • Direct object retrieval
  • Faster response times
💾 File Operations
  • GET: Download files
  • PUT: Upload files
  • DELETE: Remove files
⚠️ Limitations
  • 10MB max file size
  • Use pre-signed URLs for 5GB
  • No file validation
💡 S3 Proxy for anihpj Resume Management:
  • Use case: GET /resumes/{resumeId} → S3 bucket (no Lambda cost)
  • Authentication: IAM role allows API Gateway to access S3
  • Binary support: application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png
  • When to add Lambda: Virus scanning, file validation, image resizing
  • Alternative: S3 pre-signed URLs for files > 10MB (up to 5GB)
S3 Proxy Integration for Resume Downloads
# Python - Create S3 proxy integration
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')
sts = boto3.client('sts')
account_id = sts.get_caller_identity()['Account']

api_id = 'abc123xyz'
root_id = apigw.get_resources(restApiId=api_id)['items'][0]['id']

# Create /resumes/{resumeId} resource
resumes_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api_id,
    parentId=root_id,
    pathPart='resumes'
)

resume_id_resource = apigw.create_resource(
    restApiId=api_id,
    parentId=resumes_resource['id'],
    pathPart='{resumeId}'
)

# GET method
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=resume_id_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    authorizationType='AWS_IAM',
    requestParameters={
        'method.request.path.resumeId': True
    }
)

# S3 integration (AWS service integration)
apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=resume_id_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    type='AWS',
    integrationHttpMethod='GET',
    uri=f'arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:s3:path/anihpj-resumes/{{resumeId}}.pdf',
    credentials=f'arn:aws:iam::{account_id}:role/ApiGatewayS3ProxyRole',
    requestParameters={
        'integration.request.path.resumeId': 'method.request.path.resumeId'
    }
)

# Integration response (return PDF)
apigw.put_integration_response(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=resume_id_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    statusCode='200',
    responseParameters={
        'method.response.header.Content-Type': 'integration.response.header.Content-Type',
        'method.response.header.Content-Disposition': "'attachment'"
    }
)

# Method response
apigw.put_method_response(
    restApiId=api_id,
    resourceId=resume_id_resource['id'],
    httpMethod='GET',
    statusCode='200',
    responseParameters={
        'method.response.header.Content-Type': True,
        'method.response.header.Content-Disposition': True
    }
)

print("S3 proxy integration created!")
print("Access: GET https://api.anihpj.com/resumes/{resumeId}")
S3 Upload via API Gateway (PUT integration)
# Python - Enable direct S3 uploads
import boto3

apigw = boto3.client('apigateway')

# PUT method for uploads
apigw.put_method(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    resourceId='resume-resource-id',
    httpMethod='PUT',
    authorizationType='AWS_IAM',
    requestParameters={
        'method.request.path.resumeId': True,
        'method.request.header.Content-Type': True
    }
)

# S3 PUT integration
apigw.put_integration(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    resourceId='resume-resource-id',
    httpMethod='PUT',
    type='AWS',
    integrationHttpMethod='PUT',
    uri='arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:s3:path/anihpj-resumes/{resumeId}.pdf',
    credentials='arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/ApiGatewayS3ProxyRole',
    requestParameters={
        'integration.request.path.resumeId': 'method.request.path.resumeId',
        'integration.request.header.Content-Type': 'method.request.header.Content-Type'
    },
    passthroughBehavior='WHEN_NO_MATCH'
)

# Integration response
apigw.put_integration_response(
    restApiId='abc123xyz',
    resourceId='resume-resource-id',
    httpMethod='PUT',
    statusCode='200'
)

print("Direct S3 upload enabled!")

# Client usage:
"""
# Upload resume directly to S3 via API Gateway
curl -X PUT https://api.anihpj.com/resumes/candidate-123 \
  -H "Content-Type: application/pdf" \
  -H "Authorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 ..." \
  --data-binary @resume.pdf

# No Lambda needed! API Gateway writes directly to S3
"""
IAM Role for S3 Proxy Integration
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "AllowAPIGatewayToAssumeRole",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": {
        "Service": "apigateway.amazonaws.com"
      },
      "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"
    }
  ]
}

// IAM Policy attached to role
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "s3:GetObject",
        "s3:PutObject"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-resumes/*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "s3:ListBucket",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-resumes"
    }
  ]
}
S3 + API Gateway Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              S3 PROXY INTEGRATION ARCHITECTURE                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SCENARIO 1: Download Resumes
