💡 anihpj Next Steps
  • ✅ Start Small: Create a simple 3-state workflow (validate → save → notify) to get comfortable
  • ✅ Use Templates: Leverage Workflow Studio's pre-built templates for common patterns
  • ✅ Enable Logging: Turn on CloudWatch Logs from day one for easier debugging
  • ✅ Test Locally: Use Step Functions Local Docker image during development
  • ✅ Version Control: Store state machine definitions in Git alongside your Django code
  • ✅ Monitor Costs: Set up billing alerts to track state transition costs
  • ✅ Join Community: Follow the AWS Step Functions blog and participate in forums

Analytics & Monitoring Services

AWS Analytics & Monitoring services provide powerful tools for exploring, analyzing, and visualizing your application data. This section covers querying and analyzing logs from your anihpj job posting platform using CloudWatch Logs Insights.

CloudWatch Logs Insights

Category: Analytics & Monitoring  |  Use Case: Log Analysis & Troubleshooting  |  Pricing: $0.005 per GB scanned  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS Logs Insights Docs

Interactive log analytics service with a purpose-built query language for exploring, analyzing, and visualizing logs from your Django applications at any scale.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Interactive Log Analytics Service - "SQL for Your Logs" with Sub-second Query Performance

Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights is a fully managed, serverless log analytics service that enables you to explore, analyze, and visualize your logs instantly. Think of it as "SQL for your logs" - a powerful query engine designed specifically for log data at any scale.

🎯 Key Benefits
⚡ Lightning Fast
  • Sub-second query execution
  • Terabytes in seconds
  • Parallel processing
🔍 Auto-Discovery
  • JSON field extraction
  • No schema required
  • Dynamic field detection
📊 Visualizations
  • Time series charts
  • Bar graphs & tables
  • Dashboard widgets
🔗 Cross-Account
  • Query multiple accounts
  • Centralized logging
  • Organization-wide analysis
💾 Saved Queries
  • Reusable templates
  • Team sharing
  • Export to S3
💰 Cost Effective
  • Pay per GB scanned
  • $0.005 per GB
  • No minimum fees
💡 When to Use Logs Insights: Perfect for your anihpj Django portal when you need to:
  • Quickly analyze application errors and stack traces
  • Find slow API endpoints affecting user experience
  • Track user behavior and journey patterns
  • Investigate security incidents and unauthorized access
  • Create operational dashboards from log data
  • Debug Celery task failures and job processing issues
⚖️ Logs Insights vs. Other Analytics Services
Feature Logs Insights Athena OpenSearch CloudWatch Metrics
Data Type CloudWatch Logs S3 files (CSV, JSON, Parquet) Any (indexed) Time-series metrics
Query Language Logs Insights QL SQL (Presto) Lucene/DSL Metric Math
Query Speed Sub-second to seconds Seconds to minutes Milliseconds Milliseconds
Schema Required ❌ No (auto-discovery) ✅ Yes (tables) ⚠️ Optional (mappings) ❌ No
Pricing $0.005/GB scanned $5/TB scanned Instance-based Free (with logs)
Best For Ad-hoc log analysis S3 data lakes Full-text search Real-time monitoring
🏗️ CloudWatch Logs Insights Architecture - How Data Flows
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CLOUDWATCH LOGS INSIGHTS ARCHITECTURE                     │
│                                                                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                         LOG SOURCES (Your anihpj Stack)                    │ │
│  │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐       │ │
│  │  │   Django    │  │   Celery    │  │   Nginx     │  │   Lambda    │       │ │
│  │  │   App       │  │   Workers   │  │   Access    │  │  Functions  │       │ │
│  │  │  (Gunicorn) │  │  (Tasks)    │  │   Logs      │  │  (API)      │       │ │
│  │  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘       │ │
│  │         │                │                │                │              │ │
│  │         ▼                ▼                ▼                ▼              │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│ │
│  │  │              CloudWatch Logs Agent / watchtower Library              ││ │
│  │  │  • Structured JSON logging    • Automatic batching & compression     ││ │
│  │  │  • Request context injection  • Async log shipping                   ││ │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┘│ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                          │                                     │
│                                          ▼                                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                         CLOUDWATCH LOGS (Storage)                          ││
│  │                                                                            ││
│  │  Log Group: /anihpj/production/django                                      ││
│  │  ├── Stream: web-server-1/2026/01/18  ──▶ [event] [event] [event]...     ││
│  │  ├── Stream: web-server-2/2026/01/18  ──▶ [event] [event] [event]...     ││
│  │  └── Stream: celery-worker/2026/01/18 ──▶ [event] [event] [event]...     ││
│  │                                                                            ││
│  │  Log Group: /anihpj/production/nginx                                       ││
│  │  └── Stream: access-logs              ──▶ [event] [event] [event]...     ││
│  │                                                                            ││
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ││
│  │  │  Automatic Field Extraction (JSON logs):                             │ ││
│  │  │  {"timestamp":"...","level":"ERROR","user_id":123,"path":"/api/"}    │ ││
│  │  │       ↓           ↓              ↓            ↓                      │ ││
│  │  │   @timestamp    level        user_id       path  ← Queryable fields  │ ││
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ││
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                          │                                     │
│                                          ▼                                     │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                      LOGS INSIGHTS QUERY ENGINE                            ││
│  │                                                                            ││
│  │  Your Query:                                                               ││
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ││
│  │  │  fields @timestamp, @message, level, request_path, response_time_ms  │ ││
│  │  │  | filter level = "ERROR"                                            │ ││
│  │  │  | stats count(*) as errors, avg(response_time_ms) as avg_latency    │ ││
│  │  │  |   by request_path                                                 │ ││
│  │  │  | sort errors desc                                                  │ ││
│  │  │  | limit 10                                                          │ ││
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ││
│  │         │                                                                  ││
│  │         ▼  Parallel Processing Across Log Streams                         ││
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ││
│  │  │              RESULTS (in seconds, not minutes!)                    │   ││
│  │  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │   ││
│  │  │  │ request_path        │ errors │ avg_latency                  │   │   ││
│  │  │  │ /api/jobs/          │ 47     │ 1,234 ms                     │   │   ││
│  │  │  │ /api/applications/  │ 23     │ 890 ms                       │   │   ││
│  │  │  │ /api/users/login/   │ 15     │ 456 ms                       │   │   ││
│  │  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │   ││
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ││
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                          │                                     │
│            ┌─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┐       │
│            ▼                             ▼                             ▼       │
│     ┌────────────┐               ┌────────────┐               ┌────────────┐  │
│     │ CloudWatch │               │  Metric    │               │  Export    │  │
│     │ Dashboard  │               │  Filters   │               │  to S3     │  │
│     │  Widgets   │               │  & Alarms  │               │  (Archive) │  │
│     └────────────┘               └────────────┘               └────────────┘  │
│                                                                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🤔 When to Use CloudWatch Logs Insights

Use Case Logs Insights Alternative Recommendation
Ad-hoc log analysis ✅ Excellent Athena, OpenSearch Use Logs Insights first
Real-time dashboards ✅ Good (15-min delay) CloudWatch Metrics Combine both
Long-term analytics ⚠️ Limited (retention) S3 + Athena Export to S3 for history
Full-text search ⚠️ Pattern matching OpenSearch OpenSearch for complex search
Django error tracking ✅ Perfect fit Sentry, Rollbar Logs Insights + APM tool
🎯 Common Use Cases for anihpj Django Portal
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    LOGS INSIGHTS USE CASES FOR ANIHPJ                          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                 │
│  📍 SCENARIO 1: USER REPORTS "SITE IS SLOW"                                    │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│  ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐       │
│  │ 1. IDENTIFY      │ ──▶ │ 2. ANALYZE       │ ──▶ │ 3. RESOLVE       │       │
│  │                  │     │                  │     │                  │       │
│  │ Query slow       │     │ Find pattern:    │     │ Fix slow         │       │
│  │ requests >2s     │     │ /api/jobs/ with  │     │ database query   │       │
│  │                  │     │ N+1 query issue  │     │ in JobListView   │       │
│  └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘       │
│                                                                                 │
│  Query: fields request_path, response_time_ms | filter response_time_ms > 2000 │
│                                                                                 │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  │
│                                                                                 │
│  📍 SCENARIO 2: 500 ERRORS SPIKE IN PRODUCTION                                 │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│  ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐       │
│  │ 1. DETECT        │ ──▶ │ 2. CORRELATE     │ ──▶ │ 3. ROOT CAUSE    │       │
│  │                  │     │                  │     │                  │       │
│  │ Dashboard shows  │     │ Errors started   │     │ Database         │       │
│  │ error spike at   │     │ after deployment │     │ connection pool  │       │
│  │ 14:30 UTC        │     │ at 14:28 UTC     │     │ exhausted        │       │
│  └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘       │
│                                                                                 │
│  Query: filter level = "ERROR" | stats count(*) by bin(5m) | sort @timestamp   │
│                                                                                 │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  │
│                                                                                 │
│  📍 SCENARIO 3: SECURITY INCIDENT - SUSPICIOUS LOGIN ATTEMPTS                  │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│  ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐       │
│  │ 1. AUDIT         │ ──▶ │ 2. INVESTIGATE   │ ──▶ │ 3. MITIGATE      │       │
│  │                  │     │                  │     │                  │       │
│  │ Query failed     │     │ IP 203.0.113.42  │     │ Block IP in WAF  │       │
│  │ login attempts   │     │ has 847 attempts │     │ Notify security  │       │
│  │ by IP address    │     │ from 12 countries│     │ team             │       │
│  └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘       │
│                                                                                 │
│  Query: filter action = "login_failed" | stats count(*) by client_ip          │
│                                                                                 │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  │
│                                                                                 │
│  📍 SCENARIO 4: CELERY TASK FAILURES AFFECTING JOB APPLICATIONS                │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│  ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐       │
│  │ 1. DISCOVER      │ ──▶ │ 2. DIAGNOSE      │ ──▶ │ 3. FIX           │       │
│  │                  │     │                  │     │                  │       │
│  │ Find failed      │     │ send_email task  │     │ Update SMTP      │       │
│  │ Celery tasks     │     │ failing due to   │     │ credentials in   │       │
│  │ in worker logs   │     │ SMTP timeout     │     │ Secrets Manager  │       │
│  └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘       │
│                                                                                 │
│  Query: filter @logStream like /celery/ and status = "FAILURE"                 │
│                                                                                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🧠 Core Concepts

Understanding these foundational concepts will help you write effective queries and get the most out of CloudWatch Logs Insights.

📝 Query Syntax & Language

💡 Query Language Overview

Logs Insights uses a pipe-based query language similar to Unix commands. Each command filters, transforms, or aggregates data, passing results to the next command in the pipeline.

📐 Query Structure
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       Logs Insights Query Pipeline                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│   fields @timestamp, @message, level, user_id                               │
│      │                                                                       │
│      ▼  Select which fields to display                                       │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │                         FILTER                                        │  │
│   │   | filter level = "ERROR" and ispresent(user_id)                    │  │
│   │      │                                                                │  │
│   │      ▼  Keep only matching records                                    │  │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │                         PARSE                                         │  │
│   │   | parse @message "request_time=* ms" as request_time               │  │
│   │      │                                                                │  │
│   │      ▼  Extract values into new fields                                │  │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │                         STATS                                         │  │
│   │   | stats count(*) as error_count by user_id                         │  │
│   │      │                                                                │  │
│   │      ▼  Aggregate results                                             │  │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │                         SORT & LIMIT                                  │  │
│   │   | sort error_count desc                                            │  │
│   │   | limit 20                                                          │  │
│   │      │                                                                │  │
│   │      ▼  Order and truncate results                                    │  │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                              │
│   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│                              FINAL OUTPUT                                    │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐       │
│   │ user_id        │ error_count                                     │       │
│   │ user_12345     │ 47                                             │       │
│   │ user_67890     │ 23                                             │       │
│   │ user_11111     │ 15                                             │       │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📚 Essential Query Commands
Command Purpose Example
fields Select specific fields to display fields @timestamp, @message, level
filter Keep records matching a condition filter level = "ERROR"
parse Extract values using patterns parse @message "user=*" as user
stats Calculate aggregations stats count(*) by level
sort Order results sort @timestamp desc
limit Restrict number of results limit 100
display Specify final output fields display @message, error_count
🔧 Complete Query Example for anihpj
# Find all errors in your Django app with response times
# This query analyzes your anihpj logs for slow error responses

fields @timestamp, @message, level, request_path, response_time, user_id
| filter level = "ERROR"
| filter response_time > 1000
| parse @message "status_code=*" as status_code
| stats count(*) as error_count, 
        avg(response_time) as avg_response_time,
        max(response_time) as max_response_time
    by request_path, status_code
| sort error_count desc
| limit 20

📁 Log Groups & Streams

🗂️ Log Group Hierarchy
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CloudWatch Logs Structure                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  Log Group: /anihpj/production/django                                        │
│  ├── Stream: web-server-1/2024/01/15                                        │
│  │   └── Log Events (individual log lines)                                  │
│  │       ├── 2024-01-15T10:00:01Z INFO: Request received GET /jobs/         │
│  │       ├── 2024-01-15T10:00:01Z DEBUG: Query executed in 15ms             │
│  │       └── 2024-01-15T10:00:02Z INFO: Response sent 200 OK                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  ├── Stream: web-server-2/2024/01/15                                        │
│  │   └── Log Events                                                          │
│  │       ├── 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z ERROR: Database connection timeout        │
│  │       └── 2024-01-15T10:00:03Z WARN: Retry attempt 1 of 3                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  └── Stream: celery-worker-1/2024/01/15                                     │
│      └── Log Events                                                          │
│          ├── 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z INFO: Task started: send_email            │
│          └── 2024-01-15T10:00:05Z INFO: Task completed successfully         │
│                                                                              │
│  Log Group: /anihpj/production/nginx                                         │
│  ├── Stream: nginx-1/access                                                  │
│  └── Stream: nginx-1/error                                                   │
│                                                                              │
│  Log Group: /aws/lambda/anihpj-image-processor                               │
│  ├── Stream: 2024/01/15/[$LATEST]abc123                                     │
│  └── Stream: 2024/01/15/[$LATEST]def456                                     │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📦 Log Groups
  • Container for related logs - Group logs from the same application or service
  • Retention settings - Set per log group (1 day to 10 years)
  • Access control - IAM policies at group level
  • Subscription filters - Stream to Lambda, Kinesis, or OpenSearch
# Recommended naming convention for anihpj
/anihpj/{environment}/{component}

# Examples:
/anihpj/production/django
/anihpj/production/celery
/anihpj/staging/django
/anihpj/production/nginx
📜 Log Streams
  • Sequence of log events - From a single source (container, instance, etc.)
  • Automatic creation - CloudWatch agent creates streams automatically
  • Chronological order - Events within a stream are time-ordered
  • No retention settings - Inherit from parent log group
# Stream naming patterns
{instance-id}/{date}
{container-id}/{date}
{hostname}/{component}/{date}

# Example:
i-0abc123def/2024/01/15
⚠️ Best Practice: Log Group Organization
  • Separate by application - One log group per service component
  • Include environment - production, staging, development
  • Use consistent naming - Makes cross-group queries easier
  • Set appropriate retention - Balance cost vs. compliance needs

🏷️ Fields & Data Types

🔧 System Fields (Auto-generated)
Field Type Description Example Value
@timestamp DateTime Event ingestion time 2024-01-15 10:30:45.123
@message String Raw log message content INFO: User logged in
@logStream String Name of the log stream web-server-1/2024/01/15
@log String Full log group ARN 123456789:log-group:/anihpj/django
@ingestionTime DateTime When CloudWatch received the event 2024-01-15 10:30:46.000
🎯 Discovered Fields (From JSON Logs)

When you log structured JSON, Logs Insights automatically discovers and indexes top-level fields:

📝 anihpj Structured Log Example
# Your Django log output (using python-json-logger):
{
    "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:45.123Z",
    "level": "INFO",
    "logger": "jobpost.views",
    "message": "Job posting created",
    "user_id": 12345,
    "job_id": 67890,
    "request_path": "/api/jobs/",
    "request_method": "POST",
    "response_time_ms": 156,
    "client_ip": "192.168.1.100"
}

# Logs Insights automatically discovers these fields:
#   level          → String
#   logger         → String
#   message        → String
#   user_id        → Number
#   job_id         → Number
#   request_path   → String
#   request_method → String
#   response_time_ms → Number
#   client_ip      → String
💡 Field Discovery Tips
  • Use flat JSON - Nested fields require dot notation: user.profile.name
  • Consistent field names - Same field name = same data type across logs
  • Click "Discovered fields" - In the console to see all available fields
  • Use ispresent() - Check if a field exists before filtering

📊 Query Limits & Quotas

Limit Type Default Value Adjustable? Notes
Concurrent queries 30 per account/region ✅ Yes (via support) Includes running + scheduled
Query timeout 60 minutes ❌ No Long queries may timeout
Results returned 10,000 records max ❌ No Use aggregations for larger datasets
Log groups per query 50 log groups ❌ No Can query multiple groups at once
Query string length 10,000 characters ❌ No Usually plenty for complex queries
Time range Based on retention N/A Limited by log group retention
⚠️ Important Limits for anihpj Production

If your Django application generates high log volume, consider these strategies:

  • Use time-bounded queries - Query smaller time ranges for faster results
  • Apply filters early - Filter before aggregating to reduce data scanned
  • Use stats instead of display - Aggregations can process more data
  • Schedule off-peak queries - Avoid concurrent query limits during peak hours

🚀 Getting Started

Get up and running with CloudWatch Logs Insights in minutes. This section walks you through the prerequisites, your first query, and a complete console walkthrough.

✅ Prerequisites

📋 What You Need
  1. AWS Account with CloudWatch access
  2. Logs in CloudWatch - at least one log group with data
  3. IAM Permissions:
    • logs:StartQuery
    • logs:StopQuery
    • logs:GetQueryResults
    • logs:DescribeLogGroups
🔐 Minimum IAM Policy
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": [
      "logs:StartQuery",
      "logs:StopQuery", 
      "logs:GetQueryResults",
      "logs:DescribeLogGroups",
      "logs:DescribeLogStreams",
      "logs:GetLogEvents"
    ],
    "Resource": [
      "arn:aws:logs:*:*:log-group:/anihpj/*"
    ]
  }]
}
💡 Quick Setup for anihpj

If you haven't configured logging yet, add this to your Django settings:

# settings.py - Configure Django to send structured logs to CloudWatch

import watchtower
import logging

LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'formatters': {
        'json': {
            '()': 'pythonjsonlogger.jsonlogger.JsonFormatter',
            'format': '%(timestamp)s %(level)s %(name)s %(message)s'
        }
    },
    'handlers': {
        'cloudwatch': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'watchtower.CloudWatchLogHandler',
            'log_group': '/anihpj/production/django',
            'stream_name': '{hostname}/{datetime}',
            'formatter': 'json',
        },
    },
    'loggers': {
        '': {
            'handlers': ['cloudwatch'],
            'level': 'INFO',
        },
    },
}

# Install required packages:
# pip install watchtower python-json-logger boto3

🎯 Your First Query

📝 Query Progression: From Simple to Complex
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Building Queries Step by Step                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  STEP 1: View Recent Logs                                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  fields @timestamp, @message                                           │ │
│  │  | sort @timestamp desc                                                │ │
│  │  | limit 20                                                            │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                                    │
│         ▼ Add filtering                                                      │
│                                                                              │
│  STEP 2: Filter by Log Level                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  fields @timestamp, @message, level                                    │ │
│  │  | filter level = "ERROR"                                              │ │
│  │  | sort @timestamp desc                                                │ │
│  │  | limit 20                                                            │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                                    │
│         ▼ Add aggregation                                                    │
│                                                                              │
│  STEP 3: Count Errors Over Time                                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  fields @timestamp, @message, level                                    │ │
│  │  | filter level = "ERROR"                                              │ │
│  │  | stats count(*) as error_count by bin(1h)                           │ │
│  │  | sort @timestamp desc                                                │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│         │                                                                    │
│         ▼ Add grouping                                                       │
│                                                                              │
│  STEP 4: Errors by Endpoint                                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  fields @timestamp, @message, level, request_path                      │ │
│  │  | filter level = "ERROR"                                              │ │
│  │  | stats count(*) as error_count by request_path                      │ │
│  │  | sort error_count desc                                               │ │
│  │  | limit 10                                                            │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 Starter Queries for anihpj
📊 View All Logs (Last 100)
fields @timestamp, @message
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100
🔴 Find All Errors
fields @timestamp, @message, level
| filter level = "ERROR" 
    or @message like /(?i)error|exception|traceback/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 50
🐌 Find Slow Requests
fields @timestamp, request_path, response_time_ms
| filter response_time_ms > 1000
| sort response_time_ms desc
| limit 20
📈 Request Count by Endpoint
fields request_path
| stats count(*) as requests by request_path
| sort requests desc
| limit 10

🖥️ Console Walkthrough

📱 CloudWatch Logs Insights Console Interface
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ☁️ AWS CloudWatch > Logs > Logs Insights                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─ 1. SELECT LOG GROUPS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  📁 Select log group(s)                                              [▼ Dropdown]│ │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │ ☑ /anihpj/production/django                                                │ │ │
│  │  │ ☑ /anihpj/production/celery                                                │ │ │
│  │  │ ☐ /anihpj/staging/django                                                   │ │ │
│  │  │ ☐ /aws/lambda/anihpj-processor                                             │ │ │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─ 2. TIME RANGE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  🕐 [Last 1 hour ▼]  │  📅 Custom: [2024-01-15 00:00] to [2024-01-15 23:59]   │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─ 3. QUERY EDITOR ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │ fields @timestamp, @message, level, request_path                        │  │ │
│  │  │ | filter level = "ERROR"                                                │  │ │
│  │  │ | stats count(*) as error_count by request_path, bin(15m)              │  │ │
│  │  │ | sort error_count desc                                                 │  │ │
│  │  │ | limit 20                                                              │  │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  │                                                                                │ │
│  │  [▶ Run query]  [💾 Save]  [📋 History]  [📚 Sample queries]                  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─ 4. DISCOVERED FIELDS (Right Panel) ───────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  📋 @timestamp          String    ████████████████ 100%                       │ │
│  │  📋 @message            String    ████████████████ 100%                       │ │
│  │  📋 level               String    ███████████████░ 95%                        │ │
│  │  📋 request_path        String    ███████████░░░░░ 72%                        │ │
│  │  📋 user_id             Number    █████████░░░░░░░ 58%                        │ │
│  │  📋 response_time_ms    Number    ████████░░░░░░░░ 52%                        │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─ 5. RESULTS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  📊 Query completed in 1.2 seconds | Scanned 45.2 MB | 2,341 records matched  │ │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │ [📋 Table] [📈 Line] [📊 Bar] [🥧 Pie]                                    │ │ │
│  │  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │
│  │  │ request_path            │ error_count │ bin(@timestamp)                   │ │ │
│  │  │ /api/jobs/              │ 47          │ 2024-01-15 10:00:00              │ │ │
│  │  │ /api/users/login/       │ 23          │ 2024-01-15 10:00:00              │ │ │
│  │  │ /api/applications/      │ 15          │ 2024-01-15 10:15:00              │ │ │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                │ │
│  │  [📤 Export to CSV]  [➕ Add to Dashboard]  [🔔 Create Alarm]                 │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📝 Step-by-Step Guide
  1. Navigate to Logs Insights

    Go to CloudWatch Console → Logs → Logs Insights

  2. Select Your Log Groups

    Click the dropdown and select /anihpj/production/django (you can select multiple)

  3. Choose Time Range

    Select "Last 1 hour" for testing, or custom range for specific investigations

  4. Enter Your Query

    Start with the simple query: fields @timestamp, @message | limit 20

  5. Click "Run Query"

    Results appear in seconds. Check "Discovered fields" to see available fields

  6. Refine Your Query

    Add filters, aggregations, and sorting based on discovered fields

  7. Save Useful Queries

    Click "Save" to store queries you'll reuse

⚡ Key Features

📖 Query Commands Reference

🔧 Complete Command Reference
Command Syntax Description
fields fields field1, field2, ... Select fields to retrieve (use * for all)
filter filter expression Keep only records matching the expression
parse parse field "pattern" as name Extract values using glob or regex patterns
stats stats func() [by field] Calculate aggregations (count, avg, sum, etc.)
sort sort field [asc|desc] Order results by field value
limit limit n Return only first n records
display display field1, field2 Specify final output fields (after stats)
dedup dedup field Remove duplicate values for a field
unmask unmask(field) Unmask data protected by data protection policy
🔤 String Functions
# String manipulation functions
| filter @message like /error/i                    # Case-insensitive regex
| filter @message =~ /^ERROR:/                     # Regex match
| filter @message not like "DEBUG"                 # Exclude pattern

| parse @message "user=* action=*" as user, action # Glob pattern extraction
| parse @message /user=(?<user>\w+)/               # Regex extraction

# String functions
| fields strlen(@message) as msg_length            # String length
| filter strcontains(@message, "timeout")          # Contains check
| fields replace(@message, "ERROR", "ERR")         # Replace text
| fields toupper(level) as LEVEL                   # Uppercase
| fields tolower(request_path) as path             # Lowercase
| fields trim(@message) as clean_message           # Trim whitespace
| fields concat(level, ": ", @message) as full     # Concatenate
🔢 Numeric & Date Functions
# Numeric functions
| filter response_time_ms > 1000
| fields abs(temperature)                          # Absolute value
| fields ceil(response_time_ms / 1000)             # Round up
| fields floor(response_time_ms / 1000)            # Round down
| fields greatest(value1, value2)                  # Maximum
| fields least(value1, value2)                     # Minimum

# Date/Time functions
| fields datefloor(@timestamp, 1h)                 # Floor to hour
| fields dateceil(@timestamp, 1d)                  # Ceiling to day
| fields bin(1h)                                   # Group by time bucket
| filter @timestamp > ago(24h)                     # Last 24 hours
| fields fromMillis(epoch_ms)                      # Convert epoch to datetime
| fields toMillis(@timestamp)                      # Convert to epoch ms

📊 Aggregations & Statistics

Function Description Example
count(*) Count all records stats count(*) by level
count_distinct() Count unique values stats count_distinct(user_id) as unique_users
sum() Sum numeric values stats sum(bytes_sent) as total_bytes
avg() Calculate average stats avg(response_time_ms) as avg_latency
min() / max() Find minimum/maximum stats min(response_time_ms), max(response_time_ms)
pct(field, n) Calculate percentile stats pct(response_time_ms, 95) as p95
stddev() Standard deviation stats stddev(response_time_ms)
earliest() / latest() First/last value by time stats earliest(@message), latest(@message)
🔧 Aggregation Examples for anihpj
📈 Response Time Percentiles
# Get P50, P90, P95, P99 latencies by endpoint
fields request_path, response_time_ms
| filter ispresent(response_time_ms)
| stats 
    count(*) as requests,
    avg(response_time_ms) as avg_ms,
    pct(response_time_ms, 50) as p50,
    pct(response_time_ms, 90) as p90,
    pct(response_time_ms, 95) as p95,
    pct(response_time_ms, 99) as p99
  by request_path
| sort p95 desc
📊 Error Rate by Hour
# Calculate error rate percentage
fields level
| stats 
    count(*) as total,
    sum(level = "ERROR") as errors
  by bin(1h)
| fields 
    @timestamp,
    total,
    errors,
    (errors / total) * 100 as error_rate_pct
| sort @timestamp desc

📈 Visualizations & Charts

💡 Visualization Types

Logs Insights automatically offers different chart types based on your query results. Use stats with bin() for time series, or group by a field for categorical charts.

📊 Available Chart Types
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Visualization Options                               │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│  📋 TABLE VIEW                    📈 LINE CHART (Time Series)              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ endpoint    │ count │ p95  │  │         ╭─╮                         │ │
│  │─────────────┼───────┼──────│  │     ╭───╯ ╰──╮      ╭───╮          │ │
│  │ /api/jobs/  │ 1,234 │ 45ms │  │ ────╯        ╰──────╯   ╰────      │ │
│  │ /api/users/ │   891 │ 32ms │  │                                    │ │
│  │ /api/apply/ │   456 │ 89ms │  │ 10:00   11:00   12:00   13:00      │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                            │
│  📊 BAR CHART                     🥧 PIE/STACKED AREA                      │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ ERROR   ████████████ 234    │  │         ╱‾‾‾‾‾‾╲                    │ │
│  │ WARN    ██████ 123          │  │       ╱ INFO    ╲                   │ │
│  │ INFO    ████████████████████ │  │      │  65%     │                  │ │
│  │         │    │    │    │    │  │       ╲ ERROR   ╱                   │ │
│  │         0   100  200  300   │  │         ╲  35% ╱                    │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                            │
│  💡 Query patterns for each visualization:                                 │
│                                                                            │
│  TIME SERIES: stats count(*) by bin(15m)                                   │
│  BAR CHART:   stats count(*) by level | sort count desc                    │
│  PIE CHART:   stats count(*) by status_code                                │
│  TABLE:       Any query with multiple columns                              │
│                                                                            │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📊 Dashboard-Ready Queries for anihpj
📈 Request Volume Over Time
# Creates a time series line chart
fields @timestamp
| stats count(*) as requests by bin(5m)
| sort @timestamp asc
📊 Status Code Distribution
# Creates a bar/pie chart
fields status_code
| stats count(*) as count by status_code
| sort count desc
🔥 Error Trend with Breakdown
# Stacked area chart by error type
fields @timestamp, level
| filter level in ["ERROR", "WARN"]
| stats count(*) by level, bin(15m)
| sort @timestamp asc

💾 Saved Queries & Exports

📁 Saving Queries
  1. Write and test your query
  2. Click "Save" button
  3. Enter a descriptive name
  4. Optionally add to a folder
  5. Access later via "Saved queries" panel

Naming Convention: Use prefixes like anihpj-errors-by-endpoint or prod-latency-p95

📤 Export Options
  • CSV Download - Export results to spreadsheet
  • Add to Dashboard - Create widget from query
  • Create Alarm - Alert when conditions met
  • Export to S3 - For long-term storage (via export task)
# Export logs to S3 (via AWS CLI)
aws logs create-export-task \
  --task-name "anihpj-january-logs" \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/production/django" \
  --from 1704067200000 \
  --to 1706745600000 \
  --destination "my-log-backup-bucket" \
  --destination-prefix "exports/2024/01"

💰 Pricing

📊 Pricing Model

💡 How CloudWatch Logs Insights Pricing Works

You pay only for what you query. Charges are based on the amount of data scanned by your queries, not the amount of data stored or the number of queries run.

Component Price (US East) Notes
Data Scanned $0.005 per GB Only charged for data scanned by queries
Log Ingestion $0.50 per GB Getting logs into CloudWatch (separate charge)
Log Storage $0.03 per GB/month Based on retention period (separate charge)
Query Execution Free No charge for running queries
Saved Queries Free No charge for storing queries
📊 Cost Example for anihpj
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Monthly Cost Estimate for anihpj                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  📥 LOG INGESTION                                                           │
│  ├─ Django app: 5 GB/day × 30 days = 150 GB × $0.50 = $75.00               │
│  ├─ Celery workers: 2 GB/day × 30 days = 60 GB × $0.50 = $30.00            │
│  └─ Nginx access: 1 GB/day × 30 days = 30 GB × $0.50 = $15.00              │
│                                                          Subtotal: $120.00  │
│                                                                             │
│  💾 LOG STORAGE (30-day retention)                                          │
│  └─ 240 GB average storage × $0.03 = $7.20                                  │
│                                                          Subtotal: $7.20    │
│                                                                             │
│  🔍 LOGS INSIGHTS QUERIES                                                   │
│  ├─ Daily debugging: 10 queries × 5 GB scanned = 50 GB                     │
│  ├─ Dashboard refreshes: 100 queries × 0.5 GB = 50 GB                      │
│  └─ Weekly analysis: 20 queries × 10 GB = 200 GB                           │
│  └─ Total: 300 GB × $0.005 = $1.50                                         │
│                                                          Subtotal: $1.50    │
│                                                                             │
│  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════  │
│                                               TOTAL: ~$128.70/month         │
│                                                                             │
│  💡 Logs Insights queries are typically < 2% of total logging costs!       │
│                                                                             │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

💡 Cost Optimization

🎯 Reduce Data Scanned
  • Narrow time ranges - Query last hour vs last week
  • Use specific log groups - Don't query all groups
  • Add filters early - filter level="ERROR" first
  • Use limit - Stop scanning after enough results
# ❌ Expensive: Scans everything
fields *
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

# ✅ Cheaper: Filter before display
fields @timestamp, @message, level
| filter level = "ERROR"
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100
📉 Reduce Log Ingestion Costs
  • Log sampling - Log 10% of debug messages
  • Log levels - INFO in prod, DEBUG in staging
  • Structured logs - Smaller than verbose text
  • Remove duplicates - Log once, not per-request
# Django: Conditional debug logging
import logging
import random

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def log_debug_sampled(message, sample_rate=0.1):
    """Log debug messages with 10% sampling"""
    if random.random() < sample_rate:
        logger.debug(message)
💰 Cost Monitoring Tips
  • Set up AWS Budgets for CloudWatch costs
  • Review Cost Explorer monthly to spot trends
  • Consider Contributor Insights for high-cardinality analysis (separate pricing)
  • Use metric filters instead of queries for real-time alerting

🆓 Free Tier

Component Free Tier Allowance Duration
Log Ingestion 5 GB per month Always free
Log Storage 5 GB per month Always free
Logs Insights Queries 5 GB data scanned Always free
CloudWatch Dashboards 3 dashboards (50 metrics) Always free
💡 For anihpj Development

During development and testing, the free tier is usually sufficient. A typical development workflow generates 1-2 GB of logs per month with occasional queries. Production deployments typically exceed free tier within the first week.

🔎 Query Patterns

Real-world query patterns tailored for your anihpj Django job posting application.

🐍 Django Application Queries

📝 Request Lifecycle Analysis
# Track complete request flow for anihpj
fields @timestamp, request_id, 
       request_path, request_method,
       response_status, response_time_ms, user_id
| filter ispresent(request_id)
| sort @timestamp asc
| limit 500

# Result shows how requests flow through your app
👤 User Activity Tracking
# Find most active users in anihpj
fields user_id, request_path
| filter ispresent(user_id)
| stats count(*) as actions by user_id
| sort actions desc
| limit 20

# Identify power users and potential abuse
📊 View/API Endpoint Performance
# anihpj endpoint performance breakdown
fields request_path, response_time_ms
| filter ispresent(response_time_ms)
| stats 
    count(*) as requests,
    avg(response_time_ms) as avg_ms,
    pct(response_time_ms, 95) as p95_ms,
    max(response_time_ms) as max_ms
  by request_path
| filter requests > 10
| sort p95_ms desc
| limit 15
🗄️ Database Query Analysis
# Find slow database queries
fields @timestamp, @message, query_time_ms
| filter @message like /query_time/
| filter query_time_ms > 100
| parse @message "SQL: *" as sql_query
| stats count(*) as occurrences,
        avg(query_time_ms) as avg_time
  by sql_query
| sort avg_time desc
| limit 10

🔴 Error Analysis Patterns

💥 Exception Tracking
# Find all Python exceptions in anihpj
fields @timestamp, @message, level, logger
| filter level = "ERROR"
| filter @message like /Traceback|Exception|Error:/
| parse @message /(?<exception_type>\w+Error|\w+Exception)/ 
| stats count(*) as occurrences by exception_type
| sort occurrences desc
| limit 15
🕐 Error Timeline
# Errors over time (for incident analysis)
fields @timestamp, level
| filter level = "ERROR"
| stats count(*) as errors by bin(5m)
| sort @timestamp asc

# Visualize as time series chart
🎯 Error Correlation
# Correlate errors with specific users/endpoints
fields @timestamp, level, user_id, request_path, @message
| filter level = "ERROR"
| stats count(*) as errors by user_id, request_path
| sort errors desc
| limit 20

# Find if errors cluster around specific patterns
📈 Error Rate Trends
# Calculate error rate percentage over time
fields level
| stats count(*) as total,
        sum(level = "ERROR") as errors
  by bin(1h)
| fields @timestamp, 
         errors, 
         total,
         (errors * 100.0 / total) as error_rate_pct
| sort @timestamp asc

⚡ Performance Monitoring

🐌 Slow Request Detection
# Find slowest requests in anihpj
fields @timestamp, request_path, 
       request_method, response_time_ms,
       user_id, @message
| filter response_time_ms > 2000
| sort response_time_ms desc
| limit 50

# Investigate what's causing slowdowns
📊 Latency Percentiles by Hour
# Hourly latency distribution
fields response_time_ms
| filter ispresent(response_time_ms)
| stats 
    pct(response_time_ms, 50) as p50,
    pct(response_time_ms, 90) as p90,
    pct(response_time_ms, 99) as p99,
    max(response_time_ms) as max
  by bin(1h)
| sort @timestamp asc
🔄 Celery Task Performance
# anihpj background task analysis
fields @timestamp, task_name, task_id, 
       execution_time_ms, status
| filter @logStream like /celery/
| stats count(*) as executions,
        avg(execution_time_ms) as avg_time,
        sum(status = "SUCCESS") as successes,
        sum(status = "FAILURE") as failures
  by task_name
| sort avg_time desc
📈 Throughput Analysis
# Requests per second over time
fields @timestamp
| stats count(*) as requests by bin(1m)
| fields @timestamp, 
         requests,
         requests / 60.0 as requests_per_second
| sort @timestamp asc

🔒 Security & Audit Queries

🔐 Authentication Analysis
# Login attempt analysis for anihpj
fields @timestamp, user_id, client_ip, 
       action, status
| filter action in ["login", "logout", "login_failed"]
| stats count(*) as attempts by action, status
| sort attempts desc

# Additional: Failed logins by IP
fields @timestamp, client_ip, user_email
| filter action = "login_failed"
| stats count(*) as failed_attempts by client_ip
| filter failed_attempts > 5
| sort failed_attempts desc
🚨 Suspicious Activity Detection
# Detect potential attacks
fields @timestamp, client_ip, request_path, 
       response_status, user_agent
| filter response_status in [401, 403, 404]
| stats count(*) as blocked_requests 
  by client_ip, request_path
| filter blocked_requests > 20
| sort blocked_requests desc
| limit 20

# High count = possible scanning/attack
👑 Admin Action Audit
# Track admin actions in anihpj
fields @timestamp, user_id, user_email,
       action, target_model, target_id,
       client_ip
| filter user_role = "admin" 
    or action like /admin_/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100
🌍 Geographic Analysis
# Requests by country/region
fields client_ip, client_country, request_path
| filter ispresent(client_country)
| stats count(*) as requests by client_country
| sort requests desc
| limit 20

# Note: Requires geo-enriched logs

✅ Best Practices

⚡ Query Optimization

🎯 Query Optimization Checklist
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Query Performance Best Practices                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  1️⃣ NARROW TIME RANGE                                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ❌ BAD:  Query last 30 days when you need last hour's data            │ │
│  │  ✅ GOOD: Query specific time range matching your investigation        │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  2️⃣ FILTER EARLY IN THE PIPELINE                                             │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ❌ BAD:  fields * | stats count(*) | filter count > 10               │ │
│  │  ✅ GOOD: filter level="ERROR" | stats count(*) by endpoint            │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  3️⃣ SELECT ONLY NEEDED FIELDS                                                │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ❌ BAD:  fields *                                                      │ │
│  │  ✅ GOOD: fields @timestamp, level, request_path, response_time_ms    │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  4️⃣ USE LIMIT FOR EXPLORATORY QUERIES                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ❌ BAD:  Return all 100,000 matching records                          │ │
│  │  ✅ GOOD: | limit 100 for initial exploration                          │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  5️⃣ USE AGGREGATIONS FOR LARGE DATASETS                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ❌ BAD:  Display millions of raw log lines                            │ │
│  │  ✅ GOOD: stats count(*), avg(latency) by endpoint, bin(5m)           │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
❌ Inefficient Query
# Scans ALL data, returns too much
fields *
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 10000
| filter level = "ERROR"

Problem: Filter after limit means it processes everything first

✅ Optimized Query
# Filter first, select specific fields
fields @timestamp, @message, level, 
       request_path
| filter level = "ERROR"
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

Benefit: Scans less data, returns faster, costs less

📝 Log Formatting Guidelines

💡 Structured Logging is Key

Well-formatted logs make querying dramatically easier. Invest time in your log format upfront to save hours of query debugging later.

❌ Unstructured Logs
# Hard to parse and query
2024-01-15 10:30:45 INFO User 12345 
  created job posting 67890 from 
  IP 192.168.1.100

# Query requires complex regex:
| parse @message /User (\d+) created 
  job posting (\d+) from IP ([\d.]+)/
✅ Structured JSON Logs
{
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z",
  "level": "INFO",
  "message": "Job posting created",
  "user_id": 12345,
  "job_id": 67890,
  "client_ip": "192.168.1.100"
}

# Query is simple:
| filter user_id = 12345
🐍 Django Structured Logging Setup for anihpj
# settings.py - Recommended logging configuration

LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'formatters': {
        'json': {
            '()': 'pythonjsonlogger.jsonlogger.JsonFormatter',
            'format': '''
                %(timestamp)s %(level)s %(name)s %(message)s 
                %(pathname)s %(lineno)d %(funcName)s
            '''
        },
    },
    'handlers': {
        'cloudwatch': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'watchtower.CloudWatchLogHandler',
            'log_group': f'/anihpj/{os.environ.get("ENV", "dev")}/django',
            'stream_name': '{hostname}/{datetime}',
            'formatter': 'json',
        },
    },
    'loggers': {
        '': {
            'handlers': ['cloudwatch'],
            'level': 'INFO',
        },
        'django.db.backends': {
            'level': 'WARNING',  # Reduce database query noise
        },
    },
}

# Custom logging with context
import logging
import structlog

logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)

def create_job_posting(request, data):
    logger.info(
        "job_posting_created",
        user_id=request.user.id,
        job_id=job.id,
        company=data['company'],
        request_path=request.path,
        client_ip=get_client_ip(request),
    )

🗓️ Retention Strategies

Log Type Recommended Retention Rationale
Application Errors 90 days Long enough for incident analysis and trends
Access Logs 30-90 days Security auditing, compliance requirements
Debug Logs 7-14 days High volume, short-term debugging only
Audit/Compliance 1-7 years Legal/regulatory requirements
Performance Metrics 30 days Trend analysis, export to S3 for longer
🔧 Setting Retention via AWS CLI
# Set 30-day retention for Django logs
aws logs put-retention-policy \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/production/django" \
  --retention-in-days 30

# Set 90-day retention for error logs
aws logs put-retention-policy \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/production/errors" \
  --retention-in-days 90

# Set 7-day retention for debug logs
aws logs put-retention-policy \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/staging/django" \
  --retention-in-days 7

🔔 Alerting Integration

📊 Alerting Architecture
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    CloudWatch Logs → Alerts Pipeline                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐                 │
│  │  CloudWatch  │     │   Metric     │     │  CloudWatch  │                 │
│  │    Logs      │────▶│   Filter     │────▶│   Metric     │                 │
│  └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘                 │
│                                                   │                          │
│                                                   ▼                          │
│                                            ┌──────────────┐                  │
│                                            │  CloudWatch  │                  │
│                                            │    Alarm     │                  │
│                                            └──────────────┘                  │
│                                                   │                          │
│                    ┌─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐ │
│                    │                             │                         │ │
│                    ▼                             ▼                         ▼ │
│             ┌──────────────┐             ┌──────────────┐           ┌──────────────┐
│             │     SNS      │             │   Lambda     │           │  PagerDuty/  │
│             │  (Email/SMS) │             │  (Auto-fix)  │           │    Slack     │
│             └──────────────┘             └──────────────┘           └──────────────┘
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 Setting Up Metric Filters & Alarms
📊 Create Metric Filter
# Create metric for ERROR logs
aws logs put-metric-filter \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/production/django" \
  --filter-name "ErrorCount" \
  --filter-pattern '{ $.level = "ERROR" }' \
  --metric-transformations \
    metricName=AnihpjErrors,\
    metricNamespace=Anihpj/Application,\
    metricValue=1,\
    defaultValue=0
🔔 Create Alarm
# Alert when > 10 errors in 5 minutes
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
  --alarm-name "anihpj-high-error-rate" \
  --metric-name AnihpjErrors \
  --namespace Anihpj/Application \
  --statistic Sum \
  --period 300 \
  --threshold 10 \
  --comparison-operator \
    GreaterThanThreshold \
  --evaluation-periods 1 \
  --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:...

🔧 Troubleshooting

❌ Common Errors

Error Cause Solution
Query syntax error Invalid query syntax Check pipe placement, field names, quotes
No logs found Wrong time range or log group Verify log group has data in selected time range
Query timeout Query too broad, scanning too much data Narrow time range, add filters, use limit
Field not found Field doesn't exist in logs Check "Discovered fields" panel, verify log format
AccessDeniedException Missing IAM permissions Add logs:StartQuery, logs:GetQueryResults to role
LimitExceededException Too many concurrent queries Wait and retry, or request limit increase

🐛 Query Debugging

🔍 Debug: Why No Results?
# Step 1: Verify logs exist
fields @timestamp, @message
| limit 10

# Step 2: Check available fields
# (Look at Discovered Fields panel)

# Step 3: Test filter progressively
fields @timestamp, @message, level
| limit 100

# Then add filter:
fields @timestamp, @message, level
| filter level = "ERROR"
| limit 100

# If still no results, check level values:
fields level
| stats count(*) by level
🔧 Debug: Unexpected Results?
# Check data types
fields @timestamp, response_time_ms,
       typeof(response_time_ms) as type
| limit 10

# If type is "string" not "number":
# Your logs might be formatting numbers as strings

# Convert string to number:
fields @timestamp,
       tomillis(response_time_ms) as rt_num
| filter rt_num > 1000

# Check for null/missing fields:
fields @timestamp, user_id, 
       ispresent(user_id) as has_user
| stats count(*) by has_user
📊 Debug: Parse Not Working?
# Test parse pattern step by step

# First, see raw message:
fields @message
| limit 10

# Test glob pattern:
| parse @message "user=*" as user
| fields @message, user

# Test regex pattern:
| parse @message /user=(?<user>\d+)/
| fields @message, user

# If extraction fails, the pattern 
# doesn't match. Check:
# - Exact spacing
# - Special characters
# - Line breaks

⚡ Performance Issues

🐌 Query Running Slowly?
  • Reduce time range - Query 1 hour instead of 1 week
  • Select fewer log groups - Query specific groups
  • Add filters early - Filter before aggregations
  • Use limit - Stop after enough results
  • Simplify regex - Complex regex is slow
💰 Query Costs Too High?
  • Check data scanned - Displayed after query runs
  • Use metric filters - For continuous monitoring (cheaper)
  • Cache results - Export to S3 for repeated analysis
  • Schedule off-peak - If not time-sensitive
  • Sample logs - Query 10% of high-volume logs

🔌 API & CLI Reference

💻 AWS CLI Commands

🔍 Start a Query
# Start Logs Insights query
aws logs start-query \
  --log-group-name "/anihpj/production/django" \
  --start-time $(date -d "1 hour ago" +%s) \
  --end-time $(date +%s) \
  --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message, level
    | filter level = "ERROR"
    | sort @timestamp desc
    | limit 50'

# Returns: { "queryId": "abc123..." }
📊 Get Query Results
# Get results (poll until status = Complete)
aws logs get-query-results \
  --query-id "abc123..."

# Returns:
# {
#   "status": "Complete",
#   "results": [...],
#   "statistics": {
#     "recordsMatched": 47.0,
#     "recordsScanned": 12345.0,
#     "bytesScanned": 5242880.0
#   }
# }
⏹️ Stop a Query
# Cancel a running query
aws logs stop-query \
  --query-id "abc123..."
📋 List Log Groups
# List all anihpj log groups
aws logs describe-log-groups \
  --log-group-name-prefix "/anihpj/"

# Get log group details
aws logs describe-log-groups \
  --log-group-name-pattern "anihpj"
🔧 Complete CLI Workflow Script
#!/bin/bash
# query_logs.sh - Query anihpj logs and wait for results

LOG_GROUP="/anihpj/production/django"
QUERY='fields @timestamp, @message, level
| filter level = "ERROR"
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 20'

# Start query
QUERY_ID=$(aws logs start-query \
  --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
  --start-time $(date -d "1 hour ago" +%s) \
  --end-time $(date +%s) \
  --query-string "$QUERY" \
  --output text \
  --query 'queryId')

echo "Query started: $QUERY_ID"

# Poll for results
while true; do
  RESULT=$(aws logs get-query-results --query-id "$QUERY_ID")
  STATUS=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.status')
  
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Complete" ]; then
    echo "$RESULT" | jq '.results'
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Cancelled" ]; then
    echo "Query $STATUS"
    exit 1
  fi
  
  echo "Waiting... (status: $STATUS)"
  sleep 2
done

🐍 Boto3 Integration

📝 Python Query Function for anihpj
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta


class LogsInsightsClient:
    """CloudWatch Logs Insights client for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self, region='us-east-1'):
        self.client = boto3.client('logs', region_name=region)
        self.log_group = '/anihpj/production/django'
    
    def query(self, query_string, hours_ago=1, max_wait=60):
        """
        Execute a Logs Insights query and return results.
        
        Args:
            query_string: The Logs Insights query
            hours_ago: How many hours back to query
            max_wait: Maximum seconds to wait for results
        
        Returns:
            List of result records
        """
        end_time = int(datetime.now().timestamp())
        start_time = int((datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=hours_ago)).timestamp())
        
        # Start the query
        response = self.client.start_query(
            logGroupName=self.log_group,
            startTime=start_time,
            endTime=end_time,
            queryString=query_string
        )
        query_id = response['queryId']
        
        # Poll for results
        start = time.time()
        while time.time() - start < max_wait:
            result = self.client.get_query_results(queryId=query_id)
            status = result['status']
            
            if status == 'Complete':
                return self._parse_results(result['results'])
            elif status in ('Failed', 'Cancelled'):
                raise Exception(f"Query {status}")
            
            time.sleep(1)
        
        # Timeout - cancel query
        self.client.stop_query(queryId=query_id)
        raise TimeoutError(f"Query timed out after {max_wait}s")
    
    def _parse_results(self, results):
        """Convert Logs Insights results to list of dicts"""
        return [
            {item['field']: item['value'] for item in record}
            for record in results
        ]
    
    def get_errors(self, hours=1, limit=50):
        """Get recent errors from anihpj"""
        return self.query(f'''
            fields @timestamp, @message, level, request_path, user_id
            | filter level = "ERROR"
            | sort @timestamp desc
            | limit {limit}
        ''', hours_ago=hours)
    
    def get_slow_requests(self, threshold_ms=1000, hours=1):
        """Get requests slower than threshold"""
        return self.query(f'''
            fields @timestamp, request_path, response_time_ms, user_id
            | filter response_time_ms > {threshold_ms}
            | sort response_time_ms desc
            | limit 50
        ''', hours_ago=hours)
    
    def get_error_summary(self, hours=24):
        """Get error count by endpoint"""
        return self.query(f'''
            fields request_path, level
            | filter level = "ERROR"
            | stats count(*) as error_count by request_path
            | sort error_count desc
            | limit 20
        ''', hours_ago=hours)


# Usage in anihpj management command or view
if __name__ == '__main__':
    client = LogsInsightsClient()
    
    # Get recent errors
    errors = client.get_errors(hours=1)
    print(f"Found {len(errors)} errors in the last hour")
    for error in errors[:5]:
        print(f"  - {error.get('@timestamp')}: {error.get('@message', '')[:100]}")
    
    # Get slow requests
    slow = client.get_slow_requests(threshold_ms=2000)
    print(f"\nFound {len(slow)} slow requests (>2s)")

📚 SDK Examples

🌐 JavaScript/Node.js
const { 
  CloudWatchLogsClient,
  StartQueryCommand,
  GetQueryResultsCommand 
} = require("@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch-logs");

const client = new CloudWatchLogsClient({ 
  region: "us-east-1" 
});

async function queryLogs(queryString) {
  const now = Date.now();
  const hourAgo = now - 3600000;
  
  // Start query
  const startCmd = new StartQueryCommand({
    logGroupName: "/anihpj/production/django",
    startTime: Math.floor(hourAgo / 1000),
    endTime: Math.floor(now / 1000),
    queryString: queryString
  });
  
  const { queryId } = await client.send(startCmd);
  
  // Poll for results
  let status = "Running";
  while (status === "Running" || status === "Scheduled") {
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000));
    
    const resultCmd = new GetQueryResultsCommand({ 
      queryId 
    });
    const result = await client.send(resultCmd);
    status = result.status;
    
    if (status === "Complete") {
      return result.results;
    }
  }
  
  throw new Error(`Query ${status}`);
}

// Usage
queryLogs(`
  fields @timestamp, @message, level
  | filter level = "ERROR"
  | limit 10
`).then(results => console.log(results));
☕ Java
import software.amazon.awssdk.services.cloudwatchlogs.*;
import software.amazon.awssdk.services.cloudwatchlogs.model.*;

public class LogsInsightsQuery {
    
    private final CloudWatchLogsClient client;
    
    public LogsInsightsQuery() {
        this.client = CloudWatchLogsClient.create();
    }
    
    public List<List<ResultField>> query(
            String queryString) throws Exception {
        
        long now = System.currentTimeMillis() / 1000;
        long hourAgo = now - 3600;
        
        // Start query
        StartQueryResponse startResp = client.startQuery(
            StartQueryRequest.builder()
                .logGroupName("/anihpj/production/django")
                .startTime(hourAgo)
                .endTime(now)
                .queryString(queryString)
                .build()
        );
        
        String queryId = startResp.queryId();
        
        // Poll for results
        while (true) {
            GetQueryResultsResponse result = 
                client.getQueryResults(
                    GetQueryResultsRequest.builder()
                        .queryId(queryId)
                        .build()
                );
            
            if (result.status() == QueryStatus.COMPLETE) {
                return result.results();
            }
            
            Thread.sleep(1000);
        }
    }
}

🐍 Django Integration for anihpj

💡 Complete CloudWatch Logging Setup for anihpj

This section provides a production-ready logging configuration specifically designed for your anihpj Django job posting application.
