        segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
        
        # Add annotations for filtering in X-Ray console
        segment.put_annotation('user_id', str(request.user.id))
        segment.put_annotation('job_id', job_id)
        segment.put_annotation('environment', 'production')
        segment.put_annotation('application_type', 'job_application')
        
        # Add metadata (not searchable, but useful for debugging)
        segment.put_metadata('request_data', {
            'method': request.method,
            'path': request.path,
            'user_agent': request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT'),
            'ip_address': self.get_client_ip(request),
        }, namespace='http')
        
        # Custom subsegment for validation
        is_valid, errors = self.validate_application(request.POST)
        if not is_valid:
            segment.put_annotation('error', 'validation_failed')
            return JsonResponse({'errors': errors}, status=400)
        
        # Custom subsegment for database operations
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('save_to_database') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('operation', 'create_application')
            application = self.save_application(request, job_id)
            subsegment.put_metadata('application_id', application.id)
        
        # Custom subsegment for S3 upload (boto3 calls auto-traced)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('upload_resume') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('bucket', 'anihpj-resumes')
            resume_url = self.upload_resume_to_s3(request.FILES['resume'])
            subsegment.put_metadata('resume_url', resume_url)
        
        # Custom subsegment for external API call
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('notify_employer') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('notification_type', 'email')
            try:
                self.send_notification(job_id, application.id)
                subsegment.put_annotation('notification_sent', True)
            except Exception as e:
                subsegment.add_exception(e)
                subsegment.put_annotation('notification_sent', False)
        
        return JsonResponse({
            'status': 'submitted',
            'application_id': application.id
        }, status=201)
    
    def get_client_ip(self, request):
        x_forwarded_for = request.META.get('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR')
        if x_forwarded_for:
            return x_forwarded_for.split(',')[0]
        return request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR')
    
    @xray_recorder.capture('save_application')
    def save_application(self, request, job_id):
        """Save application to database."""
        from .models import JobApplication
        return JobApplication.objects.create(
            job_id=job_id,
            user=request.user,
            cover_letter=request.POST.get('cover_letter'),
            status='pending'
        )
    
    @xray_recorder.capture('upload_to_s3')
    def upload_resume_to_s3(self, resume_file):
        """Upload resume to S3 - boto3 calls automatically traced."""
        s3_client = boto3.client('s3')
        bucket_name = 'anihpj-resumes'
        key = f'resumes/{resume_file.name}'
        
        s3_client.upload_fileobj(resume_file, bucket_name, key)
        return f'https://{bucket_name}.s3.amazonaws.com/{key}'
    
    @xray_recorder.capture('send_notification')
    def send_notification(self, job_id, application_id):
        """Send notification via SNS - auto-traced."""
        sns_client = boto3.client('sns')
        sns_client.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:job-applications',
            Message=f'New application for job {job_id}: {application_id}',
            Subject='New Job Application'
        )
⚡ Lambda Integration

AWS Lambda has built-in X-Ray integration. Simply enable it in the function configuration - no daemon required.

✅ Enabling X-Ray in Lambda
  • Console: Configuration → Monitoring → X-Ray
  • CLI: --tracing-config Mode=Active
  • CloudFormation: TracingConfig: Mode: Active
  • SAM: Tracing: Active
  • CDK: tracing: Tracing.ACTIVE
📊 Auto-Traced Data
  • Invocation time & duration
  • Cold start detection
  • AWS SDK calls
  • Memory usage
  • Initialization time
🔧 Required Permissions
  • xray:PutTraceSegments
  • xray:PutTelemetryRecords
  • Managed policy: AWSXRayDaemonWriteAccess
  • Add to Lambda execution role
🐍 Lambda Function with X-Ray (Python)
# lambda_function.py - anihpj job processor

import json
import boto3
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all

# Patch all supported libraries BEFORE any imports that use them
patch_all()

# Initialize clients (will be auto-traced)
dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
s3_client = boto3.client('s3')
sns_client = boto3.client('sns')

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Process job application - fully traced with X-Ray.
    X-Ray automatically creates the parent segment for Lambda.
    """
    
    # Get the parent segment (created automatically by Lambda)
    segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
    
    # Add annotations for searchability
    job_id = event.get('job_id')
    user_id = event.get('user_id')
    
    segment.put_annotation('job_id', job_id)
    segment.put_annotation('user_id', user_id)
    segment.put_annotation('environment', 'production')
    segment.put_annotation('function_name', context.function_name)
    
    # Check for cold start (useful annotation)
    is_cold_start = not hasattr(lambda_handler, '_initialized')
    segment.put_annotation('cold_start', is_cold_start)
    lambda_handler._initialized = True
    
    # Add event metadata (for debugging)
    segment.put_metadata('event', event, namespace='input')
    
    try:
        # Custom subsegment for business logic
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('validate_input') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('step', 'validation')
            validate_application_data(event)
        
        # DynamoDB operations (auto-traced by patched boto3)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('fetch_job_details') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('table', 'Jobs')
            job = get_job_details(job_id)
            subsegment.put_metadata('job_status', job.get('status'))
        
        # Create application record
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('create_application') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('table', 'Applications')
            application_id = create_application(event, job)
            subsegment.put_annotation('application_id', application_id)
        
        # Upload to S3 (auto-traced)
        if event.get('resume_base64'):
            with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('upload_resume') as subsegment:
                subsegment.put_annotation('bucket', 'anihpj-resumes')
                resume_url = upload_resume(event['resume_base64'], application_id)
                subsegment.put_metadata('resume_url', resume_url)
        
        # Send notification (auto-traced)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('send_notification') as subsegment:
            subsegment.put_annotation('notification_type', 'sns')
            send_application_notification(job_id, application_id)
        
        # Success response
        segment.put_annotation('status', 'success')
        return {
            'statusCode': 201,
            'body': json.dumps({
                'message': 'Application submitted successfully',
                'application_id': application_id
            })
        }
        
    except ValidationError as e:
        segment.put_annotation('error', 'validation_error')
        segment.put_annotation('status', 'failed')
        xray_recorder.current_segment().add_exception(e)
        return {
            'statusCode': 400,
            'body': json.dumps({'error': str(e)})
        }
        
    except Exception as e:
        segment.put_annotation('error', 'internal_error')
        segment.put_annotation('status', 'failed')
        xray_recorder.current_segment().add_exception(e)
        raise  # Re-raise to let Lambda mark as failed


class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass


def validate_application_data(event):
    """Validate incoming application data."""
    required_fields = ['job_id', 'user_id', 'cover_letter']
    for field in required_fields:
        if not event.get(field):
            raise ValidationError(f'Missing required field: {field}')


def get_job_details(job_id):
    """Fetch job details from DynamoDB."""
    table = dynamodb.Table('Jobs')
    response = table.get_item(Key={'job_id': job_id})
    return response.get('Item', {})


def create_application(event, job):
    """Create application record in DynamoDB."""
    import uuid
    table = dynamodb.Table('Applications')
    application_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    
    table.put_item(Item={
        'application_id': application_id,
        'job_id': event['job_id'],
        'user_id': event['user_id'],
        'cover_letter': event['cover_letter'],
        'status': 'pending',
        'created_at': str(datetime.now())
    })
    
    return application_id


def upload_resume(resume_base64, application_id):
    """Upload resume to S3."""
    import base64
    
    resume_bytes = base64.b64decode(resume_base64)
    key = f'resumes/{application_id}.pdf'
    
    s3_client.put_object(
        Bucket='anihpj-resumes',
        Key=key,
        Body=resume_bytes,
        ContentType='application/pdf'
    )
    
    return f'https://anihpj-resumes.s3.amazonaws.com/{key}'


def send_application_notification(job_id, application_id):
    """Send SNS notification."""
    sns_client.publish(
        TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:job-applications',
        Message=json.dumps({
            'job_id': job_id,
            'application_id': application_id,
            'event': 'new_application'
        }),
        Subject='New Job Application'
    )
📄 SAM Template with X-Ray Enabled
# template.yaml - SAM template for anihpj Lambda

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: anihpj Job Application Processor with X-Ray

Globals:
  Function:
    Timeout: 30
    Runtime: python3.11
    Tracing: Active  # Enable X-Ray for all functions
    Environment:
      Variables:
        POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: anihpj
        LOG_LEVEL: INFO

Resources:
  JobApplicationFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: anihpj-job-application
      Handler: lambda_function.lambda_handler
      CodeUri: src/
      Description: Process job applications with X-Ray tracing
      MemorySize: 512
      Tracing: Active  # Explicit X-Ray enablement
      Policies:
        - DynamoDBCrudPolicy:
            TableName: !Ref JobsTable
        - DynamoDBCrudPolicy:
            TableName: !Ref ApplicationsTable
        - S3CrudPolicy:
            BucketName: !Ref ResumesBucket
        - SNSPublishMessagePolicy:
            TopicName: !GetAtt NotificationTopic.TopicName
        - Version: '2012-10-17'
          Statement:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action:
                - xray:PutTraceSegments
                - xray:PutTelemetryRecords
              Resource: '*'
      Events:
        ApiEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /jobs/{job_id}/apply
            Method: POST
            RestApiId: !Ref JobApi

  JobApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      TracingEnabled: true  # Enable X-Ray for API Gateway

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: API Gateway endpoint URL
    Value: !Sub 'https://${JobApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/'
🌐 API Gateway Integration

Enable X-Ray tracing on API Gateway to capture the complete request journey from client to backend.

✅ Enable via Console
  • API Gateway Console
  • Select your API → Stages
  • Logs/Tracing tab
  • Enable X-Ray Tracing checkbox
  • Deploy to stage
📊 Captured Data
  • Request/response timing
  • Integration latency
  • Lambda/HTTP backend time
  • Request validation time
  • Authorizer execution time
🔗 Trace Propagation
  • Auto-propagates trace ID to Lambda
  • Headers: X-Amzn-Trace-Id
  • Context passed to integrations
  • End-to-end request visibility
🔧 Enable X-Ray via CLI
# Enable X-Ray tracing on API Gateway stage
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/tracingEnabled,value=true

# Verify tracing is enabled
aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123xyz \
    --stage-name prod \
    --query 'tracingEnabled'
📚 SDK Examples

Quick reference for common X-Ray SDK patterns across different scenarios.

🔧 Common Patterns Reference
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AWS X-RAY SDK QUICK REFERENCE FOR PYTHON
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all
from aws_xray_sdk.core.models.segment import Segment
from aws_xray_sdk.core.models.subsegment import Subsegment

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. INITIALIZATION & PATCHING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Patch all supported libraries (boto3, requests, sqlite3, etc.)
patch_all()

# Or patch specific libraries only
from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch
patch(['boto3', 'requests'])

# Configure recorder
xray_recorder.configure(
    service='anihpj-api',
    daemon_address='127.0.0.1:2000',
    context_missing='LOG_ERROR',  # Options: LOG_ERROR, RUNTIME_ERROR, IGNORE_ERROR
    sampling=True,
)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. CREATING SEGMENTS & SUBSEGMENTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Method 1: Context manager
with xray_recorder.in_segment('my-segment') as segment:
    segment.put_annotation('key', 'value')
    
    with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('my-subsegment') as subsegment:
        subsegment.put_annotation('operation', 'process')
        # Your code here

# Method 2: Decorator
@xray_recorder.capture('process_job')
def process_job(job_id):
    subsegment = xray_recorder.current_subsegment()
    subsegment.put_annotation('job_id', job_id)
    # Your code here

# Method 3: Manual (for async or complex flows)
subsegment = xray_recorder.begin_subsegment('async-operation')
try:
    # Your code here
    subsegment.put_annotation('status', 'success')
finally:
    xray_recorder.end_subsegment()


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. ANNOTATIONS (Searchable, indexed)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()

# String annotation
segment.put_annotation('user_id', 'user-12345')

# Number annotation
segment.put_annotation('items_count', 42)

# Boolean annotation
segment.put_annotation('is_premium', True)

# ⚠️ Only primitives allowed (no lists, dicts, or objects)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. METADATA (Not searchable, any JSON-serializable data)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()

# Simple metadata
segment.put_metadata('key', 'value')

# With namespace (recommended for organization)
segment.put_metadata('request_body', {'name': 'John', 'email': 'john@example.com'}, namespace='http')
segment.put_metadata('user_profile', {'tier': 'premium', 'joined': '2024-01-01'}, namespace='user')


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 5. ERROR HANDLING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

try:
    risky_operation()
except Exception as e:
    # Add exception to current segment/subsegment
    xray_recorder.current_segment().add_exception(e)
    
    # Or add to subsegment
    subsegment = xray_recorder.current_subsegment()
    if subsegment:
        subsegment.add_exception(e)
    
    raise  # Re-raise or handle


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 6. HTTP TRACING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()

# Add HTTP request info
segment.put_http_meta(
    key='request',
    value={
        'method': 'POST',
        'url': 'https://api.anihpj.com/jobs/apply',
        'client_ip': '203.0.113.42',
        'user_agent': 'Mozilla/5.0...'
    }
)

# Add HTTP response info
segment.put_http_meta(
    key='response',
    value={
        'status': 201,
        'content_length': 1024
    }
)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 7. SAMPLING CONTROL
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Check if current request is sampled
if xray_recorder.is_sampled():
    # Expensive operation only for sampled requests
    detailed_logging()

# Force sampling for specific request
xray_recorder.begin_segment('forced-segment', sampling=True)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 8. ASYNC SUPPORT (asyncio)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import asyncio
from aws_xray_sdk.core.async_context import AsyncContext

# Set async context
xray_recorder.configure(context=AsyncContext())

async def async_handler():
    async with xray_recorder.in_subsegment_async('async-operation') as subsegment:
        subsegment.put_annotation('async', True)
        await some_async_operation()

✨ Best Practices

Follow these best practices to maximize the value of X-Ray while controlling costs and maintaining application performance.

🎯 Sampling Strategies
✅ DO: Smart Sampling
  • 100% sampling for errors (fault=true)
  • Higher sampling for critical paths
  • Lower sampling for high-volume endpoints
  • 0% for health checks/probes
  • Adjust based on traffic patterns
❌ DON'T: Over-Sampling
  • 100% sampling in production
  • Sampling static asset requests
  • Same rate for all environments
  • Ignoring cost implications
  • Sampling internal health checks
📊 Recommended Rates
  • Production: 1-5%
  • Staging: 10-20%
  • Development: 50-100%
  • Errors: 100% always
  • New features: 20-50%
🏷️ Annotation Guidelines
Category Recommended Annotations Use Case
Identity user_id, tenant_id, session_id Filter traces by specific user or tenant
Business order_id, job_id, transaction_id Trace specific business transactions
Environment environment, version, region Filter by deployment context
Feature feature_flag, experiment_id, ab_group A/B testing and feature analysis
Performance cold_start, cache_hit, retry_count Performance debugging
Error error_type, error_code, error_source Error categorization and filtering
⚠️ Annotation Limits:
  • Max 50 annotations per segment
  • Key length: 1-500 characters
  • String value: max 1000 characters
  • Only primitive types: string, number, boolean
  • Keys are case-sensitive
⚡ Performance Tips
🚀 Minimize Overhead
  • Use async/batch sending
  • Avoid tracing in hot loops
  • Keep metadata size small
  • Don't trace sub-millisecond ops
  • Use sampling effectively
📦 Efficient Subsegments
  • Trace meaningful operations only
  • Group related small operations
  • Use auto-patching when possible
  • Avoid deep nesting (>5 levels)
  • Close subsegments promptly
🔧 Daemon Optimization
  • Run daemon close to app (sidecar)
  • Adjust buffer size for load
  • Monitor daemon memory usage
  • Use local socket when possible
  • Consider daemon CPU allocation

🔧 Troubleshooting

Diagnose and resolve common X-Ray integration issues with these troubleshooting guides.

⚠️ Common Errors
🔴 Error Reference & Solutions
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
                    X-RAY TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ERROR: "Cannot find current segment/subsegment"                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│ SYMPTOMS:                                                                    │
│   • Runtime error when calling xray_recorder.current_segment()              │
│   • "segment cannot be None" errors                                         │
│   • Traces not appearing in console                                         │
│                                                                              │
│ CAUSES:                                                                      │
│   • Code running outside of traced context                                  │
│   • Middleware not configured                                               │
│   • Async context not set up                                                │
│                                                                              │
│ SOLUTIONS:                                                                   │
│   1. Configure context_missing='LOG_ERROR' (development) or 'IGNORE_ERROR' │
│   2. Ensure X-Ray middleware is FIRST in Django MIDDLEWARE list            │
│   3. For async: xray_recorder.configure(context=AsyncContext())            │
│   4. For threads: Create segment in each thread                            │
│                                                                              │
│ CODE FIX:                                                                    │
│   xray_recorder.configure(context_missing='LOG_ERROR')                      │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ERROR: "Failed to send segments to X-Ray daemon"                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│ SYMPTOMS:                                                                    │
│   • Traces not appearing in X-Ray console                                   │
│   • Connection refused errors in logs                                       │
│   • UDP packets not being sent                                              │
│                                                                              │
│ CAUSES:                                                                      │
│   • Daemon not running                                                      │
│   • Wrong daemon address configured                                         │
│   • Network/firewall blocking UDP 2000                                      │
│   • Container networking issues                                             │
│                                                                              │
│ SOLUTIONS:                                                                   │
│   1. Verify daemon is running: systemctl status xray                        │
│   2. Check daemon address: echo $AWS_XRAY_DAEMON_ADDRESS                    │
│   3. Test connectivity: nc -vzu 127.0.0.1 2000                             │
│   4. For ECS: Ensure sidecar container is in same task                     │
│   5. For Fargate: Link containers via localhost                            │
│                                                                              │
│ VERIFICATION COMMANDS:                                                       │
│   # Check daemon status                                                      │
│   sudo systemctl status xray                                                │
│                                                                              │
│   # Check daemon logs                                                        │
│   sudo journalctl -u xray -f                                                │
│                                                                              │
│   # Test UDP port                                                            │
│   nc -vzu localhost 2000                                                    │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ERROR: "AccessDeniedException" / IAM Permission Errors                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│ SYMPTOMS:                                                                    │
│   • "User is not authorized to perform: xray:PutTraceSegments"             │
│   • Daemon logs show permission errors                                      │
│   • Segments sent but not appearing                                         │
│                                                                              │
│ CAUSES:                                                                      │
│   • Missing IAM permissions                                                 │
│   • Wrong IAM role attached                                                 │
│   • Cross-account issues                                                    │
│                                                                              │
│ SOLUTIONS:                                                                   │
│   1. Attach AWSXRayDaemonWriteAccess managed policy                        │
│   2. For Lambda: Add to execution role                                      │
│   3. For EC2: Add to instance profile                                       │
│   4. For ECS: Add to task role (not execution role)                        │
│                                                                              │
│ REQUIRED PERMISSIONS:                                                        │
│   • xray:PutTraceSegments                                                   │
│   • xray:PutTelemetryRecords                                                │
│   • xray:GetSamplingRules                                                   │
│   • xray:GetSamplingTargets                                                 │
│   • xray:GetSamplingStatisticSummaries                                      │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ERROR: Traces appear but subsegments are missing                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│ SYMPTOMS:                                                                    │
│   • Main segment visible but no AWS SDK subsegments                         │
│   • DynamoDB/S3/etc. calls not traced                                       │
│   • Only custom subsegments appear                                          │
│                                                                              │
│ CAUSES:                                                                      │
│   • patch_all() not called                                                  │
│   • Patching after library import                                           │
│   • Library not supported                                                   │
│                                                                              │
│ SOLUTIONS:                                                                   │
│   1. Call patch_all() BEFORE importing boto3/requests                      │
│   2. Verify supported libraries are being used                              │
│   3. Check patch order in application initialization                        │
│                                                                              │
│ CORRECT IMPORT ORDER:                                                        │
│   from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch_all                                   │
│   patch_all()  # FIRST!                                                     │
│   import boto3  # AFTER patching                                            │
│   import requests  # AFTER patching                                         │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔍 Debugging Traces
🛠️ Debug Commands & Techniques
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# X-RAY DEBUGGING COMMANDS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# 1. Check if traces are being recorded (last 5 minutes)
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '-5 minutes' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query 'TraceSummaries[].Id'

# 2. Get full trace details
aws xray batch-get-traces \
    --trace-ids "1-5fb3a2c7-8b1c4d2e3f0a1b2c3d4e5f6a"

# 3. Check daemon telemetry
aws xray get-telemetry-records \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)

# 4. Validate sampling rules
aws xray get-sampling-rules

# 5. Check sampling statistics
aws xray get-sampling-statistic-summaries

# 6. Test daemon connectivity (send test segment)
echo '{"format":"json","version":1}
{"trace_id":"1-00000000-000000000000000000000000","id":"0000000000000000","name":"test","start_time":1234567890.123,"end_time":1234567890.456,"in_progress":false}' | nc -u localhost 2000

# 7. Enable SDK debug logging (Python)
import logging
logging.getLogger('aws_xray_sdk').setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

# 8. Check service graph for issues
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)
📭 Missing Data Issues
🔍 Traces Not Appearing
  • Check time range filter in console
  • Verify sampling rules aren't blocking
  • Confirm daemon is receiving data
  • Check IAM permissions
  • Wait 1-2 minutes for propagation
🔗 Broken Trace Chains
  • Verify X-Amzn-Trace-Id header propagation
  • Check async boundaries (SQS, SNS)
  • Ensure parent segment exists
  • Verify cross-service sampling
  • Check for duplicate trace IDs
📊 Incomplete Segments
  • Ensure segments are closed (end_segment)
  • Check for exceptions interrupting flow
  • Verify timeout settings
  • Look for "in_progress": true segments
  • Check Lambda timeout vs trace duration
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
🤔 General Questions

Q: How long are traces retained?

A: Traces are retained for 30 days. This is fixed and cannot be changed.

Q: Can I export traces to S3?

A: Not directly. Use the API to retrieve traces and store them yourself for longer retention.

Q: What's the maximum segment size?

A: 64 KB per segment, including all metadata and annotations.

💰 Pricing Questions

Q: Is there a free tier?

A: Yes! 100,000 traces recorded and 1,000,000 traces retrieved per month, forever.

Q: How can I reduce costs?

A: Use sampling rules (5-10% is usually sufficient), disable Insights for non-prod environments.

Q: Does the daemon cost extra?

A: No, the daemon is free. You only pay for traces recorded.

🔧 Technical Questions

Q: Do I need a daemon for Lambda?

A: No! Lambda has X-Ray built-in. Just enable it in the function configuration.

Q: Can I trace requests across accounts?

A: Yes, with proper IAM trust relationships. The trace ID propagates automatically.

Q: What's the difference between annotations and metadata?

A: Annotations are indexed and searchable (max 50). Metadata is for debugging only.

⌨️ API & CLI Reference

Quick reference for common AWS CLI commands and Boto3 operations for X-Ray.

💻 AWS CLI Commands
📋 Complete CLI Reference
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AWS X-RAY CLI REFERENCE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TRACES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Get trace summaries (last hour)
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)

# Get trace summaries with filter expression
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --filter-expression 'annotation.user_id = "user-12345"'

# Filter by error
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --filter-expression 'error = true'

# Get full trace details
aws xray batch-get-traces \
    --trace-ids "1-abc123-def456" "1-xyz789-ghi012"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SERVICE GRAPH
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Get service graph
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)

# Get service graph for specific group
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time $(date -d '-1 hour' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --group-name "anihpj-production"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GROUPS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# List all groups
aws xray get-groups

# Create group
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-errors" \
    --filter-expression 'error = true OR fault = true' \
    --insights-configuration '{"InsightsEnabled": true}'

# Update group
aws xray update-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-errors" \
    --filter-expression 'fault = true'

# Delete group
aws xray delete-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-staging"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SAMPLING RULES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# List sampling rules
aws xray get-sampling-rules

# Create sampling rule
aws xray create-sampling-rule \
    --sampling-rule '{
        "RuleName": "anihpj-jobs",
        "Priority": 100,
        "FixedRate": 0.10,
        "ReservoirSize": 5,
        "ServiceName": "anihpj-*",
        "ServiceType": "*",
        "Host": "*",
        "HTTPMethod": "POST",
        "URLPath": "/api/jobs/*",
        "Version": 1
    }'

# Update sampling rule
aws xray update-sampling-rule \
    --sampling-rule-update '{
        "RuleName": "anihpj-jobs",
        "FixedRate": 0.05
    }'

# Delete sampling rule
aws xray delete-sampling-rule \
    --rule-name "anihpj-jobs"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# INSIGHTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# List insights
aws xray get-insight-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '-24 hours' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)

# Get insight details
aws xray get-insight \
    --insight-id "insight-abc123"

# Get insight events
aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id "insight-abc123"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ENCRYPTION
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Get encryption config
aws xray get-encryption-config

# Set encryption config (use KMS key)
aws xray put-encryption-config \
    --type KMS \
    --key-id "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123"

# Set encryption config (default AWS managed)
aws xray put-encryption-config \
    --type NONE
🐍 Boto3 Integration
📋 Boto3 X-Ray Client Reference
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AWS X-RAY BOTO3 REFERENCE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

xray_client = boto3.client('xray')

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GET TRACE SUMMARIES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_recent_traces(hours=1, filter_expression=None):
    """Get trace summaries from the last N hours."""
    end_time = datetime.utcnow()
    start_time = end_time - timedelta(hours=hours)
    
    params = {
        'StartTime': start_time,
        'EndTime': end_time,
    }
    
    if filter_expression:
        params['FilterExpression'] = filter_expression
    
    response = xray_client.get_trace_summaries(**params)
    return response['TraceSummaries']

# Example: Get traces for specific user
user_traces = get_recent_traces(
    hours=24,
    filter_expression='annotation.user_id = "user-12345"'
)

# Example: Get error traces
error_traces = get_recent_traces(
    hours=1,
    filter_expression='error = true OR fault = true'
)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GET FULL TRACE DETAILS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_trace_details(trace_ids):
    """Get full details for specific traces."""
    response = xray_client.batch_get_traces(TraceIds=trace_ids)
    return response['Traces']

# Example
traces = get_trace_details(['1-abc123-def456', '1-xyz789-ghi012'])
for trace in traces:
    print(f"Trace ID: {trace['Id']}")
    print(f"Duration: {trace['Duration']}s")
    for segment in trace['Segments']:
        print(f"  Segment: {segment['Document']}")


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MANAGE SAMPLING RULES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def create_sampling_rule(name, service_name, rate, reservoir=1, priority=1000):
    """Create a new sampling rule."""
    response = xray_client.create_sampling_rule(
        SamplingRule={
            'RuleName': name,
            'Priority': priority,
            'FixedRate': rate,
            'ReservoirSize': reservoir,
            'ServiceName': service_name,
            'ServiceType': '*',
            'Host': '*',
            'HTTPMethod': '*',
            'URLPath': '*',
            'Version': 1,
        }
    )
    return response['SamplingRuleRecord']

# Example: Create rule for anihpj production
rule = create_sampling_rule(
    name='anihpj-production',
    service_name='anihpj-*',
    rate=0.05,  # 5%
    reservoir=5,
    priority=100
)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MANAGE GROUPS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def create_xray_group(name, filter_expression, enable_insights=False):
    """Create an X-Ray group."""
    response = xray_client.create_group(
        GroupName=name,
        FilterExpression=filter_expression,
        InsightsConfiguration={
            'InsightsEnabled': enable_insights,
            'NotificationsEnabled': enable_insights
        }
    )
    return response['Group']

# Example: Create production error group
group = create_xray_group(
    name='anihpj-prod-errors',
    filter_expression='annotation.environment = "production" AND fault = true',
    enable_insights=True
)


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GET SERVICE GRAPH
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_service_graph(hours=1, group_name=None):
    """Get the service graph."""
    end_time = datetime.utcnow()
    start_time = end_time - timedelta(hours=hours)
    
    params = {
        'StartTime': start_time,
        'EndTime': end_time,
    }
    
    if group_name:
        params['GroupName'] = group_name
    
    response = xray_client.get_service_graph(**params)
    return response['Services']

# Example: Get service graph for production
services = get_service_graph(hours=24, group_name='anihpj-production')
for service in services:
    print(f"Service: {service['Name']}")
    print(f"  Type: {service['Type']}")
    print(f"  Avg Latency: {service.get('ResponseTimeHistogram', [{}])[0].get('Average', 'N/A')}s")


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GET INSIGHTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_active_insights(hours=24):
    """Get active X-Ray insights."""
    end_time = datetime.utcnow()
    start_time = end_time - timedelta(hours=hours)
    
    response = xray_client.get_insight_summaries(
        StartTime=start_time,
        EndTime=end_time,
        States=['ACTIVE']
    )
    return response['InsightSummaries']

# Example: Check for active anomalies
insights = get_active_insights()
for insight in insights:
    print(f"Insight: {insight['Summary']}")
    print(f"  Root Cause: {insight.get('RootCauseServiceId', {}).get('Name', 'Unknown')}")
🌐 REST API Reference

Direct REST API endpoints for advanced X-Ray integration scenarios.

Operation HTTP Method Endpoint Description
PutTraceSegments POST /TraceSegments Upload trace segments (SDK handles this)
GetTraceSummaries POST /TraceSummaries Search traces with filter expression
BatchGetTraces POST /Traces Get full trace details by IDs
GetServiceGraph POST /ServiceGraph Get service map data
CreateSamplingRule POST /CreateSamplingRule Create a new sampling rule
CreateGroup POST /CreateGroup Create a new trace group
GetInsightSummaries POST /InsightSummaries Get X-Ray Insights
💡 API Tip: All X-Ray APIs require SigV4 authentication. Use the AWS SDKs (boto3, aws-sdk) which handle signing automatically rather than making raw REST calls.

📋 AWS X-Ray Cheat Sheet - Quick Reference

🐍 Python SDK Quick Reference
# Install
pip install aws-xray-sdk

# Django Setup (settings.py)
INSTALLED_APPS += ['aws_xray_sdk.ext.django']
MIDDLEWARE = ['aws_xray_sdk.ext.django.middleware.XRayMiddleware', ...]

# Auto-patch all supported libraries
from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch_all
patch_all()

# Manual segment creation
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder

@xray_recorder.capture('custom_operation')
def my_function():
    # Add annotation (indexed, searchable)
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('user_id', user.id)
    # Add metadata (not indexed)
    xray_recorder.put_metadata('request', request_data)
    return result

# Create subsegment
with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('db_query') as subseg:
    result = execute_query()
    subseg.put_annotation('rows', len(result))
⌨️ AWS CLI Commands
# Get trace summaries (last hour)
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)

# Get traces with filter
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --filter-expression 'annotation.user_id = "12345"'

# Get full trace details
aws xray batch-get-traces --trace-ids "1-abc123-def456"

# Create sampling rule
aws xray create-sampling-rule --cli-input-json file://rule.json

# Create group
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-errors" \
    --filter-expression "error = true"

# Get service graph
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)
🔍 Filter Expressions
# By annotation (custom values you add)
annotation.user_id = "12345"
annotation.order_total > 100
annotation.environment = "production"

# By response status
http.status = 500
http.status >= 400 AND http.status < 500

# By latency
responsetime > 5              # > 5 seconds
responsetime >= 2 AND responsetime <= 5

# By error/fault status
error = true                  # Client errors (4xx)
fault = true                  # Server errors (5xx)
throttle = true               # Throttling

# By service
service("anihpj-api")
service(id(name: "Lambda", type: "AWS::Lambda"))

# By URL
http.url CONTAINS "/api/jobs"
http.method = "POST"

# Combined expressions
service("anihpj-api") AND fault = true AND responsetime > 1
annotation.user_type = "premium" OR annotation.priority = "high"
🎯 Sampling Rule JSON Template
{
  "SamplingRule": {
    "RuleName": "anihpj-api-sampling",
    "Priority": 100,
    "FixedRate": 0.05,
    "ReservoirSize": 5,
    "ServiceName": "anihpj-api",
    "ServiceType": "*",
    "Host": "*",
    "HTTPMethod": "*",
    "URLPath": "/api/*",
    "Version": 1,
    "Attributes": {}
  }
}

# Sampling logic:
# 1. First 5 requests/sec guaranteed (ReservoirSize)
# 2. After that, 5% of requests (FixedRate: 0.05)
# 3. Lower priority number = higher precedence
🔐 IAM Policy (Minimum Required)
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "XRayWriteAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "xray:PutTraceSegments",
        "xray:PutTelemetryRecords",
        "xray:GetSamplingRules",
        "xray:GetSamplingTargets",
        "xray:GetSamplingStatisticSummaries"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}

# For read access (console, API queries), add:
"xray:GetTraceSummaries",
"xray:BatchGetTraces",
"xray:GetServiceGraph",
"xray:GetGroups",
"xray:GetInsightSummaries"
🔧 X-Ray Daemon Configuration
# /etc/amazon/xray/cfg.yaml
TotalBufferSizeMB: 24
Socket:
  UDPAddress: "127.0.0.1:2000"
  TCPAddress: "127.0.0.1:2000"
Logging:
  LogLevel: "warn"
  LogPath: "/var/log/xray-daemon.log"
LocalMode: false
ResourceARN: ""
RoleARN: ""
Region: "us-east-1"

# Run daemon
./xray -c /etc/amazon/xray/cfg.yaml

# Docker compose sidecar
services:
  xray-daemon:
    image: amazon/aws-xray-daemon:latest
    ports:
      - "2000:2000/udp"
    environment:
      - AWS_REGION=us-east-1
💰
$5/million
Traces recorded
🆓
100K/month
Free tier traces
📊
30 days
Trace retention
🔌
UDP 2000
Daemon port
🏷️
50 max
Annotations/segment
