🎯 Quick Start Checklist for anihpj:
  1. Install SDK: pip install aws-xray-sdk
  2. Add X-Ray middleware to Django settings (FIRST position)
  3. Call patch_all() before importing boto3
  4. Enable X-Ray on Lambda functions and API Gateway
  5. Add annotations for user_id, job_id, environment
  6. Create sampling rules to control costs
  7. Create groups for production/staging separation
  8. Enable Insights for production only

📈 Scalability & High Availability

X-Ray is designed to automatically scale with your application workload. Understanding scalability patterns helps you build resilient distributed tracing architectures.

🔄 Auto-Scaling

X-Ray automatically handles trace data volume without manual intervention.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    X-RAY AUTO-SCALING ARCHITECTURE                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│   Application Layer                    X-Ray Collection Layer               │
│   ┌─────────────┐                     ┌────────────────────────┐           │
│   │ EC2 (10x)   │──┐                  │                        │           │
│   └─────────────┘  │                  │   ┌────────────────┐   │           │
│                    │   Traces         │   │  X-Ray Daemon  │   │           │
│   ┌─────────────┐  ├─────────────────►│   │  (Auto-scales) │   │           │
│   │ Lambda (50x)│──┤   UDP 2000       │   └───────┬────────┘   │           │
│   └─────────────┘  │                  │           │            │           │
│                    │                  │           ▼            │           │
│   ┌─────────────┐  │                  │   ┌────────────────┐   │           │
│   │ ECS (25x)   │──┘                  │   │  X-Ray Service │   │           │
│   └─────────────┘                     │   │  (Managed)     │   │           │
│                                       │   └───────┬────────┘   │           │
│   Load Increases 3x                   │           │            │           │
│           ↓                           │           ▼            │           │
│   ┌─────────────┐                     │   ┌────────────────┐   │           │
│   │ EC2 (30x)   │──┐                  │   │  Auto-scaled   │   │           │
│   └─────────────┘  │                  │   │  Processing    │   │           │
│                    │   More Traces    │   └────────────────┘   │           │
│   ┌─────────────┐  ├─────────────────►│                        │           │
│   │ Lambda(150x)│──┤   (3x volume)    └────────────────────────┘           │
│   └─────────────┘  │                                                        │
│                    │                  Storage Layer                         │
│   ┌─────────────┐  │                  ┌────────────────────────┐           │
│   │ ECS (75x)   │──┘                  │   ┌────────────────┐   │           │
│   └─────────────┘                     │   │  S3 (Traces)   │   │           │
│                                       │   │  Auto-expands  │   │           │
│                                       │   └────────────────┘   │           │
│                                       └────────────────────────┘           │
│                                                                             │
│   💡 X-Ray automatically scales collection infrastructure based on demand   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📊 Scaling Limits
MetricLimitAdjustable
Trace segments/second50,000Yes
Max segment size64 KBNo
Annotations per segment50No
Metadata per segmentNo limit*N/A
Trace retention30 daysNo
Groups per account25Yes
Sampling rules per account25Yes

*Within 64KB segment size limit

⚡ Daemon Scaling
  • EC2: Run one daemon per instance
  • ECS/Fargate: Sidecar container pattern
  • Lambda: No daemon needed (built-in)
  • K8s: DaemonSet deployment
# Kubernetes DaemonSet for X-Ray
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: xray-daemon
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: xray-daemon
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: xray-daemon
        image: amazon/aws-xray-daemon
        ports:
        - containerPort: 2000
          hostPort: 2000
          protocol: UDP
🌐 Multi-Region Tracing

X-Ray supports tracing requests across multiple AWS regions for globally distributed applications.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     MULTI-REGION X-RAY ARCHITECTURE                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│        us-east-1                           eu-west-1                        │
│   ┌────────────────┐                  ┌────────────────┐                   │
│   │   API Gateway  │◄─── Internet ───►│   API Gateway  │                   │
│   └───────┬────────┘                  └───────┬────────┘                   │
│           │                                   │                             │
│           ▼                                   ▼                             │
│   ┌────────────────┐                  ┌────────────────┐                   │
│   │     Lambda     │                  │     Lambda     │                   │
│   │ (X-Ray Enabled)│                  │ (X-Ray Enabled)│                   │
│   └───────┬────────┘                  └───────┬────────┘                   │
│           │                                   │                             │
│           ▼                                   ▼                             │
│   ┌────────────────┐    Cross-Region  ┌────────────────┐                   │
│   │   DynamoDB     │◄────────────────►│   DynamoDB     │                   │
│   │ Global Table   │    Replication   │ Global Table   │                   │
│   └───────┬────────┘                  └───────┬────────┘                   │
│           │                                   │                             │
│           ▼                                   ▼                             │
│   ┌────────────────┐                  ┌────────────────┐                   │
│   │  X-Ray Region  │                  │  X-Ray Region  │                   │
│   │   us-east-1    │                  │    eu-west-1   │                   │
│   └───────┬────────┘                  └───────┬────────┘                   │
│           │                                   │                             │
│           └───────────────┬───────────────────┘                             │
│                           │                                                 │
│                           ▼                                                 │
│           ┌────────────────────────────────┐                               │
│           │     CloudWatch Cross-Account   │                               │
│           │     Observability Dashboard    │                               │
│           └────────────────────────────────┘                               │
│                                                                             │
│   💡 Trace ID header (X-Amzn-Trace-Id) propagates across regions           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 Cross-Region Trace Propagation
# anihpj - Cross-region Lambda calling pattern
import boto3
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all

patch_all()

# Create client for different region
eu_lambda = boto3.client('lambda', region_name='eu-west-1')

@xray_recorder.capture('cross_region_call')
def call_eu_service(payload):
    """Call Lambda in EU region - trace automatically propagates."""
    
    # Get current trace header
    header = xray_recorder.current_subsegment().get_origin_trace_header()
    
    response = eu_lambda.invoke(
        FunctionName='anihpj-eu-processor',
        InvocationType='RequestResponse',
        Payload=json.dumps(payload)
    )
    
    # Trace ID automatically included in invocation
    return json.loads(response['Payload'].read())


# Query traces from multiple regions
def get_cross_region_traces(trace_id):
    """Query same trace from multiple regions."""
    regions = ['us-east-1', 'eu-west-1', 'ap-southeast-1']
    
    all_segments = []
    for region in regions:
        xray = boto3.client('xray', region_name=region)
        response = xray.batch_get_traces(TraceIds=[trace_id])
        all_segments.extend(response.get('Traces', []))
    
    return all_segments
⚡ Performance Optimization

Optimize X-Ray performance to minimize overhead while maximizing visibility.

🎯 SDK Optimization
  • Async emission: Use async mode for high-throughput
  • Buffer sizing: Tune UDP buffer for volume
  • Selective patching: Only patch needed libraries
  • Annotation limits: Stay under 50 per segment
# Optimized X-Ray configuration
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder
from aws_xray_sdk.core import patch

# Selective patching (faster than patch_all)
patch(['boto3', 'requests'])  # Only what you need

# Configure for high throughput
xray_recorder.configure(
    daemon_address='127.0.0.1:2000',
    context_missing='LOG_ERROR',  # Don't throw exceptions
    streaming_threshold=10,  # Stream after 10 subsegments
)
📊 Performance Impact
OperationOverheadNotes
Segment creation<1msMinimal impact
Annotation add<0.1msVery fast
Metadata add<0.1msWatch size
Segment close<2msUDP send
Auto-patched call+2-5msPer call
💡 Tip: For latency-sensitive paths, use manual instrumentation with subsegments rather than auto-patching to control overhead precisely.

🎓 Advanced Topics

Deep dive into advanced X-Ray capabilities for sophisticated distributed tracing scenarios.

🔧 Custom Segments

Create custom segments and subsegments for precise control over trace visualization.

📝 Manual Segment Management
# anihpj - Advanced custom segmentation
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder
from aws_xray_sdk.core.models.segment import Segment
from aws_xray_sdk.core.models.subsegment import Subsegment

# Create a manual segment (useful for background workers)
segment = Segment('anihpj-batch-processor')
xray_recorder.set_trace_entity(segment)

try:
    # Add business context
    segment.put_annotation('batch_id', 'batch-12345')
    segment.put_annotation('job_count', 150)
    
    # Process jobs with subsegments
    for job in jobs:
        subsegment = segment.add_subsegment('process_job')
        subsegment.put_annotation('job_id', job.id)
        
        try:
            # Nested subsegments for detailed breakdown
            with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('validate') as validate_sub:
                validate_job(job)
                validate_sub.put_metadata('validation_rules', job.rules)
            
            with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('execute') as exec_sub:
                result = execute_job(job)
                exec_sub.put_annotation('status', 'success')
                
        except Exception as e:
            subsegment.add_exception(e, stack=traceback.extract_stack())
            subsegment.put_annotation('status', 'failed')
        finally:
            subsegment.close()
            
finally:
    segment.close()
    xray_recorder.send_entity(segment)


# Custom subsegment for external non-HTTP calls
def call_legacy_system(data):
    """Trace calls to systems without auto-instrumentation."""
    with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('legacy_mainframe') as subsegment:
        subsegment.namespace = 'remote'  # Mark as external
        subsegment.put_http_meta('url', 'tcp://mainframe.internal:5000')
        
        start = time.time()
        try:
            result = legacy_client.send(data)
            subsegment.put_http_meta('status', 200)
            return result
        except Exception as e:
            subsegment.add_exception(e)
            subsegment.put_http_meta('status', 500)
            raise
        finally:
            subsegment.put_annotation('duration_ms', int((time.time() - start) * 1000))
🔌 X-Ray Plugins

Use plugins to automatically capture metadata from AWS compute environments.

📦 Available Plugins
PluginCapturesAuto-Enabled
EC2PluginInstance ID, AZ, AMIYes (on EC2)
ECSPluginContainer ID, Task ARNYes (in ECS)
BeanstalkPluginEnv name, versionYes (on EB)
ElasticBeanstalkPluginEnv ID, deploymentYes
⚙️ Plugin Configuration
# Manual plugin configuration
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder
from aws_xray_sdk.core.plugins.ec2_plugin import EC2Plugin
from aws_xray_sdk.core.plugins.ecs_plugin import ECSPlugin

# Configure with specific plugins
xray_recorder.configure(
    plugins=[EC2Plugin(), ECSPlugin()],
    service='anihpj-api'
)

# Check what plugin data was captured
segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
print(segment.origin)  # 'AWS::EC2::Instance' or 'AWS::ECS::Container'
print(segment.aws)     # Contains instance_id, availability_zone, etc.
🔍 Advanced Filtering

Master complex filter expressions for precise trace queries.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    X-RAY FILTER EXPRESSION SYNTAX                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│   BASIC OPERATORS                                                           │
│   ─────────────────                                                         │
│   =    Equal                    annotation.user_id = "user-123"             │
│   !=   Not equal                service(id(type: "client")) != "browser"    │
│   >    Greater than             responsetime > 5                            │
│   >=   Greater or equal         annotation.order_value >= 100               │
│   <    Less than                duration < 1                                │
│   <=   Less or equal            responsetime <= 2                           │
│                                                                             │
│   BOOLEAN OPERATORS                                                         │
│   ─────────────────                                                         │
│   AND  Both conditions          fault = true AND responsetime > 3           │
│   OR   Either condition         error = true OR fault = true                │
│   !    Negation                 !ok                                         │
│                                                                             │
│   KEYWORDS                                                                  │
│   ─────────────────                                                         │
│   ok           Request succeeded (2xx)                                      │
│   error        Request had error (4xx)                                      │
│   fault        Request had fault (5xx)                                      │
│   partial      Some segments missing                                        │
│   inferred     Some segments inferred                                       │
│   responsetime Total response time (seconds)                                │
│   duration     Segment duration (seconds)                                   │
│   rootcause    Root cause analysis                                          │
│                                                                             │
│   FUNCTIONS                                                                 │
│   ─────────────────                                                         │
│   service(name)          Filter by service name                             │
│   service(id(...))       Filter by service ID properties                    │
│   edge(source, dest)     Filter by service connection                       │
│   annotation.key         Filter by annotation value                         │
│   group.name             Filter by group membership                         │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📝 Complex Filter Examples
# anihpj - Advanced Filter Expressions

# Find slow job applications with errors
annotation.environment = "production" AND 
annotation.feature = "job_application" AND 
responsetime > 3 AND 
(error = true OR fault = true)

# Trace requests from specific employer
annotation.employer_id = "emp-12345" AND 
service(id(name: "anihpj-api"))

# High-value transactions with latency issues
annotation.job_type = "premium" AND 
annotation.application_value >= 50 AND 
responsetime > 2

# Database bottlenecks
edge(
  service(id(name: "anihpj-api")), 
  service(id(type: "AWS::RDS::DBInstance"))
) AND 
responsetime > 1

# Failed Lambda invocations
service(id(type: "AWS::Lambda::Function")) AND 
fault = true AND 
annotation.function_version != "$LATEST"

# Multi-service trace analysis
service(id(name: "anihpj-api")) AND 
edge(
  service(id(name: "anihpj-api")),
  service(id(name: "anihpj-notification"))
) AND 
annotation.notification_type = "email"

# Root cause in specific service
rootcause = true AND 
service(id(name: "anihpj-payment"))
🔗 Correlation IDs

Implement custom correlation ID strategies for end-to-end request tracking.

🔧 Correlation ID Implementation
# anihpj - Custom correlation ID strategy
import uuid
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder

class CorrelationMiddleware:
    """Django middleware for correlation ID handling."""
    
    HEADER_NAME = 'X-Correlation-Id'
    
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
    
    def __call__(self, request):
        # Get or generate correlation ID
        correlation_id = (
            request.META.get(f'HTTP_{self.HEADER_NAME.upper().replace("-", "_")}') or
            request.META.get('HTTP_X_AMZN_TRACE_ID', '').split('Root=')[-1].split(';')[0] or
            str(uuid.uuid4())
        )
        
        # Store in request
        request.correlation_id = correlation_id
        
        # Add to X-Ray segment
        segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
        if segment:
            segment.put_annotation('correlation_id', correlation_id)
            segment.put_metadata('correlation', {
                'id': correlation_id,
                'source': 'header' if self.HEADER_NAME in request.META else 'generated'
            })
        
        # Process request
        response = self.get_response(request)
        
        # Add to response headers
        response[self.HEADER_NAME] = correlation_id
        
        return response


# Use in async Celery tasks
from celery import shared_task

@shared_task(bind=True)
def process_job_application(self, application_id, correlation_id=None):
    """Celery task with correlation ID tracking."""
    
    # Create segment with correlation ID
    segment = xray_recorder.begin_segment(
        name='celery-process-application',
        traceid=correlation_id  # Continue existing trace
    )
    
    try:
        segment.put_annotation('correlation_id', correlation_id or self.request.id)
        segment.put_annotation('application_id', application_id)
        
        # Process application
        result = _process_application(application_id)
        
        return result
    finally:
        xray_recorder.end_segment()
🐳 ECS & Fargate Integration

Deploy X-Ray daemon as a sidecar container for ECS and Fargate workloads.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ECS/FARGATE X-RAY SIDECAR PATTERN                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│                           ECS Task / Fargate Task                           │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│   │                                                                     │  │
│   │  ┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────┐       │  │
│   │  │                         │     │                         │       │  │
│   │  │   Application Container │     │   X-Ray Daemon Sidecar  │       │  │
│   │  │   (anihpj-api)         │     │   (amazon/aws-xray-     │       │  │
│   │  │                         │     │    daemon)              │       │  │
│   │  │   Port: 8000           │     │                         │       │  │
│   │  │                         │UDP  │   Port: 2000 (UDP)      │       │  │
│   │  │   X-Ray SDK ───────────┼────►│                         │       │  │
│   │  │                         │     │   Batch sends traces    │       │  │
│   │  └─────────────────────────┘     └───────────┬─────────────┘       │  │
│   │                                              │                     │  │
│   │  Shared network namespace (localhost)        │                     │  │
│   │                                              │                     │  │
│   └──────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                  │                        │
│                                                  │ HTTPS                  │
│                                                  ▼                        │
│                                        ┌─────────────────┐                │
│                                        │   AWS X-Ray     │                │
│                                        │   Service API   │                │
│                                        └─────────────────┘                │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📝 ECS Task Definition with X-Ray Sidecar
{
  "family": "anihpj-api-task",
  "networkMode": "awsvpc",
  "executionRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/ecsTaskExecutionRole",
  "taskRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/anihpj-task-role",
  "requiresCompatibilities": ["FARGATE"],
  "cpu": "512",
  "memory": "1024",
  "containerDefinitions": [
    {
      "name": "anihpj-api",
      "image": "ACCOUNT.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-api:latest",
      "essential": true,
      "portMappings": [
        {
          "containerPort": 8000,
          "protocol": "tcp"
        }
      ],
      "environment": [
        {
          "name": "AWS_XRAY_DAEMON_ADDRESS",
          "value": "localhost:2000"
        },
        {
          "name": "AWS_XRAY_CONTEXT_MISSING",
          "value": "LOG_ERROR"
        }
      ],
      "logConfiguration": {
        "logDriver": "awslogs",
        "options": {
          "awslogs-group": "/ecs/anihpj-api",
          "awslogs-region": "us-east-1",
          "awslogs-stream-prefix": "api"
        }
      },
      "dependsOn": [
        {
          "containerName": "xray-daemon",
          "condition": "START"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "xray-daemon",
      "image": "amazon/aws-xray-daemon:latest",
      "essential": true,
      "cpu": 32,
      "memoryReservation": 256,
      "portMappings": [
        {
          "containerPort": 2000,
          "protocol": "udp"
        }
      ],
      "logConfiguration": {
        "logDriver": "awslogs",
        "options": {
          "awslogs-group": "/ecs/anihpj-xray",
          "awslogs-region": "us-east-1",
          "awslogs-stream-prefix": "xray"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
💡 Fargate Tip: For Fargate tasks, ensure the task role has xray:PutTraceSegments and xray:PutTelemetryRecords permissions. The daemon will use the task role credentials automatically.
🔐 Security Best Practices

Implement secure X-Ray configurations to protect trace data and maintain compliance.

🛡️ Data Protection
  • Encryption at rest: X-Ray encrypts all trace data with AWS-managed keys
  • Encryption in transit: TLS for all API communications
  • CMK encryption: Use your own KMS keys for enhanced control
  • PII handling: Never put sensitive data in annotations
# Enable CMK encryption for X-Ray
aws xray put-encryption-config \
  --type KMS \
  --key-id arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:key/KEY-ID
👤 IAM Best Practices
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "XRayWriteOnly",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "xray:PutTraceSegments",
        "xray:PutTelemetryRecords"
      ],
      "Resource": "*",
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {
          "aws:RequestedRegion": "us-east-1"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "Sid": "XRayReadFiltered",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "xray:GetTraceSummaries",
        "xray:BatchGetTraces"
      ],
      "Resource": "*",
      "Condition": {
        "ForAnyValue:StringEquals": {
          "xray:GroupNames": [
            "anihpj-production",
            "anihpj-staging"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
⚠️ Security Warning: Never include these in X-Ray annotations or metadata:
  • Passwords, API keys, or secrets
  • Personal Identifiable Information (PII)
  • Credit card numbers or financial data
  • Health information (PHI)
Use hashed/anonymized identifiers instead.

💼 Common Use Cases

Practical X-Ray implementation patterns for common distributed system scenarios.

🔀 Microservices Tracing

Trace requests across multiple microservices to understand service dependencies and performance.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 ANIHPJ MICROSERVICES TRACE FLOW                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│   User Request                                                              │
│       │                                                                     │
│       ▼                                                                     │
│   ┌───────────────┐ Trace: Root-abc123                                     │
│   │ API Gateway   │ ══════════════════════════════════════                 │
│   │ 12ms          │                                                        │
│   └───────┬───────┘                                                        │
│           │                                                                 │
│           ▼                                                                 │
│   ┌───────────────┐ Segment: api-gateway-abc123                            │
│   │ Auth Service  │ ════════════════════════                               │
│   │ Lambda: 45ms  │ → Validates JWT token                                  │
│   └───────┬───────┘   → Adds user_id annotation                            │
│           │                                                                 │
│           ▼                                                                 │
│   ┌───────────────┐ Segment: anihpj-api-abc123                             │
│   │ Job Service   │ ════════════════════════════════════════════           │
│   │ ECS: 156ms    │ → POST /api/v1/jobs/apply                              │
│   │               │ → annotation.job_id = "job-789"                        │
│   └───────┬───────┘ → annotation.employer_id = "emp-456"                   │
│           │                                                                 │
│     ┌─────┴─────┐                                                          │
│     │           │                                                           │
│     ▼           ▼                                                           │
│ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│ │ DynamoDB│ │ S3 Upload   │ Subsegment: s3-upload-abc123                   │
│ │ 23ms    │ │ 89ms        │ ════════════════════════                       │
│ └─────────┘ └──────┬──────┘ → Resume PDF upload                            │
│                    │                                                        │
│                    ▼                                                        │
│            ┌───────────────┐ Segment: notification-abc123                  │
│            │ Notification  │ ════════════════════════════                  │
│            │ Lambda: 234ms │ → Sends confirmation email                    │
│            └───────┬───────┘ → annotation.notification_type = "email"      │
│                    │                                                        │
│              ┌─────┴─────┐                                                 │
│              │           │                                                  │
│              ▼           ▼                                                  │
│          ┌───────┐  ┌───────────┐                                          │
│          │  SES  │  │ SNS Push  │                                          │
│          │ 45ms  │  │ 12ms      │                                          │
│          └───────┘  └───────────┘                                          │
│                                                                             │
│   Total Trace Duration: 621ms                                              │
│   Services Involved: 5 | Subsegments: 8                                    │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 Microservices Tracing Setup
# anihpj - Service-to-service tracing
import requests
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all

patch_all()

class JobApplicationService:
    """Job application service with X-Ray tracing."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.notification_service_url = "https://notification.anihpj.internal"
        self.resume_service_url = "https://resume.anihpj.internal"
    
    @xray_recorder.capture('apply_for_job')
    def apply_for_job(self, user_id, job_id, resume_file):
        """Process job application with full trace context."""
        
        # Add business context
        xray_recorder.put_annotation('user_id', user_id)
        xray_recorder.put_annotation('job_id', job_id)
        xray_recorder.put_annotation('feature', 'job_application')
        
        # Upload resume (traced automatically via patched requests)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('upload_resume') as subseg:
            resume_url = self._upload_resume(user_id, resume_file)
            subseg.put_annotation('resume_size_kb', len(resume_file) // 1024)
        
        # Save application to DynamoDB (traced automatically)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('save_application') as subseg:
            application = self._save_application(user_id, job_id, resume_url)
            subseg.put_annotation('application_id', application['id'])
        
        # Send notification (propagates trace)
        with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('send_notification') as subseg:
            self._send_notification(user_id, job_id, application['id'])
            subseg.put_annotation('notification_sent', True)
        
        return application
    
    def _send_notification(self, user_id, job_id, application_id):
        """Call notification service - trace propagates via headers."""
        # X-Ray SDK automatically adds trace header to requests
        response = requests.post(
            f"{self.notification_service_url}/send",
            json={
                'user_id': user_id,
                'job_id': job_id,
                'application_id': application_id,
                'type': 'application_confirmation'
            }
        )
        return response.json()
🔍 Performance Debugging

Use X-Ray to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks in your application.

📊 Performance Analysis Workflow
  1. Identify slow traces: Filter by responsetime > 3
  2. Analyze service map: Find services with high latency
  3. Drill into traces: Examine segment timeline
  4. Find bottleneck: Longest subsegment = bottleneck
  5. Check metadata: Query size, payload info
  6. Correlate with logs: Use trace ID in CloudWatch
🔧 Performance Annotations
# Add performance context
@xray_recorder.capture('search_jobs')
def search_jobs(query, filters):
    """Search with performance annotations."""
    
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('query_length', len(query))
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('filter_count', len(filters))
    
    # Time individual operations
    with xray_recorder.in_subsegment('elasticsearch_query') as sub:
        start = time.time()
        results = es.search(query, filters)
        duration = time.time() - start
        
        sub.put_annotation('result_count', len(results))
        sub.put_annotation('query_time_ms', int(duration * 1000))
        
        # Flag slow queries for analysis
        if duration > 1.0:
            sub.put_annotation('slow_query', True)
            sub.put_metadata('query_details', {
                'query': query,
                'filters': filters
            })
    
    return results
⚠️ Error Tracking

Track and analyze errors across your distributed system.

📝 Comprehensive Error Tracking
# anihpj - Error tracking with X-Ray
import traceback
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder

class XRayErrorHandler:
    """Custom error handler with X-Ray integration."""
    
    @staticmethod
    def capture_error(error, context=None):
        """Capture error with full context in X-Ray."""
        
        segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
        if not segment:
            return
        
        # Add error to segment
        segment.add_exception(
            exception=error,
            stack=traceback.extract_stack(),
            remote=False  # Set True if error from downstream service
        )
        
        # Add error annotations for filtering
        segment.put_annotation('error', True)
        segment.put_annotation('error_type', type(error).__name__)
        
        # Add detailed metadata
        segment.put_metadata('error_details', {
            'message': str(error),
            'type': type(error).__name__,
            'traceback': traceback.format_exc(),
            'context': context or {}
        }, namespace='errors')
    
    @staticmethod
    def capture_http_error(status_code, error_body=None):
        """Capture HTTP error response."""
        
        segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
        if not segment:
            return
        
        if 400 <= status_code < 500:
            segment.put_annotation('http_error', True)
            segment.put_http_meta('status', status_code)
        elif status_code >= 500:
            segment.add_fault_flag()
            segment.put_annotation('http_fault', True)
            segment.put_http_meta('status', status_code)
        
        if error_body:
            segment.put_metadata('error_response', error_body)


# Usage in Django view
def apply_job(request, job_id):
    try:
        application = process_application(request.user, job_id)
        return JsonResponse({'application_id': application.id})
    
    except ValidationError as e:
        XRayErrorHandler.capture_error(e, {'job_id': job_id})
        XRayErrorHandler.capture_http_error(400, {'error': str(e)})
        return JsonResponse({'error': str(e)}, status=400)
    
    except Exception as e:
        XRayErrorHandler.capture_error(e, {'job_id': job_id, 'user': request.user.id})
        XRayErrorHandler.capture_http_error(500)
        return JsonResponse({'error': 'Internal server error'}, status=500)

│   │  ████████████████████████████████████████████████  p75: 234ms     │   │
│   │  ████████████████████████████████████████████████████  p90: 412ms │   │

│   │  SNS Notification   ██████████  27ms (19%)                         │   │

│   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                             │

│              Consider pre-signed URLs for direct upload optimization.       │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📊 CloudWatch Integration

│                                                                             │

🔧 Trace-Log Correlation
# anihpj - CloudWatch Logs with X-Ray trace correlation
import logging
import json
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder

class XRayLogFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    """Custom formatter that adds X-Ray trace ID to logs."""
    
    def format(self, record):
        # Get current trace ID
        trace_id = None
        try:
            segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
            if segment:
                trace_id = segment.trace_id
        except:
            pass
        
        # Add trace ID to log record
        log_dict = {
            'timestamp': self.formatTime(record),
            'level': record.levelname,
            'logger': record.name,
            'message': record.getMessage(),
            'trace_id': trace_id,
            'location': f"{record.filename}:{record.lineno}"
        }
        
        # Add exception info if present
        if record.exc_info:
            log_dict['exception'] = self.formatException(record.exc_info)
        
        return json.dumps(log_dict)


# Configure logging
logger = logging.getLogger('anihpj')
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
handler.setFormatter(XRayLogFormatter())
logger.addHandler(handler)

# Usage - logs automatically include trace ID
@xray_recorder.capture('process_payment')
def process_payment(order_id, amount):
    logger.info(f"Processing payment for order {order_id}")  
    # Log includes: {"trace_id": "1-abc123-def456", "message": "Processing payment..."}
    
    try:
        result = payment_gateway.charge(amount)
        logger.info(f"Payment successful: {result.transaction_id}")
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Payment failed: {e}")
        raise
📈 CloudWatch Metrics from X-Ray

X-Ray automatically publishes these metrics to CloudWatch:

  • TracesProcessedCount - Traces processed
  • TraceSegmentsCount - Segments received
  • ProvisionedWriteCapacityUnits - Capacity used
💡 Tip: Create CloudWatch alarms on X-Ray Insights anomalies for proactive alerting.
🔎 Logs Insights Query
-- Find all logs for a specific trace
fields @timestamp, @message
| filter trace_id = "1-abc123-def456789"
| sort @timestamp asc

-- Find errors with trace context
fields @timestamp, level, message, trace_id
| filter level = "ERROR"
| filter trace_id != ""
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

📨 SNS & SQS Tracing

Trace messages through SNS topics and SQS queues for async workflow visibility.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    SNS/SQS TRACE PROPAGATION                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│   Producer                    Message Queue                   Consumer      │
│   ┌─────────────┐            ┌─────────────┐            ┌─────────────┐    │
│   │   Lambda    │            │             │            │    ECS      │    │
│   │  Producer   │───────────►│    SQS      │───────────►│  Consumer   │    │
│   │             │   Publish  │             │   Receive  │             │    │
│   └─────────────┘            └─────────────┘            └─────────────┘    │
│         │                          │                          │            │
│         │ Trace Header             │ Preserved in             │ Extracted  │
│         │ Added                    │ Message Attr             │ & Continued│
│         ▼                          ▼                          ▼            │
│   ┌─────────────┐            ┌─────────────┐            ┌─────────────┐    │
│   │ Segment A   │────────────│ (Queue Wait)│────────────│ Segment B   │    │
│   │ trace-123   │            │             │            │ trace-123   │    │
│   └─────────────┘            └─────────────┘            └─────────────┘    │
│                                                                             │
│   💡 Same trace ID links producer and consumer for end-to-end visibility   │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔧 SQS Message Tracing
# anihpj - SQS producer with trace propagation
import boto3
import json
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder, patch_all

patch_all()

sqs = boto3.client('sqs')

@xray_recorder.capture('send_job_notification')
def send_job_notification(job_id, event_type):
    """Send job event to SQS with trace propagation."""
    
    # Get current trace header
    header = xray_recorder.current_subsegment().get_origin_trace_header()
    
    message = {
        'job_id': job_id,
        'event_type': event_type,
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
    }
    
    # Send with trace header in message attributes
    response = sqs.send_message(
        QueueUrl='https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ACCOUNT/anihpj-jobs',
        MessageBody=json.dumps(message),
        MessageAttributes={
            'AWSTraceHeader': {
                'DataType': 'String',
                'StringValue': header.to_header_str() if header else ''
            }
        }
    )
    
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('sqs_message_id', response['MessageId'])
    return response


# SQS Consumer with trace continuation
@xray_recorder.capture('process_job_event')
def process_job_event(message):
    """Process SQS message, continuing the trace."""
    
    # Extract trace header from message
    trace_header = message.get('MessageAttributes', {}).get(
        'AWSTraceHeader', {}
    ).get('StringValue')
    
    if trace_header:
        # Continue existing trace
        xray_recorder.current_segment().trace_id = parse_trace_id(trace_header)
    
    body = json.loads(message['Body'])
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('job_id', body['job_id'])
    xray_recorder.put_annotation('event_type', body['event_type'])
    
    # Process the event
    process_event(body)
🔀 Step Functions Integration

Enable X-Ray tracing for Step Functions state machines.

📝 Step Functions with X-Ray
{
    "Comment": "Job Application Workflow with X-Ray",
    "StartAt": "ValidateApplication",
    "States": {
        "ValidateApplication": {
            "Type": "Task",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:function:anihpj-validate",
            "Next": "CheckResumeFormat",
            "ResultPath": "$.validationResult"
        },
        "CheckResumeFormat": {
            "Type": "Choice",
            "Choices": [
                {
                    "Variable": "$.validationResult.resumeValid",
                    "BooleanEquals": true,
                    "Next": "ProcessResume"
                }
            ],
            "Default": "NotifyInvalidResume"
        },
        "ProcessResume": {
            "Type": "Task",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:function:anihpj-process-resume",
            "Next": "NotifyEmployer"
        },
        "NotifyEmployer": {
            "Type": "Task",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::sns:publish",
            "Parameters": {
                "TopicArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:anihpj-employer-notifications",
                "Message.$": "$.application"
            },
            "End": true
        },
        "NotifyInvalidResume": {
            "Type": "Task",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:function:anihpj-notify-user",
            "End": true
        }
    }
}
# Enable X-Ray for Step Functions
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
  --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:ACCOUNT:stateMachine:anihpj-application-workflow \
  --tracing-configuration enabled=true

🚀 Deployment & CI/CD

Integrate X-Ray into your deployment pipelines for observability from day one.

📦 Deployment Methods
🔧 CloudFormation
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: X-Ray Configuration for anihpj

Resources:
  # Sampling Rule
  ProductionSamplingRule:
    Type: AWS::XRay::SamplingRule
    Properties:
      SamplingRule:
        RuleName: anihpj-production
        Priority: 1000
        FixedRate: 0.05
        ReservoirSize: 5
        ServiceName: anihpj-api
        ServiceType: '*'
        Host: '*'
        HTTPMethod: '*'
        URLPath: '*'
        Version: 1

  # X-Ray Group
  ProductionGroup:
    Type: AWS::XRay::Group
    Properties:
      GroupName: anihpj-production
      FilterExpression: 'annotation.environment = "production"'
      InsightsConfiguration:
        InsightsEnabled: true
        NotificationsEnabled: true

  # Lambda with X-Ray enabled
  ApiFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: anihpj-api-handler
      Runtime: python3.11
      TracingConfig:
        Mode: Active
      Environment:
        Variables:
          AWS_XRAY_CONTEXT_MISSING: LOG_ERROR
📜 AWS SAM
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31

Globals:
  Function:
    Tracing: Active  # Enable X-Ray for all functions
    Environment:
      Variables:
        AWS_XRAY_CONTEXT_MISSING: LOG_ERROR

Resources:
  AnihpjApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: anihpj-api
      CodeUri: ./src
      Handler: app.handler
      Runtime: python3.11
      Events:
        ApiEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /{proxy+}
            Method: ANY
            RestApiId: !Ref ApiGateway

  ApiGateway:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      TracingEnabled: true  # Enable X-Ray for API Gateway
🔄 CI/CD Integration
📝 GitHub Actions with X-Ray Validation
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy with X-Ray Validation

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: us-east-1
      
      - name: Deploy Application
        run: sam deploy --stack-name anihpj-prod
      
      - name: Wait for Deployment
        run: sleep 60
      
      - name: Validate X-Ray Traces
        run: |
          # Check that traces are being received
          TRACE_COUNT=$(aws xray get-trace-summaries \
            --start-time $(date -d '5 minutes ago' -u +%s) \
            --end-time $(date -u +%s) \
            --filter-expression 'annotation.environment = "production"' \
            --query 'length(TraceSummaries)')
          
          if [ "$TRACE_COUNT" -lt 1 ]; then
            echo "Warning: No traces received after deployment"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          echo "✅ X-Ray traces confirmed: $TRACE_COUNT traces"
      
      - name: Check for Errors
        run: |
          # Check for faults in recent traces
          FAULT_COUNT=$(aws xray get-trace-summaries \
            --start-time $(date -d '5 minutes ago' -u +%s) \
            --end-time $(date -u +%s) \
            --filter-expression 'fault = true' \
            --query 'length(TraceSummaries)')
          
          if [ "$FAULT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "⚠️ Warning: $FAULT_COUNT traces with faults after deployment"
          fi
🏗️ Terraform Configuration
📝 Terraform X-Ray Resources
# terraform/xray.tf

# X-Ray Sampling Rule
resource "aws_xray_sampling_rule" "production" {
  rule_name      = "anihpj-production"
  priority       = 1000
  version        = 1
  reservoir_size = 5
  fixed_rate     = 0.05
  url_path       = "*"
  host           = "*"
  http_method    = "*"
  service_type   = "*"
  service_name   = "anihpj-*"
  resource_arn   = "*"

  attributes = {
    environment = "production"
  }
}

# X-Ray Group
resource "aws_xray_group" "production" {
  group_name        = "anihpj-production"
  filter_expression = "annotation.environment = \"production\""

  insights_configuration {
    insights_enabled      = true
    notifications_enabled = true
  }
}

# X-Ray Encryption (CMK)
resource "aws_xray_encryption_config" "main" {
  type   = "KMS"
  key_id = aws_kms_key.xray.arn
}

resource "aws_kms_key" "xray" {
  description             = "KMS key for X-Ray encryption"
  deletion_window_in_days = 7
  enable_key_rotation     = true
}

# Lambda with X-Ray
resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" {
  function_name = "anihpj-api"
  role          = aws_iam_role.lambda.arn
  handler       = "app.handler"
  runtime       = "python3.11"

  tracing_config {
    mode = "Active"
  }

  environment {
    variables = {
      AWS_XRAY_CONTEXT_MISSING = "LOG_ERROR"
    }
  }
}

# IAM Policy for X-Ray
resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "xray" {
  name = "xray-write"
  role = aws_iam_role.lambda.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = [
          "xray:PutTraceSegments",
          "xray:PutTelemetryRecords",
          "xray:GetSamplingRules",
          "xray:GetSamplingTargets"
        ]
        Resource = "*"
      }
    ]
  })
}

🛡️ Compliance & Governance

X-Ray supports various compliance requirements for enterprise deployments.

🏆 Security Certifications
✅ Compliance Programs
CertificationStatusNotes
SOC 1/2/3✅ IncludedAnnual audit
ISO 27001✅ IncludedSecurity management
PCI DSS✅ IncludedPayment data
HIPAA✅ EligibleBAA required
FedRAMP✅ AuthorizedGov regions
GDPR✅ CompliantEU data protection
🔐 Data Handling
  • Encryption at Rest: AES-256 with AWS-managed or CMK
  • Encryption in Transit: TLS 1.2+
  • Data Retention: 30 days (fixed)
  • Data Location: Regional, in selected region
  • Access Control: IAM policies
  • Audit Logging: CloudTrail integration
🔒 Data Privacy
⚠️ GDPR/CCPA Considerations:
  • Avoid storing PII in annotations (names, emails, etc.)
  • Use pseudonymized identifiers (hashed user IDs)
  • Document what data X-Ray collects in privacy policy
  • Trace data auto-deletes after 30 days
  • Use IAM to restrict trace data access
📝 GDPR-Compliant Annotation Strategy
# anihpj - GDPR-compliant annotations
import hashlib
from aws_xray_sdk.core import xray_recorder

def anonymize_user_id(user_id):
    """Create anonymized user identifier."""
    return hashlib.sha256(f"{user_id}:salt".encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

def add_compliant_annotations(user, job):
    """Add GDPR-compliant annotations."""
    segment = xray_recorder.current_segment()
    
    # ✅ DO: Use anonymized identifiers
    segment.put_annotation('user_hash', anonymize_user_id(user.id))
    segment.put_annotation('job_id', job.id)
    segment.put_annotation('job_category', job.category)
    
    # ❌ DON'T: Never include PII
    # segment.put_annotation('user_email', user.email)  # WRONG
    # segment.put_annotation('user_name', user.name)    # WRONG
    # segment.put_metadata('user', user.__dict__)       # WRONG - may contain PII
✅ Production Checklist
🚀 Pre-Production
  • ☐ Enable X-Ray on all Lambda functions
  • ☐ Enable X-Ray on API Gateway stages
  • ☐ Deploy X-Ray daemon sidecar for ECS/EKS
  • ☐ Configure SDK with patch_all()
  • ☐ Add environment annotation to all services
  • ☐ Create sampling rules for cost control
  • ☐ Verify IAM permissions for X-Ray
📊 Production Setup
  • ☐ Create production X-Ray group
  • ☐ Enable X-Ray Insights
  • ☐ Set up SNS notifications for anomalies
  • ☐ Configure CMK encryption (if required)
  • ☐ Create CloudWatch dashboard with X-Ray widgets
  • ☐ Document annotation standards
  • ☐ Train team on X-Ray console usage
🚀 X-Ray Implementation Guide for anihpj
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    X-RAY IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP FOR ANIHPJ                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  Phase 1: Foundation Setup (Week 1)                                                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Install aws-xray-sdk in Django project                                       │ │
│  │     pip install aws-xray-sdk boto3                                              │ │
│  │  ☐ Add XRayMiddleware to Django settings.py                                     │ │
│  │  ☐ Configure AWS credentials (IAM role or environment variables)               │ │
│  │  ☐ Enable patch_all() in wsgi.py/asgi.py for auto-instrumentation             │ │
│  │  ☐ Test locally with X-Ray daemon running                                       │ │
│  │  ☐ Verify traces appear in X-Ray console                                        │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                       │
│  Phase 2: Service Integration (Week 2)                                               │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Enable X-Ray tracing on API Gateway                                          │ │
│  │     AWS Console → API Gateway → Stage → Enable X-Ray Tracing                    │ │
│  │  ☐ Enable Active Tracing on all Lambda functions                                │ │
│  │     AWS Console → Lambda → Configuration → Monitoring → Active tracing          │ │
│  │  ☐ Deploy X-Ray daemon sidecar in ECS task definitions                          │ │
│  │  ☐ Configure trace header propagation between services                          │ │
│  │  ☐ Add custom annotations for business context:                                 │ │
│  │     - user_id, job_id, employer_id                                              │ │
│  │     - request_type, environment                                                 │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                       │
│  Phase 3: Sampling & Groups (Week 3)                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Create sampling rules for cost optimization:                                 │ │
│  │     • Health check endpoints: 1% sampling                                       │ │
│  │     • API endpoints: 5% sampling (production)                                   │ │
│  │     • Error traces: 100% sampling (always capture errors)                       │ │
│  │  ☐ Create X-Ray groups:                                                         │ │
│  │     • anihpj-production: service("anihpj-*")                                    │ │
│  │     • anihpj-errors: fault = true OR error = true                               │ │
│  │     • anihpj-slow: responsetime > 2                                             │ │
│  │     • anihpj-job-apply: http.url CONTAINS "/api/jobs/apply"                     │ │
│  │  ☐ Enable X-Ray Insights for anomaly detection                                  │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                       │
│  Phase 4: Monitoring & Alerting (Week 4)                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Create CloudWatch dashboard with X-Ray widgets                               │ │
│  │     • Service map widget                                                        │ │
│  │     • Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p99)                                       │ │
│  │     • Error rate trends                                                         │ │
│  │  ☐ Set up EventBridge rule for X-Ray Insights notifications                     │ │
│  │  ☐ Configure SNS topic for alerting (Slack, PagerDuty, email)                   │ │
│  │  ☐ Create runbook for common X-Ray troubleshooting scenarios                    │ │
│  │  ☐ Document annotation standards for team                                       │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                       │
│  Phase 5: Advanced Implementation (Week 5+)                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Implement custom subsegments for critical business logic                     │ │
│  │     • Job matching algorithm                                                    │ │
│  │     • Resume parsing                                                            │ │
│  │     • Email template rendering                                                  │ │
│  │  ☐ Add correlation IDs for cross-service debugging                              │ │
│  │  ☐ Implement Celery task tracing with custom segments                           │ │
│  │  ☐ Export traces to third-party tools (if needed)                               │ │
│  │  ☐ Performance baseline establishment and SLO definition                        │ │
│  │  ☐ Regular trace review sessions with development team                          │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                       │
│  Estimated Timeline: 4-5 weeks for full implementation                               │
│  Estimated Monthly Cost: $1-5/month (with proper sampling)                           │
│                                                                                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🔍 X-Ray Debugging Decision Tree
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        X-RAY TROUBLESHOOTING DECISION TREE                            │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  No traces appearing in console?                                                      │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 1: Is X-Ray daemon running?                                               │
│  │   └─ curl http://localhost:2000 → Should not refuse connection                    │
│  │      └─→ NO: Start daemon or deploy sidecar container                             │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 2: IAM permissions correct?                                               │
│  │   └─ Must have xray:PutTraceSegments, xray:PutTelemetryRecords                   │
│  │      └─→ NO: Attach AWSXRayDaemonWriteAccess managed policy                       │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 3: SDK configured correctly?                                              │
│  │   └─ XRayMiddleware in MIDDLEWARE list (Django)                                   │
│  │   └─ patch_all() called at startup                                                │
│  │      └─→ NO: Add middleware and patch_all() to settings.py/wsgi.py                │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 4: Environment variables set?                                             │
│  │   └─ AWS_XRAY_DAEMON_ADDRESS=127.0.0.1:2000                                       │
│  │   └─ AWS_XRAY_SDK_ENABLED=true                                                    │
│  │      └─→ NO: Set environment variables in your deployment                         │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  └─→ Check 5: Sampling rule not blocking?                                            │
│      └─ Default rule might have 0% fixed rate                                        │
│         └─→ YES: Create sampling rule with higher rate for testing                   │
│                                                                                       │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  Traces incomplete or missing downstream calls?                                       │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 1: Is library patched?                                                    │
│  │   └─ patch_all() only patches supported libraries                                 │
│  │      └─→ NO: Manually create subsegments for unpatched calls                      │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 2: Trace header propagated?                                               │
│  │   └─ X-Amzn-Trace-Id header must be passed to downstream services                 │
│  │      └─→ NO: Ensure header forwarding in HTTP client calls                        │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  └─→ Check 3: Downstream service instrumented?                                       │
│      └─ All services need X-Ray SDK or active tracing enabled                        │
│         └─→ NO: Instrument downstream service or enable active tracing               │
│                                                                                       │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  High latency but traces look normal?                                                 │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check for gaps between segments (indicates uninstrumented code)                 │
│  ├─→ Look at subsegment breakdown for database queries                               │
│  ├─→ Check for cold start patterns in Lambda traces                                  │
│  └─→ Verify network latency vs application processing time                           │
│                                                                                       │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📊 X-Ray vs Alternatives Comparison

Compare AWS X-Ray with other distributed tracing solutions.

Feature AWS X-Ray Jaeger Zipkin Datadog APM
AWS Integration ✅ Native (Lambda, API GW, ECS) ⚠️ Manual ⚠️ Manual ⚠️ Via Agent
Pricing Model Pay-per-trace ($5/M) Self-hosted (infra costs) Self-hosted (infra costs) $$/host/month
Managed Service ✅ Fully managed ❌ Self-hosted ❌ Self-hosted ✅ SaaS
Trace Retention 30 days Configurable Configurable 15 days (standard)
Anomaly Detection ✅ X-Ray Insights ❌ Manual ❌ Manual ✅ ML-powered
Service Map ✅ Auto-generated ✅ Yes ✅ Yes ✅ Yes
OpenTelemetry ✅ Via ADOT ✅ Native ✅ Supported ✅ Supported
CloudWatch Integration ✅ ServiceLens, Dashboards ❌ None ❌ None ⚠️ Via Integration
Best For AWS-native apps, serverless K8s, multi-cloud, OSS fans Simple tracing, learning Enterprise, full observability
💡 When to Choose X-Ray:
  • Best choice: AWS-native applications, Lambda/serverless, tight CloudWatch integration needed
  • Consider alternatives: Multi-cloud environments, need longer retention, prefer self-hosted/OSS
  • Hybrid approach: Use OpenTelemetry (ADOT) to export to both X-Ray AND other backends

│                                                                                        │

│           Click any node to see detailed traces, latency distribution, error breakdown │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📖 Glossary

Key terms and concepts in AWS X-Ray distributed tracing.

TermDefinition
Trace A collection of segments generated by a single request as it travels through your application. Identified by a unique Trace ID.
Segment A unit of work performed by a single service. Contains timing data, annotations, metadata, and subsegments.
Subsegment A subdivision of a segment that provides more granular timing for downstream calls or local operations.
Annotation Key-value pairs with string/number/boolean values that are indexed for searching. Limited to 50 per segment.
Metadata Key-value pairs with any JSON-serializable values. Not indexed but useful for debugging. Organized by namespaces.
Service Map Visual representation of services and their connections, showing request flow and latency statistics.
Sampling The process of selecting which requests to trace. Controlled by sampling rules to balance visibility and cost.
Reservoir A fixed number of traces per second guaranteed to be recorded before the fixed rate is applied.
X-Ray Daemon A software process that listens on UDP port 2000, batches segment data, and sends it to the X-Ray service.
Group A collection of traces defined by a filter expression. Used to organize traces and enable Insights.
Insights Automated anomaly detection feature that identifies unusual patterns in latency or error rates.
Trace Header HTTP header (X-Amzn-Trace-Id) that propagates trace context across services.
Inferred Segment A segment created by X-Ray for services that don't have SDK instrumentation (e.g., downstream AWS services).
Root Segment The first segment in a trace, created by the service that received the initial request.
Filter Expression A query syntax used to search and filter traces based on annotations, response time, errors, and other criteria.

📝 Changelog & Updates

Recent X-Ray feature updates and improvements.

🆕 2024 Updates
  • Jan 2024: X-Ray SDK Python 2.13+ with async support improvements
  • Feb 2024: Enhanced CloudWatch ServiceLens integration
  • Mar 2024: New filter expression functions for edge analysis
  • Apr 2024: Improved sampling rule evaluation performance
📅 2023 Updates
  • Q4 2023: X-Ray Insights GA with improved ML models
  • Q3 2023: Cross-account trace sharing
  • Q2 2023: Enhanced EKS integration with ADOT
  • Q1 2023: Improved console with new trace timeline view
🔮 Roadmap Highlights
  • OpenTelemetry native support expansion
  • Enhanced ML-based root cause analysis
  • Improved multi-region trace visualization
  • Extended retention options (beyond 30 days)

Java2.11.02.14.x
.NET2.10.02.13.x
