  • Subscribe to AWS What's New for X-Ray announcements
  • Follow the SDK GitHub releases
  • Check X-Ray Documentation History

Amazon Athena

Category: Analytics  |  Use Case: Serverless SQL Queries on S3  |  Pricing: $5 per TB scanned  |  Free Tier: None (pay-per-query)  |  Last Updated: January 2026  |  Documentation: AWS Athena Docs

Serverless interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. No infrastructure to manage - just point to your data, define the schema, and start querying.

🚀 Quick Start: Query anihpj Job Data in 4 Steps
Step 1: Export Data to S3
aws s3 cp jobs.csv \
  s3://anihpj-data/jobs/
Upload Django data exports
Step 2: Create Database
CREATE DATABASE 
  anihpj_analytics;
In Athena Query Editor
Step 3: Create Table
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE
  jobs (id INT, ...)
  LOCATION 's3://...';
Point to S3 data location
Step 4: Run Queries
SELECT * FROM jobs 
  WHERE is_active=true
  LIMIT 100;
Analyze your data with SQL

📋 Overview & Introduction

Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy for you to analyze data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. Athena is serverless, so there's no infrastructure to manage, and you pay only for the queries you run - charged at $5 per terabyte of data scanned.

🎯 Key Benefits
  • Serverless: No infrastructure to set up or manage - just point and query
  • Standard SQL: Uses Presto/Trino under the hood with ANSI SQL support
  • Pay-Per-Query: $5 per TB scanned - optimize with compression & partitioning
  • Fast: Queries run in seconds, even on petabyte-scale datasets
  • Integrated: Works with AWS Glue Data Catalog, QuickSight, and more
  • Open Formats: Supports CSV, JSON, Parquet, ORC, Avro, and more
💡 When to Use Athena for anihpj: Perfect for ad-hoc analytics on your job posting data - analyze application trends, generate reports on job categories by location, track employer posting patterns, and query historical data without impacting your production database.
⚖️ Athena vs. Other Analytics Services
Service Type Best For anihpj Use Case
Athena Serverless SQL Ad-hoc queries on S3 data Job analytics, user reports
Redshift Data Warehouse Complex analytics, large datasets Historical trend analysis
EMR Big Data Spark/Hadoop, ML workloads Job recommendation ML
Glue ETL Service Data preparation, transforms Data pipeline, schema discovery
QuickSight BI Tool Dashboards, visualizations Employer analytics dashboards
OpenSearch Search & Log Full-text search, real-time logs Job search, log analysis
🏗️ Athena Architecture for anihpj Analytics
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           AMAZON ATHENA ARCHITECTURE FOR ANIHPJ                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                              DATA SOURCES (S3)                                       ││
│  │                                                                                      ││
│  │  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐                   ││
│  │  │ 📁 anihpj-data/  │  │ 📁 anihpj-logs/  │  │ 📁 anihpj-events/│                   ││
│  │  │   jobs/          │  │   alb/           │  │   user-activity/ │                   ││
│  │  │   applications/  │  │   cloudfront/    │  │   search-queries/│                   ││
│  │  │   employers/     │  │   django/        │  │   job-views/     │                   ││
│  │  │   users/         │  │   api-gateway/   │  │   applications/  │                   ││
│  │  └────────┬─────────┘  └────────┬─────────┘  └────────┬─────────┘                   ││
│  │           │                     │                     │                              ││
│  │           └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘                              ││
│  │                                 │                                                    ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                    ▼                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                          AWS GLUE DATA CATALOG                                       ││
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ││
│  │  │  Database: anihpj_analytics                                                   │  ││
│  │  │  ├── Table: jobs        (Parquet, partitioned by date)                        │  ││
│  │  │  ├── Table: applications (Parquet, partitioned by status)                     │  ││
│  │  │  ├── Table: users       (JSON, partitioned by registration_month)             │  ││
│  │  │  ├── Table: alb_logs    (Parquet, partitioned by year/month/day)              │  ││
│  │  │  └── Table: cf_logs     (Parquet, partitioned by year/month/day)              │  ││
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                    │                                                     │
│                                    ▼                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                              AMAZON ATHENA                                           ││
│  │                                                                                      ││
│  │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ││
│  │   │                         Presto/Trino Query Engine                           │   ││
│  │   │                                                                             │   ││
│  │   │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐        │   ││
│  │   │  │  Parser &   │  │   Query     │  │  Execution  │  │   Result    │        │   ││
│  │   │  │  Analyzer   │→ │  Optimizer  │→ │   Workers   │→ │   Merger    │        │   ││
│  │   │  │             │  │             │  │  (Parallel) │  │             │        │   ││
│  │   │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘        │   ││
│  │   │                                                                             │   ││
│  │   │  Features:                                                                  │   ││
│  │   │  • ANSI SQL Support     • Federated Queries    • UDF Support               │   ││
│  │   │  • Window Functions     • CTEs & Subqueries    • EXPLAIN Plans             │   ││
│  │   │  • Partition Pruning    • Predicate Pushdown   • Columnar Scanning         │   ││
│  │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ││
│  │                                                                                      ││
│  │   Query Results → s3://anihpj-athena-results/                                       ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                    │                                                     │
│                                    ▼                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │                          QUERY INTERFACES                                            ││
│  │                                                                                      ││
│  │  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐                   ││
│  │  │  🖥️ AWS Console  │  │  🐍 boto3 SDK    │  │  📊 QuickSight   │                   ││
│  │  │  Athena Editor   │  │  Python/Django   │  │  BI Dashboards   │                   ││
│  │  │  - Run queries   │  │  - Programmatic  │  │  - Visualize     │                   ││
│  │  │  - View history  │  │  - Async/Sync    │  │  - Share reports │                   ││
│  │  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘                   ││
│  │                                                                                      ││
│  │  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐                   ││
│  │  │  💻 AWS CLI      │  │  📝 JDBC/ODBC    │  │  🔄 Step Functions│                   ││
│  │  │  Command line    │  │  BI Tools/Excel  │  │  Automated ETL   │                   ││
│  │  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘                   ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  💰 Cost Estimation for anihpj:                                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Scenario: 100GB job data, 50 queries/day, Parquet format, proper partitioning    │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Without optimization: 100GB × 50 queries × $5/TB = $25/day = $750/month          │ │
│  │  With Parquet (10x compression): 10GB scanned = $2.50/day = $75/month             │ │
│  │  With partitioning (scan 1%): 1GB scanned = $0.25/day = $7.50/month ✅            │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Core Concepts

Understanding Athena's fundamental concepts will help you design efficient and cost-effective analytics solutions.

🗄️ Databases & Tables

Athena uses the AWS Glue Data Catalog to store table metadata. Tables are pointers to S3 data - they don't store data themselves.

Concept Description anihpj Example
Database Logical grouping of tables (like a schema) anihpj_analytics, anihpj_logs
Table Metadata definition pointing to S3 location jobs, applications, users
External Table Data stays in S3, Athena only reads it All Athena tables are external by default
View Virtual table based on a query active_jobs_view, top_employers
📚 Data Catalog

The AWS Glue Data Catalog acts as a central metadata repository for Athena. It stores table schemas, partition information, and data locations.

🔍 What's in the Catalog?
  • Table Schema: Column names, data types
  • Location: S3 path to data
  • Format: Parquet, CSV, JSON, etc.
  • Partitions: Partition column definitions
  • SerDe: Serializer/Deserializer settings
💡 Key Benefits
  • Shared across AWS services (Glue, EMR, Redshift Spectrum)
  • Automatic schema discovery with Glue Crawlers
  • Version control for schema changes
  • Centralized governance and access control
📂 Partitions

Partitioning divides your data into logical segments, allowing Athena to scan only relevant partitions. This is the #1 way to reduce costs and improve performance.

🗂️ Partitioning Strategy for anihpj
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          PARTITIONING BEST PRACTICES                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  S3 Bucket Structure with Partitions:                                             │
│                                                                                    │
│  s3://anihpj-data/                                                                │
│  │                                                                                │
│  ├── jobs/                           ← Table: jobs                                │
│  │   ├── year=2025/                                                              │
│  │   │   ├── month=01/                                                           │
│  │   │   │   ├── day=01/                                                         │
│  │   │   │   │   └── jobs-20250101.parquet                                       │
│  │   │   │   ├── day=02/                                                         │
│  │   │   │   │   └── jobs-20250102.parquet                                       │
│  │   │   │   └── ...                                                             │
│  │   │   ├── month=02/                                                           │
│  │   │   └── ...                                                                 │
│  │   └── year=2026/                                                              │
│  │       └── month=01/                                                           │
│  │           └── day=19/                                                         │
│  │               └── jobs-20260119.parquet                                       │
│  │                                                                               │
│  ├── applications/                   ← Table: applications                        │
│  │   ├── status=pending/                                                         │
│  │   │   └── year=2026/month=01/                                                 │
│  │   ├── status=reviewed/                                                        │
│  │   ├── status=accepted/                                                        │
│  │   └── status=rejected/                                                        │
│  │                                                                               │
│  └── user_activity/                  ← Table: user_activity                       │
│      └── event_type=job_view/                                                    │
│          └── year=2026/month=01/day=19/                                          │
│                                                                                    │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  Partition Selection Guidelines:                                                   │
│                                                                                    │
│  ┌─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Partition By        │ When to Use                                           │  │
│  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ year/month/day      │ Time-series data, most queries filter by date         │  │
│  │ status              │ Workflow data (pending, approved, rejected)           │  │
│  │ region/location     │ Geo-based queries (jobs by city)                      │  │
│  │ category            │ Categorical queries (job types)                       │  │
│  └─────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                                    │
│  ⚠️ Don't over-partition: Keep partition count under 20,000 for optimal perf      │
│  ⚠️ Keep files > 100MB: Too many small files hurt performance                      │
│                                                                                    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
👥 Workgroups

Workgroups allow you to isolate queries, control costs, and manage permissions for different teams or use cases.

🎯 Workgroup Use Cases
  • Cost Control: Set per-query data scan limits
  • Team Isolation: Separate dev, staging, prod queries
  • Results Management: Define S3 output locations
  • Query Metrics: Track usage per team/project
💼 anihpj Workgroup Structure
anihpj-prod
├─ Data scan limit: 1TB/query
├─ Results: s3://anihpj-results/prod/
└─ Requester pays: Disabled

anihpj-dev  
├─ Data scan limit: 100GB/query
├─ Results: s3://anihpj-results/dev/
└─ Requester pays: Disabled

anihpj-analytics-team
├─ Data scan limit: 5TB/query
├─ Results: s3://anihpj-results/analytics/
└─ Requester pays: Disabled
⚡ Query Execution

Understanding how Athena executes queries helps optimize performance and costs.

🔄 Athena Query Lifecycle
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ATHENA QUERY EXECUTION FLOW                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                 │
│  1. SUBMISSION                                                                  │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │
│     │  User submits SQL → Athena API → Query Queue               │             │
│     │  • Query receives ExecutionId                              │             │
│     │  • Status: QUEUED                                          │             │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │
│                              ↓                                                  │
│  2. PLANNING                                                                    │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │
│     │  Athena analyzes query:                                    │             │
│     │  • Reads table metadata from Glue Catalog                  │             │
│     │  • Determines which S3 files/partitions to scan            │             │
│     │  • Creates execution plan (Presto/Trino engine)            │             │
│     │  • Status: RUNNING                                         │             │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │
│                              ↓                                                  │
│  3. EXECUTION                                                                   │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │
│     │  Distributed processing:                                   │             │
│     │  • Athena workers read S3 data in parallel                 │             │
│     │  • Apply filters, joins, aggregations                      │             │
│     │  • Columnar formats (Parquet) = read only needed columns   │             │
│     │  • Partition pruning = skip irrelevant partitions          │             │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │
│                              ↓                                                  │
│  4. RESULTS                                                                     │
│     ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │
│     │  Results written to S3:                                    │             │
│     │  • Output: s3://your-bucket/query-results/.csv    │             │
│     │  • Metadata: .metadata file with stats                     │             │
│     │  • Status: SUCCEEDED (or FAILED)                           │             │
│     │  • Data scanned: X GB → Cost = X * $5/TB                   │             │
│     └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │
│                                                                                 │
│  ⏱️ Query Time Breakdown:                                                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  Queue Time:     < 1 second (usually instant)                            │  │
│  │  Planning Time:  1-5 seconds (read metadata, create plan)                │  │
│  │  Execution Time: Varies (depends on data size, complexity)               │  │
│  │  • Small query:  5-30 seconds                                            │  │
│  │  • Large query:  1-10 minutes                                            │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📦 Supported Data Formats

Athena supports various data formats. Choosing the right format significantly impacts query performance and cost.

📊 Data Format Comparison
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ATHENA DATA FORMAT COMPARISON                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  Format      │ Type      │ Compression │ Best For              │ anihpj Use Case │
│  ────────────┼───────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────│
│  CSV         │ Row       │ None/GZIP   │ Simple exports        │ Quick exports   │
│  JSON        │ Row       │ None/GZIP   │ Nested/semi-struct    │ API logs        │
│  Parquet ⭐  │ Columnar  │ Snappy      │ Analytics, large data │ Jobs, users     │
│  ORC         │ Columnar  │ Zlib/Snappy │ Hive workloads        │ Legacy data     │
│  Avro        │ Row       │ Snappy      │ Schema evolution      │ Event streams   │
│                                                                                    │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  💡 RECOMMENDATION FOR ANIHPJ:                                                     │
│                                                                                    │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  1. Use PARQUET for all analytics tables (jobs, applications, users)       │   │
│  │     • 10x smaller than CSV                                                 │   │
│  │     • Columnar = only scan needed columns                                  │   │
│  │     • Built-in compression (Snappy)                                        │   │
│  │                                                                            │   │
│  │  2. Use JSON for log data (ALB, CloudFront, API logs)                      │   │
│  │     • Native format from AWS services                                      │   │
│  │     • Can convert to Parquet later with Glue                               │   │
│  │                                                                            │   │
│  │  3. CSV only for quick one-time imports                                    │   │
│  │     • Easy to generate from Django                                         │   │
│  │     • Convert to Parquet for repeated queries                              │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                    │
│  Cost Impact Example (100GB dataset, SELECT 3 columns from 50 columns):           │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  CSV:     100GB scanned → $0.50 per query                                  │   │
│  │  Parquet:  6GB scanned  → $0.03 per query  (94% savings!)                  │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📂 Partitioning

Partitioning divides your data into logical segments, allowing Athena to scan only relevant partitions. This is the #1 way to reduce costs and improve performance.

🗂️ Partitioning Strategy for anihpj
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          PARTITIONING BEST PRACTICES                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  S3 Bucket Structure with Partitions:                                             │
│                                                                                    │
│  s3://anihpj-data/                                                                │
│  │                                                                                │
│  ├── jobs/                           ← Table: jobs                                │
│  │   ├── year=2025/                                                              │
│  │   │   ├── month=01/                                                           │
│  │   │   │   ├── day=01/                                                         │
│  │   │   │   │   └── jobs-20250101.parquet                                       │
│  │   │   │   ├── day=02/                                                         │
│  │   │   │   │   └── jobs-20250102.parquet                                       │
│  │   │   │   └── ...                                                             │
│  │   │   ├── month=02/                                                           │
│  │   │   └── ...                                                                 │
│  │   └── year=2026/                                                              │
│  │       └── month=01/                                                           │
│  │           └── day=19/                                                         │
│  │               └── jobs-20260119.parquet                                       │
│  │                                                                               │
│  ├── applications/                   ← Table: applications                        │
│  │   ├── status=pending/                                                         │
│  │   │   └── year=2026/month=01/                                                 │
│  │   ├── status=reviewed/                                                        │
│  │   ├── status=accepted/                                                        │
│  │   └── status=rejected/                                                        │
│  │                                                                               │
│  └── user_activity/                  ← Table: user_activity                       │
│      └── event_type=job_view/                                                    │
│          └── year=2026/month=01/day=19/                                          │
│                                                                                    │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                    │
│  Partition Selection Guidelines:                                                   │
│                                                                                    │
│  ┌─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Partition By        │ When to Use                                           │  │
│  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │
│  │ year/month/day      │ Time-series data, most queries filter by date         │  │
│  │ status              │ Workflow data (pending, approved, rejected)           │  │
│  │ region/location     │ Geo-based queries (jobs by city)                      │  │
│  │ category            │ Categorical queries (job types)                       │  │
│  └─────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                                    │
│  ⚠️ Don't over-partition: Keep partition count under 20,000 for optimal perf      │
│  ⚠️ Keep files > 100MB: Too many small files hurt performance                      │
│                                                                                    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🚀 Practical Setup

Step-by-step guide to setting up Athena for the anihpj job portal analytics.

🪣 Step 1: Create S3 Bucket
📦 S3 Bucket Setup
# Create S3 bucket for Athena data
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-data --region us-east-1

# Create S3 bucket for Athena query results
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-athena-results --region us-east-1

# Enable versioning on data bucket
aws s3api put-bucket-versioning \
  --bucket anihpj-data \
  --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled

# Set lifecycle policy to expire query results after 30 days
cat > lifecycle.json << EOF
{
  "Rules": [{
    "Id": "ExpireQueryResults",
    "Status": "Enabled",
    "Prefix": "",
    "Expiration": { "Days": 30 }
  }]
}
EOF

aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
  --bucket anihpj-athena-results \
  --lifecycle-configuration file://lifecycle.json
🗄️ Step 2: Create Database
📝 Create anihpj Analytics Database
-- Create database for anihpj analytics
CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics
COMMENT 'Analytics database for anihpj job portal'
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/';
📊 Step 3: Create Table
📋 Create Jobs Table (Parquet, Partitioned)
-- Create jobs table (Parquet format, partitioned by date)
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics.jobs (
    job_id              STRING,
    title               STRING,
    company_name        STRING,
    description         STRING,
    requirements        STRING,
    salary_min          DECIMAL(10,2),
    salary_max          DECIMAL(10,2),
    job_type            STRING,          -- full-time, part-time, contract
    experience_level    STRING,          -- entry, mid, senior
    location_city       STRING,
    location_state      STRING,
    location_country    STRING,
    is_remote           BOOLEAN,
    skills              ARRAY,
    posted_by_user_id   STRING,
    views_count         INT,
    applications_count  INT,
    is_active           BOOLEAN,
    created_at          TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at          TIMESTAMP
)
PARTITIONED BY (year STRING, month STRING, day STRING)
STORED AS PARQUET
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/jobs/'
TBLPROPERTIES ('parquet.compression'='SNAPPY');

-- Create applications table
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics.applications (
    application_id      STRING,
    job_id              STRING,
    applicant_user_id   STRING,
    resume_s3_path      STRING,
    cover_letter        STRING,
    status              STRING,          -- pending, reviewed, accepted, rejected
    applied_at          TIMESTAMP,
    reviewed_at         TIMESTAMP,
    reviewer_notes      STRING
)
PARTITIONED BY (status STRING, year STRING, month STRING)
STORED AS PARQUET
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/applications/'
TBLPROPERTIES ('parquet.compression'='SNAPPY');

-- Create user activity table for analytics
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics.user_activity (
    event_id            STRING,
    user_id             STRING,
    event_type          STRING,          -- job_view, job_search, job_apply, profile_update
    job_id              STRING,
    search_query        STRING,
    search_filters      STRING,          -- JSON string
    session_id          STRING,
    ip_address          STRING,
    user_agent          STRING,
    timestamp           TIMESTAMP
)
PARTITIONED BY (event_type STRING, year STRING, month STRING, day STRING)
STORED AS PARQUET
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/user_activity/'
TBLPROPERTIES ('parquet.compression'='SNAPPY');

-- Add partitions (run after data is uploaded)
MSCK REPAIR TABLE anihpj_analytics.jobs;
MSCK REPAIR TABLE anihpj_analytics.applications;
MSCK REPAIR TABLE anihpj_analytics.user_activity;
🔍 Step 4: Query Data
📊 Job Portal Analytics Queries
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 1: Top 10 Most Viewed Jobs This Month
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELECT 
    job_id,
    title,
    company_name,
    location_city,
    views_count,
    applications_count,
    ROUND(applications_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(views_count, 0), 2) AS conversion_rate
FROM anihpj_analytics.jobs
WHERE year = '2026' 
  AND month = '01'
  AND is_active = true
ORDER BY views_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 2: Application Status Distribution
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELECT 
    status,
    COUNT(*) as count,
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS percentage
FROM anihpj_analytics.applications
WHERE year = '2026'
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY count DESC;

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 3: Jobs by Location (Top Cities)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELECT 
    location_city,
    location_state,
    COUNT(*) as job_count,
    AVG(salary_max) as avg_max_salary,
    SUM(applications_count) as total_applications
FROM anihpj_analytics.jobs
WHERE is_active = true
  AND year = '2026'
GROUP BY location_city, location_state
ORDER BY job_count DESC
LIMIT 20;

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 4: Most In-Demand Skills
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELECT 
    skill,
    COUNT(*) as job_count,
    AVG(salary_max) as avg_salary
FROM anihpj_analytics.jobs
CROSS JOIN UNNEST(skills) AS t(skill)
WHERE is_active = true
  AND year = '2026'
GROUP BY skill
ORDER BY job_count DESC
LIMIT 25;

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 5: User Engagement Funnel
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WITH funnel AS (
    SELECT 
        event_type,
        COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as unique_users,
        COUNT(*) as total_events
    FROM anihpj_analytics.user_activity
    WHERE year = '2026' AND month = '01'
    GROUP BY event_type
)
SELECT 
    event_type,
    unique_users,
    total_events,
    ROUND(unique_users * 100.0 / FIRST_VALUE(unique_users) 
        OVER (ORDER BY CASE event_type 
            WHEN 'job_search' THEN 1
            WHEN 'job_view' THEN 2
            WHEN 'job_apply' THEN 3
            ELSE 4 END), 2) as funnel_percentage
FROM funnel
ORDER BY CASE event_type 
    WHEN 'job_search' THEN 1
    WHEN 'job_view' THEN 2
    WHEN 'job_apply' THEN 3
    ELSE 4 END;

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- QUERY 6: Time-to-Hire Analysis
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELECT 
    j.company_name,
    AVG(DATE_DIFF('day', a.applied_at, a.reviewed_at)) as avg_days_to_review,
    COUNT(*) as total_applications,
    SUM(CASE WHEN a.status = 'accepted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as accepted_count
FROM anihpj_analytics.applications a
JOIN anihpj_analytics.jobs j ON a.job_id = j.job_id
WHERE a.status IN ('accepted', 'rejected')
  AND a.year = '2026'
GROUP BY j.company_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
ORDER BY avg_days_to_review ASC
LIMIT 20;
📂 Step 5: Add Partitions
🔧 Partition Management Commands
-- Method 1: Automatic partition discovery (easiest)
MSCK REPAIR TABLE anihpj_analytics.jobs;
-- Scans S3 and automatically adds all partitions

-- Method 2: Add specific partitions manually
ALTER TABLE anihpj_analytics.jobs 
ADD PARTITION (year='2026', month='01', day='19')
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/jobs/year=2026/month=01/day=19/';

-- Method 3: Add multiple partitions at once
ALTER TABLE anihpj_analytics.jobs ADD IF NOT EXISTS
  PARTITION (year='2026', month='01', day='15') 
    LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/jobs/year=2026/month=01/day=15/'
  PARTITION (year='2026', month='01', day='16') 
    LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/jobs/year=2026/month=01/day=16/'
  PARTITION (year='2026', month='01', day='17') 
    LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data/jobs/year=2026/month=01/day=17/';

-- View existing partitions
SHOW PARTITIONS anihpj_analytics.jobs;

-- Drop old partitions (cleanup)
ALTER TABLE anihpj_analytics.jobs 
DROP PARTITION (year='2025', month='01', day='01');
💡 Pro Tip: Set up a Lambda function to automatically run MSCK REPAIR TABLE whenever new data is uploaded to S3. This keeps partitions up-to-date without manual intervention.

🐍 Python (boto3) Integration

Integrate Athena queries into your Django application using the boto3 SDK.

⚙️ Setup & Authentication
📦 Installation & Configuration
# Install required packages
pip install boto3 pandas pyarrow

# Django settings.py configuration
AWS_ATHENA_DATABASE = 'anihpj_analytics'
AWS_ATHENA_WORKGROUP = 'anihpj-prod'
AWS_ATHENA_RESULTS_BUCKET = 'anihpj-athena-results'
AWS_REGION = 'us-east-1'
🔍 Run Queries
📝 athena_client.py - Reusable Athena Client for anihpj
📝 athena_client.py - Reusable Athena Client for anihpj
"""
Athena client for anihpj Django application.
Provides async query execution with result retrieval.
"""
import boto3
import time
import pandas as pd
from typing import Optional, Dict, List, Any
from django.conf import settings
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class AthenaClient:
    """
    A client for executing Athena queries from Django.
    
    Usage:
        client = AthenaClient()
        results = client.execute_query(
            "SELECT * FROM jobs WHERE is_active = true LIMIT 10"
        )
    """
    
    def __init__(
        self,
        database: str = 'anihpj_analytics',
        workgroup: str = 'anihpj-prod',
        output_location: str = None,
        region: str = None
    ):
        self.database = database
        self.workgroup = workgroup
        self.output_location = output_location or f's3://{settings.AWS_ATHENA_RESULTS_BUCKET}/results/'
        self.region = region or settings.AWS_REGION
        
        self.client = boto3.client(
            'athena',
            region_name=self.region,
            aws_access_key_id=getattr(settings, 'AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID', None),
            aws_secret_access_key=getattr(settings, 'AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY', None)
        )
    
    def execute_query(
        self,
        query: str,
        timeout_seconds: int = 300,
        return_dataframe: bool = True
    ) -> Optional[Any]:
        """
        Execute an Athena query and wait for results.
        
        Args:
            query: SQL query to execute
            timeout_seconds: Maximum wait time for query completion
            return_dataframe: If True, return pandas DataFrame; else return dict
            
        Returns:
            Query results as DataFrame or dict, None if failed
        """
        try:
            # Start query execution
            response = self.client.start_query_execution(
                QueryString=query,
                QueryExecutionContext={'Database': self.database},
                WorkGroup=self.workgroup,
                ResultConfiguration={'OutputLocation': self.output_location}
            )
            
            query_execution_id = response['QueryExecutionId']
            logger.info(f"Started Athena query: {query_execution_id}")
            
            # Wait for query to complete
            state = self._wait_for_query(query_execution_id, timeout_seconds)
            
            if state == 'SUCCEEDED':
                return self._get_results(query_execution_id, return_dataframe)
            else:
                logger.error(f"Query {query_execution_id} failed with state: {state}")
                return None
                
        except Exception as e:
            logger.exception(f"Athena query failed: {e}")
            raise
    
    def _wait_for_query(self, query_execution_id: str, timeout: int) -> str:
        """Poll for query completion status."""
        start_time = time.time()
        
        while True:
            response = self.client.get_query_execution(
                QueryExecutionId=query_execution_id
            )
            state = response['QueryExecution']['Status']['State']
            
            if state in ['SUCCEEDED', 'FAILED', 'CANCELLED']:
                return state
            
            if time.time() - start_time > timeout:
                self.client.stop_query_execution(QueryExecutionId=query_execution_id)
                raise TimeoutError(f"Query {query_execution_id} timed out after {timeout}s")
            
            time.sleep(1)  # Poll every second
    
    def _get_results(
        self,
        query_execution_id: str,
        return_dataframe: bool
    ) -> Any:
        """Retrieve query results."""
        paginator = self.client.get_paginator('get_query_results')
        
        rows = []
        columns = []
        
        for page in paginator.paginate(QueryExecutionId=query_execution_id):
            result_set = page['ResultSet']
            
            # Get column names from first page
            if not columns:
                columns = [
                    col['Label'] for col in result_set['ResultSetMetadata']['ColumnInfo']
                ]
            
            # Process rows (skip header row on first page)
            for i, row in enumerate(result_set['Rows']):
                if i == 0 and not rows:  # Skip header
                    continue
                rows.append([
                    field.get('VarCharValue', None) for field in row['Data']
                ])
        
        if return_dataframe:
            return pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=columns)
        else:
            return {'columns': columns, 'rows': rows}
    
    def execute_async(self, query: str) -> str:
        """
        Start query execution without waiting for results.
        Returns query_execution_id for later retrieval.
        """
        response = self.client.start_query_execution(
            QueryString=query,
            QueryExecutionContext={'Database': self.database},
            WorkGroup=self.workgroup,
            ResultConfiguration={'OutputLocation': self.output_location}
        )
        return response['QueryExecutionId']
    
    def get_query_status(self, query_execution_id: str) -> Dict:
        """Get status of a running or completed query."""
        response = self.client.get_query_execution(
            QueryExecutionId=query_execution_id
        )
        execution = response['QueryExecution']
        return {
            'state': execution['Status']['State'],
            'state_change_reason': execution['Status'].get('StateChangeReason'),
            'data_scanned_bytes': execution['Statistics'].get('DataScannedInBytes', 0),
            'execution_time_ms': execution['Statistics'].get('EngineExecutionTimeInMillis', 0),
            'query_cost': execution['Statistics'].get('DataScannedInBytes', 0) / (1024**4) * 5  # $5/TB
        }
📊 Retrieve Results
📥 Results Processing Examples
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# USAGE EXAMPLES IN DJANGO VIEWS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# views.py
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.views import View
from .athena_client import AthenaClient


class JobAnalyticsView(View):
    """API endpoint for job analytics."""
    
    def get(self, request):
        client = AthenaClient()
        
        # Get top jobs this month
        query = """
            SELECT title, company_name, views_count, applications_count
            FROM jobs
            WHERE year = '2026' AND month = '01' AND is_active = true
            ORDER BY views_count DESC
            LIMIT 10
        """
        
        df = client.execute_query(query)
        
        return JsonResponse({
            'top_jobs': df.to_dict(orient='records')
        })


class SkillsDemandView(View):
    """API endpoint for skills demand analytics."""
    
    def get(self, request):
        client = AthenaClient()
        
        query = """
            SELECT skill, COUNT(*) as demand_count, AVG(salary_max) as avg_salary
            FROM jobs CROSS JOIN UNNEST(skills) AS t(skill)
            WHERE is_active = true AND year = '2026'
            GROUP BY skill
            ORDER BY demand_count DESC
            LIMIT 20
        """
        
        df = client.execute_query(query)
        
        return JsonResponse({
            'skills_demand': df.to_dict(orient='records')
        })
⚡ Async Execution
🚀 Asynchronous Query Pattern
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ASYNC PATTERN: Start query, check status later
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from django.core.cache import cache
from celery import shared_task

@shared_task
def run_daily_analytics_report():
    """
    Celery task to run expensive analytics queries overnight.
    """
    client = AthenaClient()
    
    # Start multiple queries asynchronously
    queries = {
        'top_jobs': """
            SELECT title, company_name, views_count 
            FROM jobs 
            WHERE year = '2026' AND month = '01'
            ORDER BY views_count DESC LIMIT 100
        """,
        'application_trends': """
            SELECT DATE(created_at) as date, COUNT(*) as count
            FROM applications
            WHERE year = '2026' AND month = '01'
            GROUP BY DATE(created_at)
            ORDER BY date
        """,
        'user_engagement': """
            SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) as count
            FROM user_activity
            WHERE year = '2026' AND month = '01'
            GROUP BY event_type
        """
    }
    
    # Start all queries
    execution_ids = {}
    for name, query in queries.items():
        exec_id = client.execute_async(query)
        execution_ids[name] = exec_id
        cache.set(f'athena_query_{name}', exec_id, timeout=3600)
    
    # Poll for completion (or use another task)
    import time
    for name, exec_id in execution_ids.items():
        while True:
            status = client.get_query_status(exec_id)
            if status['state'] in ['SUCCEEDED', 'FAILED', 'CANCELLED']:
                break
            time.sleep(5)
        
        if status['state'] == 'SUCCEEDED':
            results = client.get_query_results(exec_id)
            cache.set(f'analytics_report_{name}', results, timeout=86400)
            logger.info(f"Query {name} completed: ${status['query_cost']:.4f}")
        else:
            logger.error(f"Query {name} failed: {status['state_change_reason']}")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DJANGO VIEW: Retrieve cached async results
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class AnalyticsReportView(View):
    """Serve pre-computed analytics reports."""
    
    def get(self, request, report_name):
        results = cache.get(f'analytics_report_{report_name}')
        
        if not results:
            return JsonResponse({
                'status': 'pending',
                'message': 'Report is being generated'
            }, status=202)
        
        return JsonResponse({
            'status': 'ready',
            'data': results
        })
📤 Exporting Django Data to S3
📝 data_export.py - Export Django Models to Parquet
"""
Export Django models to Parquet files for Athena analysis.
Run as a management command or Celery task.
"""
import boto3
import pandas as pd
import pyarrow as pa
import pyarrow.parquet as pq
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from io import BytesIO
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from jobpost.models import JobPost, Application, UserActivity


class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Export job data to S3 for Athena analytics'
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument(
            '--date',
            type=str,
            help='Date to export (YYYY-MM-DD), defaults to yesterday'
        )
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        export_date = options.get('date')
        if export_date:
            target_date = datetime.strptime(export_date, '%Y-%m-%d').date()
        else:
            target_date = datetime.now().date() - timedelta(days=1)
        
        self.stdout.write(f"Exporting data for {target_date}")
        
        # Export each model
        self.export_jobs(target_date)
        self.export_applications(target_date)
        self.export_user_activity(target_date)
        
        self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS('Export completed successfully!'))
    
    def export_jobs(self, target_date):
        """Export JobPost model to Parquet."""
        jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
            created_at__date=target_date
        ).values(
            'id', 'title', 'company__name', 'description', 'requirements',
            'salary_min', 'salary_max', 'job_type', 'experience_level',
            'location__city', 'location__state', 'location__country',
            'is_remote', 'posted_by_id', 'views_count', 'applications_count',
            'is_active', 'created_at', 'updated_at'
        )
        
        if not jobs:
            self.stdout.write(f"No jobs found for {target_date}")
            return
        
        df = pd.DataFrame(list(jobs))
        
        # Get skills as array
        df['skills'] = df['id'].apply(
            lambda x: list(JobPost.objects.get(id=x).skills.values_list('name', flat=True))
        )
        
        # Upload to S3 with partitioning
        self._upload_parquet(
            df=df,
            bucket='anihpj-data',
            prefix=f"jobs/year={target_date.year}/month={target_date.month:02d}/day={target_date.day:02d}",
            filename=f"jobs-{target_date.strftime('%Y%m%d')}.parquet"
        )
    
    def export_applications(self, target_date):
        """Export Application model to Parquet."""
        applications = Application.objects.filter(
            applied_at__date=target_date
        ).values(
            'id', 'job_id', 'applicant_id', 'resume_path',
            'cover_letter', 'status', 'applied_at', 'reviewed_at', 'reviewer_notes'
        )
        
        if not applications:
            return
        
        df = pd.DataFrame(list(applications))
        
        # Group by status for partitioning
        for status in df['status'].unique():
            status_df = df[df['status'] == status]
            self._upload_parquet(
                df=status_df,
                bucket='anihpj-data',
                prefix=f"applications/status={status}/year={target_date.year}/month={target_date.month:02d}",
                filename=f"applications-{target_date.strftime('%Y%m%d')}.parquet"
            )
    
    def export_user_activity(self, target_date):
        """Export UserActivity model to Parquet."""
        activities = UserActivity.objects.filter(
            timestamp__date=target_date
        ).values(
            'id', 'user_id', 'event_type', 'job_id', 'search_query',
            'search_filters', 'session_id', 'ip_address', 'user_agent', 'timestamp'
        )
        
        if not activities:
            return
        
        df = pd.DataFrame(list(activities))
        
        # Group by event_type for partitioning
        for event_type in df['event_type'].unique():
            event_df = df[df['event_type'] == event_type]
            self._upload_parquet(
                df=event_df,
                bucket='anihpj-data',
                prefix=f"user_activity/event_type={event_type}/year={target_date.year}/month={target_date.month:02d}/day={target_date.day:02d}",
                filename=f"activity-{target_date.strftime('%Y%m%d')}.parquet"
            )
    
    def _upload_parquet(self, df, bucket, prefix, filename):
        """Convert DataFrame to Parquet and upload to S3."""
        # Convert to Parquet in memory
        table = pa.Table.from_pandas(df)
        buffer = BytesIO()
        pq.write_table(table, buffer, compression='snappy')
        buffer.seek(0)
        
        # Upload to S3
        s3 = boto3.client('s3')
        key = f"{prefix}/{filename}"
        s3.upload_fileobj(buffer, bucket, key)
        
        self.stdout.write(f"Uploaded s3://{bucket}/{key}")

⌨️ AWS CLI Reference

Common AWS CLI commands for managing Athena resources.

🗄️ Database Operations
🖥️ Database & Table Management
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATABASE & TABLE MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# List databases
aws athena list-databases --catalog-name AwsDataCatalog

# List tables in a database
aws athena list-table-metadata \
    --catalog-name AwsDataCatalog \
    --database-name anihpj_analytics

# Get table details
aws athena get-table-metadata \
    --catalog-name AwsDataCatalog \
    --database-name anihpj_analytics \
    --table-name jobs
🔍 Query Operations
⚡ Execute and Manage Queries
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# QUERY EXECUTION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Start a query (returns QueryExecutionId)
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM jobs WHERE is_active = true" \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --query-execution-context Database=anihpj_analytics

# Check query status
aws athena get-query-execution \
    --query-execution-id abc123-def456-ghi789

# Get query results
aws athena get-query-results \
    --query-execution-id abc123-def456-ghi789

# Stop a running query
aws athena stop-query-execution \
    --query-execution-id abc123-def456-ghi789

# List recent query executions
aws athena list-query-executions \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --max-results 20
👥 Workgroup Management
🔧 Workgroup Operations
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WORKGROUP MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Create workgroup with cost controls
aws athena create-work-group \
    --name anihpj-prod \
    --configuration '{
        "ResultConfiguration": {
            "OutputLocation": "s3://anihpj-athena-results/",
            "EncryptionConfiguration": {"EncryptionOption": "SSE_S3"}
        },
        "EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration": true,
        "PublishCloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
        "BytesScannedCutoffPerQuery": 10737418240
    }' \
    --description "Production workgroup for anihpj analytics"

# List workgroups
aws athena list-work-groups

# Update workgroup (e.g., increase query limit)
aws athena update-work-group \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --configuration-updates '{
        "BytesScannedCutoffPerQuery": 107374182400
    }'

# ----- NAMED QUERIES (Saved Queries) -----

# Create a named query
aws athena create-named-query \
    --name "top-jobs-monthly" \
    --database anihpj_analytics \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --query-string "SELECT title, company_name, views_count FROM jobs WHERE is_active = true ORDER BY views_count DESC LIMIT 10"

# List named queries
aws athena list-named-queries --work-group anihpj-prod

# Get named query details
aws athena get-named-query --named-query-id abc123

# ----- PREPARED STATEMENTS -----

# Create prepared statement (parameterized query)
aws athena create-prepared-statement \
    --statement-name jobs_by_location \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --query-statement "SELECT * FROM jobs WHERE location_city = ? AND is_active = ?"

# Execute prepared statement
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "EXECUTE jobs_by_location USING 'New York', true" \
    --work-group anihpj-prod \
    --query-execution-context Database=anihpj_analytics

💰 Cost Optimization

Athena charges $5 per TB of data scanned. Optimizing your queries can reduce costs by 90%+ while improving performance.

💵 Pricing Model
📊 How You're Charged
  • $5.00 per TB of data scanned
  • Minimum 10 MB per query
  • Rounded up to nearest MB
  • No charge for DDL queries (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
  • No charge for failed queries
  • S3 storage and data transfer charged separately
💡 Cost Examples
1 GB scanned:    $0.005
10 GB scanned:   $0.05
100 GB scanned:  $0.50
1 TB scanned:    $5.00

Monthly estimate (100 queries/day, 1GB each):
100 × 30 × $0.005 = $15/month
📉 Reduce Data Scanned
📊 Cost Reduction Techniques
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          ATHENA COST OPTIMIZATION GUIDE                                │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                        │
│  Athena Pricing: $5.00 per TB of data scanned                                         │
│  Minimum charge: 10 MB per query                                                      │
│                                                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                     OPTIMIZATION TECHNIQUES                                      │ │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  1. USE COLUMNAR FORMATS (Parquet/ORC)                                          │ │
│  │     ├─ Parquet typically reduces data scanned by 80-90%                         │ │
│  │     ├─ Only columns needed for query are read                                   │ │
│  │     └─ Built-in compression (Snappy, GZIP, LZO)                                 │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │     Example Savings:                                                            │ │
│  │     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │ │
│  │     │ 100GB CSV table, SELECT 5 of 50 columns                     │             │ │
│  │     │ CSV:     100 GB scanned = $0.50 per query                   │             │ │
│  │     │ Parquet: ~2 GB scanned  = $0.01 per query (98% savings!)    │             │ │
│  │     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  2. PARTITION YOUR DATA                                                         │ │
│  │     ├─ Partition by commonly filtered columns (date, status, region)            │ │
│  │     ├─ WHERE clause on partition column = partition pruning                     │ │
│  │     └─ Can reduce data scanned by 99%+ for targeted queries                     │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │     Example Savings:                                                            │ │
│  │     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐             │ │
│  │     │ 1TB table partitioned by year/month/day (365 partitions)    │             │ │
│  │     │ Query for single day: ~2.7 GB scanned = $0.014 per query    │             │ │
│  │     │ Without partitions:   1 TB scanned    = $5.00 per query     │             │ │
│  │     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘             │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  3. COMPRESS DATA                                                               │ │
│  │     ├─ GZIP for CSV/JSON (slower but smaller)                                   │ │
│  │     ├─ Snappy for Parquet (faster, good compression)                            │ │
│  │     └─ Typical 4-10x size reduction                                             │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  4. SELECT ONLY NEEDED COLUMNS                                                  │ │
│  │     ├─ Avoid SELECT * - always specify columns                                  │ │
│  │     └─ With Parquet, this directly reduces bytes scanned                        │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  5. USE WORKGROUP LIMITS                                                        │ │
│  │     ├─ Set BytesScannedCutoffPerQuery to prevent expensive queries              │ │
│  │     └─ Create separate workgroups for dev/prod with different limits            │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  6. CACHE RESULTS WITH VIEWS & CTAS                                             │ │
│  │     ├─ Create views for common query patterns                                   │ │
│  │     ├─ Use CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) for materialized results               │ │
│  │     └─ Query the materialized table instead of raw data                         │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
│  Cost Calculator for anihpj:                                                          │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Jobs Data: 10GB/month, 100 queries/day                                         │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  Unoptimized (CSV, no partitions):                                              │ │
│  │  10GB × 100 queries × 30 days × $0.005/GB = $150/month                          │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  Optimized (Parquet + partitions + select columns):                             │ │
│  │  0.1GB × 100 queries × 30 days × $0.005/GB = $1.50/month ✅                     │ │
│  │                                                                                  │ │
│  │  SAVINGS: 99%                                                                   │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                        │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📊 Columnar Formats
🎯 Why Columnar Formats Save Money
  • Columnar storage: Data organized by column, not row
  • Selective scanning: Read only columns needed for query
  • Better compression: Similar data types compress better
  • Parquet recommended: Industry standard, well-supported
💾 Format Comparison
Format Size Query Cost Speed
CSV 100 GB $0.50 Slow
Parquet 10 GB $0.05 Fast
JSON 120 GB $0.60 Slow
🔒 Cost Controls
⚙️ Implement Cost Guardrails
-- Set workgroup-level data scan limit (10 GB per query)
-- Queries exceeding this will fail
CREATE WORKGROUP anihpj_prod
WITH (
    output_location = 's3://anihpj-athena-results/',
    bytes_scanned_cutoff_per_query = 10737418240
);

-- Monitor costs with CloudWatch
-- Create alarm for monthly Athena spending
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name athena-monthly-cost \
    --alarm-description "Alert when Athena costs exceed $50/month" \
    --metric-name EstimatedCharges \
    --namespace AWS/Billing \
    --statistic Maximum \
    --period 86400 \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 50 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold

✅ Best Practices

Follow these best practices to ensure optimal performance, security, and cost-efficiency.

⚡ Query Optimization
🔍 Query Writing Tips
  • Always specify columns - never use SELECT *
  • Filter on partition columns first
  • Use LIMIT during development
  • Avoid scanning unnecessary partitions
  • Use EXPLAIN to understand query plans
  • Prefer JOIN over subqueries when possible
📈 Performance Patterns
❌ Bad: SELECT * FROM jobs
✅ Good: SELECT id, title FROM jobs

❌ Bad: WHERE created_at > '2026-01-01'
✅ Good: WHERE year='2026' AND month='01'

❌ Bad: COUNT(*) on entire table
✅ Good: Use CTAS to materialize counts
📂 Data Organization
📦 File & Folder Best Practices
  • Use Parquet format for all analytics data
  • Partition by commonly filtered columns
  • Keep files 100MB-1GB for optimal performance
  • Use Snappy compression for Parquet
  • Organize S3 paths with Hive-style partitioning
  • Avoid too many small files (< 10MB)
🗂️ Ideal Partition Strategy
✅ Good partitioning:
s3://bucket/table/year=2026/month=01/

✅ Multi-level partitioning:
s3://bucket/jobs/status=active/
                  year=2026/month=01/

❌ Too granular:
s3://bucket/table/year=2026/month=01/
                  day=19/hour=14/minute=30/
🚀 Performance Tips
⚡ Speed Optimization
  • Use columnar formats (Parquet, ORC)
  • Enable compression (Snappy, GZIP)
  • Optimize file sizes (100MB-1GB sweet spot)
  • Use partition projection for predictable partitions
  • Create views for complex, repeated queries
  • Use CTAS to materialize intermediate results
💰 Cost Optimization
  • Set workgroup query limits
  • Monitor with CloudWatch metrics
  • Use CTAS to materialize expensive queries
  • Create separate dev/prod workgroups
  • Review query history for optimization opportunities
  • Enable query result reuse (60 minutes)
🔒 Security Best Practices
🛡️ Access Control
  • Use IAM policies to restrict database/table access
  • Enable encryption for results in S3
  • Use workgroups to isolate users/teams
  • Enable CloudTrail for audit logging
  • Use Lake Formation for fine-grained access
  • Implement row-level security with views
🔐 Encryption
# Enable encryption for query results
aws athena create-work-group \
  --name encrypted-wg \
  --configuration '{
    "ResultConfiguration": {
      "EncryptionConfiguration": {
        "EncryptionOption": "SSE_KMS",
        "KmsKey": "arn:aws:kms:..."
      }
    }
  }'

🔐 IAM Policies & Permissions

Configure IAM policies to control access to Athena resources and data.

📄 IAM Policy Examples
📝 IAM Policy for anihpj Athena Access
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "AthenaQueryAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "athena:StartQueryExecution",
                "athena:StopQueryExecution",
                "athena:GetQueryExecution",
                "athena:GetQueryResults",
                "athena:GetWorkGroup",
                "athena:ListQueryExecutions"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:athena:us-east-1:123456789012:workgroup/anihpj-*"
            ]
        },
        {
            "Sid": "GlueDataCatalogAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "glue:GetDatabase",
                "glue:GetDatabases",
                "glue:GetTable",
                "glue:GetTables",
                "glue:GetPartition",
                "glue:GetPartitions",
                "glue:BatchGetPartition"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:glue:us-east-1:123456789012:catalog",
                "arn:aws:glue:us-east-1:123456789012:database/anihpj_*",
                "arn:aws:glue:us-east-1:123456789012:table/anihpj_*/*"
            ]
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3DataAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:GetObject",
                "s3:ListBucket"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-data",
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-data/*"
            ]
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3ResultsAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:GetObject",
                "s3:PutObject",
                "s3:ListBucket",
                "s3:GetBucketLocation"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-athena-results",
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-athena-results/*"
            ]
        }
    ]
}

� Cheat Sheet

Quick reference for common Athena commands and patterns.

⚡ Quick Commands
🔑 SQL Quick Reference
-- Create database
CREATE DATABASE db_name;

-- Create table from S3
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE t (
  col1 STRING,
  col2 INT
) PARTITIONED BY (dt STRING)
STORED AS PARQUET
LOCATION 's3://bucket/path/';

-- Add partitions
MSCK REPAIR TABLE t;

-- Query with partition filter
SELECT * FROM t 
WHERE dt = '2026-01-19';

-- Create view
CREATE VIEW v AS 
SELECT * FROM t WHERE col2 > 0;

-- CTAS (Create Table As Select)
CREATE TABLE new_t AS
SELECT * FROM t WHERE col2 > 0;
📊 Common Functions
-- Date functions
DATE_FORMAT(dt, '%Y-%m-%d')
DATE_DIFF('day', dt1, dt2)
DATE_ADD('day', 7, dt)
CURRENT_DATE

-- String functions
CONCAT(s1, s2)
LOWER(s), UPPER(s)
REGEXP_LIKE(s, pattern)
SPLIT(s, delimiter)

-- Aggregations
COUNT(*), COUNT(DISTINCT col)
SUM(col), AVG(col)
MIN(col), MAX(col)
APPROX_DISTINCT(col)

-- Array functions
UNNEST(array_col)
ARRAY_AGG(col)
CARDINALITY(array_col)
CONTAINS(array_col, val)
⚡ Performance Tips
DoDon't
Use ParquetUse CSV for analytics
Partition dataScan full tables
SELECT columnsSELECT *
Filter on partitionsFilter after scan
Use LIMIT in devUnlimited queries
Files 100MB-1GBMany small files
💵 Pricing Quick Facts
  • Query cost: $5 per TB scanned
  • Minimum charge: 10 MB per query
  • DDL queries: Free (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
  • Failed queries: Not charged
  • Cancelled queries: Charged for data scanned
  • S3 storage: Separate S3 charges apply
  • Data transfer: Free within same region

✅ Production Checklist

🚀 Pre-Production
  • ☐ Create S3 buckets for data and results
  • ☐ Set up Glue Data Catalog database
  • ☐ Convert data to Parquet format
  • ☐ Implement partitioning strategy
  • ☐ Create production workgroup with limits
  • ☐ Set up IAM roles and policies
  • ☐ Enable S3 encryption at rest
  • ☐ Test queries and validate performance
📊 Production Setup
  • ☐ Configure CloudWatch alarms for costs
  • ☐ Set BytesScannedCutoffPerQuery limit
  • ☐ Create named queries for common analytics
  • ☐ Set up data export pipeline (daily/hourly)
  • ☐ Configure MSCK REPAIR automation
  • ☐ Document table schemas and partitions
  • ☐ Train team on query optimization
  • ☐ Set up QuickSight dashboards (optional)

🔧 Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions when working with Amazon Athena.

🔍 Athena Troubleshooting Decision Tree
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ATHENA TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE                                   │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  Query returns no results?                                                            │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 1: Is data in the correct S3 location?                                    │
│  │   └─ aws s3 ls s3://bucket/path/ → Verify files exist                             │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 2: Are partitions registered?                                             │
│  │   └─ MSCK REPAIR TABLE table_name; → Discovers new partitions                     │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 3: Is partition filter correct?                                           │
│  │   └─ Check WHERE clause matches partition values (case-sensitive!)                │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  └─→ Check 4: Is SerDe format correct?                                               │
│      └─ SHOW CREATE TABLE table_name; → Verify format matches data                   │
│                                                                                       │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  Query is slow or expensive?                                                          │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 1: Are you using SELECT *?                                                │
│  │   └─ Always specify only needed columns                                           │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 2: Are you filtering on partition columns?                                │
│  │   └─ Add WHERE clause on partition columns                                        │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check 3: Is data in columnar format?                                            │
│  │   └─ Convert CSV/JSON to Parquet using CTAS or Glue                               │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  └─→ Check 4: Too many small files?                                                  │
│      └─ Combine files to 100MB-1GB using Glue or CTAS                                │
│                                                                                       │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│  Permission denied errors?                                                            │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  ├─→ Check IAM policy includes:                                                       │
│  │   • athena:StartQueryExecution, GetQueryResults                                   │
│  │   • glue:GetTable, GetDatabase                                                    │
│  │   • s3:GetObject on data bucket                                                   │
│  │   • s3:PutObject on results bucket                                                │
│  │                                                                                    │
│  └─→ Check S3 bucket policies allow access                                           │
│                                                                                       │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📖 Glossary

Key terms and concepts in Amazon Athena.

TermDefinition
Data Catalog AWS Glue's metadata repository that stores table definitions, schemas, and partition information.
External Table A table where data remains in S3; Athena only reads it. Dropping the table doesn't delete data.
Partition A subdivision of table data based on column values, stored in separate S3 prefixes for efficient querying.
Workgroup A resource that isolates queries, controls costs, and manages permissions for groups of users.
SerDe Serializer/Deserializer - defines how Athena interprets data format (JSON, CSV, Parquet, etc.).
CTAS CREATE TABLE AS SELECT - creates a new table from query results, useful for data transformation.
Presto/Trino The distributed SQL query engine that powers Athena's query processing.
Columnar Format Data format (Parquet, ORC) that stores data by column, enabling efficient column-based queries.
Partition Pruning Optimization where Athena skips reading partitions not needed for the query based on WHERE clause.
Named Query A saved query with a name that can be reused and shared within a workgroup.

pandas>=2.0.0
pyarrow>=14.0.0
# Optional for async
aioboto3>=12.0.0
📢 Stay Updated:
  • Subscribe to AWS What's New for Athena announcements
  • Check the Athena Documentation History
  • Monitor Athena Pricing Page for updates

🎯 When to Choose Athena

✅ Choose Amazon Athena when:
  • ✅ You need ad-hoc SQL queries on data in S3 without ETL pipelines
  • ✅ You want serverless analytics with zero infrastructure management
  • ✅ You have infrequent or unpredictable query workloads (pay-per-query)
  • ✅ You need to analyze logs (CloudTrail, VPC Flow, ALB, CloudFront)
  • ✅ You want to query multiple data formats (CSV, JSON, Parquet, ORC, Avro)
  • ✅ You need quick insights without provisioning a data warehouse
  • ✅ You're building a data lake architecture on S3
  • ✅ You want to integrate analytics directly into Django dashboards
⚠️ Consider alternatives when:
  • ❌ You need sub-second response times for OLTP workloads → Use RDS/Aurora
  • ❌ You have constant, heavy analytical workloads → Use Redshift (provisioned capacity)
  • ❌ You need real-time streaming analytics → Use Kinesis Analytics
  • ❌ You require complex ML/AI transformations → Use EMR + Spark
  • ❌ You need graph database queries → Use Neptune
  • ❌ You prefer no-code analytics → Use QuickSight Q (natural language)
📊 Analytics Service Comparison Matrix
Feature 🔍 Athena 📊 Redshift ⚡ EMR 🌊 Kinesis
Pricing Model ✅ Per-query ($5/TB) 💰 Per-node (hourly) 💰 Per-cluster (hourly) 💰 Per-shard (hourly)
Query Latency ⚠️ Seconds-minutes ✅ Sub-second (cached) ⚠️ Minutes ✅ Milliseconds
Data Location ✅ S3 (no loading) ⚠️ Must load data ✅ S3 + HDFS ✅ Streaming
Management ✅ Serverless ⚠️ Cluster management ⚠️ Cluster management ✅ Serverless option
Concurrency ⚠️ Limited (default 5) ✅ High (WLM tuned) ✅ High (cluster-based) ✅ High
Use Case Ad-hoc, logs, data lake BI, dashboards, reporting Big data processing, ML Real-time analytics
anihpj Fit ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent ⭐⭐⭐ Good (overkill) ⭐⭐ Limited ⭐⭐⭐ Moderate

│  │   (Django)      │                                                           │
│  └────────┬────────┘                                                           │
│           │                                                                     │

│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │

│  ├─────────────────┬─────────────────┬─────────────────┬───────────────────┤   │

│  │  ─────────────  │  ────────────   │  ─────────────  │  ────────────     │   │
