│  📊 WHAT'S PROTECTED:                                                                │
│  ├── jobpost_job (10,000+ job listings)                                             │
│  ├── jobpost_company (500+ companies)                                               │
│  ├── jobpost_application (25,000+ applications)                                     │
│  ├── auth_user (5,000+ registered users)                                            │
│  └── django_session (active sessions)                                               │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⏱️ RECOVERY SCENARIOS:                                                              │
│  ├── Accidental deletion: Restore specific tables (RTO: 30 min)                     │
│  ├── Data corruption: Point-in-time restore (RTO: 45 min)                           │
│  ├── Region outage: Cross-region restore (RTO: 2 hours)                             │
│  └── Full DR failover: Complete database restore (RTO: 1 hour)                      │
│                                                                                      │
│  💰 COST: ~$9.50/month (100GB database, 35-day retention)                           │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📁 User Uploads Protection (EFS)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    USE CASE: PROTECTING USER UPLOADS                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  SCENARIO: Backup resumes, cover letters, company logos uploaded to EFS             │
│                                                                                      │
│  📂 EFS STRUCTURE:                                                                   │
│  /mnt/efs/anihpj/                                                                   │
│  ├── resumes/                                                                        │
│  │   ├── 2025/01/                                                                   │
│  │   │   ├── user_1234_resume_v1.pdf                                                │
│  │   │   └── user_5678_resume_v2.docx                                               │
│  │   └── 2025/02/...                                                                │
│  ├── company_logos/                                                                  │
│  │   ├── company_123_logo.png                                                       │
│  │   └── company_456_logo.svg                                                       │
│  └── cover_letters/                                                                  │
│      └── application_789_cover.pdf                                                  │
│                                                                                      │
│  🔄 BACKUP SCHEDULE:                                                                 │
│  ├── Daily incremental backup at 3:00 AM UTC                                        │
│  ├── Weekly full backup on Sundays                                                  │
│  └── Lifecycle: Warm (7 days) → Cold (28 days) → Delete (90 days)                  │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⚡ RESTORE OPTIONS:                                                                 │
│  ├── Single file restore: Recover specific resume (5 min)                           │
│  ├── Directory restore: Recover all files from specific date (15 min)               │
│  └── Full EFS restore: Complete file system recovery (30 min)                       │
│                                                                                      │
│  💰 COST: ~$1.50/month (50GB EFS, warm + cold storage mix)                          │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🌍 Disaster Recovery Scenario
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    USE CASE: COMPLETE DISASTER RECOVERY                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  SCENARIO: Primary region (us-east-1) becomes unavailable                           │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⚠️ DISASTER DETECTED:                                                               │
│  └── Route 53 health check fails → DNS failover initiated                           │
│                                                                                      │
│  🔄 RECOVERY TIMELINE:                                                               │
│                                                                                      │
│  T+0 min    │ Disaster detected, CloudWatch alarm triggered                         │
│  T+5 min    │ DR runbook initiated, team notified via SNS                           │
│  T+10 min   │ Start RDS restore from cross-region backup vault                      │
│  T+25 min   │ RDS instance available in us-west-2                                   │
│  T+30 min   │ Start EFS restore from cross-region backup                            │
│  T+45 min   │ EFS mount points ready                                                │
│  T+50 min   │ Update Django settings (DB endpoint, EFS mount)                       │
│  T+55 min   │ Deploy application to DR EC2 instances                                │
│  T+60 min   │ Update Route 53 to point to DR ALB                                    │
│  T+65 min   │ ✅ SERVICE RESTORED in DR region                                       │
│                                                                                      │
│  📊 RECOVERY METRICS:                                                                │
│  ├── RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 65 minutes                                      │
│  ├── RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 24 hours (daily backups)                       │
│  └── Data Loss: Maximum 24 hours of data                                            │
│                                                                                      │
│  📋 DR CHECKLIST:                                                                    │
│  ☐ Verify cross-region vault has latest backups                                     │
│  ☐ Test restore procedure quarterly                                                 │
│  ☐ Keep DR infrastructure pre-provisioned (minimal)                                 │
│  ☐ Document recovery runbook with step-by-step commands                             │
│  ☐ Train team on DR procedures                                                      │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔗 Integration with AWS Services

AWS Backup integrates seamlessly with other AWS services for comprehensive data protection.

📊 CloudWatch Integration
Backup Monitoring Dashboard
# Create CloudWatch Dashboard for AWS Backup Monitoring
import boto3
import json

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

# Dashboard for anihpj backup monitoring
dashboard_body = {
    "widgets": [
        {
            "type": "metric",
            "properties": {
                "title": "Backup Job Status",
                "metrics": [
                    ["AWS/Backup", "NumberOfBackupJobsCompleted", {"color": "#2ca02c"}],
                    ["AWS/Backup", "NumberOfBackupJobsFailed", {"color": "#d62728"}],
                    ["AWS/Backup", "NumberOfBackupJobsRunning", {"color": "#1f77b4"}]
                ],
                "period": 3600,
                "stat": "Sum",
                "region": "us-east-1"
            }
        },
        {
            "type": "metric",
            "properties": {
                "title": "Backup Storage (GB)",
                "metrics": [
                    ["AWS/Backup", "NumberOfRecoveryPoints"],
                ],
                "period": 86400,
                "stat": "Maximum"
            }
        },
        {
            "type": "alarm",
            "properties": {
                "title": "Backup Alarms",
                "alarms": [
                    "arn:aws:cloudwatch:us-east-1:123456789012:alarm:anihpj-backup-failed"
                ]
            }
        }
    ]
}

cloudwatch.put_dashboard(
    DashboardName='anihpj-backup-dashboard',
    DashboardBody=json.dumps(dashboard_body)
)
CloudWatch Alarms for Backup Failures
# Create alarm for failed backup jobs
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-backup-failed" \
    --alarm-description "Alert when AWS Backup job fails" \
    --metric-name "NumberOfBackupJobsFailed" \
    --namespace "AWS/Backup" \
    --statistic Sum \
    --period 3600 \
    --threshold 1 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --alarm-actions "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts" \
    --treat-missing-data notBreaching

# Create alarm for backup job duration (detect slow backups)
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-backup-slow" \
    --alarm-description "Alert when backup takes longer than expected" \
    --metric-name "BackupJobDuration" \
    --namespace "AWS/Backup" \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 3600 \
    --threshold 7200 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --alarm-actions "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts"
📬 SNS Notifications
Configure Backup Event Notifications
# Set up SNS notifications for AWS Backup events
import boto3

sns = boto3.client('sns')
backup = boto3.client('backup')

# Create SNS topic for backup notifications
topic_response = sns.create_topic(Name='anihpj-backup-notifications')
topic_arn = topic_response['TopicArn']

# Subscribe email for alerts
sns.subscribe(
    TopicArn=topic_arn,
    Protocol='email',
    Endpoint='devops@anihpj.com'
)

# Subscribe Lambda for automated remediation
sns.subscribe(
    TopicArn=topic_arn,
    Protocol='lambda',
    Endpoint='arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:backup-failure-handler'
)

# Configure backup vault notifications
backup.put_backup_vault_notifications(
    BackupVaultName='anihpj-prod-vault',
    SNSTopicArn=topic_arn,
    BackupVaultEvents=[
        'BACKUP_JOB_STARTED',
        'BACKUP_JOB_COMPLETED',
        'BACKUP_JOB_FAILED',
        'RESTORE_JOB_STARTED',
        'RESTORE_JOB_COMPLETED',
        'RESTORE_JOB_FAILED',
        'COPY_JOB_STARTED',
        'COPY_JOB_FAILED'
    ]
)

print(f"Notifications configured for vault: anihpj-prod-vault")
⚡ EventBridge Automation
Automated Response to Backup Events
{
    "Comment": "EventBridge rule to trigger Lambda on backup failure",
    "Source": ["aws.backup"],
    "DetailType": ["Backup Job State Change"],
    "Detail": {
        "state": ["FAILED", "ABORTED"]
    }
}

// Lambda handler for automated remediation
// backup_failure_handler.py
import boto3
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Handle AWS Backup failure events"""
    
    backup = boto3.client('backup')
    sns = boto3.client('sns')
    
    # Extract failure details
    job_id = event['detail']['backupJobId']
    resource_arn = event['detail']['resourceArn']
    status_message = event['detail'].get('statusMessage', 'Unknown error')
    
    # Log the failure
    print(f"Backup failed for {resource_arn}: {status_message}")
    
    # Attempt automatic retry for transient failures
    if 'throttling' in status_message.lower() or 'timeout' in status_message.lower():
        print(f"Retrying backup for {resource_arn}")
        backup.start_backup_job(
            BackupVaultName='anihpj-prod-vault',
            ResourceArn=resource_arn,
            IamRoleArn='arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole'
        )
    
    # Send detailed alert
    sns.publish(
        TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts',
        Subject=f'🚨 AWS Backup Failed: {resource_arn.split(":")[-1]}',
        Message=json.dumps({
            'job_id': job_id,
            'resource': resource_arn,
            'error': status_message,
            'action': 'Automatic retry attempted' if 'retry' in status_message.lower() else 'Manual intervention required'
        }, indent=2)
    )
    
    return {'statusCode': 200}
🏢 AWS Organizations Integration
Centralized Backup Policies
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AWS ORGANIZATIONS BACKUP ARCHITECTURE                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                     MANAGEMENT ACCOUNT (Root)                                │   │
│  │  • Define organization-wide backup policies                                  │   │
│  │  • Centralized backup vault (cross-account)                                 │   │
│  │  • Compliance monitoring & reporting                                         │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                        │                                            │
│              ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐                 │
│              │                         │                         │                 │
│              ▼                         ▼                         ▼                 │
│  ┌───────────────────┐   ┌───────────────────┐   ┌───────────────────┐           │
│  │ Production OU     │   │ Staging OU        │   │ Development OU    │           │
│  │ ───────────────── │   │ ───────────────── │   │ ───────────────── │           │
│  │ anihpj-prod       │   │ anihpj-staging    │   │ anihpj-dev        │           │
│  │ • Daily backups   │   │ • Daily backups   │   │ • Weekly backups  │           │
│  │ • 35-day retention│   │ • 14-day retention│   │ • 7-day retention │           │
│  │ • Cross-region DR │   │ • Same-region     │   │ • Same-region     │           │
│  │ • Vault lock ON   │   │ • Vault lock OFF  │   │ • Vault lock OFF  │           │
│  └───────────────────┘   └───────────────────┘   └───────────────────┘           │
│                                                                                      │
│  📋 ORGANIZATION BACKUP POLICY (applied to all OUs):                                │
│  {                                                                                   │
│    "plans": {                                                                        │
│      "OrgDailyBackup": {                                                            │
│        "rules": { "DailyRule": { "schedule": "cron(0 2 * * ? *)" } },              │
│        "selections": { "tags": { "Backup": "daily" } }                             │
│      }                                                                               │
│    }                                                                                 │
│  }                                                                                   │
│                                                                                      │
│  ✅ BENEFITS:                                                                        │
│  • Single policy for entire organization                                            │
│  • Automatic compliance enforcement                                                  │
│  • Centralized audit reports                                                         │
│  • Cross-account backup consolidation                                               │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📊 Monitoring & Observability

Comprehensive monitoring ensures backup reliability and quick issue detection.

📈 Key Metrics to Monitor
  • NumberOfBackupJobsCompleted: Daily successful backups
  • NumberOfBackupJobsFailed: Failed backup count (alert if >0)
  • BackupJobDuration: Time to complete backup
  • NumberOfRecoveryPoints: Total recovery points stored
  • RestoreJobDuration: Time to restore (for DR planning)
🚨 Alerting Strategy
CriticalBackup failedImmediate (PagerDuty)
WarningBackup slow (>2hr)Email + Slack
InfoBackup completedDaily digest
CriticalNo backup in 48hrImmediate
📋 Backup Audit Manager
  • Daily Reports: Backup job summary
  • Compliance Reports: Policy adherence
  • Resource Coverage: Unprotected resources
  • Cross-Region Status: DR vault sync
🔍 CloudTrail Logging
  • API Calls: All backup operations logged
  • Console Actions: User activity audit
  • Restore Events: Who restored what/when
  • Policy Changes: Backup plan modifications
Backup Health Check Script
# Daily backup health check for anihpj
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def check_backup_health():
    """
    Daily health check for AWS Backup
    Run via Lambda + EventBridge schedule
    """
    backup = boto3.client('backup')
    sns = boto3.client('sns')
    
    # Check backups from last 24 hours
    yesterday = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=1)
    
    # Get backup jobs
    jobs = backup.list_backup_jobs(
        ByCreatedAfter=yesterday,
        ByBackupVaultName='anihpj-prod-vault'
    )['BackupJobs']
    
    # Analyze results
    completed = sum(1 for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'COMPLETED')
    failed = sum(1 for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'FAILED')
    running = sum(1 for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'RUNNING')
    
    # Build health report
    health_status = "✅ HEALTHY" if failed == 0 else "🚨 UNHEALTHY"
    
    report = f"""
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    📊 ANIHPJ BACKUP HEALTH REPORT - {datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    
    Status: {health_status}
    
    📈 Last 24 Hours:
    ├── ✅ Completed: {completed}
    ├── ❌ Failed: {failed}
    └── 🔄 Running: {running}
    
    🗄️ Protected Resources:
    ├── RDS: anihpj-production ({'✅' if any('rds' in j.get('ResourceArn', '').lower() for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'COMPLETED') else '❌'})
    ├── EFS: anihpj-uploads ({'✅' if any('elasticfilesystem' in j.get('ResourceArn', '').lower() for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'COMPLETED') else '❌'})
    └── DynamoDB: anihpj-sessions ({'✅' if any('dynamodb' in j.get('ResourceArn', '').lower() for j in jobs if j['State'] == 'COMPLETED') else '❌'})
    
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
    """
    
    # Send report
    if failed > 0:
        sns.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-critical',
            Subject='🚨 AWS Backup Health Check FAILED',
            Message=report
        )
    else:
        sns.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-daily-digest',
            Subject='✅ AWS Backup Daily Report',
            Message=report
        )
    
    return {'status': health_status, 'completed': completed, 'failed': failed}

# Run health check
if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = check_backup_health()
    print(result)

🎯 When to Choose AWS Backup

✅ Choose AWS Backup When:
  • Multiple AWS Services: You need to back up EC2, RDS, DynamoDB, EFS, S3, and other services from a single console
  • Compliance Requirements: You need to meet regulatory requirements (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC, GDPR) with audit-ready backup policies
  • Cross-Region DR: You need disaster recovery with automatic replication to different AWS regions
  • Cross-Account Backups: You manage multiple AWS accounts and need centralized backup management via AWS Organizations
  • Automated Lifecycle: You want automatic transition from warm to cold storage to optimize costs
  • Point-in-Time Recovery: You need continuous backup with granular recovery options
  • Centralized Policies: You want to define backup policies once and apply across your organization
  • Application Consistency: You need VSS-enabled backups for Windows workloads
⚠️ Consider Alternatives When:
  • Single-Service Focus: If only backing up one service type (e.g., only RDS), native service snapshots may be simpler
  • Real-Time Replication: AWS Backup is point-in-time; for zero-RPO, consider DynamoDB Global Tables or Aurora Global Database
  • On-Premises Primary: For hybrid environments with on-prem primary storage, consider AWS Storage Gateway or partner solutions
  • Sub-Minute RPO: Continuous backup has minimum 1-minute granularity; for sub-minute, consider database-native replication
  • Backup to Non-AWS: AWS Backup stores data within AWS; for multi-cloud backup, consider third-party solutions
Use Case AWS Backup Native Snapshots Third-Party Tools
Multi-service backup ⭐ Best choice ❌ Manual per service ✅ Good
Compliance/Audit ⭐ Best choice ⚠️ Manual tracking ✅ Good
Cross-region DR ⭐ Best choice ⚠️ Manual copy ✅ Good
Cost optimization ✅ Cold storage tiers ❌ No lifecycle ✅ Varies
Single database backup ✅ Good ⭐ Simplest ✅ Good
Hybrid/Multi-cloud ❌ AWS only ❌ AWS only ⭐ Best choice
🗺️ AWS Backup Implementation Roadmap
PHASE 1: Foundation (Week 1)
├─ Day 1-2: Vault Setup
│  ├─ Create backup vault (anihpj-prod-vault)
│  ├─ Configure KMS encryption key
│  ├─ Set vault access policy
│  └─ Test: Verify vault creation
│
├─ Day 3-4: Backup Plan Configuration
│  ├─ Create backup plan (anihpj-daily-backup)
│  ├─ Define daily rule (cron 0 2 * * ? *)
│  ├─ Set 35-day retention policy
│  └─ Configure SNS notifications
│
└─ Day 5-7: Resource Assignment
   ├─ Assign RDS database (critical)
   ├─ Assign EFS file system (user uploads)
   ├─ Tag resources for automatic backup
   └─ Run first manual backup - VERIFY!

PHASE 2: Enhanced Protection (Week 2-3)
├─ Week 2: Multi-Service Coverage
│  ├─ Add DynamoDB tables to backup plan
│  ├─ Add S3 buckets (media, static assets)
│  ├─ Configure EC2 AMI backups (weekly)
│  └─ Add Secrets Manager backups
│
└─ Week 3: Disaster Recovery
   ├─ Create DR vault in us-west-2
   ├─ Configure cross-region copy rules
   ├─ Set up lifecycle (warm → cold at 7 days)
   └─ Document restore procedures

PHASE 3: Production Hardening (Month 2)
├─ Week 4: Monitoring & Alerting
│  ├─ Set up CloudWatch alarms (backup failures)
│  ├─ Configure backup job metrics dashboard
│  ├─ Enable AWS Backup Audit Manager
│  └─ Create compliance reports
│
└─ Week 5-8: Optimization & Testing
   ├─ Perform monthly restore drills
   ├─ Analyze backup costs and optimize
   ├─ Enable continuous backup for RDS (optional)
   └─ Review and adjust retention policies

🎯 SUCCESS METRICS:
├─ RPO (Recovery Point Objective): < 24 hours (daily backups)
├─ RTO (Recovery Time Objective): < 1 hour for most resources
├─ Backup Success Rate: 99.9%+
├─ Monthly Cost: $15-30 for typical anihpj setup
└─ Compliance: 100% audit-ready with Backup Audit Manager

Final Recommendation for anihpj

🎯 AWS Backup Implementation Roadmap

For your Django job posting application (anihpj), AWS Backup provides enterprise-grade data protection that grows with your platform.

📅 Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1)
  • Create backup vault with KMS encryption
  • Define daily backup plan for RDS/PostgreSQL
  • Set 30-day retention for warm storage
  • Configure SNS notifications
🚀 Phase 2: Expansion (Week 2-3)
  • Add EBS volume backups (EC2 instances)
  • Implement cross-region copy to DR region
  • Set up lifecycle rules (warm → cold)
  • Create compliance reports
⚡ Phase 3: Optimization (Month 2+)
  • Enable continuous backup for critical DBs
  • Integrate with CloudWatch dashboards
  • Automate restore testing (quarterly)
  • Review and optimize retention policies
💡 Key Benefits for anihpj:
✓ Automated daily backups ✓ 15-minute RTO possible ✓ Cross-region disaster recovery ✓ Compliance-ready audit logs ✓ ~$5-15/month estimated cost

💬 Start with Phase 1 immediately to protect your job posting data. The Python boto3 examples above integrate directly with your Django views for programmatic backup management.

🎉 AWS Backup Quick Start Command: 
# Create your first backup vault for anihpj (run this now!)
aws backup create-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name anihpj-production-vault \
    --backup-vault-tags Project=anihpj,Environment=Production

# Verify creation
aws backup describe-backup-vault --backup-vault-name anihpj-production-vault

🧊 Amazon S3 Glacier

Category: Storage / Archive  |  Use Case: Long-term Archival, Compliance, Data Preservation  |  Pricing: $0.004/GB-month (Glacier) to $0.00099/GB-month (Deep Archive)  |  Documentation: S3 Glacier Docs

Amazon S3 Glacier is the industry's lowest-cost cloud storage for data archiving and long-term backup. Perfect for data you need to retain but rarely access - like compliance records, historical backups, and media archives for your anihpj platform.

🧊 Quick Start: Archive anihpj Data

1️⃣

S3 Lifecycle Rule
Auto-transition old data

2️⃣

Choose Tier
Glacier or Deep Archive

3️⃣

Set Retention
90-day minimum

4️⃣

Save 90%+
vs Standard S3

# Archive old job applications to Glacier (via S3 lifecycle)
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
    --bucket anihpj-archives \
    --lifecycle-configuration '{
        "Rules": [{
            "ID": "ArchiveOldApplications",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {"Prefix": "applications/"},
            "Transitions": [
                {"Days": 90, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"},
                {"Days": 365, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}
            ]
        }]
    }'

📋 Overview & Introduction

Amazon S3 Glacier is a secure, durable, and extremely low-cost cloud storage service for data archiving and long-term backup. It's designed for data that is infrequently accessed and where retrieval times of minutes to hours are acceptable.

🎯 Key Benefits
  • Lowest Cost: Starting at $0.00099/GB-month (Deep Archive) - 95% cheaper than S3 Standard
  • Durability: 99.999999999% (11 9's) durability - data stored across 3+ AZs
  • Compliance Ready: WORM (Write Once Read Many) with Vault Lock for regulatory compliance
  • Flexible Retrieval: Choose retrieval speed (minutes to hours) based on urgency
  • S3 Integration: Seamless lifecycle policies transition data automatically
  • Security: Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (SSL/TLS)
  • Scalability: Store unlimited data with no capacity planning
  • Query-in-Place: Run SQL queries on archived data with S3 Glacier Select
💡 Perfect for anihpj: Archive old job applications, expired job listings, historical user data, compliance logs, and database backups. Keep 7 years of data for just pennies per GB!
⚖️ S3 Glacier vs Storage Alternatives
Feature 🧊 S3 Glacier 🧊🧊 Deep Archive 📦 S3 Standard ❄️ S3 Glacier IR
Cost/GB-month $0.004 $0.00099 $0.023 $0.01
Retrieval Time 1-5 min to 12 hrs 12-48 hours Instant Milliseconds
Min Storage 90 days 180 days None 90 days
Best For Archives, backups Compliance, 7+ yr retention Frequent access Rare but fast access
anihpj Use Old applications Compliance archives Active media Quarterly reports
100GB/month $0.40 $0.10 $2.30 $1.00
🏗️ S3 Glacier Architecture
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           AMAZON S3 GLACIER ARCHITECTURE                              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                       │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │                         DATA LIFECYCLE FLOW                                  │   │
│   │                                                                              │   │
│   │  ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐                 │   │
│   │  │ S3 Standard  │     │ S3 Glacier   │     │ Deep Archive │                 │   │
│   │  │ (Active)     │────▶│ (Archive)    │────▶│ (Long-term)  │                 │   │
│   │  │ $0.023/GB    │ 90d │ $0.004/GB    │365d │ $0.00099/GB  │                 │   │
│   │  └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘                 │   │
│   │        │                     │                    │                          │   │
│   │        │ Instant             │ 3-12 hrs           │ 12-48 hrs                │   │
│   │        ▼                     ▼                    ▼                          │   │
│   │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │   │
│   │  │                    RETRIEVAL OPTIONS                                │     │   │
│   │  │  Expedited: 1-5 min ($$$) │ Standard: 3-5 hrs ($$) │ Bulk: 5-12 hrs ($) │     │   │
│   │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                       │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │                     ANIHPJ ARCHIVE STRATEGY                                  │   │
│   │                                                                              │   │
│   │   ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐          │   │
│   │   │ 📝 Applications │   │ 📋 Job Listings │   │ 🗄️ DB Backups   │          │   │
│   │   │ ─────────────── │   │ ─────────────── │   │ ─────────────── │          │   │
│   │   │ Active: S3 Std  │   │ Active: S3 Std  │   │ Daily: AWS Backup│          │   │
│   │   │ >90d: Glacier   │   │ Expired: Glacier│   │ >35d: Glacier   │          │   │
│   │   │ >1yr: Deep Arc  │   │ >1yr: Deep Arc  │   │ >90d: Deep Arc  │          │   │
│   │   └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘          │   │
│   │                                                                              │   │
│   │   ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐          │   │
│   │   │ 📄 Resumes      │   │ 📊 Analytics    │   │ 📜 Audit Logs   │          │   │
│   │   │ ─────────────── │   │ ─────────────── │   │ ─────────────── │          │   │
│   │   │ Active: S3 Std  │   │ Recent: S3 Std  │   │ <1yr: Glacier   │          │   │
│   │   │ >180d: Glacier  │   │ >90d: Glacier IR│   │ >1yr: Deep Arc  │          │   │
│   │   │ >2yr: Deep Arc  │   │ >1yr: Deep Arc  │   │ 7yr retention   │          │   │
│   │   └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘          │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                       │
│   💰 ANIHPJ SAVINGS EXAMPLE:                                                          │
│   ├── 500GB old applications: S3 Standard = $11.50/mo → Glacier = $2.00/mo          │
│   ├── 200GB compliance logs: S3 Standard = $4.60/mo → Deep Archive = $0.20/mo       │
│   └── Total Annual Savings: ~$165/year (93% cost reduction)                          │
│                                                                                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Core Concepts

Understanding Glacier's architecture helps you design effective archival strategies.

🗄️ Storage Classes
🧊 S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval
  • Cost: $0.004/GB-month
  • Retrieval: Milliseconds
  • Min Duration: 90 days
  • Use: Rarely accessed, instant need
  • anihpj: Quarterly reports, old dashboards
🧊 S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
  • Cost: $0.0036/GB-month
  • Retrieval: 1-5 min to 12 hrs
  • Min Duration: 90 days
  • Use: Backups, archives
  • anihpj: Old applications, expired jobs
🧊🧊 S3 Glacier Deep Archive
  • Cost: $0.00099/GB-month
  • Retrieval: 12-48 hours
  • Min Duration: 180 days
  • Use: Compliance, 7+ yr retention
  • anihpj: Tax records, legal compliance
🏦 Vaults & Archives
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        GLACIER VAULTS & ARCHIVES STRUCTURE                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  📦 VAULT (Container)                                                                │
│  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────     │
│  A vault is a container for storing archives. Similar to an S3 bucket.              │
│                                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  🏦 anihpj-compliance-vault (us-east-1)                                      │   │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │   │
│  │  │                                                                          │   │
│  │  │  📁 Archives (immutable data units)                                      │   │
│  │  │  ├── archive-001: applications_2023_q1.tar.gz (1.2 GB)                  │   │
│  │  │  ├── archive-002: applications_2023_q2.tar.gz (1.5 GB)                  │   │
│  │  │  ├── archive-003: db_backup_2023_01_01.sql.gz (500 MB)                  │   │
│  │  │  └── archive-004: audit_logs_2023.tar.gz (200 MB)                       │   │
│  │  │                                                                          │   │
│  │  │  🔒 Vault Lock Policy: WORM (compliance mode)                            │   │
│  │  │  📋 Notification: SNS topic for job completion                          │   │
│  │  │  📊 Inventory: Daily inventory report to S3                              │   │
│  │  │                                                                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⚠️ KEY DIFFERENCES FROM S3:                                                         │
│  ├── Archives are immutable (cannot modify, only delete)                            │
│  ├── No real-time listing (inventory takes hours)                                   │
│  ├── Retrieval requires job initiation (async)                                      │
│  └── Archive ID is auto-generated (not human-readable)                              │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📤 Retrieval Options
Retrieval Tier Glacier Flexible Deep Archive Cost (per GB) When to Use
⚡ Expedited 1-5 minutes N/A $0.03 Urgent: legal discovery, emergency restore
📋 Standard 3-5 hours 12 hours $0.01 Normal: planned restores, periodic access
📦 Bulk 5-12 hours 48 hours $0.0025 Cost-optimized: large datasets, batch jobs
🔄 Lifecycle Policies
Automatic Data Tiering with S3 Lifecycle
{
    "Rules": [
        {
            "ID": "AnihpjApplicationsArchive",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {
                "Prefix": "applications/"
            },
            "Transitions": [
                {
                    "Days": 90,
                    "StorageClass": "GLACIER"
                },
                {
                    "Days": 365,
                    "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"
                }
            ],
            "Expiration": {
                "Days": 2555
            },
            "NoncurrentVersionTransitions": [
                {
                    "NoncurrentDays": 30,
                    "StorageClass": "GLACIER"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "ID": "AnihpjLogsArchive",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {
                "Prefix": "logs/"
            },
            "Transitions": [
                {
                    "Days": 30,
                    "StorageClass": "GLACIER_IR"
                },
                {
                    "Days": 180,
                    "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"
                }
            ],
            "Expiration": {
                "Days": 2555
            }
        }
    ]
}

🚀 Practical Setup

Step-by-step guide to implementing Glacier archiving for anihpj.

🏦 Step 1: Create Glacier Vault (Direct API)
# Create a Glacier vault for compliance archives
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault

# Verify vault creation
aws glacier describe-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault

# Set vault notifications (SNS topic for job completion)
aws glacier set-vault-notifications \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --vault-notification-config '{
        "SNSTopic": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:glacier-notifications",
        "Events": ["ArchiveRetrievalCompleted", "InventoryRetrievalCompleted"]
    }'

# Enable vault lock for WORM compliance (initiate)
aws glacier initiate-vault-lock \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --policy '{
        "Policy": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[{\"Sid\":\"deny-delete\",\"Effect\":\"Deny\",\"Principal\":\"*\",\"Action\":\"glacier:DeleteArchive\",\"Resource\":\"*\",\"Condition\":{\"NumericLessThan\":{\"glacier:ArchiveAgeInDays\":\"365\"}}}]}"
    }'
📤 Step 2: Upload Archives
# Upload a single archive (small file <100MB)
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --archive-description "Job applications Q1 2024" \
    --body applications_2024_q1.tar.gz

# Response includes archive-id (SAVE THIS!)
# {
#     "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-compliance-vault/archives/abc123...",
#     "checksum": "sha256hash...",
#     "archiveId": "abc123def456..."
# }

# For large files (>100MB), use multipart upload
aws glacier initiate-multipart-upload \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --archive-description "Database backup Jan 2024" \
    --part-size 1048576
📥 Step 3: Retrieve Data
# Step 1: Initiate retrieval job (Standard tier - 3-5 hours)
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "abc123def456...",
        "Tier": "Standard",
        "Description": "Retrieve Q1 applications for audit",
        "SNSTopic": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:glacier-notifications"
    }'

# Returns job ID - wait for completion notification

# Step 2: Check job status
aws glacier describe-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --job-id "job-id-from-previous-command"

# Step 3: Download when job completes (StatusCode: Succeeded)
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --job-id "job-id" \
    output_file.tar.gz

# For expedited retrieval (1-5 minutes, higher cost)
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "abc123def456...",
        "Tier": "Expedited"
    }'
🔗 Step 4: S3 Lifecycle Integration (Recommended)
Configure S3 bucket to auto-archive to Glacier
# Create S3 bucket for archivable data
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-archives --region us-east-1

# Apply lifecycle policy
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
    --bucket anihpj-archives \
    --lifecycle-configuration file://lifecycle-policy.json
lifecycle-policy.json
{
    "Rules": [
        {
            "ID": "ArchiveApplications",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {"Prefix": "applications/"},
            "Transitions": [
                {"Days": 90, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"},
                {"Days": 365, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}
            ]
        },
        {
            "ID": "ArchiveJobListings",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {"Prefix": "jobs/expired/"},
            "Transitions": [
                {"Days": 30, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"},
                {"Days": 180, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}
            ]
        },
        {
            "ID": "ArchiveResumes",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {"Prefix": "resumes/"},
            "Transitions": [
                {"Days": 180, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"},
                {"Days": 730, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}
            ],
            "Expiration": {"Days": 2555}
        },
        {
            "ID": "ArchiveAuditLogs",
            "Status": "Enabled",
            "Filter": {"Prefix": "audit-logs/"},
            "Transitions": [
                {"Days": 90, "StorageClass": "GLACIER_IR"},
                {"Days": 365, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}
            ],
            "Expiration": {"Days": 2555}
        }
    ]
}
💡 S3 vs Direct Glacier API:
  • S3 Lifecycle (Recommended): Easier management, automatic transitions, S3 console access
  • Direct Glacier API: More control, vault lock for compliance, but complex inventory management
For anihpj, use S3 lifecycle rules for most archiving needs!

💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

Maximize savings with proper Glacier tier selection and retrieval strategies.

💵 Pricing Model
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         S3 GLACIER PRICING BREAKDOWN                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  💾 STORAGE COSTS (per GB-month, us-east-1)                                          │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Storage Class          │ Cost/GB    │ Min Duration │ 1TB/month │ vs S3 Standard   │
│  ───────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────┼───────────┼─────────────────  │
│  S3 Standard            │ $0.023     │ None         │ $23.00    │ Baseline         │
│  S3 Glacier IR          │ $0.004     │ 90 days      │ $4.00     │ 83% cheaper      │
│  S3 Glacier Flexible    │ $0.0036    │ 90 days      │ $3.60     │ 84% cheaper      │
│  S3 Glacier Deep Archive│ $0.00099   │ 180 days     │ $0.99     │ 96% cheaper      │
│                                                                                      │
│  📤 RETRIEVAL COSTS (per GB retrieved)                                               │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Tier          │ Glacier Flexible │ Deep Archive │ Time           │               │
│  ──────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────┼────────────────┼               │
│  Expedited     │ $0.03            │ N/A          │ 1-5 min        │               │
│  Standard      │ $0.01            │ $0.02        │ 3-5 hr / 12 hr │               │
│  Bulk          │ $0.0025          │ $0.0025      │ 5-12 hr / 48 hr│               │
│                                                                                      │
│  📋 REQUEST COSTS                                                                    │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  • Lifecycle transition: $0.05 per 1,000 requests                                   │
│  • Archive retrieval request: $0.05 per 1,000 requests (Glacier)                   │
│  • Deep Archive retrieval: $0.10 per 1,000 requests                                │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⚠️ EARLY DELETION CHARGES                                                           │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  • Glacier (90 day min): Prorated charge if deleted before 90 days                 │
│  • Deep Archive (180 day min): Prorated charge if deleted before 180 days          │
│  • Example: Delete 10GB from Deep Archive after 30 days = charged for 150 days     │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📉 Cost Strategies
💡 Cost Optimization Tips
  • Right-size tiers: Use Deep Archive for truly cold data
  • Batch retrievals: Use Bulk tier for large restores (75% cheaper)
  • Plan transitions: Avoid early deletion fees
  • Aggregate small files: Combine into tarballs before archiving
  • Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering: For unpredictable access patterns
📊 anihpj Monthly Estimate
Archive Storage:
├── Applications (Glacier): 
│   └── 500GB × $0.0036 = $1.80
├── Compliance (Deep Archive):
│   └── 200GB × $0.00099 = $0.20
├── Old Resumes (Glacier):
│   └── 100GB × $0.0036 = $0.36
│
├── Retrieval (Bulk, 10GB/mo):
│   └── 10GB × $0.0025 = $0.03
│
└── TOTAL: ~$2.50/month
    vs S3 Standard: ~$18.40/month
    SAVINGS: 86%

🐍 Python (boto3) Integration

Programmatic access to Glacier for automation and Django integration.

⚙️ Setup & Authentication
# Install required packages
# pip install boto3

import boto3
from botocore.config import Config

# Configure Glacier client
glacier = boto3.client(
    'glacier',
    region_name='us-east-1',
    config=Config(
        retries={'max_attempts': 3},
        connect_timeout=60,
        read_timeout=60
    )
)

# For S3 Glacier (via S3 lifecycle) - more common approach
s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')

# Verify connection
vaults = glacier.list_vaults()
print(f"Found {len(vaults['VaultList'])} vaults")
🔧 Glacier Operations
Complete Glacier Utility Class
import boto3
import json
import hashlib
import os
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

class GlacierArchiver:
    """
    AWS Glacier archiving utility for anihpj
    Handles vault management, uploads, and retrievals
    """
    
    def __init__(self, vault_name: str = 'anihpj-compliance-vault'):
        self.glacier = boto3.client('glacier')
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3')
        self.vault_name = vault_name
        self.account_id = '-'  # Use '-' for current account
        
    def create_vault(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Create a new Glacier vault"""
        response = self.glacier.create_vault(
            accountId=self.account_id,
            vaultName=self.vault_name
        )
        print(f"✅ Vault created: {self.vault_name}")
        return response
    
    def upload_archive(self, file_path: str, description: str) -> str:
        """
        Upload a file to Glacier vault
        Returns archive ID (SAVE THIS for retrieval!)
        """
        with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
            body = f.read()
        
        response = self.glacier.upload_archive(
            accountId=self.account_id,
            vaultName=self.vault_name,
            archiveDescription=description,
            body=body
        )
        
        archive_id = response['archiveId']
        print(f"✅ Uploaded: {description}")
        print(f"📋 Archive ID: {archive_id[:50]}...")
        
        # IMPORTANT: Store archive_id in your database!
        return archive_id
    
    def initiate_retrieval(
        self, 
        archive_id: str, 
        tier: str = 'Standard',
        description: str = 'Archive retrieval'
    ) -> str:
        """
        Initiate archive retrieval job
        Tiers: 'Expedited' (1-5 min), 'Standard' (3-5 hr), 'Bulk' (5-12 hr)
        """
        response = self.glacier.initiate_job(
            accountId=self.account_id,
            vaultName=self.vault_name,
            jobParameters={
                'Type': 'archive-retrieval',
                'ArchiveId': archive_id,
                'Tier': tier,
                'Description': description
            }
        )
        
        job_id = response['jobId']
        print(f"✅ Retrieval job started: {job_id}")
        print(f"⏱️ Tier: {tier}")
        return job_id
    
    def check_job_status(self, job_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Check status of retrieval job"""
        response = self.glacier.describe_job(
            accountId=self.account_id,
            vaultName=self.vault_name,
            jobId=job_id
        )
        
        status = response['StatusCode']
        print(f"📋 Job Status: {status}")
        
        if status == 'Succeeded':
            print("✅ Job complete! Ready to download.")
        elif status == 'InProgress':
            print(f"⏳ In progress... Check back later.")
            
        return response
    
    def download_output(self, job_id: str, output_path: str) -> str:
        """Download completed job output"""
        response = self.glacier.get_job_output(
            accountId=self.account_id,
            vaultName=self.vault_name,
            jobId=job_id
        )
        
        with open(output_path, 'wb') as f:
            f.write(response['body'].read())
        
        print(f"✅ Downloaded to: {output_path}")
        return output_path

# Usage example
if __name__ == '__main__':
    archiver = GlacierArchiver()
    
    # Upload old applications
    archive_id = archiver.upload_archive(
        'applications_2024_q1.tar.gz',
        'Job applications Q1 2024 - anihpj compliance archive'
    )
    
    # Later: retrieve archive
    job_id = archiver.initiate_retrieval(
        archive_id,
        tier='Standard',
        description='Audit retrieval request #12345'
    )
    
    # Check status (run after a few hours)
    status = archiver.check_job_status(job_id)
🐍 Django Integration
Archive old job applications automatically
# jobpost/services/archiver.py
import boto3
import tarfile
import tempfile
import os
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from django.conf import settings
from jobpost.models import Application, Job

class ApplicationArchiver:
    """
    Archive old job applications to S3 Glacier
    Run as a scheduled task (e.g., monthly via Celery)
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3')
        self.archive_bucket = 'anihpj-archives'
        
    def archive_old_applications(self, days_old: int = 90):
        """
        Archive applications older than X days to Glacier storage class
        """
        cutoff_date = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=days_old)
        
        # Get old applications
        old_apps = Application.objects.filter(
            created_at__lt=cutoff_date,
            archived=False
        ).select_related('job', 'applicant')
        
        if not old_apps.exists():
            print("No applications to archive")
            return
        
        # Create archive file
        archive_name = f"applications_{cutoff_date.strftime('%Y_%m')}.tar.gz"
        
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.tar.gz', delete=False) as tmp:
            with tarfile.open(tmp.name, 'w:gz') as tar:
                for app in old_apps:
                    # Create JSON for each application
                    app_data = {
                        'id': app.id,
                        'job_title': app.job.title,
                        'job_company': app.job.company.name,
                        'applicant_email': app.applicant.email,
                        'applied_at': app.created_at.isoformat(),
                        'status': app.status,
                        'resume_url': app.resume.url if app.resume else None,
                        'cover_letter': app.cover_letter
                    }
                    
                    # Add to archive
                    json_bytes = json.dumps(app_data, indent=2).encode('utf-8')
                    info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"app_{app.id}.json")
                    info.size = len(json_bytes)
                    tar.addfile(info, io.BytesIO(json_bytes))
            
            # Upload to S3 with Glacier storage class
            self.s3.upload_file(
                tmp.name,
                self.archive_bucket,
                f"applications/{archive_name}",
                ExtraArgs={
                    'StorageClass': 'GLACIER',
                    'Metadata': {
                        'archived-date': datetime.now().isoformat(),
                        'record-count': str(old_apps.count()),
                        'cutoff-date': cutoff_date.isoformat()
                    }
                }
            )
        
        # Mark applications as archived
        old_apps.update(archived=True, archived_at=datetime.now())
        
        # Clean up temp file
        os.unlink(tmp.name)
        
        print(f"✅ Archived {old_apps.count()} applications to Glacier")
        return archive_name
    
    def restore_application(self, archive_key: str, days: int = 7):
        """
        Initiate restore from Glacier (async - takes 3-5 hours)
        """
        self.s3.restore_object(
            Bucket=self.archive_bucket,
            Key=archive_key,
            RestoreRequest={
                'Days': days,  # Keep restored copy for X days
                'GlacierJobParameters': {
                    'Tier': 'Standard'  # or 'Expedited', 'Bulk'
                }
            }
        )
        print(f"✅ Restore initiated for {archive_key}")
        print("⏱️ Will be available in 3-5 hours")

# Django management command
# jobpost/management/commands/archive_applications.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from jobpost.services.archiver import ApplicationArchiver

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Archive old job applications to S3 Glacier'
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument('--days', type=int, default=90)
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        archiver = ApplicationArchiver()
        result = archiver.archive_old_applications(options['days'])
        self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS(f'Archived to: {result}'))

⌨️ AWS CLI Reference

Essential CLI commands for Glacier operations.

🗄️ Vault Management
# Create a vault
aws glacier create-vault \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id -

# List all vaults
aws glacier list-vaults --account-id -

# Describe a vault
aws glacier describe-vault \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id -

# Delete a vault (must be empty)
aws glacier delete-vault \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id -

# Set vault notifications (for job completions)
aws glacier set-vault-notifications \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-notification-config '{
        "SNSTopic": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:glacier-notifications",
        "Events": ["ArchiveRetrievalCompleted", "InventoryRetrievalCompleted"]
    }'
📦 Archive Operations
# Upload an archive
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --body applications_2024_q1.tar.gz \
    --archive-description "Job applications Q1 2024"

# Delete an archive (requires archive ID)
aws glacier delete-archive \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --archive-id "ARCHIVE_ID_HERE"

# Multipart upload for large files (>100MB)
# Step 1: Initiate
aws glacier initiate-multipart-upload \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --archive-description "Large archive" \
    --part-size 4194304

# Step 2: Upload parts (repeat for each part)
aws glacier upload-multipart-part \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --upload-id "UPLOAD_ID" \
    --range "bytes 0-4194303/*" \
    --body part1.dat

# Step 3: Complete upload
aws glacier complete-multipart-upload \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --upload-id "UPLOAD_ID" \
    --archive-size 8388608 \
    --checksum "TREE_HASH"
📥 Retrieval Operations
# Initiate archive retrieval (Standard tier - 3-5 hours)
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "ARCHIVE_ID_HERE",
        "Tier": "Standard",
        "Description": "Audit retrieval request"
    }'

# Initiate archive retrieval (Bulk tier - cheapest, 5-12 hours)
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "ARCHIVE_ID_HERE",
        "Tier": "Bulk"
    }'

# Request vault inventory (to list all archives)
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --job-parameters '{"Type": "inventory-retrieval"}'

# List jobs (check retrieval status)
aws glacier list-jobs \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id -

# Describe specific job
aws glacier describe-job \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --job-id "JOB_ID_HERE"

# Download job output (after job completes)
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --vault-name anihpj-compliance-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --job-id "JOB_ID_HERE" \
    output_file.tar.gz
🔗 S3 Glacier Integration (via S3)
# Copy to Glacier storage class directly
aws s3 cp applications.tar.gz s3://anihpj-archives/backups/ \
    --storage-class GLACIER

# Copy to Deep Archive
aws s3 cp compliance_data.tar.gz s3://anihpj-archives/compliance/ \
    --storage-class DEEP_ARCHIVE

# Check storage class of an object
aws s3api head-object \
    --bucket anihpj-archives \
    --key backups/applications.tar.gz \
    --query 'StorageClass'

# Restore from Glacier (initiate)
aws s3api restore-object \
    --bucket anihpj-archives \
    --key backups/applications.tar.gz \
    --restore-request '{
        "Days": 7,
        "GlacierJobParameters": {"Tier": "Standard"}
    }'

# Check restore status
aws s3api head-object \
    --bucket anihpj-archives \
    --key backups/applications.tar.gz \
    --query 'Restore'

# Bulk restore multiple objects (via S3 Batch)
aws s3api create-job \
    --account-id 123456789012 \
    --operation '{"S3InitiateRestoreObject": {"ExpirationInDays": 7}}' \
    --manifest '{"Spec": {"Format": "S3BatchOperations_CSV_20180820", "Fields": ["Bucket", "Key"]}, "Location": {"ObjectArn": "arn:aws:s3:::manifest-bucket/restore-manifest.csv", "ETag": "ETAG"}}' \
    --report '{"Bucket": "arn:aws:s3:::report-bucket", "Format": "Report_CSV_20180820", "Enabled": true, "ReportScope": "AllTasks"}' \
    --priority 10 \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/S3BatchRole

✅ Best Practices

Production-ready guidelines for Glacier archiving.

🏗️ Architecture
  • Use S3 lifecycle rules over direct Glacier API
  • Aggregate small files into tarballs before archiving
  • Store archive IDs in a database (not retrievable later!)
  • Enable versioning on source S3 buckets
  • Use separate buckets for different data classifications
💰 Cost Control
  • Use Bulk retrieval tier for non-urgent restores (75% cheaper)
  • Calculate minimum storage duration before archiving
  • Monitor retrieval costs with AWS Cost Explorer
  • Set up billing alerts for unexpected retrievals
  • Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering for unpredictable access
🔒 Security
  • Enable Vault Lock for WORM compliance
  • Use SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS encryption
  • Implement least-privilege IAM policies
  • Enable CloudTrail for audit logging
  • Use S3 Object Lock for immutable archives
⚙️ Operations
  • Set up SNS notifications for job completions
  • Document retrieval procedures for DR
  • Test restore process regularly
  • Maintain inventory manifest for compliance
  • Tag archives with retention metadata
⚠️ Critical Reminders:
  • Archive IDs are not retrievable: Store them in your database when uploading
  • Retrieval takes hours: Plan ahead for audits and DR scenarios
  • Early deletion costs: Don't archive files you might need within 90-180 days
  • No partial restores: Must restore entire archive, then extract what you need

💼 anihpj Use Cases

Real-world archiving scenarios for the job posting platform.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       ANIHPJ GLACIER ARCHIVING STRATEGY                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  📊 DATA FLOW & LIFECYCLE                                                            │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  [Active Data]     [Infrequent]      [Archive]        [Deep Archive]                │
│       ▼                 ▼                ▼                  ▼                       │
│  ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────────┐                 │
│  │   S3    │  →   │   S3    │  →   │ Glacier │  →   │    Deep     │                 │
│  │Standard │ 30d  │   IA    │ 60d  │Flex/IR  │ 180d │   Archive   │                 │
│  └─────────┘      └─────────┘      └─────────┘      └─────────────┘                 │
│       │                                                    │                        │
│  Applications     Old postings      Compliance       7-year retention              │
│  Resumes          Closed jobs       Audit logs       Legal hold                    │
│                                                                                      │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  📁 BUCKET ORGANIZATION                                                             │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  anihpj-production/                                                                 │
│  ├── uploads/             ← Active resumes & documents (Standard)                  │
│  ├── static/              ← Website assets (Standard)                              │
│  └── exports/             ← Reports & analytics (Standard)                         │
│                                                                                      │
│  anihpj-archives/                                                                   │
│  ├── applications/        ← Old applications (Glacier Flexible)                    │
│  │   ├── 2023/                                                                      │
│  │   ├── 2024/                                                                      │
│  │   └── manifest.json    ← Archive inventory                                      │
│  ├── jobs/                ← Closed job postings (Glacier Flexible)                 │
│  ├── audit-logs/          ← CloudTrail & app logs (Glacier IR)                     │
│  └── compliance/          ← Legal retention (Deep Archive + Object Lock)           │
│                                                                                      │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⏱️ LIFECYCLE RULES                                                                 │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  Rule 1: Applications Archive                                                        │
│  ├── Target: applications/*                                                         │
│  ├── Transition: Standard → Glacier Flexible after 90 days                         │
│  └── Expire: Never (compliance requirement)                                        │
│                                                                                      │
│  Rule 2: Job Postings Archive                                                        │
│  ├── Target: jobs/closed/*                                                          │
│  ├── Transition: Standard → Glacier IR after 30 days                               │
│  ├── Transition: Glacier IR → Deep Archive after 365 days                          │
│  └── Expire: After 7 years                                                          │
│                                                                                      │
│  Rule 3: Audit Logs Retention                                                        │
│  ├── Target: audit-logs/*                                                           │
│  ├── Transition: Standard → Glacier IR after 7 days                                │
│  └── Object Lock: Governance mode, 7 years retention                               │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📄 Application Archives

Use Case: Store job applications after hiring decision

  • Data: Resumes, cover letters, interview notes
  • Volume: ~50GB/year (10,000 applications)
  • Storage: Glacier Flexible Retrieval
  • Retention: 5 years (compliance)
  • Access: Rare (audit requests only)
📊 Compliance Data

Use Case: Legal and regulatory retention

  • Data: EEO reports, audit trails, contracts
  • Volume: ~10GB/year
  • Storage: Deep Archive + Vault Lock
  • Retention: 7+ years (WORM)
  • Access: Legal discovery only
📝 Audit Logs

Use Case: Security and access logs

  • Data: CloudTrail, application logs, access logs
  • Volume: ~100GB/year
  • Storage: Glacier Instant Retrieval
  • Retention: 1-3 years
  • Access: Monthly for security reviews
🗃️ Database Backups

Use Case: Long-term DB snapshots

  • Data: PostgreSQL dumps, RDS snapshots
  • Volume: ~200GB/year
  • Storage: Glacier Flexible → Deep Archive
  • Retention: 30 days active, 7 years cold
  • Access: Disaster recovery only

🔐 IAM Policies

Least-privilege policies for Glacier access.

👁️ Read-Only Access
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "GlacierReadOnly",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "glacier:DescribeVault",
                "glacier:ListVaults",
                "glacier:ListJobs",
                "glacier:DescribeJob",
                "glacier:GetVaultAccessPolicy",
                "glacier:GetVaultNotifications"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:glacier:us-east-1:123456789012:vaults/*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3GlacierRead",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:GetObject",
                "s3:GetObjectVersion",
                "s3:ListBucket",
                "s3:GetBucketLocation"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives",
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives/*"
            ]
        }
    ]
}
📦 Archiver Role (Upload Only)
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "GlacierUpload",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "glacier:UploadArchive",
                "glacier:UploadMultipartPart",
                "glacier:InitiateMultipartUpload",
                "glacier:CompleteMultipartUpload",
                "glacier:AbortMultipartUpload",
                "glacier:ListParts",
                "glacier:ListMultipartUploads"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:glacier:us-east-1:123456789012:vaults/anihpj-*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3GlacierUpload",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:PutObject",
                "s3:PutObjectTagging"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives/*",
            "Condition": {
                "StringEquals": {
                    "s3:x-amz-storage-class": [
                        "GLACIER",
                        "DEEP_ARCHIVE",
                        "GLACIER_IR"
                    ]
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}
📥 Restorer Role (Retrieval Only)
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "GlacierRetrieve",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "glacier:InitiateJob",
                "glacier:DescribeJob",
                "glacier:ListJobs",
                "glacier:GetJobOutput"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:glacier:us-east-1:123456789012:vaults/anihpj-*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3GlacierRestore",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:RestoreObject",
                "s3:GetObject"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives/*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "RequireMFA",
            "Effect": "Deny",
            "Action": "glacier:*",
            "Resource": "*",
            "Condition": {
                "BoolIfExists": {
                    "aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "false"
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}
🔑 Full Admin (with Vault Lock)
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "GlacierFullAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": "glacier:*",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:glacier:us-east-1:123456789012:vaults/anihpj-*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "S3GlacierFullAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "s3:*"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives",
                "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archives/*"
            ]
        },
        {
            "Sid": "DenyVaultLockAbort",
            "Effect": "Deny",
            "Action": "glacier:AbortVaultLock",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:glacier:us-east-1:123456789012:vaults/anihpj-compliance-*",
            "Condition": {
                "StringNotEquals": {
                    "aws:PrincipalTag/Department": "Legal"
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}

📋 Quick Reference Cheat Sheet

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           S3 GLACIER QUICK REFERENCE                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                      │
│  📦 STORAGE CLASSES AT A GLANCE                                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Class              │ Cost/GB   │ Min Store │ Retrieval      │ Best For           │
│  ───────────────────┼───────────┼───────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────│
│  Glacier IR         │ $0.004    │ 90 days   │ Milliseconds   │ Monthly access     │
│  Glacier Flexible   │ $0.0036   │ 90 days   │ 1-12 hours     │ Yearly access      │
│  Deep Archive       │ $0.00099  │ 180 days  │ 12-48 hours    │ Compliance/DR      │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⏱️ RETRIEVAL TIMES & COSTS                                                         │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  Tier       │ Glacier Flexible  │ Deep Archive   │ Cost/GB                         │
│  ───────────┼───────────────────┼────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────│
│  Expedited  │ 1-5 minutes       │ N/A            │ $0.03 (Glacier only)            │
│  Standard   │ 3-5 hours         │ 12 hours       │ $0.01 / $0.02                   │
│  Bulk       │ 5-12 hours        │ 48 hours       │ $0.0025                         │
│                                                                                      │
│  🔧 ESSENTIAL CLI COMMANDS                                                          │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Upload to Glacier via S3                                                         │
│  aws s3 cp file.tar.gz s3://bucket/key --storage-class GLACIER                     │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Restore from Glacier                                                             │
│  aws s3api restore-object --bucket BUCKET --key KEY \                              │
│      --restore-request '{"Days":7,"GlacierJobParameters":{"Tier":"Standard"}}'     │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Check restore status                                                             │
│  aws s3api head-object --bucket BUCKET --key KEY --query 'Restore'                 │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Direct Glacier upload                                                            │
│  aws glacier upload-archive --vault-name VAULT --body file.tar.gz                  │
│                                                                                      │
│  🐍 PYTHON ESSENTIALS                                                               │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Upload with Glacier storage class                                                │
│  s3.upload_file('file.tar.gz', 'bucket', 'key',                                    │
│      ExtraArgs={'StorageClass': 'GLACIER'})                                        │
│                                                                                      │
│  # Restore object                                                                   │
│  s3.restore_object(Bucket='bucket', Key='key',                                     │
│      RestoreRequest={'Days': 7, 'GlacierJobParameters': {'Tier': 'Standard'}})     │
│                                                                                      │
│  📊 KEY LIMITS                                                                       │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  • Max archive size (single PUT): 4GB                                               │
│  • Max archive size (multipart): 40TB                                               │
│  • Max vaults per account: 1,000                                                    │
│  • Min object size for Glacier: 128KB (smaller billed as 128KB)                    │
│  • Inventory frequency: Daily (vault) or Lifecycle (S3)                            │
│                                                                                      │
│  ⚡ LIFECYCLE RULE TEMPLATE                                                          │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  {                                                                                   │
│    "Rules": [{                                                                       │
│      "ID": "ArchiveOldData",                                                        │
│      "Filter": {"Prefix": "archives/"},                                             │
│      "Status": "Enabled",                                                           │
│      "Transitions": [                                                               │
│        {"Days": 30, "StorageClass": "GLACIER"},                                     │
│        {"Days": 365, "StorageClass": "DEEP_ARCHIVE"}                                │
│      ]                                                                               │
│    }]                                                                                │
│  }                                                                                   │
│                                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions for Glacier operations.

❌ RestoreAlreadyInProgress

Cause: Restore already initiated for this object

Solution: Wait for current restore to complete, check status with head-object

aws s3api head-object \
  --bucket bucket \
  --key key \
  --query 'Restore'
❌ GlacierExpeditedRetrievalNotAvailable

Cause: Expedited capacity not available

Solution: Purchase provisioned capacity or use Standard/Bulk tier

# Purchase provisioned capacity
aws glacier purchase-provisioned-capacity \
  --account-id -
❌ ObjectNotInActiveTierError

Cause: Object is in Glacier, can't be accessed directly

Solution: Initiate restore, wait for completion, then access

# Restore first
aws s3api restore-object \
  --bucket bucket --key key \
  --restore-request '{"Days":7,
    "GlacierJobParameters":
    {"Tier":"Standard"}}'
❌ InvalidObjectState

Cause: Object storage class doesn't support operation

Solution: Check storage class, ensure object is restored before GET

# Check storage class
aws s3api head-object \
  --bucket bucket \
  --key key \
  --query 'StorageClass'
🔍 Debugging Retrieval Issues
# Check object status and restore progress
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

def check_glacier_object(bucket, key):
    """Check Glacier object status and restore progress"""
    try:
        response = s3.head_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)
        
        storage_class = response.get('StorageClass', 'STANDARD')
        restore_status = response.get('Restore', 'Not in Glacier')
        
        print(f"📦 Object: s3://{bucket}/{key}")
        print(f"   Storage Class: {storage_class}")
        print(f"   Restore Status: {restore_status}")
        
        if 'ongoing-request="true"' in str(restore_status):
            print("   ⏳ Restore in progress...")
        elif 'ongoing-request="false"' in str(restore_status):
            # Parse expiry date
            import re
            match = re.search(r'expiry-date="([^"]+)"', str(restore_status))
            if match:
                print(f"   ✅ Restored! Available until: {match.group(1)}")
        elif storage_class in ['GLACIER', 'DEEP_ARCHIVE', 'GLACIER_IR']:
            print("   ❄️ Not yet restored. Initiate restore first.")
        
        return response
        
    except s3.exceptions.ClientError as e:
        print(f"❌ Error: {e.response['Error']['Message']}")
        return None

# Usage
check_glacier_object('anihpj-archives', 'applications/2024/apps.tar.gz')

📖 Glossary

Term Definition
